text: Prophecy
It was a resounding victory.

The light shined bright from the hole in the ceiling of the ruined underground city of an accursed Empire of NoirElDems, a broken husk of the once unstoppable monster of dark magic, slavery, and biomancy. Right on top of the rubble, basking in the light, stood a flag of victors.

A blue cloth with the silver image of the globe surrounded by leaves and a sword piercing it, a flag of the United Earth Federation. Dozens of dropships were pouring through the hole, and columns of tanks were passing around the rubble, on top of which stood the flag. The light has won.
Yet Miala stood there in confusion. How could the ancient prophecy be wrong?
How could anyone challenge fate itself and prevail?
Yet it also was her deepest and darkest secret when so many years ago she came to humans asking for help, knowing full well she was dragging them into the end of the world.

“The end of the world is nigh, the day when the order shall fall, and shadow shall be cast upon you all by the forces greater than you. The day when your gods shall be cast low, and you shall bow before those who cast shadows upon you.” Miala pronounced in confusion as general Jake Abramson approached her.

“A bit of a strange choice of words for the victory speech High council Miala.” He said in confusion if anything, he was finally looking forward to returning home to Earth. A home he had not seen for years by now, and the last thing he needed was some ominous gibberish from an old friend.

“Oh those are not my words, general those are the words from a prophecy of one of our greatest oracles who is long dead now. And you have yourself witnessed that our oracles are never wrong, so can you see my confusion? When I came before your people begging for help…” Miala’s eyes started to tear up in shame.

“Say what you need to say. You are the leader of MaxEldem’s it is not befitting of your stature not to say what you think, besides we’ve been friends through some tough times what could go wrong now” Jake began realizing where it was going, and the spiritual nature of her people as well their ability to see things in perspective given their lifespan of millennia.

“I knew of that prophecy and kept it hidden from everyone in fear of damaging the will to fight, but I knew those were the end times for us and I, without hesitation, dragged your people into our downfall. All to buy us just a little more time away from the tyranny of our cousins, just get an extra day in the light” She finally confessed the secret she had been carrying for so long, but it was so hollow in victory, and one word kept pulsating in her head, coward.

“Here’s what I learned about your so-called oracles and prophets they are no different from all those charlatans from Earth's history they create self-fulfilling prophecies and play with words to appear to be right all the time as such, I learned never to take heed to the prophecies and do what you believe is right just like the way you did so take solace in that.” General spoke with a kind smile on his face, and then he looked at his marching forces. As he stared into those heavily armored columns going into the dark caverns of the NoirElDem home planet, his face looked the same as at the start of this war 10 years ago, determined, alert and predatory, but something was different this time around.

“What troubles you, exalted general of Steel Eagle?” Miala noticed the change in the face of her friend, who so expertly raised her spirits just a moment ago, hoping that it was something she could offer in return.

“Your prophecy was not wrong after all, the only difference is you chose the winning side” Jake said as the shadow of the dropship, like the shadow of a bird of prey, passed over both of them. The chill ran down the Miala’s back, and her eyes widened as the triumph of those humans came in a different light. Entire planets were scorched to the ground, gods were slain, and legendary monsters crawled back into the deepest darkest corners in utter terror as the human war machine marched to victory. And It was not going to stop here.
She stood in the presence of a new order, and she was the one who unleashed it.
The Hounds of Federation.

Archive file: 3039. NE 10A engagement record.
Operation: Noble Endeavor.
Taskforce: Noble.

Taskforce Noble formed as an expeditionary and exploratory force into the Xeno territories to respond to the plea for help from Xeno faction MaxElDem and their allies to combat Xeno Faction NoirElDem.
Task Force mission objective:
* Evaluate potential threat from Xeno Faction NoirEldem (Success)
* Assist allied Xeno factions (Partial Success)
* Map the territories (Partial Success)
* Evaluate Xeno technologies and capabilities (Partial Success)
* Develop ways to counteract Xeno technologies and capabilities (Partial Success)
* Contain any threat to human territories (Failure).
Unit in question:
Special Operations Force Delta
1st Special Operational Detachment Delta team Super 6
Team members:
Commanding officer: Major Raymond Crawford call sign Super 6.1 (Specialty psychological warfare, Bachelor’s degree in psychology, sociology)
Second in command: Captain Daniel Farnsworth call sign Super 6.2 (Specialty technology expert Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering and computer Science)
Operator: Lieutenant Marcus Lynch call sign Super 6.3 (Specialty Explosives Expert Bachelor’s degree in Applied Sciences)
Operator: Lieutenant Arnold Dupree (Specialty Medical expert, MD, Certified surgeon)
Engagement transcript:
Darius and Ratmesia were among their dead friends and surrounded by NoirEldem's hunting party led by the witch.

“Well, well, I guess you finally came to your senses. I offer you an opportunity you will both make a fine addition to our gladiatorial arenas, a better fate than to be sent to Fertalia for Ether harvesting. Make it easy on yourself and hand over the Stone of the Ancients to me, and I will personally vouch for you to fight in the arenas on behalf of my circle.”The witch covered in black spiky armor spoke with a devious smile on her face. She was only too happy to bring such two fine specimens to fight in the arenas, a warrior disciple of Res and a blessed hunter of Artem.
Meanwhile, a SpyHawk drone was silently hovering over the canopy of trees, witnessing what was transpiring below, utterly unseen by those below.
“6.2 what are we dealing with?” Crawford spoke into his encrypted radio set as his adoptive weave camouflage changed its patterns to match the environment.

“By the looks of it we have two groups of natives, a lot of magic used on both sides, and like of which I have never seen. One side just got their asses handed to them.” Farnsworth took his position on the cliff overseeing the glade upon which a battle just took place. One eye was in the scope of the rifle, the other on the telemetry in his HUD from SpyHawk drone.
“Which one?” Crawford stopped for a moment realizing his reconnaissance mission just took a turn.

“Well, the one with shiny bronze armor and this green chick with a bow. Looks like they are surrendering, and if the translator software is working correctly, the conditions of surrender are that they give some McGuffin to them. If they give willingly, they will end up in the gladiatorial arena” Farnsworth took his eye out of the scope of his rifle and spoke once he read the info drone picked.
“Not that bad considering our specialized training Farnsworth” Crawford stood in the middle of the woods looking up, reminiscing on his experimental training days.
“Careful, sir some of us don’t have the clearance”
Crawford chuckled, also remembering the bloody “training” they underwent.

“Oh cmon guys, we all know the rumors that you two participated in Battle Royal games with convicts in Siberia” Lynch spoke as he was trying to make sure no one was around him as he was making more noise with his movements than others, thanks to a massive machine gun and exoskeleton that helped him to carry it.

“Careful there, Lynch they might have to write you off as blue on a blue casualty.” Arnold took a jab at his brother-in-arms. They all were quite spread out, 50 meters in between them, and only Farnsworth was somewhat far away from them, holding the high ground.

“Just keep it in mind Super 6.3, anyway McGuffin clearly is important. Natives in trouble are considered friendlies, and the other guys are hostiles. Primary mission objective: We must not let them have the McGuffin. Secondary mission objective: ensure the safety of friendlies. 6.2 Link the info to all the PDAs. The rest begin executing surprise strike.”
“Roger that, providing overwatch. 6.2 out”
“I will double time it around them and light them up on your move Sir. 6.4 out.”
“I’m a bit noisy with that gun so I will move in under the cover of chaos from the flank. 6.3 out.”
Everyone knew what they had to do and began executing an attack pattern having complete trust in
one another.
Datrius looked at bleeding and unconscious Ratmesia as he held her in his arms trying to apply pressure on the wound.
“Fine, just make sure she lives, you vile woman” He reached into the bag and pulled out an orb covered in glowing runes.
“Are you hiding something else from me?” The witch announced as something distracted her.
“What do you mean?” Datrius was confused he had nothing else of value to her.
“This chaotic under-light noise, don’t you hear it?” The witch looked into Datrius' eyes and determined that he was oblivious to it.

“Well, excuse me, I’m not quite as well attuned in ways of magic to hear the under light” Datrius began slowly putting the Orb back into the bag while the witch began looking from side to side, trying to determine where the noise came from. Then she finally focused her eyes in the direction of SpyHawk Drone.

“What manner of bird is it?” She spoke that’s when she noticed some manner of metal tube appearing from the branches of trees. By instinct, she waved her hand around her, and by the wave of her hands upward, the magic shield formed around her. The flashes began appearing out of the tube. A metallic spear of three small metal objects traveling at mind-boggling speed hit her shield, which was hardly a problem for her shield. She heard another buzz behind her, and once again, 3 metal objects hit her shield. She turned back and saw a silhouette of what she could only assume was another bipedal creature not so different from herself.

Then those metal objects began hitting her escort, rapidly dropping them and easily puncturing their armor. Finally, her prized minotaur warrior charged into the forest, ignoring the damage done to him. He charged bravely into one of the shadowy creatures that were unleashing those metal objects, only to see it explode. One of her crossbow archers finally overcame confusion and aimed his crossbow at the silhouette. That’s when she saw his head blow up, and the energized bolt was sent flying just above the silhouette as whip crack noise reached her ears.

The situation was utter chaos, her people were dropping left and right, but finally, they began forming around her, sending energized bolts into the trees in the direction of muzzle flashes. That’s when she finally came to her senses, touched her staff, and waved her hand away from it, forming 6 glowing fires, then she squeezed her hand, and the fires began compressing. Then she threw her hand in the direction of the moving silhouette. She directed those fire darts with her mind to seek out this creature, and sure it finally hit it as it dropped behind a log.

“Now to where is the other one” She spoke, turning around and trying to find another silhouette. That’s when a stream of metal objects first cut down everything that was left of her hunting party and then focused on her. She felt as if the shield she put up was losing its power, so she focused on maintaining it. Yes, it took considerable effort for her to keep up with this stream of metal objects, but it was enough. Then she saw muzzle flashes coming again from the direction she had just sent her fire darts. The creature came out of the woods holding this weird staff, sending metal objects from it at her shield. The creature was armored in heat-resistant armor as she saw her hits leaving scorched marks on its armor. And suddenly, the stream of shots coming from the staff of this creature stopped. This creature dropped the container attached to this boom stick and rapidly replaced it, and again began his assault.

If not for the other hellish stream of those metal objects forcing her to focus on her shield, she would have snuffed the life from this creature in a matter of seconds, but alas. However, she thought to herself.

"Well, looks like this container is what powers those weapons. Stream of metal will not be endless since if they have to replace those containers surely means that they have to replace containers on other things as well." And she could keep it going for the whole day, and finally, a smile came onto her face. She smiled in the face of that creature.

“Farnsworth, this bitch clearly knows she can stand up to our focused fire. I need solutions FAST!” Crawford knew that he had seconds left before Lynch would bingo on ammo as he kept sending one clip after another into that shielded hostile.

“It’s a shield, right? What if EMP will have the same results as with our shield technology.” 6.2 spoke into the radio, after which Crawford rapidly pulled out both EMP grenades and threw them right next to a shielded opponent.
That’s when the suppressive fire from Lynches machine gun seized, and the witch turned to face Crawford to crisp him on the spot for the audacity he had just shown.

A Flash of light engulfed her shield and collapsed. Mere moments later, she saw a blinding light, and she felt as if her blood began boiling as Ether coursing through her blood erupted in a blinding light.
She screamed in agony and then tried to get her bearings once again.
Once the vision returned to her, the first and the last thing she saw was a cold and focused look of Crawford pointing a pistol at her head.
Muzzle Flash.
Leviathan Arrive

Miala stood on the top tower waiting for the members of the Ivory Council of whom she was a member of. A small circle of leaders of those who dared to oppose the darkness that was the NoirElDem Empire, an empire of their cousins. She stood hopeful in her ornate white robes as the leader of UrboSaptia of one of the last strongholds standing in defiance of giant monsters of an Empire of NoirElDems.

“So Miala what did those soulless TerraDems say? Were they too blinded by their petty reasons to help us, or were their greedy hearts found a reason to assist us?” Serune said his words were filled with desperate cynicism. He was an ArchDruid of SylvEldems, a strange cousin of her people MaxElDems, who lived their lives under the patronage of goddesses Artem and Demtra. They were people unified by their beliefs and ways of life but they were so spread out across countless worlds in their duty to preserve the sacred forests chosen by their goddesses. His people felt the conquest of NoirElDems most acutely, for they saw them in every world they had their hallowed groves, and countless more were lost and enslaved.

“In time I will answer that question, Serune, let's wait on the other Council members,” Miala said calmly as two other members walked in. A short but robustly built PetraDem in his bronze armor covered in gems. The other one was Maginus, a taller bald individual having blue glowing veins running across its olive-colored scalp and in the blue lavishly ornate robes.
“You don’t need to wait for us Miala. Serune not very prudent of you to speak so ill of potential allies.” The archmage Tyrus said calmly as he looked at the archdruid covered in the cloak of leaves.

“Tyrus, waste no time on this fool. He just never saw what it’s like to see everything you hold dear to be taken away from you. Sure many of his tree-hugging tribes were lost, but he will always have one hidden grove for himself” High King Goros of PetraDems said as he elbowed his taller companion.

“Listen to me very carefully PetraDem if you think I have not felt how my groves are burned, you are dead wrong. If your people were enslaved, maybe such was the fate your god Hestus thought was needed to temper the arrogance of your people.” Tyrus spat in the direction of short PetraDem whose eyes filled with rage upon hearing it. He was about to draw his hammer in anger when the loud sound of a metal spear hitting the floor stopped him.

“Alright that’s enough last thing we need is a feud among ourselves over petty differences, you're supposed to be better than that you are an ElDem after all; as for you, High King Gorros, I understand your pain is great, but you should be more respectful of Serune for he not only saw your dynasty rise up and unify your clans, he was also a great friend and an ally to your great grandfather. Friends, I urge you not to give into despair” That was King Maras in his silver Mithril armor with gold engraving and long, lavish red cloak. He was MaxElDem, king of the Reslian kingdom, the highest ranking priest of the god of war Res, and though calm his voice commanded respect from everyone in the room.
“Maras, it is a grand joy to see you.” Miala spoke with loving softness upon seeing her counterpart of the Reslian kingdom.
“So what did TerraDems say?” Maras looked at Miala with a concerned look.

“Let me show you.” Miala said as she extended her arms and her eyes lit up in blue light as the grand projection showed itself. It was the huge building size of the mountain compared to her, with a giant symbol on its side of a globe pierced by the sword and surrounded by leaves as swarms of aircraft floated all around. At first, everyone who looked at it failed to see the size of the building, but then they saw how small Miala was compared to it.
“What is that?” High King Gorros spoke in utter shock at the sheer size of the structure.

“This is the building where they house their government. I myself could not believe they built anything as big and tall as that. I felt as if I stood in the presence of a god manifested in its true splendor.” She spoke as the image closed in on her as she stared at this giant symbol bearing down on her like at an insignificant ant.

“They are not a kingdom as we once thought but a nation, one among many. The nation as we commonly known as the Kingdom of Steel Eagle is called the United Earth Federation. A union known as a republic of 54 worlds and thousands of kingdoms all having their representatives sent here to govern the affairs of their nation.” Then the image showed the grand circular filled to the brim with tens of thousands of senators and two special tables against one another where the President and the Supreme Commander sat. In the middle stood Miala as tens of thousands of eyes stared at her as she pleaded for help. All that stood in the room saw it through Mialas eyes and felt as a tiny ant pleading for help from a giant who spoke with thousands of incoherent voices.

“I’m Miala Avenue, the High Councilor of the Silver Star clan and leader of the city of UrboSaptia. I came here to you noble humans for help. For all the people, I have been told that you value liberty and justice above all. I came here begging for your assistance in the war against despicable slavers that desire nothing else but complete subjugation of our worlds and soon of yours!” Miala shouted out with her magically empowered voice.

“High Councilor, though you have my sympathy but as you know, we are bound by an Agreement of 2611 that we shall not interfere in the affairs of your territories as you have agreed not to interfere in the affairs of human territories. What right do we have to speak on the behalf of other human nations?” One of the senators rose from his lavish seats. He was dressed in a business suit and rose up as his voice was heard loud and clear across the entire room, amplified by the speakers.

“Oh drop it Carlos. We all know that we signed that agreement without the consent of our breakaway colonies and that the Senate does not recognize so-called other human nations as legitimate bodies! And the only reason other human nations uphold it, is only out of their own prudence rather than respecting the mandates of this government. We made that agreement, and we can annul it.” A woman rose up in opposition to this senator and was only allowed to speak once Carlos finished his speech.

“Though Senator Carlos Walsh made a dumb argument, this however does deny the fact that we suppose to intervene in a war in which we have no interest being Senator Olivia Brown. What do we even know of the enemy they ask to fight against, and what benefits does it pose to our people?” Then the other senator rose up. This one was dressed far more regally, clearly indicating he was more than just another citizen of the Federation.

“I have to agree with Senator Ernest Rodrigez on this one; we have our borders and interests already challenged by other human nations whether we refuse to recognize their legitimacy or not. We still have not retaken planet Zen from the Imperials, not to mention constant skirmishes and attempts by the Fanatics to stop the reintegration of Drinor back into the Federation. If we divert our fleets and armies to help you out we might leave ourselves vulnerable.” That’s when Senator Walsh spoke once again.

“If you do not believe what I’m saying, the honored Senators, let me show you!” Miala said and, with the movement of her hands, made a projection large enough to depict the atrocities done by the NoirElDems. It was something humans had not seen before, a display of magic unlike any other. The image has shown that not only they were enslaving entire people, but they conducted horrible rituals upon them, freakishly mutating them and then subjecting their young to the most brutal re-education camps if not using them as sacrifices to conduct spells or out for entertainment.
Upon seeing this display, the Supreme Commander looked directly into the eyes of the president who just nodded in agreement.

“I’ve seen enough! Ladies and gentlemen, I believe that we can not in good conscience leave this plea for help unanswered! I have guided that nation for almost a millennia and trust me when I’m telling you that this brutality we have just witnessed shall not end in their territories. It is only a matter of time until it will come to human territories, and even though our rogue colonies declared their independence due to the Great Divide, they are still our colonies, and we, as the United Earth Federation, are mandated to protect them!” The Supreme Commander said in a calm and commanding voice. Though it was still just the voice of one person, it echoed with strange power through the entire gigantic room.

“Was it not the same words that you used to drag us into the ill-fated Dargorian War, Supreme Commander?” Suddenly, one of the senators dared to speak against the proposal but quickly regretted it when the gaze of gray predatory eyes of the Supreme Commander fell upon him.

“So what, Senator Dorian Malos we should have stood and seen as the largest genocide in human history was happening? No, and neither shall we stand idly by when entire species are being enslaved just because it did not affect us. I don’t believe that this is what we are or what our values are! We shall answer that call for help, and I believe that the president agrees with my judgment upon that, don’t you Preston?” He said calmly as he looked at President Joshua Preston.

“I fully support the Supreme Commander in said notion. We are a nation that was built upon the values of freedom, justice, tolerance, and willingness to fight for those values wherever they take us! Especially when it is a plea for help!” Preston said as he rose up holding his PDA, and all of a sudden, some manner of beeps began appearing all across the room, indicating that matter indeed was now up to the vote to stop the motion rather than both leaders of the Federation were in agreement.

“Well, ladies and gentlemen, you will need a supermajority to stop it from happening, but as you make that decision, I want you to know that I not only see it as answering a plea for help but also as a sign! A sign that it is for humanity to embrace a larger galaxy than just our own territories and carry the light of our humanity to a new age.” The Supreme Commander said calmly as he sat back down behind his table.

With those words, the image has changed to a room overlooking a human city. It was filled with dozens of towers the size of mountains and millions of tiny lights representing aircraft buzzing all around them. Those were the apartments of the Supreme Commander.

“High Councilor, I’ll be honest with you. You must understand by asking us for our help, you did in fact, force me to make a rather hard decision. As you heard back there, we are indeed at war with other human nations. Nothing too intense, just border conflicts, but other nations are always waiting for an opportunity to bring about our downfall, and more importantly, they do not share our beliefs and that when we annulled the agreement of 2611, we gave everyone a signal that new lands are free game.” The Supreme commander said as he stared out at the city.
“Is that not a good thing, then? Finally, your people shall join a larger world?” Miala said in confusion.

“Warden, bring up the Eye and on the window display please.” The Supreme commander said, and all of sudden, the image overlay of hundreds of different social network accounts came up. Each had its own tag and profile highlighted but most importantly where in the city was this person located?
“What is that?” Miala said, looking not sure what to make of it

“This is what humanity really is, the Eye allows me to see a selection of opinions and people sorted out by the AI all across social networks. Like, take a look at this one. See it says that this particular individual is not all too happy with the decision I made. Calling me out as a dictator playing democracy. Or take a look at this individual, a xenophobe who thinks that it serves you right to suffer like that and that the only reason we should go in is to enslave you or eradicate you ourselves. Or this individual thinks this is just a good idea because it only plays into his sexual fantasies. Or, obviously, those who actually stand in support of what has been said. You can also see them arguing, insulting one another, agreeing, and debating philosophical and practical implications. I’m showing you this so that you understand what you called in for help. In our case, you called a leviathan that has billions of voices in its head, all distilled by our government and sometimes guided by me.” The Supreme Commander said calmly.
“So they agreed to help us?” Maras said once the projection has stopped.

“Come and look out into the skies.” Miala pointed in the direction of the balcony overlooking their great ivory city of UrboSaptia, and everyone rushed towards it to see hundreds of human dropships descending from an orbit carrying thousands of their best warriors, their most advanced equipment, and their most devastating weaponry.
With a gun, a good word, and a bag of gold.

As the dropship descended from the skies General Jake Ambramson looked out of the window. The fire from retiring into the atmosphere has already dissipated and he could see as his army was making planetfall.

“Eghm Sir,what is next? We have no clearance for landing or any signals coming.” The pilot said in confusion. They made the planetfall so hastily he was not exactly sure what was the whole procedure going forward other than flying here.
“Patch me in for the rest of the landing fleet” Jake said as he grabbed the headset ready to address the fleet.
“Yes sir.” The pilot answered without asking any more questions as he reached with his hand for the switch that would patch general to entire force.

“This is general Jake Abramson. Ok ladies and gentlemen, standard combat deployment protocols with potentially hostile local populations are in effect. Circle around the city, find an appropriate spot, and start building the base camp. Above all things, this is a military op. We are in unknown territories here so the security of our own operations is paramount. Over.” Jake announced in the headset and without question the entire landing fleet began changing its course. For now it was circling around the city looking for the best place where they could land and begin the construction as terrified locals looked up at hundreds of alien vessels circling around their city.

“Pilot, now circle around that biggest tower and find us a landing spot. Our scout teams have already determined that that’s where our so-called allies have their primary body of government.” Jake said to the pilot calmly.
“Ten four.” With those words the pilot readjusted his course and began circling around the highest of the central spires.

“Is it not a very prudent General?” The man in a business suit said as he stared judgmentaly at the general in front of him. The two colonels that flanked him looked at the man in a business suit, somewhat shocked that he was questioning the general in such a way however were quickly put at ease as Jake smiled.

“Agent Tadison it is better to ask for forgiveness than for permission, besides they should have outlined our whole deployment beforehand and since they didn’t we do it my way. More aggressively we make our entrance more freedom of action we will have in the future and more room for concessions that they will think we made out of the kindness of heart rather than trying to play nice. They asked for our help, which means they need us more than we need them. They must understand they don’t get to boss us around and it will put us in a better position to dictate the terms.” Jake said as he leaned forward toward the Central Intelligence Department Agent ready to spare his reasoning.
“Still it is a rather bad first impression General.” Tadison leaned forward as well.

“Agent Tadison, make no mistake, we are entering into war with an alien empire of unknown capabilities. And if it is the first impression you worried about ain’t no more honest depiction than to see us waging an all-out war. War is a test, an ultimate demonstration of everything that makes us good and strong, washing out all the subjective nonsense and proof that we earned our right to stand among the stars.” Jake said as he clenched his fist in front of Tadison not as a threat but as a demonstration of his reasons.
“So, might makes right?” Tadison said as he tilted his head, not exactly pleased with what he was hearing.
“Always did, Agent Tadison it always did. It is our job to make sure that the mighty are the good guys and in other words, us” Jake pointed his thumb at himself.

“Well you are the ultimate authority here, General. I’m here only to provide my expertise to your effort.” Tadison gestured towards the General to which Jake leaned back into his seat and looked out of the window as the dropship finally found the landing spot and was heading towards it..

“I still answer to the public and without supplies from the Federation we won't have a lot of legs to stand on. So don’t worry I will show discretion in our activities after all, might is not only brute force but smarts and diplomacy as well” Jake said as he saw a couple of guards dressed in some manner of silver armor pointing their spears at the dropship. There was a look of fear and confusion on their faces. Exactly what Jake was counting on.

“Set up the perimeter but don’t provoke them.” One of the colonels said to the 4 soldiers that were providing escort to the command staff. Soldiers followed orders without a question as they rapidly opened the doors and rolled guns in hand and the side minigun of the dropship ready to lay waste to the guards.

“Let’s hope those translation apps are working fine otherwise we are looking at full-scale occupation hehe.” Jake said as he pulled out his PDA and activated the translation application. Tadison just shook his head in disapproval and followed Jake out of the dropship.

“Take me to your leaders.” Jake said as he stood right in front of the guards and the PDA rapidly began translating his speech to what guards became even more baffled. He was speaking a strange alien language yet that strange trinket on his chest produced a familiar tongue albeit it did sound oddly mechanical. They exchanged words but then some runner came up to them and they looked even more confused.

Meanwhile, the other dropships finally found a suitable patch of land and began landing as well right outside of the walls of the city in farmlands. First was the deployment of Rangers who quickly began setting up the defensive positions and routing up all the confused farmers.

They quickly put them in one location. Those farmers were more terrified than anything. Those strange warriors spoke through their mouths in alien tongues but their trinkets sounded with a mechanical voice in the tongue they could understand.

“You there, move here! It is for your own safety!” The trinkets on the chest of those warriors said loudly, and out of sheer terror, they obeyed. As more dropships began landing this time, those were larger ones as more soldiers began getting out along with big exo-suits and they quickly began forming up in different positions. Then other dropships began dropping off huge metal sheets and rapidly dusting off up into the orbit.

Those farmers quickly found themselves right in the middle of highly orchestrated construction as those exo-suits and soldiers began building walls all around them. They did not dare to move out of the zones that they were placed in as it became quite clear to them that they were there indeed for their own safety because those strange people seemed to work like a well-oiled mechanism and would not bat an eye if they would run them over in their process.

Once the walls were set up, they brought in parts for fusion reactors and within minutes set them up and encased them in those same metal sheets quickly forming them into sizable buildings. They quickly built 5 such buildings.

Then came in diesel generators dozens of them once again they were all positioned all around the base. Then came solar panels and were put on top of their buildings. All of those parts were built in such a way that they fitted in like lego parts clearly indicating that they could be arranged in any way those humans wanted.
Then came other aspects of infrastructure: power cables, plumbing and computers. This took a bit longer for them to set up as it required more precise work rather than heavy lifting.
“General Jake Abramson I presume?” The high Councilor Miala approached the General who stood at the landing pad still surrounded by the guards and protected by his soldiers.
“Yes, that would be me.” Jake raised his hand upon hearing the English language being spoken by Miala.

“I see you wasted no time in setting up your base camp.” She said as she approached the railing to see the human military already putting up defensive structures like turrets and AA sites around the perimeter of the base.
“No we did not.” Jake said as he looked down as well.

“You do realize those farmlands fed families and were the property of people, you at least could have waited for us to find an appropriate spot for your forces.” Miala said calmly now realizing that she indeed was no longer in a position to negotiate where humans should put up their forces.

“Well I’m sorry but it seemed like a perfect spot for us, close enough to your city and quite defendable. Besides, we already set up, it would be such a hustle to pack it all back up. Also, I hear your people use gold as the currency we are bringing compensation to the farmers as we speak. A golden bar like that for every 10 square meters of land we took I believe would be more than enough” Jake said as one of the soldiers brought a golden bar stamped with the United Earth Federation sigil and handed it over to Miala. Miala felt the weight of it and then looked down to the base camp.

“I see you are quite generous as well.” She said calmly, noticing that farmers were indeed cheering with the luck they were stricken by the amount of gold that was offered to them for their land was above anything they could have dreamt of.
Magic of the locals
It has been a couple of weeks since operation Noble Endeavor was launched but the science corps just arrived on the planet of Elveron, a capital world of MaxEldems.

“Doctor Hunter, I’m Colonel Eliza Snyder of 1st SOFD. I’m the primary coordinator of Delta force operations for the duration of this mission. I'm also to be your liaison with command staff. If you need any manner of request from the military you come to me first and I see what can be arranged. Also we have already set up your laboratory.” Max just came out of the dropship, finally happy to breathe some actual air after being cooked up in a spaceship for weeks when women in the black camo uniform approached him.
“Eghm thank you, Colonel Snyder. I was kind of wondering where I should begin?”
Max was rather surprised it was the first time he actually was to lead a research team, so he still was rather unsure what he should do.

“Well, for starters, magic. We are dealing with magic here that we have never encountered before. Everything that the Church of holy light has thrown at us before, has nothing on those NoirElDem images. Portable shields and self-guided plasma bolts are just tip of the iceberg. We have all the reports ready for you. Start from there, I guess.” Eliza pointed in the direction of the prefabricated structures set up by the Federal military right next to the huge city wall of UrboSaptia, the capital city of the planet. The structures of the base stood out like a sore thumb compared to the elegant structure of an alien city which Max still could not make sense of.

“Were they made out of plastic of some sort or was it metal, or stone? So much to learn, and the military obviously only interested in what can kill. But hey they have been paying his bills and let him work on the most advanced technology in Area 51, so he was not complaining.” Max thought to himself.

“Those reports... A 1000 rounds of 6.8 armor piercing rounds did nothing to the shield, but an EMP seems to work just the same as on our shields, but if it could stand up to 1000 rounds and EMP8 would not overload it. Maybe the issue is the continuous output. It’s just speculation though I need actual tests.” Max spoke, reading the report from one of the teams engaging a mage.
He knew he had to get some firsthand experience but how would that work?

“MaxElDems were using magic extensively as well, maybe I should ask them? Sounds like a plan. Ok who do I talk to? Colonel? Is she even authorized to speak to aliens? Stoner would have known what to do.”His social anxiety was overwhelming him so he turned back to working with the reports at least they did not talk back.

“Doctor Hunter, I see you read one of my reports.” The calm voice of Major Crawford spooked Max out of his. When he turned around, he saw this soldier in black armor accompanied by MaxElDem women covered in white robes with golden threads.
“Oh hello, your report? Egham… Major Crawford? And a?” He was lost for words trying to figure out what he should say in this situation.

“Maximilian Hunter, good major, told me that you are one of the finest minds among humans and that you would like to learn about the way of magic. I’m Miala Avenue, the High Councilor and the leader of the Silver Star '' Though still with some accent, the voice of this MaxElDem woman was very soothing and kind, but the title was a terrifying addition.

“Doc relaxes, breathes in and breathes out. I figured some hands-on experience would be far more useful for you and our friend here was eager to help.”Major approached, utterly lost for words Max and grabbed him by the shoulder and looked at him as if it was Major who was in need of help. That actually kind of made Max feel better, after all, he was not doing it for himself.
“I’m sorry High Councilor, it’s all just so new to me.” Max finally came to his senses.

“You are quite shy. It is not a trait you should be ashamed of, Scholar Maximilian.” Miala was quite pleased to see it and understood quite well so she raised her hand and manifested an assortment of dancing lights and showed it to Max.
“Wow, no holographic projectors. How do you do that?” Max was utterly captivated by what he saw.

“I think of how light comes from the air then just think of the image I want to manifest, and I feel the emotions I associate with the image and Ether manifests it. That was the first spell my mentor showed to me. But it takes some training to make images more complex than dancing lights” Miala explained as the dancing lights began turning into the image of a dancing figurine.
“Ok we are not quite interested in image projection, but more destructive applications.” Crawford intervened once he saw that Max completely forgot about his anxiety.
“Then we should go to the training ground if you want a proper demonstration, Major Crawford” Miala turned to Crawford with some annoyance.
The training grounds turned out to be an arena of sorts filled with all manner of stones, metal shields, and melee weapons scattered all over the place.
“Let’s begin with the fireball then.” Miala visibly turned angry as she extended her arms outwards, and the air around her hands began heating up.

“I turn Ether coursing through my body into chaos in the air. Chaos is violent and fueled by the emotions of anger, but one must be directing this chaos away from one body.” She explained as the air around her hands began getting so hot, it was glowing.

“But when more chaos wants to spread, you must focus on it and compress it. You must focus your rage for it to have an effect” She explained as she began slowly closing her arms compressing the hot glowing air into the ball of glowing plasma. Her face looked furious yet focused and then she violently released it in the direction of one of the stones. It exploded upon contact shattering the stone.

“She says chaos; I wonder if she understands that heat is the vibration of particles.” Max whispered to Crawford who in return looked at him then at Miala beginning to realize something about his allies and his enemies.

“Powerful and quite destructive as you asked Major Crawford. But let me show you something else, something I would use to defeat more armored opponents like your war machines.” She calmed herself but gave a cold and condescending look to Crawford. She extended her left arm forward while pressing her right arm closer to her face as if she was holding a cylindrical object.

“Ice lance requires calmness as you expunge chaos from the air and compress it at the same time.” She explained as her left palm expanded as if it was releasing something while her right palm looked like she was squeezing something.
“You must feel and see how air becomes so still it becomes rock solid” She explained as the steaming ice shard was forming in the place where she would hold the imaginary cylinder.

“But all that chaos in the air has to go somewhere, so you turn it into force to help you throw it at your target,” She said and immediately pushed her right arm forward in the direction of the huge metal shield. The recognizable whip cracks and noise of broken sound barriers echoed across the arena.

“Hmm that’s interesting they don’t create matter but manipulate the energy around it and by the looks of it this Ether of their acts as an energy source since they still fall under the effects of the law of conservation” Max once again whispered to Crawford who was more interested in an emotional state of Miala, but once heard comments made by Max a thought crossed his mind.

“High councilor I believe Max here can do it, as I understand all that is needed is Ether.” He winked at Max to which Max was utterly terrified, but his social awkwardness once again kept him speechless. Something Crawford counted on.

“Agh yes, your people can not generate either on their own.”She pulled out a flask of water and directed her finger into it, and blue glowing mist began depositing itself in the flask of water. Then she gave the flask to Max who was not sure what to do with it.

“Drink it, doctor, you wouldn’t want to get me embarrassed in front of the high councilor now would you Max.” Crawford looked straight into Max’s eyes practically forcing him to drink it. Max could not master the will to oppose him and slowly drank the water. He felt as if his mind was on fire as some sort of energy began coursing through his veins. He panicked as the lights began flashing all around him.
“Calm your thoughts Scholar Hunter” Miala was expecting such a reaction but even her soothing voice did nothing to calm him down.

“Doctor, focus on my words, breathe. Breathe in and breathe out, breathe in and breathe out.” Crawford grabbed Max by the shoulder and once again looked directly into his eyes. Oddly enough, following the commands of Crawford did in fact calm him down despite his mind firing like crazy, and the lights ceased appearing all around him.

“Now let’s make an ice bolt doctor. Remember what Miala said: calm yourself down and focus on how you reduce those vibrations.” Crawford released Max who nodded at him. Miala was somewhat surprised to hear something about vibrations but was not about to interfere.

Max extended his hand and remembered the calmness with which he conducted his experiments. Precise, clean, and expected results. Then he focused on the air above his palm as he began imagining air particles dashing all around. He imagined that he would reduce their speed. The temperature rapidly began dropping now, he imagined how those air particles clumped together and formed a lattice structure. But more than that he focused on making sure that other air particles would not disturb the solid. As he was doing it he felt more Ether coursing through his veins.

Miala looked in utter awe as Max formed a perfect shard of crystal clear ice that was not fuming out. With the same violent action, he has thrown this small ice shard into the stone. He expended all the Ether into this action. The whip-crack noise once again echoed through the arena as an ice shard passed right through the metal shield.
Later at the laboratory, Max sat staring at the flask of water with Ether.
“How did you know this would work so well Major?”He asked Crawford who was also reviewing the videos from helmet cameras in light of his new discoveries.

“She called particle vibrations chaos, at this point I realized they have no idea how the universe functions. They just follow their perception of the universe and since you have the magic gifted to you why would you need to know every detail pertaining to the functions of the universe? On the other hand, your doctor has one of the best pictures as to how exactly the universe functions.”
Anticipated Losses
Lieutenant Dray Martinez stood at the entrance of the huge temple of the ancients. His platoon of 40 men was tasked to provide a security detail to the science team and aliens that accompanied them.
“Lieutenant Martinez, I was hoping that your Delta warriors would accompany us. The task at hand is of great importance.” A warrior in bronze armor approached him.
“This guy with medieval weapons has issues with my Rangers?” Martinez was rather offended by the notion but he knew that he had to remain cool for diplomatic reasons.

“Datrius right? The command thought that a 4-men team of Delta force would not be able to provide adequate protection and they most likely had other tasks to fulfill.” Martinez answered, but he could not deny that he was rather disappointed that he was stuck with babysitting duty after all those medals did come with some good payment bonuses.

“I see your people, give quantity over quality when it comes to important tasks.” Datrius’s English was still quite broken but it was rather impressive that he managed to learn it in a matter of a month.

“Datrius we are the elite force as much as Delta’s or any other tier 1 unit. The difference is we are more numerous because tasks that are required of us, is different from what is required of tier 1 one unit.”Martinez tried to be as diplomatic as possible, but he could not deny that Rangers were considered tier 2 unit. Still, it was a bit disrespectful toward the effort individuals put into becoming Rangers even if it came from a place of ignorance.

That’s when Ratmisia approached them a Hunteress of Artem and from what Dray understood they served as scouts of sorts in the army of those strange aliens. She was not speaking English, but she clearly was disturbed by something.
“Lieutenant Martinez, Ratmisia just told me…” Datrius began his speaking but had to be interrupted by Martinez who just received a message on his coms.
“Sir our drone overwatch just spotted hostiles approaching from the north.” The technical specialist informed his commanding officer.
“Ok everyone, get to your posts we have hostiles approaching from the north. Inform the science team to pack their bags we are bugging out” Martinez barked his orders into the coms.

“Lieutenant Martinez, the attack from the north is a distraction. Ratmisia just told me that her companions have spotted Shadows moving in from the south.” Datrius said as he realized that Martinez was well aware of the attack but not having full information.

“Sir it’s Volkov, Shasira said that we should hold our ground and since she was put in charge, she is strongly insisting on defending the temple” Sargent Volkov, and his squad were inside of the temple providing armed escort to the science team and Shasira a champion of Zes.

“Great... Change of plans gentlemen we are not to make combat retreat but to dig in and repel the attack” Martinez readied his rifle then looked Datrius, then looked at the telemetry from his drone that identified only a large force of about 100 heat signatures approaching from the north.“Ok trust the locals, they’ve been here longer than we did,” He thought to himself.

“Datrius I need you and Ratmisia to double-time it… To assist my men at southern approach.” Dray ordered Datrius who was pleased to hear that this human commander heeded to his advice. Datrius grabbed Ratmisia and headed toward the southern side of the temple.

“Madison I’m going to need your squad on southern approach on the double we have a sneak attack heading from there, grab the miniguns. They expect us to put all our firepower on the north let’s serve them a surprise of our own. Fujima press forward to the north and then do fighting retreat onto our position. Fox set up kill zones to cover the Fujima’s retreat. Dropship is Alamo we can’t let them have it. Volkov if Alamo collapse scenarios are a go I want you to grab those civics and double time it here if Shasira has a problem with it she can stay.” Dray gave out a command to his sergeants without hesitation and approached the cockpit of the dropship. After knocking on the dropship cockpit, he made a helicopter movement with his hand. The dropship pilot immediately realized it was an order to start the engines.
His soldier knew what to do all that was needed of him now was to observe the situation from the PDA. That’s when the familiar buzz of M22 rifles announced the start of the battle.

Fujima’s squad was to draw the first blood, enemy archers did not expect that Rangers would meet them head-on. NoirElDem archers returned fire as bolts with glowing energized tips arced in the direction of the Rangers.
“I thought they were a bunch of primitives; what kind of weapon is that?” One of the Rangers screamed as one of those energized bolts just pierced and set a tree on fire.

“Aghh… AGHHHH!!!” Another Ranger screamed in agony at first for being hit and a second, feeling his flesh was burning with a bolt sticking out of his armor. The bolt punched through both the tree he used as cover and composite armor.
“Shit, Doorman got hit.” Another Ranger screamed into his coms picking off the archer he just hit his brother in arms.
“Don’t stay in one place god damn it, the cover is of no use” Fujima was about to get back into cover a bolt grazed him as it passed through the tree.
“Sarge We can’t abandon Doorman.” One of the Rangers screamed into coms as he was returning fire at an ever-increasing amount of hostiles.

“We are not leaving him behind. Throw some smokes…” Fujima was formulating an extraction plan as he was dodging the energized bolts of enemy fire when he heard a continuous buzz of M22. Somebody just emptied a clip of a 1500 rounds per minute rifle, a bad sign…
“What in the actual fuck?! HOW MUCH LEAD CAN YOU EAT” The Ranger who was closest to Doorman just emptied a clip into the minotaur, just ran up to an injured soldier, and drove its axe into his face.

“Doorman is a goner,” Another Ranger announced on the coms as he emptied another clip into a minotaur and followed it up with a shot from a grenade launcher seeing that the damn thing was still standing.

“Shit, fall back will grab the dog tags once it is done, FIGHTING RETREAT people just like during the drills, and for god sake, don’t get shot.” Fujima was furious, but he had to keep his head in the game.
Meanwhile on the southern approach, fighting also broke out.

“What the? Are you seeing this?” One of the Rangers said on its coms as he was seeing as some manner of black mist was darting around the trees and, from time to time, forming into what he could only say was a female with huge knives.

“Open fire.” The short buzz of 3 round bursts was unleashed from another Ranger, but it passed right through the mist. Again and again, the human soldier was sending rounds in the direction of the blinking enemy as she was rapidly closing the distance on him. Finally, she materialized in point-blank range, he switched to fully automatic fire and unleashed the full fury of his rifle into the black mist. The red blood spilled on the ground as his rifle gave out a recognizable cling of the emptied magazine.
The same scenarios started happening all across the southern approach as more and more of those shadows descended on Rangers.

That’s when Datrius pointed his spear in the direction right behind one of the Rangers. Datrius released some manner of war cry, and his body, like a bolt of lightning, propelled itself in the direction the spear was pointing. He just scored a hit as at the end of the spear, NoirElDem female dropped both of her knives, still smoking in black mist.
“What the hell? Eghm thanks I guess” Ranger announced realizing that his life was just saved.

“Don’t fire where they are, fire where they will be.” Datrius explained to the soldier. The zing of metal hitting metal and sparks were unleashed as Datrius used his shield to protect himself from another blinking enemy.
“Fall back to Miniguns, guys” Madison screamed into the coms as he saw his men dropping like flies trying to hold forward positions.

“We are retreating to miniguns.” Ranger shouted at Datrius as he decided to double-time it to defensive positions. Datrius tried to stop him but was too late as a blinking female first cut his throat, then the arteries on his leg and arms right where there was no armor protection. Datrius did not have this moment, though, as he had thrown his spear at the enemy with a whip crack noise of breaking a sound barrier and immediately returned his spear back into his arm.
The southern entrance to the temple was guarded by 2 miniguns as 2 of the 5 Rangers that went to meet shadows in the forest were running for their lives.
The minguns sprang into action, trying to shoot down the blinking opponents but to no avail.
“Shit, we can’t hit them!” One of the minigun operators screamed into coms while every other Rangers was trying to pick them off with their accurate burst fire.

“FIRE FOR EFFECT” Madison screamed as he switched his rifle into fully automatic mode and unleashed a mag just in the general direction of the enemy and by the looks of it managed to graze one of those NoirElDem shadow dancers. The miniguns started firing in waves instead of trying to track the enemy. That’s when one of those shadow dancers got hit in the shoulder and she blinked again but dropped onto the ground and looked up in horror as a stream of bullets cut her to pieces.

“Got one!” The Ranger shouted into the coms and the closer the shadow dancers got, the more difficult it became for them to avoid the wall of bullets flying in their direction. Two more were killed when they managed to close in the distance.

Madison just reloaded his weapon when he saw his minigun crew was cut to pieces. He pressed his back against the wall and unloaded his weapon as he saw one of the mist rapidly moving in his direction. The face appeared right in front of him, barely cutting his neck. The eyes were filled with fury and black mist, but then they looked down, staring at the rifle barrel covered in her blood. She collapsed as Madison was still squeezing the trigger of the now long empty rifle. He looked around and saw all his men were dead. He locked eyes with the other shadow dancer who just saw him kill one of her sisters. He reached for the pistol only to find his arm pinned to the wall by the blade and metal claws holding him by the throat.
She spoke something in a language he did not understand, and he was utterly paralyzed by it. Then multiple arrows hit her in the back as Ratmisia stood right behind her.

“Volkov perimeter breached, you have enemies approaching, they killed all of my men, and I think we only managed to kill 5 of them. But I have no idea how many are heading your way.” Madison spoke on his coms as he pulled the knife out of his right arm, still trying to catch his breath.

“Volkov Alamo, I repeat, ALAMO '' Dray screamed into his coms. Even though Fujima’s squad was still executing a fighting retreat and reaching his positions after he saw how easily those shadows cut through the Madisons defenses, he was not willing to risk the lives of the science team.

“Yes sir… They are here, retreat is problematic,” Volkov responded as the buzz of M22 rifles echoed away from the temple halls. Dray looked at his PDA and realized he had no man to spare to go and help Volkov’s squad. Everyone on Fox’s squad was covering tactically essential positions especially given the fact that Fujima’s squad was leading enemies into the ambush. Also, he realized that he could not keep the dropship on the ground; it would be a sitting duck, and if those were as powerful as he thought, they would make short work of their only way out of this place.

“Son of a… Fox, since Fujima is a bit busy, you are in charge of the defense; Badger gets into the air and provides air support. If holding the position is not an option, move to extraction sight Bravo.” Dray screamed orders into his com, grabbed an extra couple of mags, and headed to the entrance to the temple.
“Lt?” Fox looked at his lieutenant, who was about to intentionally put himself in harm’s way, which was against a textbook.
“Volkov needs some help there. I'm the only spare body right now, so yeah, Fox, I rely on you to make sure I’m not probed from the rear.”
Dray said as he entered the temple.
“GodSpeed and give them hell, Sir” Fox said, understanding that situation required extraordinary measures.
Dray was walking down the halls of alien architecture as his HUD gave him directions. That’s when he runs into one of his soldiers leading a science team.
“1,2,3 where is 4th ? Where is Doctor Hunter?” Dray thought to himself as he was doing the headcount of the science team.
“LT?” Ranger was surprised to see his commanding officer as he tried to catch his breath.
“Paulson where the fuck is Doctor Hunter?” Dray asked his soldier loudly but without any anger in his voice.
“Wait… where the fuck is Baker?” Ranger just realized he lost his brother in arms.

“Paulson gets to the entrance and makes sure those civics are safe if anything happens to them, it is your ass on the line. I’ll find Baker and Hunter.” Dray pointed in the direction of the exit and dashed forward. As he reached around the corner, he saw his soldier lying dead on the floor in a puddle of blood and this woman. She was barely dressed, she had long, flowing black hair and pale beige skin covered in alien tattoos. She was elegantly built, with smooth curves flowing around her body, elegant yet clearly built for war. In One hand, she was holding Doctor Hunter by the throat, speaking something in the alien language. In the other, she held a blood-soaked curved blade dripping with blood.

“Hey, how about you find someone of your own size!” Dray shouted out, trying to grab the attention of the woman to make sure she was not snapping the poor civilian in half. That was a mistake as she immediately turned into a black mist.

“Oh, I should have just shot her.” Dray thought to himself as the first burst from his rifle passed right just through the mist. She was blinking and closing in zig zags as Dray was sending one burst after another into the mist. He started to move backward trying to maintain the advantage of distance, but she was moving way too fast. She blinked within 2 meters of him, jumping off the wall towards him as he pointed his rifle in her direction.

The time slowed down as he saw the supersonic round leave his rifle, and just as they were about to hit this woman in the face, her body and once again phased out, and they passed right through her. He saw as the black mist enveloped him, and he leaped forward into the mist. He felt the knife cut at the back of his neck as he turned his body in flight. By the time he hit the ground, he managed to unleash another burst. The bullets passed and disappeared in the black mist along the shadow dancer. She rematerialized, standing right on top of Dray, passing him over, scratching his helmet and combat glasses.

Dray looked in her direction and saw her coughing out blood, trying to reach for him with her knife but quickly losing strength. When her hand finally lost all strength, all he could see was this determined but dead gaze filled with hatred.
Dray rose up and looked at the dead body of his opponent, and a smile came to his face.

“HELL YEAH!” He screamed on the top of his lungs, but then he immediately scanned the place around him for any more hostiles, only to see Doctor Hunter trying to put some manner of sphere back together.
“Oh, no, Shasira will be so pissed with me” Doctor Hunter was clearly upset about the sphere more than he was about the wellbeing of Martinez.
“I’m fine, thanks for asking Doc, Baker on the other hand…” Dray spoke as he reached down for Baker’s dog tags.

“Oh my god, I completely ignored it… What is wrong with me?” Hunter was clearly losing his marbles as he dropped the pieces of the sphere, damaging them even further. He was not sure what to do at first. He tried to reach for the sphere, then for the dead body, then for his head.
“Wow, doc relax, just calm down what’s so important about that sphere?” Dray grabbed Doctor Hunter by the shoulders, trying to calm him down.

“Egh… The sphere…” Hunter began saying something about photons, something about mind and other technobabble which Dray could not make heads or tails about, but it clearly was calming him down, so he let him talk as they continued to move forward.
“Ok, hold on to the sphere and just stay close,” Dray thought about sending Hunter back to the entrance but leaving him alone would be a bad decision. They finally reached the inner chamber.

Dray and Hunter were greeted with quite a sight. The lightning was coursing all around the room all the while the black mists were almost enveloping the lightning. Dray looked around, trying to find where Volkov and his team were, only to find them standing on the other side of the room utterly stupefied by the sight.
“Is that Shasira?” Hunter pointed his fingers into one of the lightings that resembled Shasira as it seemed to attack one of the shadow dancers the moment she materialized back.

“No, she is…” Dray pointed at the other lighting which actually materialized into Shasira and just had to defend herself from what looked like a shadow specter and two other shadow dancers attacking her. She skillfully parried all attacks and once again turned into the lighting while the shadow dancers blinked away from her however one was too late. The shadow dancer got hit by an electric discharge, and her black mist seemed enveloped by the electricity as she materialized back.
Dray was trying to make sense of what was happening, his eyes darted from one place to another.

“Two lightings that look like Shasira, about 5 hostiles, and two more of those weird smoky specters. Ok I can work with that” He mumbled to himself as he raised his rifle trying to aim it at the smoky specter.
“I know I can kill those blinking hostels but I need to see if I can kill those specters.” He thought to himself as he tried to predict its movements.

The targeting on the HUD was all messed up; they were never designed to engage this kind of opponent, so Dray just turned it off so that all those false signatures would not distract him. The gun’s sights still showed the amount of ammunition left in the magazine as well as the mode of fire, a 3-burst mode. Finally, he got the specter caught in its sights, but he did not squeeze the trigger just yet.
“OK let’s see if your body language tells me anything.” Dray was observing the specter and realized something they would look in the direction of their blink.

That was the moment when Shasira was hit by this specter, and it seemed to throw her off as shadow dancers smelt blood and capitalized on the advantage. They jumped in on her from all sides, placing more cuts, but Shasira returned the favor as well. She electrocuted the specter with her knife and shadow dancer who also managed to place a hit in her abdomen. The specter dematerialized as the electricity was systematically destroying it. The shadow dancer was convulsing in agony as electricity was surging through her body and also her veins seemed to glow as well like something inside of her was shining brightly due to electricity.

Dray quickly adjusted his aim and put a couple of bursts around Shasira to give her some breathing room. If shadow dancers actually stepped back from Shasira the remaining specter actually stepped into the bullets. When bullets hit the specter it coursed with pulsating waves from hits and then shattered and evaporated. 15 rounds left in the magazine.
Remaining shadow dancers quickly looked at Dray and Hunter, who was still holding two parts of a broken sphere.
“BRING IT ON!!!” Dray shouted out with fury but in all honesty, he was not expecting to get out alive.

They all stood for a second evaluating the situation. The silence in the chamber was only disturbed by the firefight outside of the temple, but it was nothing more but background noise. Hunter began backing away into the hallway. Shasira was holding her wounded stomach, her eyes still sparkling with electricity. Volkov and his team finally came to their senses and also raised their rifles but clearly not exactly sure how this should play out. Shadow dancers glanced at Dray then at Shasira then at Volkov’s team and then at one another. Dray already made a mistake by shooting in the direction of his soldiers to save Shasira if the firefight to break out in this situation blue on blue casualty is well within realms of possibility.

“VOLKOV HIT THE FLOOR!!!” He shouted out and opened fire, and Volkov’s team immediately dropped to the ground. Just like that, chaos of battle resumed. Shadow dancers immediately phased out two in the direction of wounded Shasira and two in the direction of Dray. Dray aimed his rifle closely observing the remaining ammunition in the sights, 12 rounds. He also was mindful of the direction in which shadow dancers were looking. He already knew how far their blink would carry them before materializing and anticipated it. 9 rounds. The shadow dancer dropped to the floor as 3 rounds hit her in the chest.

He rapidly changed the direction of his rifle and switched to full auto, and squeezed the trigger. The first rounds just passed right through the mist but the following ones just ran through the soft body of the shadow dancer. She dropped on her back as the bullets dragged her backward, ripping to shreds her unarmored body.
Shasira was having a tough time. Injury was taking a toll on her, but one of the shadow dancers saw Dray just take care of two others. She saw that he adjusted firing patterns and knew how he did it.

Dray dropped the empty magazine and reached for another one. That’s when he saw this shadow dancer breaking off her assault on Shasira. To his surprise, she blinked in an utterly different direction than where she was looking. He rapidly placed a new magazine in a rifle while keeping track of the erratic movements of the shadow dancer. She was not telegraphic about her movements anymore, but they clearly were not quite as precise anymore. She constantly had to readjust her balance a bit after each blink. He opened fire, miss. 27 rounds. Another burst, miss. 24 rounds.
Yes, she was closing in, but she had to zig-zag more, and in more unpredictable ways. She knew that this odd-shaped fire staff was a very dangerous weapon indeed.

21, 18, 15, 12, 9, the ammo counter was rapidly changing its number with each squeeze of the trigger. Finally, she was within striking distance, this is It. The urge to switch to full auto was great, but something told Dray he will need those 3 bursts instead of the full-on barrage.
First burst when right in front of him, but she materialized to the side of him, clearly trying to catch her balance.

Shadow dancer realized she had to catch her balance once again, problems of not seeing exactly where you were going. She saw the gun barrel moving towards her. Not enough time to cut this human before he fired but an opportunity struck him from another side, so she phased out again.
She felt the hot metal pass through her mist form, way too close, but she dodged it, and now is the time to cut this human down.
“What a shame such a good specimen would have made an excellent gladiator.” She thought to herself as she was forming back her dagger, ready to slit the throat.

She materialized, pulling her dagger towards herself, but it was way too easy as if she was cutting air. She felt something at her chest and saw that this human warrior was kneeling with his fire staff pressing towards her chest. It was hot, and she could not feel her heart beating anymore. Her knees started to give out. She dropped her daggers on the floor and stumbled backward as human rose. She looked into his brown eyes behind this weird glass covering and could partially see the reflection of her own eyes. The partial reflection of her eyes was not filled with anger or hatred that fueled so much of her life but awe, while the eyes of this human looked surprised and then saddened. She reached with what strength was left in her arm to touch his face.
“Magnifis” She uttered with her dying breath and collapsed into the arms of this human, dying with a smile on her face and thought she will live on forever in the memory of this magnificent warrior.
Though Shasira managed to kill the last shadow dancer and the fight outside quieted down, Rangers with assistance of dropship managed to fend off the attack but with some losses of their own as well.

Shasira was utterly furious with Hunter for screwing up the sphere. She screamed at the poor scientist and only her injury probably was stopping her from screaming even louder. Dray could not comprehend the language and still was haunted by alien words he could not comprehend himself but the good doctor needed help.

“Enough!” He shouted as he approached Shasira, the Champion of Zes and Hunter. Shasira actually heeded even though not understanding the language. Hunter was completely morally destroyed and tried to find solace in the image of his boots, the whole situation reminded him of his childhood of being constantly reprimanded by his parents for screwing up social conventions.
“Doc, what is the problem? Why is the sphere so important to her?” Dray was pissed himself he just lost half of his platoon and this image of this dying woman still hanging over his head.
“Well the thing Lieutenant, the sphere was the one of kind key to open the vault where Shasira was to receive a blessing from her father, their god Zes.”
“WHAT? We just lost 20 men for her daddy issues…” Dray now was really furious but then he shook his head and looked at the dead body of his soldier in the chamber who was part of Volkov’s team.
“Doc this can’t be a mission failure not after we’ve lost so many… You said it’s the key right?”
Dray finally centered himself, he was an officer of United Earth Federation and mission failure was not in his vocabulary not after this.

“Yes it is, the sphere is having an imprint of the mind of an ancient creature which then activates a sequence of photon emissions and which I measured and it is actually quite…” Doctor Hunter was about to go on another lecture about the sphere but was interrupted by the sharp look from Dray.

“You said you measured it, so you practically have the mold of the key, all you have to do is to replicate those photon emissions, which is light.” Dray looked at Max clearly showing he was not interested in details of work but in solutions.
“I’ll see what I can do.” Doctor Hunter head out of the temple towards his science team to see if they could replicate the effects of the sphere.
While the science team was hard at work on the new device, Dray starred in 20 black body bags. 20 men he led into this alien world light years away from home, to fight in an alien war and for what?
“So what was so important about this ops?” Volkov approached his commanding officer who stared at the body bags.
“Classified sergeant and for very stupid reason.” Dray continued to stare at the body bags feeling utterly defeated.
“How stupid, sir?” Volkov pressed on, realizing that Dray was hiding something not because it was actually classified but rather because he did it to keep up morale.

“You know we went out here thinking we were gonna shoot up some exotic shit and pack it. Well turns out this story isn’t about us. This Shasira well it was her story and it was supposed to be character development for her. We just stepped into the world of daddy issues, chosen ones, and destiny shit. You know, like in all those fantasy stories. But here is the kicker, we are the cannon fodder in it by the looks of it” Dray explained, still staring at the 20 body bags of men he just lost for some brat.
“So character development, you think she at least learned something?”
Volkov sat next to his commanding officer trying to make sense of the situation.

“If the Greek tragedy is of any indication, hubris is the thing to work with and I doubt that she learned anything from this situation. Doc is about to fix his mistake and grant this brat want she came here for but his mental health is probably busted just like Madison’s who is probably never going to be able to look his daughter in the eyes without seeing this bitch that tried to kill him. Me, I'm stuck with this alien word in my head now and a dead end to my career after so many losses.”
Dray was still staring at the body bags of his soldiers now being loaded into the dropship.
“C'mon there's gotta be something positive that we learned here?”
Volkov rose with anger, staring at the body bags.
“Shoot where they will be, not where they are.” Martinez shrugged so many things suddenly became so pointless…
The Temple.

Lieutenant Dray Martinez sat staring at the black plastic body bags being carried over by his Rangers into the dropship. Next to him stood Sergeant Volkov shaking his head. This was a disaster, for the first time he experienced such losses under his command and the worst part was probably for nothing.

“I don’t believe it; we really are here not for strategic reasons but for sorting out personal issues of some princess?” Volkov said as he collapsed on the tree trunk on which Martinez was sitting a giant temple behind them pulsating with strange glowing engravings and then seizing.

“Well command said that we are here to retrieve the artifact, some powerful weapon but Shasira seems to think that it is hers by birth right. I honestly think it was just a PR stunt on behalf of the command. I’m just going through it all again, you know they teach you things like that in the academy that you will lose people under your command and you will look for answers constantly second guessing yourself and then they tell you just stop doing that. Not as easy as it sounds. Though it raises a question, why would NoirElDems be so interested in it as well?” Dray said calmly not noticing the change in workings of the temple his gaze still focused on the black plastic body bags.
“Maybe there is more to the artifact than we think? Huh something has happened.” Volkov looked back at the temple then reached for his rifle to make sure it was ready for a fight.
A familiar figure in the beige tool jacket and blue jeans walked out of the temple, dragging his feet, hands in the pocket and back slouched.

He was heading towards the drop ship but the moment his face rose up from the ground he saw the black body bags being loaded and that’s when he just collapsed on the ground hugging his knees and rocking from side to side.
“Wow doc whats wrong?” Dray rushed toward doctor Maximillian Hunter followed by Volkov.

“Just get me home… I want to go home back to Earth… It was a huge mistake coming here… Stoner should have been the one to come here… I’m worthless…” Max was rambling incoherently through the sobbing. He was drawing in the attention of soldiers all around him.

“Fucking hell, now the civy is having nervous breakdown just exactly what we needed.” Volkov said in disgust as some soldiers were shaking their heads in disapproval. Martinez first looked at Volkov then at the rest of his soldiers and released a sigh.
“Shut it Dima. Everyone get back to your jobs!” Martinez barked out and without hesitation everyone followed his command leaving him and doctor Hunter alone though still throwing glances at him.

“I’m sorry lieutenant but all of it is my fault, I broke the sphere and now your people died for no reason. Shasira is right, I'm a worthless bookworm…” Max spoke through the tears as he could not dare to look into the face of Martinez trying to find comfort in the image of brown dirt to the right of him. Dray stared at the sorry display of what he could only call as cowardice. But that was a soldier talking to him and he knew that. Dray looked upwards not sure what even to do in this situation.
‘He needs a friend’ a thought flashed across Drays mind and with it he descended to him and sat down next to him shoulder to shoulder.
“Hey doc, cheer up, we are alive and that’s a good thing.” Dray was looking for words but that was the best he could come up with.

“Well no thanks to me. All I did was mess up the one thing I was entrusted with, to protect the sphere and I broke it because I was scared. I wish I could be as brave as you are, as strong as you are, Lieutenant.” Max moved his shoulder away from Martinez in shame.
