“Doc it was not your job to guard it. Your job here is to figure things out and find solutions to the problems we grunts can’t.” Dray elbowed Max in the shoulder who still was unable to come to terms with his situation.
“I was entrusted and I failed, how can I be trusted to do anything anymore.” Max still would try to find comfort in the dirt rather than look at Dray.
“Max, look at me.” Dray put his hand on the shoulder of Max however he would still not look at him.
“Doctor Maxamilian Hunter, look at me it’s an order” Dray commanded with a somewhat raised voice and as if compelled Max looked at Dray.

“One of the first things they put us through in the academy is to give us an exercise where there were no right answers but we didn’t know that. Obviously we all screwed up in one way or another and they punished us for it and only a week later they told us it was a set up. But by this time we had about 10% of guys that just quit. They sat us down and told us that if we are unwilling to make mistakes and grow from them we have no place leading people. Now that I think of it, what is the point of living if you are unwilling to make mistakes and grow from them.” Dray spoke calmly yet confidently and speaking it out kind of put him at ease as well.
“Well my mistake just cost us the mission.” Doctor Hunter said, washing tears out of his eyes and yet he sounded a bit more assured.
“So how are we going to correct it?” Dray asked calmly without judgment.
“Honestly I don’t know.” Hunter once again turned away in shame.

“Doc I’ve been told you are one of the smartest people in the world and when you are telling me you don’t know I don’t believe you, I think you just don’t want to and now that would be true cowardice worthy of being ashamed of.” Dray spoke fast but clearly with his hand still on the shoulder of Doctor Hunter.
“Well without whatever was left of the sphere I can’t even begin fixing it.” Doctor Hunter once again looked back at Dray.
“And what is the problem with getting it?” Dray said with a coy smile on his face.

“Shasira the chosen of Zes told me that she will kill me if I even dare to look at the sphere or her. That my clumsy bookworm’s hands are good for nothing, that I’m just filled with nonsense that serves no purpose, that my presence here only wastes the air that she breathes and I’m nothing more but a waste of resources of the war effort.” Hunter actually spoke not even sure whether he was angry with himself or with that MaxElDem champion.

“You go get yourself a hot tea to drink. I’ll get you that sphere.” Dray said as he helped the doctor to rise from the ground. Dray first went for the rifle of his, checked if it was loaded and then checked his armor brushing off all the dust from his chevrons indicating his rank as Lieutenant of the Federal Army Rangers. That’s when sergeant Volokov noticed that his commanding officer was preparing himself for something. With a jerk of his head he commanded Volkov to follow him.
“Care to tell me what we are up to sir?” Volkov said as he followed his commanding officer.

“After I lost 20 soldiers in a single day my career is pretty much at a dead end so I no longer care about being nice to our allies for bullying our good doctor. We need that sphere and she is not giving it to us so I’m going to have a chat with her.” Dray said calmly as he once again entered the temple.

“Yeah now that I think of it was totally uncalled for on my part. I sometimes forget that civilians are not soldiers plus he is just a kid” Volkov said somewhat ashamed of his own reaction to the nervous breakdown Doctor Hunter suffered.

“Water under the bridge, Dimon.” Dray said as he marched through the hallway past the dried pool of blood where he lost private Baker. Dray reached for the pocket where he had Bakers dog tags just to make sure they were still there as he walked.

He entered the central chamber and could see Shasira venting out her frustration on the remaining members of the human science team while two of other companions just stood there just hoping to avoid her wrath.

“ENOUGH!” Dray shouted across the room actually startling everyone. Shasira looked furious and despite having deep cuts on her body she rushed towards Dray as he calmly reached for his PDA to activate the translation software.
“TerraDem warrior, you know what your so-called scholars did!?” She spoke furiously as the machine translated it without any emotion.
“You will not speak to my people in such fashion Shasira.” He said through the teeth.

“How dare you talk to me like that you peasant, I am the Chosen of Zes…” Shasira eyes sparked with lightning fury making Volkov actually step back in fear for he saw what she was capable of but so did Martinez.

“I don’t give a shit who you are but I know that if not for me you would be dead and we have a mission to accomplish and your hissy fit is interfering with achieving it.” Martinez said through his teeth as he raised his rifle. Volkov gave a glance to his commanding officer and raised his rifle as well, ready to die.
“You would strike me?!” Shasira said as lightning began rushing through her body as slowly reached for her daggers.

“Try me, I ain't afraid of you.” Dray stepped forward with intent in his brown eyes. As one of the companions rushed towards them realizing that there was a fight about to break out. It was Datrius, a warrior of Res, while the other companion, Ratmisia the huntress of Artem, just followed him.
“Exalted Shasira, honored Dray Martinez. I beg off you come to your senses. We are not enemies here.” The armored body of Datrius blurred in between Dry and Shasira.
“I’m in the right here and she just does not want to admit it, Datrius.” Dray said as he lowered his rifle.
“I do owe you my life, TerraDem.” The lighting stopped rushing through Shasiras body however she still spoke through her teeth.

“Good now take that damn sphere and tell doctor Hunter that you are sorry and you want him to fix it.” Dray said with calm fury in his voice and felt really sorry that PDA could not translate emotions.
“What?” She stepped back in shock.

“Swollow your fucking pride and do it, you can think of it as test your god is putting you through. I personally don’t give a damn how it is going to happen but it will happen are we clear?” Dray stepped forward right into the hand of Datrius who still stood in between them. Shasira jerked her hand upwards and thunderous lighting came out of it hitting the ceiling. Than she lowered her hand and began walking towards the exit from the chamber, breathing with anger and pieces of strange sphere on her belt.
“You will regret this TerraDem.” She said as she walked past Dray.
“Just get it done” Dray answered back, not even giving her the courtesy of the look.

“It is somewhat unwise to anger chosens of the gods. They have a great destiny to fulfill and going against their will is usually a good way to anger the gods but now that I think of what you said. Maybe it was Shasiras destiny to have you in her journey to teach her a lesson in humility.” Datrius said in broken English as he breathed out in relief.

“I have to say lieutenant you are far wiser than I thought.” That’s when Ratmisia dared to speak though unlike Datrius he could not speak human language and as such she spoke in the direction of those marvelous trinkets humans were wearing to help them communicate.

“Seriously guys what I just heard from you sounded really fucked up to me. No one is destined for greatness and as such no one is privileged to be in someone's life's story.” Dray just stepped back shaking his head hearing something so stupid out of his allies.

“I beg to differ, Lieutenant. Our fates are already written. We might not understand it very well but I was fated to be a warrior and Shasira was fated to do great things as chosen by Zes” Datrius spoke looking at Dray as a fool who was going against fate itself.

“Datrius TerraDems were never written in the strings of fate. They have no destiny written for them yet here they are standing strong and doing great things.” Ratmisia touched Datrius on the shoulder as she looked at Dray in a strange mix of confusion and awe.
“You are right, Ratmisia.” That’s when Datrius looked at Dray with the same expression as Ratmisia.
“Ok stop looking at me like that, it’s creepy.” Dray stepped back terrified of what he was witnessing on the faces of those two strange aliens.

Doctor Maximillian Hunter stood in his beige sleeveless jacket in front of the one particular black body bag that was still to be loaded onto the dropship by Federal Army Rangers. After making a gulp and taking a deep breath he reached out to unzip the black, plastic body bag to reveal, peaceful and pale face of the young soldier.

“I’m so sorry it happened to you, private Baker, I’m so sorry I was such a clumsy mess that you had to stay behind to save my worthless life. And for what? In the end I broke the damn thing because I couldn’t keep it together when everyone relied on me. They all say that I’m one of the most treasured minds in the world but in all honesty I’m just a coward, a pansy that always needed someone to stand up for him and now it cost a life of man far more deserving of it than I am.” Max was barely holding back his tears as he spoke.

“Scholar Hunter” He heard his name pronounced by the angry female voice that he learned to dread so much. He immediately shrank, not sure what to do, still staring at the closed eyes of private Baker as Shasira, the chosen of Zes, stood behind barely holding her fury back. She was breathing heavily clearly about what she was not pleased about.

“Stand up and turn around TerraDem when I’m talking to you!” She shouted out in ElDemian as her patience with Max’s paralysis expired. Even though Max’s ElDemian was still rusty, he clearly understood the command as he rapidly rose and turned to face her. For a moment she wanted to snap at him again but her outburst drew the attention of human soldiers. They did not know what she was saying but even if this kid was a worm he was still their worm and they all instinctively reached for their rifles as tensions rose. Shasira did pick up on that at least.

“Scholar Hunter… I want to apologize for my outburst… And would like you to help me out with fixing the sphere.” She said not trying to look at him and clearly forcing words out of herself as she extended the two pieces of arcane device towards Hunter.
“Thank you Shasira.” Hunter took both pieces with trembling hands and still sounded utterly defeated.

“Now go tell your warrior that I did as he asked.” She once again commanded Hunter through her teeth. Hunter made a step forward towards the temple but then stopped and Shasira’s eyes sparkled with lighting fury upon seeing it. She was about to unload on him again but he did not pay any attention to her as he descended back to the body bag and zipped it up back again.
“I’m sorry once again private Baker.” He said as he picked up the pieces of the sphere and began walking back to the temple while Shasira just shook her head with cooling frustration.

He walked towards the temple not even sure what he was going to do. Once again somebody had to stand up for him. First it had to be his brother at school, then Stoner at the university and now Lieutenant Dray Martinez. Always someone had to fight his battles for him because he was a spineless coward who’s only thing was the ability to soak up academic information like breathing air and do advanced calculus in his head.

“So you see these lines, Lieutenant they seem to fall in a certain pattern it is actually quite unbelievable. I think the precursors actually were big on the whole basic geometry tricks. Like you see this particular lines here it makes a perfect tetrahedral if we take the point of origin this point at the center of the chamber where the sphere was supposed to go'' A woman in white shirt and beige cargo pants said as she was waving her laser measuring device to draw imaginary lines to show to Lieutenant Dray Martinez and Sargent Dmitri Volkov. The rest of the science team were taking measurements of the chamber that looked like a six sided star covered in all manner of what were supposed to be glowing lines. Those lines stopped glowing the moment Shasira tried to insert two broken pieces of this strange sphere in the center of the room.

“Doctor Akilah Fahir, I have no idea what you are talking about. All I asked about, can we crack open the vault with shaped explosives to get to that thing we came here for. I assume it is underground right like below this point?” Dray said with frustration pointing his rifle at the central point on the floor and at this moment Sergeant Volkov pulled a square brick of plastic explosives out of his pocket.

“She does not know. Nobody does, she is just fascinated with aesthetics of this place but whether they play an actual role in opening the so-called “vault of the ancients” she does not know. As for shaped charges, if you want to just make a massive hole in the floor, yeah they would work but our ultra-sonic scans revealed there is only rock below us.” Max said in a depressed voice as he approached them.
“More than just the rock Max and you know that.” Akilah said with some level of disrespect towards the head of the science team.
“Well a couple of other structures but no cavities. Maybe part of some machinery but no vault.” Max said as he shrugged in an uncaring manner.
“Ok where is the vault then?” Dray said as he began looking around for a place where to place a shaped charge.

“I doubt that there is one. For all we know the place could have been made only for worship and the sphere activated some lights to reveal some philosophical insight of the precursors.” Max said with some disdain as he tracked the lines in the chamber with his gaze.
“My men died for this shit I want to know what it is for. Can you fix this damn sphere?” Dray actually raised his voice pointing his finger at one of the broken pieces of the sphere.
“I don’t know.” Max stepped back away from Dray and once again shrank in stature as if he was to be beaten.
“What do you mean you don’t know?” Dray said through his teeth as he tilted his head and pressed forward at Max.

“It means I do not know alright so yeah you wasted your time standing up for me, Lieutenant. Shasira was right to scream at me.” Max spoke as he turned away from Dray as his eye began getting wet once again.
“Take a look at the sphere, doctor.” Dray said calmly after he stepped back, closed his eyes and took a deep breath.

“Fine” Max said as he put down both parts of the sphere and began poking at it with his pen. He was twisting and turning for a couple of minutes examining its contents with. A bronze shell with the core cracked in half crystal and a bunch of strange lines arranged in strange geometric patterns which almost seemed to be made of some manner of optical fiber. The very process of inquiry actually seemed to put Max at ease, he actually felt some manner of safety as he was examining the device.

“Hmm, interesting I don’t see any processing units. As matter of fact I don't do anything that would look like a computing unit. Only that broken crystal at the center of it. It was supposed to produce this light of specific frequency but how did it regulate it then?” Max said, still holding the half of the sphere in his hands, his eyes focused on the cracked crystal.
“It is alien tech, do not presume it operates in the same way as our tech does.” Doctor Fahir said with some surprise that Max would not take that into account.

“Fair point, but if we don’t understand how it works how do we fix it or replicate it?” Max said as he raised his eyes to look at doctor Fahir who upon hearing it looked upwards touching her mouth with a hand.
“Eghmm I’m no egghead but if it quacks like a duck, it’s a duck.” Dray said after a single moment of contemplation as he looked at the sphere.
“Excuse me Lieutenant but what?” Max said as he and Fahir looked at him not exactly sure what he meant but unlike them he did have at least some idea.
“Like do we know what light it makes? If we do, shouldn’t we just be able to replicate it with our tech?” Dray said somewhat reserved manner having a fear of sounding stupid.

“Good point Lieutenant, we did after all measure the wave harmonics of the sphere produced with its light. Hermitz should be able to come up with something” Max actually lightened up as he said and immediately rushed towards one of the science team members who was making measurements.

Shasira, after cooling off and not feeling welcome among human warriors, decided to return back to the temple only to see humans building some manner of contraption with their measuring trinkets. Upon seeing their Chosen of the Gods, Datrius and Ratmisia rushed to Shasira’s side.
“What are those TerraDems doing?” She asked in confusion, staring at the contraption they were setting up.

“I don’t know, your excellency but they seemed to come up with a way to mimic the sphere of the ancients with their craftsmanship.” Datrius said as he landed his spear and straightened up. Ratmisia just kept her head bowed while Datrius spoke.
“But that’s impossible, the art of the ancients is so fine pitched that even the most talented of mages could not repeat their spells.” Shasira said in utter disbelief as looked at Datrius.
“They seem to be quite sure that this will work. They said something about measuring and recording the light song of the sphere.” Datrius said still with hands
“There is more than just a light song to the key of the ancients though but let’s see them fail will only serve as proof to Zes that Miala was wrong to invite those people without fate.”

“Ok it all set up let’s do it.” Doctor Edgar Hermitz said as he finally wrote up an application on his PDA that regulated the output of laser measuring devices. With a couple of touches on the screen of his PDA the lasers run a basic diagnostics changing their colors from deep red all the way to ultraviolet and back as some manner of rainbow machine. With a self-satisfied smile he activated the special legs he constructed upon which this contraption stood and with robotic precision legs began raising the contraption up. Once they reached a certain height, he activated lasers once again.

The light from lasers hit specific spots all over the chamber and the entire chamber echoed with some strange noise as the light began rushing across the lines all over the chamber reaching out for the spots where lasers hit but then stopped as nothing happened. They deactivated the laser and chamber echoed with similar noise but in backwards fashion and lights retreated. He repeated the process a couple of times with a similar outcome.

“Well at least it is something, we are on the right track that’s for sure.” Max was actually somewhat glad for this outcome after all it did mean that there was more to the mystery of this temple to figure out.

“As I expected TerraDems, your trinkets might be quite interesting but in the end they are nothing more than toys compared to ancient knowledge.” Shasira walked triumphantly towards the group of scientists and two Rangers.
“Of course the aether component, their entire technology runs on it.” Max said as Shasira approached them to scold them once again but now wanted to rub it into Drays face.

“So your so-called scholar is worthless just like the light song is worthless without the sound of aether.” She said to the face of Dray with a gloating smile. Dray just looked at her not bothering to turn on the translation software on his PDA while Max tried to move behind Dray's back as unlike him he did know some Eldemian.
“Quacks like a duck.” Max whispered as suddenly his eyes brightened up as if a light bulb just lit up in his head.

“Doctor Hunter?” Dray actually picked up on that as he turned away from Shasira. That maddened her and she was about to start shouting at Dray but was stopped by the index finger of Dray raised right in front of her eyes.

“She said that a light song is nothing without an aether sound. I think light harmonics actually act as aether encryption which in turn activates the temple.” Max said once again getting lost in comfort of his mind as he looked at the points which laser struck to no avail.
“So we will need a mage to direct this magic stuff along the laser lines, Shasira maybe?” Dray said calmly as he activated the translation software on his PDA.
“What are you talking about TerraDems?” Shasira sprung up upon hearing her name.
“I think we might need your help.” Dray said calmly as he turned back to face Shasira.
“Excellent with what?” Shasira lit up with glee, ready to once again demonstrate her divine superiority to those humans.

“Can you direct your magic along the laser lines matching their harmonics, vibrations, notes?” Dray said not exactly sure if he was using the right words as he turned to Max as if looking for the correct answer.
“She can’t do it” Max whispered as he once again avoided Shasira and Dray widened his eyes with surprise upon hearing it.

“No I can’t do that because ancient knowledge is lost to us and to do so requires understanding of nature that is beyond us.” Shasira said dismissively as if Dray suggested something utterly idiotic. Dray looked at Shasira as realization began dawning on him that aliens were not as advanced as he once thought and then he looked back at Doctor Hunter.
“Doctor Hunter?” Dray uttered, staring intensely at him ready to hear why Shasira was unable to do it.

“That’s because it requires precision which they can’t do with their magic. I bet these fiber optics tubes were actually responsible for photonic computation while the crystal acted as an Aether focusing array.” Max said looking back at the broken sphere as if beginning to gain greater appreciation for the precursor technology.
“Can’t you explain it to her?” Dray said calmly his gaze was still focused on Doctor Hunter who clearly was piecing together something in his mind as he stared intensely at the sphere.

“No I can’t, because it would require her to at least be able to understand the Hamiltonian Path problem and she can’t do even the advanced calculus let alone dealing with Graph theories.” Max said with a guilty smile as he looked at Dray.

“Can’t really blame her. I still have nightmares about doing advanced calculus in school, and the other shit you said I will not even touch with a ten foot stick.” Dray’s expression changed to graceful defeat as he stepped back away from Max and the sphere.
“But you can do it Max.” That’s when Doctor Fahir pointed her finger at Hunter with a triumphant smile.
“He can?” With shock Dray looked at Akilah.

“Yes, he has been practicing magic with High Councilor Miala and I’ve seen him do quantum calculations in his head for funsies.” Akilah rushed to grab Hunter by the shoulders as if presenting the best toy ever made to Dray. Hunter began dashing his head from side to side in a complete panic not sure what was happening.
“Well Doctor Hunter it is your moment to shine.” Lieutenant Dray Martinez said with a coy smile, eyes focused on Doctor Hunter.
“Eghm I will need Aether to do so.” Max was clearly trying to weasel his way out of it while Dray just shook his head in gleeful denial.
“Oh you are not getting out of this so easy, go and get it.” Dray nodded in the direction of Shasira.
“You want me to ask her to give it to me.” Max gulped in terror.
“Yes you need to start standing up for yourself Max.” Dray put his hand on Max’s shoulder as he spoke directly into his face.
“Eghm ok.” Max said as he looked down and took a deep breath and then walked up to Shasira.
“Shasira…” Max uttered, his hands were trembling, his heart was beating like crazy and panic rushed over his head as he was abruptly interrupted.
“What?” Shasira barked at Max with fury. Max blinked for moment and stepped forward
“I will need that flask of Aether water from you?” He said confidently pointing at the flask of blue glowing water on her belt.
“What for, worm?” Shasira said as she tilted her head and leaned forward at him.
“I will about to do what you can’t” Max put down his foot only to meet Shasiras gaze head on.

“Oh really go ahead worm make a fool of yourself even more?” Shasira said handing over the flask with disdain. Oddly enough Shasiras disdain actually gave more confidence to him as he opened the flask and downed it.

The energy rushed over his body as his veins and eyes began glowing, wasting the precious energy. He started doing the breathing exercises that this Delta operator showed him and managed to center his mind as the glow in his veins and eyes stopped.
“Edgar hit it again” He said as he looked at Hermitz who just waited for to see what happens
“Sure Max. Let’s see the light show.” Edgar once again touched his PDA to activate his contraption while Max was making final reviews of his notes on the light harmonics.

The lasers hit the spots, the light across chambers lines rushed towards them as the strange powering up noise once again echoed. Max extended his arms towards the contraption as some manner of rings formed out of his arms. They hit the contraption and lit up the laser lines with pulsating energies as if following some manner of pattern. The spots glowed up and even brighter light flooded over the lines out from the spots and then began projecting inward creating something akin to holographic tetrahedra. The tetrahedron began spinning as it descended downwards encapsulating all present in the chamber. It began spinning so fast that its light literally turned everything the group was seeing into but white haze.
Within moments they found themselves presented somewhere else yet still there as if extra dimensions folded up on this chamber revealing some manner of artifact. An ethereal dagger of some sorts.
Preliminary Analysis.

CID Agent Lamar Tadison sat in the tavern in the ivory MaxElDem city of UrboSaptia. His PDA laid in front of him running the translation software converting the song of the MaxElDemian bard into English text while he held a black tablet with the ElDemian alphabet and dictionary on its screen. He was a man out of place in his gray business suit, white shirt and black tie, he would take a deep breath from time to time to put his mind at ease.

“You really look out of place here Lamar.” A human in black digital patterned camo uniform approached him. The pistol on his leg was holstered, his right hand rested on it quite comfortably and his gray eyes looked rather calm and condescending.
“Well ain’t that my favorite psychopath Major Raymond Crawford. I need a star blown up.” Lamar lifted his eyes away from his PDA to greet Delta Force commander.
“Just one? Which one and when?” Major asked with a condescending smirk on his face.
“Cocky bastards” Lamar chuckled for a moment.
“Have not seen you since the Resort. You were an instructor, you should know better to blend in with the crowd.” Crawford sat down in front of Lamar completely ignoring the accusation.

“And you should have been more offended by me calling you out Ray, at least give me a tiny bit of micro-response like tense up your facial expression or clench your fist for a moment something to remind us that you can actually feel things. It would be a more human response.” Lamar said with a smile his brown eyes stared inquisitively at his old student.
“Don’t need to hide my true nature here.” Crawford put his hands on the table spread wide open, fingers wide open the gray uncaring eyes focused on the agent of Central Intelligence Department
“You’ve got the light I seem to have forgotten my one” Lamar pulled out a pack of cigarettes and started checking his pockets for the lighter.

“Those will kill you.” Crawford without hesitation pulled out the black lighter with a symbol of anarchy on it. With gentle flame he lit up the cigarette in the mouth of Lamar who seemed to pay more attention to the lighter than to Crawford.

“Occupational hazard, nice lighter by the way. So this is what you have chosen. I wonder though why you so casually show it? I trained you to notice things like this and you knew I would notice it and what it means.” Lamar said as he pulled out his own lighter showing it to Crawford happy with a small victory he just achieved.
“It is nothing more but lighter Lamar, don’t look into it too hard.” Crawford said with a smile closing the zippo lighter with a signature metallic cling.

“Sure Ray, it is just lighter and the advice Lexington asked me about metaphorical rebirth had nothing to do with it.” Lamar exhaled the smoke out of his mouth in the direction of Crawford who did not pay any attention to it.
“So what are you doing here?” Crawford said as he looked over the table noticing both the tablet and the PDA laid out.

“Getting a better understanding of what we are dealing with here and also learning the language. Other than academic description we have no idea what this world is like and we both know there is a lot more to the world than textbook definitions.” Lamar leaned backwards enjoying the fumes of poison of choice in his profession.

“So collecting stories and seeing how the populace interact with one another in a less professional environment.” Crawford threw a look at the people in the tavern. There were all kinds of people here and only a few of them were ElDems which the United Earth Federation assumed to be the dominant race if anything to were Maginus people that were the most prominent race in what was known as the Main World. A strange human like people without any hair and elongated bald heads.
“Yes and oddly enough they already have a story about you.” Lamar pointed a cigarette at Crawford with a smile.
“Really?” Crawford raised his eyebrows in surprise and leaned forward ready to hear a story.

“The bard here already sang about you and your team. Here, take a look.” Lamar grabbed his PDA, navigated through the applications on it and brought out the text translation of the song he recorded then handed it over to Crawford.
“With the assuredness of giants they march,
with swiftness of wind they run,
with subtlety of cats they stalk
with precisions of an eagle they strike.
Woe the name of Delta Warrior.
The dark cousins have bested the might of Res and undone the swiftness of Artem.
Gloated the cousins, assured of their victory they were
Only to be stuttered by the echoes of something not known
Swiftly the witch acted to raise her protection
With the thundering buzz of bees
what was not known revealed themselves.
With the assuredness of giants they march
With swiftness of wind they run
With subtlety of cats they stalk
With the eye of an eagle they strike
Woe the name of Delta Warrior
TeraDems warriors rained their wrath on the dark cousins with their staffs of buzzing thunder.
Their armour is of metal yet one with nature,
Their hearts is of ice yet one with the battle
Their minds an enigma yet with clarity of the diamonds.
Bested they have the servants of the witch
Assured the witch was of her might against sigils of Pyramid of The Dagger.
Quick with their minds, they used trinkets of the fury of the thunderstorm
With blink of an eye the protections of the witch were blown away
Without a moment of thought, with a heart of ice his thunder stick brought a swift end to the vile witch.
With the assuredness of giants they march,
With swiftness of wind they run,
With subtlety of spiders they stalk,
With the eye of an eagle they strike.
Woe the name of Delta Warrior.”
“Well that’s definitely written differently from the report I filled.” Crawford put down the PDA somewhat unimpressed with what he just read.

“Ray look at the words, '' Woe the Name of Delta Warrior, Hearts of Ice. They hint at fear rather than salvation” Lamar leaned forward pointing at the PDA quite disappointed with the reaction of Major Crawford.

“PR is not my department, Lamar. We are Delta Force and we were trained for clandestine operations not to be the poster boys. We are here to do the job and the fact they have hints of fear is good cause if our allies fear us that means our enemies do so as well and I intend to capitalize on that.” Crawford leaned backwards as he raised his arm and clenched his fists, his eyes somewhat absent in thought. Lamar, noticing the fact that Crawford was already formulating a plan based on that information, released a frustrated sigh and put out a cigarette.

“Ray, we are making first impressions here. They will always judge us from the prism of that first impression.” Lamar said as he moved his hands in the direction of Crawford as if holding an imaginary box in them. Crawford tilted his head and focused his gaze at Lamar.
“Then we need to put a spotlight on someone else cause you know the nature of our work is quite messy.” Crawford spoke as he looked to the side, changing the direction of his thoughts.
“Do you have anyone in mind?” Lamar leaned even further towards Crawford as he picked up on him already solving his request.

“Lieutenant Dray Martinez of the 245th Ranger Regiment. The guy can think on his feet, knows how to deal with combat losses, has a heart, isn't afraid to put his life on the line and can throw a textbook out of the window when it is needed. A good commander by all accounts and is perfect for being a poster boy.” Crawford returned his gaze back at Lamar and spoke fast and clear as if giving a report to his commanding officer. Upon hearing that Lamar reached for his tablet and began searching through military files on lieutenant Dray Martinez. He was rather disappointed to see his record given his last engagement he lost half of his force to NoirElDem attack. Then he began reading into the report more closely and began piecing together the fact that Dray Martinez made the best of a really bad situation.

“General Abramson listens to you Ray, so this one is up to you not me.” He said still going through the report realizing that unfortunately the career of Martinez was most likely over after such losses no matter how good his actions were.
“So other than learning about local cultures what else are you doing here?” Now Crawford sounded with a hint of minor frustration in his voice.
“Ivory alliance, our allies have no idea about the full size of the NoirElDem empire.” Lamar leaned backwards, putting the tablet down and shaking his head.
“What?” Crawford sounded calm but it did surprise him.

“The Ivory alliance is just but a fraction of different factions and city states among thousands. Many of which were already conquered.” Lamar said, finally letting his expertly crafted guard down as he sounded really concerned.
“How do you know that?” Crawford tilted his head looking at Lamar.
“See this Maginus there, the bold one with elongated head.” Lamar nodded in the direction of a bold figure in torn rugs clearly suffering from malnutrition.
“Yeah” Crawford said as he looked at the poor refugee.
“He is a refugee from the Govargo Kingdom” Lamar said as he began navigating through applications on his tablet looking for map applications.
“Never heard of it?” Crawford said, shaking his head and not quite sure why this should bother him.

“Neither did any of people here but from what I gathered it is on the other side of known world and that’s at least hundred jumps away from here” Lamar said as he showed star charts he managed to compile so far and Govargo Kingdom appeared somewhere in unexplored territory on the opposite end from where they were.

“That would mean that the Size of the NoirElDem empire is at least that of all the human territories combined,” Crawford said looking at the star charts that also included those strange portals between planets that allowed travel between them without reliance on space travel.
“You see the problem?” Lamar said, his heart raced and barely kept his breath calm.
“Just an obstacle to overcome,” Crawford said, putting the tablet down, not an ounce of concern in his gray, uncaring eyes.
The gifts of the Gods

Doctor Akilah Fahir moved through the bridge connecting two enormous white towers. The coy smile on her face was under the shade of the brown cowboy hat she was wearing as the beige scarf made of synthetic material not dissimilar to silk wrapped around her neck. She was extremely satisfied with herself since she just managed to ditch the protection detail of Federal Army Rangers. Not that they were problematic people but she wanted to see the people of this grandiose MaxElDem city of UrbaSaptia without them constantly stopping what they were doing the moment a squad of heavily armed alien soldiers entered their vicinity.

Sure she still looked quite out of place in her white baggy shirt and beige cargo pants, pockets filled with all manner of archeological tools. Most people here were wearing some manner of togas, strange elaborate dresses and in some cases armored breast plates. This was truly a center of commerce of this world as there were all manner of different humanoid races present here.

All manner of different shops selling foods, trinkets, potions, some manner of herbs, textiles and weapons could be seen everywhere. To her greatest surprise despite it being the MaxElDem city, Eldems were not the majority race present as matter of fact it were those strange Maginus people with their elongated bald heads that reminded her of those ancient Egyptian pictures. Cities architecture is truly grandiose but after 5-kilometer-tall arcologies of Earth it was hard to find anything to be as impressive in terms of size at least.

The artistry however of this place was truly impressive if Federal architecture prioritized function this place went all out on the form. Large white columns made of this strange stone that felt as light as plastic with beautiful symmetric curves protruding as if branches of the trees, not to mention the way the whole place worked with the natural light of the local star.

The entire city strangely reminded her of the forest with giant white pillars for trees and elaborately decorated bridges as lianas connecting those pillars. It was far cry from artificially lit streets of arcologies she spent most of her life in.

She finally reached the end of the bridge that connected it to some manner of balcony wrapping around the pillar. There was a staircase wrapping around the pillar leading to the upper level. The whole pillar must have been at least 50 stories high and it was not even the largest one but one of medium sized ones ending with this upside down tear like dome.

She walked around the balcony and could see the Federal military base outside of the wall looking like some manner of refugee camp, sticking out like a sore thumb with its prefabricated metal structures. A city of its own erected in a matter of a day with the large streets for those clunky tanks to pass through, large landing pads for human aircraft to land and take off from.

It was also quite busy with life but it was so different from life up here, everything down there had its place all following the military organization. It all seemed quite unnatural to her but the sight did draw a lot of attention from the locals. They clearly were quite fascinated by the arrival of Task Force Noble. After all they never saw people as alien as humans with all their complex machinery, strange military cloth and armor.
“Do you think all of their cities look like that?”
“If so I wouldn’t want to live in them. They are quite tasteless and so boring. I mean look at that every building looks the same and so dull.”
“It does seem quite practical though.”
“From what I understand this is their warrior orders, maybe ordinary people live in a different place.”

Akilah overheard conversation between four onlookers as she passed by them. Though she was still quite rusty in ElDemian but she did manage to get the gist of it. She actually chuckled for a moment. Surely they thought that this city was the greatest city all across the stars. She could only imagine their awe if they saw giant arcologies of Earth. Maybe one day they will visit it, things are changing after all now that non-interference agreement was lifted.

There it was the entrance into this white pillar and the music coming out of it did assure her that it was indeed a tavern. A good sign after all how many taverns were out in this place? Answer is a lot but she was hopeful it was the one she was looking for and she wouldn’t have to walk anymore. It did take a good while to navigate this place especially since she could not find any means of transportation just yet that is.

Without hesitation she walked into the place. Those columns were actually hollow inside, must be because they were more like bamboo trees. The place looked astounding, the white stone out of which the whole city was made actually allowed light to enter right through despite being at least a 10 centimeters thick. The tavern had 3 levels, rooms with balconies hanging over what appeared to be like some manner of bar in the center. She easily saw some MaxElDems with the use of magic, cooking food and making some manner of drinks.

The bards were clearly amplifying their performance with magic but what was most fascinating they all clearly were singing different songs yet she could hear only one of them. The moment she crossed some manner of imaginary line she could clearly hear another song and a different voice no longer able to hear the other one.She jumped from side to side like a child amazed with such a normal occurrence for the locals.

“Oh this is amazing. I wonder how it works, a sound laser of some sorts?” She said as she shifted her body from one side to another her head switched from bard to bard while everyone inside seemed to look at her like at an idiot.

“Doctor Fahir, I see you are quite enthralled with local music performance” The sound of English was so easily recognizable in this cacophony of alien sounds and music that Akilah immediately pinpointed the location it was coming from.

Her thick long brown hair waved underneath her hat as she turned the gaze of her olive eyes at the location of the familiar language. There he was. A man sitting in a gray business suit and a black tie. PDA, a holographic tablet and a cup of what looked like ale on the table in front of him.
“Agh Mr.Smith of Global News Network. Nice to meet you if it is your name that is.” Akilah rushed over with a coy smile extending her arm to greet the man that invited her.
“I do not know what you are implying, Doctor Fahir?” The man in the gray suit said with genuine confusion on his face as he rose up to greet Akilah with a firm handshake
“Oh cut the bullshit if anything you are not trying all too hard to hide the fact that you are a CID agent.” Akilah sat down in front of the man in the gray suit.

“Hey locals have no idea what CID agents look like, for all they know how all TerraDem archivists look like. But you are right, no point in hiding who I am from you at least. Lamar Tadison, pleasure to meet you.” Tadison said with a smile as he adjusted his black tie.

“I’ll be honest; you are the first Central Intelligence Department agent I have ever met. It is all so exciting.” Akilah said as she threw an enthusiastic look at the tavern basking in all the different people she was seeing and not one of them was human.
“So I guess you are enjoying being here.” Tadison said as he looked over the tavern as well but his trained eye was picking up on subtle differences in body language.

Like for example that Maginus kid was fiddling with hands clearly nervous about something probably that Maginus girl that was sitting in the company of what was clearly a seasoned warrior whose arms were spread out wide open clearly confident that nothing here could harm him.

Or that twitching leg on the MaxElDem warrior as he was throwing dice, clearly playing some gambling games with his far more confident compatriot who clearly was playing a numbers game not worried about one loss in a single game but aiming to win over all.

Or this example of fascinating social interaction: the couple of other Maginus people constantly throwing glances over to the entrance just when the other was not looking, someone was having an affair.

“Are you kidding me? It is a dream come true for me. I get to explore alien cultures, explore their myths, uncover ancient secrets, work with the brightest minds from all over the United Earth Federation, be a pain in the ass of the military, see dashing heroes in action, have adventures and meet sinister CID agents.” Akilah dropped her hands on the table and leaned forward with a devious smile.

“Oh I’m not as sinister as you think I am. If anything I’m quite friendly.” Tadison said calmly as he slowly turned his head back to Akilah the gray silver on the sides of his head did give off this silver fox vibe.
“Well that’s what a sinister CID agent would say.” Akilah pointed a finger at Tadison with childish joy.
“How about a drink? My treat, well the Federations treat given it is on the discretionary budget.” Tadison said as he leaned backwards and pointed a finger at the cup of ale he had on the table.
“Oh you are establishing rapport with me now agent Tadison, please keep it up.” Akilah locked her hands together above the table as she rested her head on them.

“Barkeep, give me another cup of ale for the lady here.” Tadison shouted out in Eldemian as he raised his hand. Akilah looked towards the bar and to her surprise the barkeep did in fact acknowledge Tadison’s request and was clearly pleased with progress made by the human.
“Wow you already can speak their language so fluently.” Akilah's eyes lit up in awe as she turned back to face Tadison when the barkeep did in fact bring the cup of ale to her.

“Still need to iron out the pronunciation but languages become a lot simpler to learn once you learn a third one and I can already speak seven, not counting ElDemian. Russian, French, Latin, German, Mandarin, Farsi and of course English. So it is a huge change from your usual work back on Earth?” Tadison asked Akilah as he took a small gulp of ElDemina ale that had this odd blueberry aroma to it and yet did not have any sweetness of the cider.

“Oh, a massive change. You know back on Earth the field of archeology is considered a dead end science. I mean we have uncovered all the past civilizations, learned everything about them and neo-archeology is practically just sitting there and comparing historical records, delving into the social network archives and watching a bunch of movies. I mean we did not even have a chance to be on any archeological dig sight because there were none left. Just unearthed cities and temples turned into museums. Anything close I got to doing any digging was practically LARPing on the university grounds. This however is everything I wanted from the profession when I first saw it in the movies.” Akilah said once again turning back to bask in the sight and sounds of this alien tavern.
“Ancient Secrets with Doug VanLein in the main role, I suppose you are a fan?” Tadison said his calm eyes focused on Akilah. Upon hearing the Doug VanLein Akilah threw Tadison a guilty look.

“Oh those corny series… I admit it was a guilty pleasure of mine but I was hooked long before the series came out. My father was a museum head-curator in Egypt. So I was always surrounded by ancient history and he took me to see all other museums all over the planet. Yeah, earthborn girl from an upper class family and all that thought she might uncover the next great mystery. Only to end up, sitting behind the screen reviewing the finding of 18-21st century archeologists and delving into historical records rather than uncovering anything new. So as I said this job is a dream come true for me. What about you, agent Tadison?” Akilah asked Tadison as she grabbed her cup with both hands ready to hear the story of agent Tadison. Maybe he will spill some juicy classified material.

“Personally, my guilty pleasure was this old series called ‘Balancer’ , the story about an ex-CID agent using his skills and knowledge acquired during his time with the Department to right the wrongs on the streets of arcologies. Then a Dargorian War broke out so I wanted to become a CID agent working behind enemy lines and by the time I finally managed to land a job in the Department and finished my Resort training the war ended. So was living off the tales veteran agents were telling and was put in the analyst job. Caught a couple of corporate spy plots and right before this gig was an instructor for new CID agents and spec ops operators at the Resort. Actually asked to be here to see how my skills would work in the new environment.” Tadison has been practicing those lines for some time just enough to keep conversation going and yet not telling anything that would give out too much information to piece together something that was indeed classified.
“I see but I imagine that you did not invite me here just for a social call.” Akilah said somewhat disappointed as she wanted to hear more details about those corporate spy plots.

“You are the right doctor. I’m trying to get a better sense of what the hell we are dealing here with. If we proceed to work with the locals, we need to understand their culture in order not to unwittingly step on any toes here and see how we can utilize the local culture to our advantage. For starters I read this one report that they have a predetermined fate set for all of them so I want to know how exactly it works.” Tadison reached out for his holographic tablet and showed the condensed report he had on MaxElDem society but it was clearly just a bunch of stories put together along with reports given by the Federal military during a couple of operations they had working with the ElDemians.

“That’s actually kind of an interesting aspect about them, I myself am not exactly sure about. I guess they have this deterministic outlook on the universe that everything is written and pre-determined and that we have no choice in our destiny. That does however make me wonder, why do they still have concepts of free will and that they don’t know the future outcome of events?” Akilah touched her mouth with her hand as she leaned back her gaze dropped from Tadison to the table as she was trying to figure out that problem.
“So where do you suggest we start our exploration?” After a good minute Tadison finally broke the silence. Akilah upon hearing looked up and raised her finger as if the idea visited her.
“The House of Oracles in the Clepus temple complex.” She said as she pulled out her PDA and activated the map of the UrboSaptia she compiled for herself and showed it to Tadison.

“Clepus temple complex?” Tadison said looking at the map not exactly sure what he was looking at and then reached for his tablet to check in with the map he had. The names on both maps were different, names on the map of Akilah all had those Eldemian words while the names on the Tadisons map had been assigned for him by the military.
“The medical quarter.” Akilah said without hesitation realizing what was the source of Tadisons confusion as pointed on the holographic projection on Tadisons tablet.
“Why would they have House of Oracles in the medical quarter?” Tadison stared at the map in confusion not fully understanding what oracles had to do with medicine.
“I’m not sure but I think this has to do with reading the future for the new born babies.” Akilah said after a couple of moments of tossing ideas in her head.
“Oh… I see.” Tadison now was beginning to see the logic behind it.

“It is on the other side of the city though. Are you sure you are ok walking there in your suit Agent Tadison?” Akilah rose up ready to take a long walk but was actually excited to see this CID agent sweat in his suit.
“Who said anything about walking? I'll just get myself a local cab.” Tadison reached for the pocket and pulled out some manner of glowing stone.

“Wait a minute, you want to tell me that all this time I had the chance to have a ride?” Akilah at this point was expecting anything to happen from a dragon coming out of this stone to some manner of teleportation. Was it possible, she did not know but after what she had witnessed so far she was not counting it out.
“Yes, did you not notice those flying platforms piloted by those MaxElDem images?” Tadison casually tapped on the certain marking on the stone.
“What?” Akilah said in disbelief, she could have saved so much hustle and here she finds out this CID agent had the way to travel around without putting in the leg work.
“Seriously you’ve been walking around this city all this time and did not notice one?” Tadison was just rubbing the salt in the wound with a coy smile.
“No.” Akilah looked up and away from Tadison as if it was beneath her to be concerned with that.

“Hmm well there are not a lot of them and not everyone can use them but I managed to talk my way into having permission to use it. For god sake I would not walk all the way up here from the base in this suit.” Tadison explained condescendingly.
“As I said sinister CID agent.” Akilah said, pointing a finger at Tadison.

After Tadison gave a couple of golden coins to the barkeep, both walked out to the balcony and saw this ivory circular platform waiting for them. It had marble railings and some manner of blue glow emanating from below it. On top of it on a pedestal with a giant crystal ball stood this MaxEldemian male. He had long golden hair and dressed in white robes with silver shoulder pads and glowing blue crystals embedded in them.
“Archivist Smith I see you have company.” The MaxElDem spoke with resonance in his voice as his eyes glowed with blue light.

“Yes, that's scholar Akilah Fahir. She studies the lives of ancient people and those who are a mystery to ours.” Tadison was flexing his language skills though it was clear that he still had to think of what to speak prior to speaking.

“Agh a nobble fate it is to learn of the olden people, a pleasure to meet you, scholar Akilah, my name is Argotius of the house Khalias” Argotius made a small bow as he spoke his name in the direction of Akilah.

“Greetings Argotius of the house Khalias” Akilah barely managed to squeeze out the words out of herself, clearly messing up the pronunciation with each word. A thought creeped up through her mind was she establishing a human accent?

“I have to say to archivist Smith your mastery of ElDemian tongue has indeed improved. I wonder how fast scholar Akilah will take to master it as well.” Argotius said as he found it rather amusing the way Akilah tried so hard to speak the language.
“Argotius I summoned you here to deliver us to the House of Oracles.” Tadison said as he straightened up.
“To archive the fates of our new born?” Argotius tilted his head in confusion.
“Yes and learn more about how fate is chosen.” Tadison nodded in the direction of Argotius.

“Come on in TerraDems but fate is not chosen, it has already been written, oracles merely read it.” Argotius said as the platform lowered even further allowing for humans to easily step on board. Akilah jumped in without hesitation but quickly found herself looking for chairs to strap herself onto while Tadioson just walked up and leaned on the railing.

“Wait Second… Oh wow.” Akilah screamed out the moment the platform began moving and she rushed to grab onto the railing. To her surprise there was no sudden jolt of movement, it appeared to be the thing had inertial dampers but where were they? She could not claim herself to be an engineer to know anything about how inertial dampers worked but she was pretty sure it required at least some manner of enclosure or a cabin. Then a sudden gust of wind made her grab on to her hat as the platform flew in between the white towers of the city.

“You see that it's a MaxElDemian cruiser. From what I hear Argotius here is training to become a voidwalker, a pilot of sorts. But for now he is merely a neophyte as such this sky chariot as they call them. By the time his training finishes he will be honored to become a member of the crew of the ship not dissimilar to this one.” Tadison said as he pointed at the ship below them.

A huge MaxElDemian spaceship was moving in between the pillars. It looked like some manner of galleon made of the same material as those towers. It had a huge blue dome at the back of it with at least a dozen more dotting its sides of it. There was a red ornamented line covering its entire hull and two eyes painted at its nose, giving off a feeling that the entire ship looked like fish. Its crystalline engines gave off a blue glow pushing it forward.

What amazed the Akilah the upper deck was utterly exposed with all manner of robed MaxElDemians doing something. Entire thing was utterly different from anything humans had. Human space ships were akin to submarines utterly enclosed and certainly could not fly within the atmosphere of the planet.

“It’s a spaceship? We still need to keep our hanks of metal in orbit. How the hell do they keep it up in the air?” Akilah said, still holding on to the hat as she leaned over to take a better look at the ship.
“We don’t know, just one of the many things they can do that we still can’t.” Tadison said with a smile on his face.
“This is just getting better and better.” Akilah turned to face Tadison as her thick long brown hair flowed on the wind and kind of obstructed her vision.

Their flight continued as they crossed into another section of the city. Though pillars here were the same but their tips were made of all the manner of differently colored crystals. All those pillars surrounded a single huge domed structure in the middle of this section of the city. The ivory chariot landed on one of the balconies protruding from the dome structure.

“Archivist Smith, Scholar Fahir we are at the House of Oracles. Please tread with care. It is a sacred place for our people.” Argotius said as he turned away from the crystal he used to pilot the platform.
“We shall treat it with the utmost respect. I think we can take it from here, till fate crosses us again” Tadison said as he jumped off the platform.
“Till fate crosses us again, archivist Smith, scholar Fahir.” Argotius said as he gave a small bow.
“Till fate crosses us again, Argotius.” Akilah said desperately trying not to make a fool of herself with her amateur grasp on ElDemian language.
“Boy they really take this fate stuff to a next level don’t they?” Akilah was finally happy to speak the language she knew well.
“Yes they do. Still a bit of a shock to me to be honest.” Tadison said as they walked towards the entrance to be greeted with two hooded figures.

Their robes looked different unlike the monotone robes they were accustomed to seeing. Those actually looked like the night sky and not just painted but like an actual night sky with an actual star glow and some manner of symbols moving in circular fashion around one another.

“TerraDems, what brings you to the House of Oracles?” The two hooded figures spoke in unison but to the utter surprise of both humans despite speech still being ElDemian they understood it as if it was spoken in English. Both Tadison and Akilah threw glances at one another to see if the other experienced the same thing.
“Eghm I’m archivist Smith and this is scholar Fahir, we are with the Task Force Noble we are here to learn of the Oracles.” Tadison spoke in English to see if the communication worked both ways.
“Your people have no use of Oracles guidance; your strands of fate do not exist TerraDems. Why would you need to learn about oracles?” The two figures spoke in monotone unison once again.
“Wait what?” Akilah actually stepped back in surprise.

“Ok. As an archivist of TerraDems I would like to record the fate of others and how fate is read, scholar Fahir here to learn of how fate unfolded for your people. Is acquisition of knowledge not a virtue?” Tadison was picking his words very carefully trying to find the best way to connect with those figures.
“It is one of the virtues of Goddess Atna, Archivist Smith. So be it you may enter but under our escort.” The hooded figures spoke in unison as they turned to face the entrance and began walking.
“So about our fate not existing.” Akilah grabbed Tadison by the hand.

“Hey I’m as surprised as you are here.” Tadison said, as he threw a worried glance at Akilah. There was one thing that did pulse through his mind though, what if they could see through his minor deception. They did not call him out on it, at least it was a good sign.

As they entered the room they saw that the structure was in fact one giant chamber with a balcony running around the inner circumference. There were multiple bridges that connected the balcony to platforms upon which altars were erected. As they looked above they saw that the dome itself was not dissimilar to the cloaks of those hooded figures that met them. Stars and strange symbols moved in circular fashion on it. But this sight was crossed over by the additional bridges connecting similar balconies they stood upon to those strange platforms as well. Below were thousands of altars next to which couples of all races stood, handing over their children to those MaxElDem priests with white blank eyes.

“Take note Archivist as you look oracles determine the fate of the newborns, this is the service entitled to all free of charge for the child must know one’s fate and not to struggle against it mistake of one’s will” The hooded figures spoke as both Akilah and Tadison leaned over to see what was happening below.
“How does it happen?” Akilah asked as she turned to face the hooded figures.

“Observe signs of the gods.” The hooded figures pointed their fingers to the dome and then as if drawing a line from they descended their fingers to the one of the thousands altars below. That’s when they noticed there were indeed some barely visible strands of glowing light moved from the dome unto the oracles as they held children in their hands.
“Ok so Gods determine the fate of the child.” Tadison said, finally turning his gaze away from the dome.

“Wrong archivist gods are merely a conduit of fate. Oracles lost their sight to read the strands of fate and path it takes. At childbirth the services provided by oracles only tell which God shall serve as guide to the child.” The hooded figures explained in monotone unison.

“Hmm it is like a caste system, similar to some cultures on ancient Earth. When you would be born into a particular caste and as such it will determine the path your life would take but here it is determined by astrology rather than the caste you were born into.” Akilah said as she looked over the thousands of oracles serving thousands of couples.

“So they just determine which career path a person will take and that’s all?” Tadison now was focused on the behavior of this one particular couple to see their body language. The male was clearly a warrior of some sorts while the female he couldn’t tell but most likely she was working in some menial job, a farmer? No, her physic did not point to hard labor. A trader also would not be the case. Her stance indicates a timid demeanor. A seamstress or a bookworm most likely. Hmm and look at that she seems happier than the dad must be because the path fated for their children is more in line with hers.
“This but an initial reading of the fabric of fate, archivist Smith, a deeper reading comes with a fee and is done on this level.” The figures answered the question without hesitation.
“So let me guess that the more detailed the reading the higher the level?” Akilah said as she looked up now far more interested in the purpose of architecture of the place than people involved.
“That would be the correct scholar Fahir.” Hooded figures answered again in their monotone unison.
“Can we see one of those readings up close?” Tadison stepped in the direction of the two figures with a smile.

“Only if the person in question agrees to know one’s fate is an intimate matter.” The hooded figures clearly looked down as they spoke with their hands extended as a stop sign. ‘A clear sign of professional ethics on their behalf’ Tadison thought to himself.
“Can we take a walk and see from the affair than maybe find a willing person?” Tadison asked courteously as he winked at Akilah.

“Yes, this can be permitted.” The hooded figure spoke in unison and began walking. As they walked across the bridge they could see those strands coming from the below closer and each actually seemed somewhat different from the other and there were thousands of them impossible to track each one of them. That’s when Akilah noticed a familiar face as she raised her hand to hail it. A MaxElDem warrior encased in silverfish armor.
“Datrius! What a surprise to see you here. Are you here to read your fate?” Akilah rushed towards Datrius who was actually rather pleased to see her.
“Scholar Fahir, a pleasure to meet you, I see the quest to the Temple of the Ancients did not diminish your spirit and I see you have company.” Datrius said in broken English as he looked at Tadison.
“Datrius this John Smith from Global New Network he is a journalist or what you call archivists.” Akilah winked at Tadison as she spoke.
“Agh the person who records tales.” Datrius nodded his head with a smile as looking at Tadison.

“Pleasure to meet you. I heard a lot about you. You’ve accompanied Lieutenant Dray Martinez on the exploration of that temple and you were the first MaxElDem ally Major Raymond Crawford met.” Tadison said as he extended his arm.
“You are quite well informed, Archivist Smith. Yes, that would be me.” Datrius without hesitation grabbed the hand of Tadison.
“So how would you describe your encounter with Lieutenant Dray Martinez?” Tadison asked Datrius joyfully.

“As I told to his superiors, he is a brave and skilled warrior, not to mention a wise commander. Though I think the losses drew too heavily on him, it must be the first time he suffered them.” At first Datrius sounded reserved, clearly not exactly pleased to comment on the performance of a fellow warrior but then turned his gaze somewhat away from Tadison as he began remembering the ill spirits Martinez was after the battle.

“Well yeah, it is the first time he suffered such heavy losses under his command despite his extensive military career. Anyway we are here to learn about how the fate of your people is being read. And we could use someone willing to share their fate with us.” Tadison somewhat stepped forwards as he was making his request.

“Agh I see. You know what I myself am not exactly sure any more about my fate and having you TerraDems to sit in on my reading would be a clarifying addition as I think you have already meddled with it. So please do accompany me.” Datrius said cautiously as he gestured in the direction of the altar where this blind oracle sat on a bunch of blue pillows, orb in front of her and some manner of potions around her.

“You have been granted permission we shall not go any further.” The hooded duo spoke as they stepped back. Datrius, ignoring the two hooded figures, stepped on to the Altair point to the pillows for the two humans to sit upon. Both Tadison and Akilah sat on those pillows flanking Datrius from both sides.
“What do you wish to know Datrius of the House Sagoros the mighty Acolyte of the Temple of Res?” The oracle spoke in an echoing voice.

“I wish to know of my fate regarding my love and my further path as what I have expected of my fate was wrong.” Datrius said after taking a deep breath ready to see his fate as he extended his arms towards the orb. The lines began flowing out of his hands towards the orb and out of the orb into the hands of the oracle. The oracle looked up towards the dome as the lines began coming out of her eyes into the thousands of strands.

“Let’s see. Fated you are to be a warrior of Res and sit within his halls of Elysium, your deeds are already many and in high regards but more choices still await you.” The oracle spoke as the image in the orb first showed quite definitive images from the point of view of Datrius and his battles reaching out to that fateful day when for the first time he met humans.

“You were fated to love the follower of Artem and yet never to have the chance to live it out.” The image changed to the strand with markings of Res on it moved and twisted around the stars. Then one of the stars directed it to some other strand with markings of artem on it. They moved closer together and then a pink star forced those strands to twist and turn around one another. But then they passed through the purple fog disappearing from the sight with markings of Dis and then just for a moment appearing back in sight but separated. Akilah stared at the orb and then looked up towards the dome noticing that those thousands of strands did indeed twist and move around the stars and signs. More than that many portions of the dome were indeed shrouded in this purple fog which seemed like a cancerous tumor growing on the body of the dome.

“Hmmm this is strange you were fated to fight under the shrouds of Dis and was the reason why you should have not been able to live out the love you hide in your soul.” The oracle began sounding rather disturbed but not exactly surprised as the image within the orb at first became murky with a fading sign of Dis as the strands began twisting and turning around each other once again.

“The strands of fate change the shroud of Dis has lifted from your fate.” Oracle once again seemed to feel more confident in her speech and at this point Tadison looked at Datrius who stared into the orb nervously.

“You are to live and fight against the servants that chose to shroud their fate with Dis.” The markings of Res and Artem on the flared up pushing the fog aside as they moved under the effects of the stars. Akilah looked back at the orb then pulled out the holographic magnifying glass out of her pocket. She looked at the star dome then at the oracle and with a magnifying glass began tracking the strands coming out of her eyes.

“The path of your love twist and turn yet still I see no knot in it.” The oracle spoke, her blank eyes still staring out to the star dome as the image of the two strands still was just moving under the effects of the stars. Akilah's eyes flared up with excitement as she saw what was playing out in the orb on the star dome. Her eyes darted back and forth from the magnifying glass to the orb as she reached out for the other pocket pulling out her laser measurement device.

“It is difficult to read why. The stars that do not allow for it to knot are unseen yet not shrouded by fog Dis.” The oracle said in surprise as suddenly the two strands just changed course with no stars affecting them when suddenly a red straight line passed right next to them. Tadison just realized what happened and rushed over to take the laser out of Akilah’s hands, almost knocking over the orb.
“You dare to disturb the readings TerraDem?” The oracle was furious as her blank gaze dropped from the star dome landing squarely at utterly confused Akilah.
“Oh this is nothing, it's just a chew toy see.” Tadison stuck the laser in his mouth as his chick lit up in the red light. Than thought crossed his mind ‘she is fucking blind’
“You soulless creatures disturb the strands of fate!” Oracle rose up in fury as the strands in the orb unraveled themselves in thousands of directions.

“Did she just call me a soulless creature?!” Akilah was furious upon hearing that and was about to smack the blind MaxElDem with her magnifying glass. At this point a hooded duo clearly were preparing some manner of attack as their hands began squeezing air into firebolts.

Tadison, laser pointer in the mouth, noticed it and even to his greater horror he saw a familiar black quartet of Delta. Operators right behind the hooded duo and one of them already pulled out the EMP grenade.

“Wow, easy.” Tadison screamed out looking at Akilah as he pulled the laser out of his mouth and then nodded in the direction of the orb. He put the laser back in his mouth once again lighting up his cheeks.

The strand with the marking of Res would abruptly end and then appear again, then take a rapid turn away from the strand with markings of Artem then back to it, for no reason whatsoever. It was complete chaos. That’s when Akilah just backed off staring in terror at the orb.
“By the gods what is happening.” Datrius spoke in horror, his attention completely focused on the image in the orb.

“The fate is changing, the strands of it run wild no longer guided by stars of fate. You, soulless creatures, it is all your fault!” Oracle shouted out her hands had lightening through them ready strike as a laser dot appeared on her forehead.

“Cmon, how can a bunch of soulless meatbags change fate? Just calm down, it's just your nerves acting up because we just disturbed you.” Tadison pulled out the damn laser out of his mouth and raised his hands up in the air in act of surrender and the strand in the orb suddenly stabilized.
“You might be right soulless one.” Oracle said once the strands she was seeing once again behaved in a familiar manner as the star separated the lines marked with signs of Res and Artem.

“Yes, now I see the stars of battle prevented from forming a knot. To see further and see if it is due to the end of the strand you should go deeper where I do not dare to go, Datrius. If you dare to know more, go to higher oracles.” Oracle said as she slowly sat back down on to the pillows and two hooded guardians relaxed. The hooded guardians turned around and only then realized they were in the crosshairs of death they never saw. The head Delta operator put finger to his lips indicating that they should keep quiet. The two hooded figures glanced at one another and nodded in the direction of human soldiers.
“I see, thank you, oracle.” Datrius rose in somewhat low spirits.

“Well that was more intense than I expected.” Tadison said as he stepped away from the altar looking at the human soldiers. He glanced back at the orb then back at soldiers. He pulled out a pack of cigarettes to calm his nerves.

“Need a light Tadison?” The familiar voice of Major Raymond Crawford came out from the mask with skull on it as he approached Tadison with gentle flame coming off his black zippo lighter with red sign of anarchy on it.

‘6 times on our knees but gotten up 7 times, the deepest waters wouldn’t take us the highest falls wouldn’t break us, blood and sweat is what made us, it is not even question of chance but matter of choice’ Tadison recited the mantra with which those operators came to him to Resort to learn spy craft as the gentle flame from the black lighter lit up the cigarette.
“Jessus fucking Crhist, what have we done…” He mumbled to himself as he inhaled the nicotine infused fumes.
“How the hell did you guys get here?” Akilah said in complete confusion looking at the quartet of soldiers in black armor.
“Tracked your PDA, doctor. The rest is just what we do.” Crawford said calmly.
“You TerraDems changed my fate. I wonder if you can do it again.” Datrius approached the humans’ eyes full of hope.
“Oh, fuck no…” Tadison said as he face-palmed himself, smoking a cigarette in hand.

“Seriously doctor Fahir, you almost caused a serious diplomatic shit storm down there.” Agent Lamar Tadison said, going through his second cigarette as he walked out of the House of Oracles to the balcony where the black Federal dropship was sitting.
“She called me a soulless creature! She called us soulless! I have a soul, so do you, so do all of us!” Doctor Akilah Fahir vented out in her defense, still furious with being insulted like that.
“That’s completely beside the point.” Tadison said as he approached the black dropship trying to put as much distance between himself and the entrance to that place.
“How is that beside the point? She started it!” Akilah grabbed Tadison by the arm expecting him to explain himself with his remark.
“You did.” Tadison said calmly as he looked right into Akilah's eyes.
“Excuse me?” Akilah stepped back with the expression of being offended.
“The laser pointer, you clearly interfered with their process at least in their eyes.” Tadison pulled out the Akilah’s laser measuring device out of his pocket showing it to her.
“How could a stream of coherent photons affect their so-called reading of fate?” Akilah said as she grabbed her laser pointer presented to her.

“I don’t know and don’t know if it actually did but something did in fact affect the fate of Datrius. And now he wants us to do it again.” Tadison pointed index finger at himself, clearly not pleased with the proposition.
“Then why shouldn’t we?” Akilah leaned as if throwing a challenge to Tadison.

“Why should we? Did you even consider the consequences that it might have down the line? They already have a difficult time with this Fog of Dis obscuring the fate of their people and is clearly causing them huge distress. And you want us to start messing around with it.” Tadison pointed in the direction of the entrance as the black quartet of Delta soldiers walked out followed by two hooded guardians of House of Oracles.

“That’s big coming from the CID agent. You people mess with the fate of other nations all the time. Regime changes, spread of ideologies and propping up puppets. You people always do that for the Federation. Why not do something so minute to help out some poor guy to ensure he gets his love?” Akilah was relentless with her arguments to which Tadison just rolled his eyes. The hooded guardians clearly noticed the argument happening but immediately were stopped by the quartet forming a line, guns in hand.

“And how many times did it bite us in the ass in the long term? Sure we do all manner of subterfuge but at least we do so because we know of outcomes and do so because we know what manner of consequences we will have down the line. Here we have no idea of the consequences. It was the first time we met with anything like that. Human history always had to deal with the unknown and with the idea that nobody knows what tomorrow brings. Their culture clearly does. Do you know what happens to people when this kind of certainty is taken away from them? They lose their shit and that’s us, who have to always take into account not knowing what the future brings. Now think about what will happen to them.” Tadison was trying to justify himself when one of the Delta operators turned his head in the direction of the arguing duo, his eyes hidden behind the combat goggles. The 5 lines spread out as the fan on his shoulder indicated he held the rank of the Major and the name tag on his chest pieces spelled out Maj. Crawford.

“Well it clearly looks like Datrius wants his fate changed. What if it's a good thing?” Akilah pressed on again, not exactly paying a lot of attention to the words of Tadison. ‘There is no arguing with her, well let’s redirect her zeal then’ Tadison thought to himself before speaking.

“We need to know more before we go meddling into other cultures.” Tadison put his hands forward as if holding some manner of imaginary ball offering it to Akilah. She stopped for a moment staring at the imaginary ball in the hands of Tadison. When a black armored soldier approached them.

“Doctor Fahir piece of advice. The protection detail is there to ensure your safety. I understand the whole adventure impulse but when bullets begin flying you rather have it than not. Keep it in mind next time you decide to ditch it.” Crawford spoke in standard military fashion as if the words he spoke were rehearsed a thousand times.
“The protection detail…” Akilah was about to snap at this military grunt when she was abruptly interrupted by Crawford raising his hand.
