“Before you say anything have you considered what kind of position you put Lieutenant Martinez in? He was already under a lot of scrutiny for losing half of his platoon trying to protect you and now you managed to ditch him. An admirable achievement but also really puts him in a bad light. So here’s the question: is your fantasy worth living out when you throw people under a bus for it?” Crawford combat goggles reflected back the image of Akilah as he spoke calmly, clearly and without judgment yet there was unnatural coldness in his voice.
“I…I… I have not thought of it in that way, Major Crawford.” Akilah looked away from the calm face of Crawford feeling guilty for her stunt.
“I know that your answer is yes, X-Ray.” Tadison stepped forward in the direction of Crawford to what Crawford just smiled.

“By the way, Tadison. I couldn’t help but to overhear your conversation and well we did listen in on that astrology session you had. I’m not sure but you yourself taught me that whoever takes away agency from people does so to control them and more often than not uses the divinity argument to do so. After all, CID does use this tactic when utilizing cults to subvert local populace to advance Federal agenda. Taking that into account those so-called gods of theirs may lie to locals about not controlling their fate to suit their own agenda.” Crawford said as he tilted his head slightly with a small smile on his face.
“Lamar, is that true?” Akilah asked Tadison in shock, she heard of a lot of questionable things CID did in the past but this one just really struck a nerve with her.
“It is” Tadison began speaking, still staring at Crawford and then turned to Akilah ready to confess “Not that I ever did it but it is part of the CID textbook.”
“Major, why are you telling me that?” After a moment of thought she looked back at Crawford as all of sudden the reality of the situation that they actually were in began sinking in.

“Information sharing and transparency doctor Fahir. I need to make sure we are all on the same page in advancement of our mission. I believe even the civilian personnel are familiar with mission objectives. And I believe agent Tadison may have his moral compass interfering with it.” Crawford said calmly, looking at Tadison.
“Really nice Raymond, putting my judgment into question. Maybe my moral compass is what is needed to establish trust with our allies.” Tadison rapidly parried Crawfords remark.

“I do not question your commitment to the cause but it is disingenuous to our own people if they are not presented with all the facts that we know because we want to spare them the ugly things Federation is ready to do to advance its agenda. We all must be on the same page here. Don’t you agree with that, Lamar?” Crawford lowered his goggles revealing his gray eyes.
“I know what you are doing here, Major.” Tadison spoke through the teeth.
“Does not mean I’m wrong Lamar.” Crawford pulled the goggles back on not paying much attention to Lamar’s anger.
“And to what end, Major?” Tadison finally decided to know what Crawford was going for.

“Let’s see how much we can mess with this so-called fate with Datrius being our test subject. Seems harmless enough a basic matchmaking after all, he will even thank us afterwards. However, I do agree with you Lamar, on the fact that we need to gather more information on those so called gods as well. We need to understand what role they play in the local populace other than religious significance. After all, we are trying to displease them by interfering with Datrius’s fate. Just keep me informed about a little project of yours” Crawford said as he opened the hatch on the side of the dropship the 3 was talking to and around this time another dropship was coming around.
“And where are you going?” Tadison asked Crawford who was clearly done talking to them as the rest of his soldiers realized it was time for them to go as well.
“Classified,” Crawford answered with a smirk.
“After all this talk about transparency.” Tadison said as Crawford began climbing into the dropship to what he just chuckled.

“There are NoirElDem hunting parties out there and I plan to send them a message and force their hand to act in a particular way so I’ll be gone for a long time. Also try to keep Lieutenant Martinez in the loop he is smarter than he lets on and smarter than he thinks he is.” Crawford said as he looked at Akilah right before disappearing in the hull of the dropship followed by his men.
Another dropship landed the moment the black dropship of Deltas left the balcony. Several rangers along with the men in beige jackets came out.
“Doctor Fahir, seriously what were you thinking? We've been worried sick looking for you?” Lieutenant Dray Martinez said clearly holding back his anger.

“Akilah thank goodness you are alright. I wouldn’t know what to do if something were to happen to you.” Doctor Maximilian Hunter said as he stepped from the back of the lieutenant. He seemed to actually mean it regarding his cluelessness in the event of the worst.
“Lieutenant Martinez I presume.” Tadison extended his arm in a greeting to Lieutenant.
“CID?” Martinez said as he grabbed Tadison’s arm.
“GNN.” Tadison answered calmly, hand locked in the handshake.
“John Smith or John Doe?” Martinez said with a coy smile.
“Smith” Tadison smiled back.
“Wait a minute I thought that there was no media coverage for the Task Force.” Hunter chipped in confusion.
“Max, sometimes you can be dense as a brick.” Akilah's face palmed herself with a chuckle.
“Pardon me?” Max looked at Akilah not sure what was that he missed.
“He is CID, Max.” Akilah said, looking at Max like a naïve child.
“Oh…” Max released a long sound as the puzzle started falling into place in his head.
“So are we going back to base?” Dray pointed back at the dropship.
“Actually, no. Right now we want to figure out the whole functions of gods in their society.” Tadison explained as he pulled out a folded data pad out of his inner pocket.
“So where to?” Martinez extended his hand, stopping Tadison from trying to show him his theories.
“I heard that there is an initiation ceremony happening at the temple of DemTra.” Akilah showed the map of the city on her PDA for everyone to see with DemTra temple district being highlighted.
“And that would be god of what?” Martinez said out of curiosity.
“A Goddess Lieutenant, a goddess of harvest and life.” Akilah said as she put the PDA back into the pocket of her cargo pants.
“I thought it’s Artem.” Martinez said in confusion.
“That would be goddess of the wilds and the hunt. At least that’s what I read.” Doctor Hunter said still not sure what the difference between all those gods was.
“Great well hop in and please don’t try to ditch us this time doctor Fahir.” Martinez once again pointed to the open hatch of the dropship.

The DS20A StarDiver dropship was not exactly spacious. A one of the smaller variants employed by the Federal Army rangers it was capable of transporting ten men squadrons from ground to lower orbit and back with its two plasma engines. The hatches could slide back and forth while the interior lacked any seats so everyone had to find their place on the floor of the dropship all but one agent Tadison. Tadison decided to stand rather than to dirty his business suit.

The dropship instead of flying in between the spires took to the skies just above the highest spire and just flew over to the DemTra temple complex. It circled around it revealing to the occupants something similar to a giant ivory tree with circular overgrown temples suspended in the air like branches of trees. Each such temple was different from one another as each had a specific type of biome presented in it. Some had trees, others flower fields and some even had a completely alien sight of giant mushrooms. There was one biggest temple right on top with a giant fountain in the middle of it and sectioned into multiple sections clearly presenting each biome present upon that complex.

The dropship made several passes around it looking for a place to land. As it began closing in it began getting rather obvious there was a large gathering around the outer circle of the temple. The locals were clearly preparing for something. When they noticed the metal bird approach with the roar of its plasma jet engines. They saw it many times and generally got accustomed to it but never saw it up close since the Human military base was off limits. As such when it landed it attracted a lot of onlookers which stared at it with cautious curiosity. It looked like some manner of machine yet unlike the flying chariots produced so much sound and wind almost like a mighty dragon.

The hatches slit open and 6 rangers led by Martinez jumped out rapidly establishing the perimeter with military precisions surprising the locals even more. This was clearly a defensive formation those strange humans established as if ready to be attacked.
“Oh for god sake is that necessary?” Akilah said as she jumped out of the dropship.

“Just part of the protocol as far as we are concerned we are in hostile and unknown territory.” Dray responded gun in hand staring at the crowd of cautious onlookers, eyes focused on any movements of their hands instinctively looking for a gun or in this case some manner of magic.

“We are among allies, in their city not in the middle of some dangerous enchanted forest Lieutenant.” Akilah said, putting herself in front of Dray as if protecting the crowd from him. Dray clearly tried to look past her and was clearly getting rather frustrated. That’s when Tadison grabbed Akilah by the hand and pulled her away from Martinez.
“Akilah let them do their job, they are trained to operate among the civilian population.” Tadison said, looking directly into the eyes of Akilah.
“Ok, still it puts a serious barrier between us and the locals.” She looked away from Tadison heeding his advice.

“Honored Dray Martinez are you here to witness the initiation of the acolytes of DemTra?“ Green tinted ElDemian women said as she approached her hands raised. Rangers seemed to recognize her and let her approach.

“Ratmisia, nice to meet you here.” Dray said as his PDA rapidly picked up the speech among hundreds of other voices and translated it for Martinez. Ratmisia once again was amazed at the level of sophistication those human trinkets possessed as she approached staring at the front pocket of Dray where PDA was located.
“Scholar Fahir, Scholar Hunter and who would that be?” Ratmisia noticed an odd looking individual in somewhat restrictive clothing but which did give him strange aura of regality.

“Archivist John Smith,” Tadison said, extending his arm in a greeting. Ratmisia was pleasantly surprised to hear this human speak Eldemian without need to rely on their trinkets as she grabbed his arm in a handshake.
“Ratmisia the huntress of Artem. So that’s what your archivists look like.” She said as she sized up Tadison.
“You really need to stop going out in a suit, Mr. Smith.” Dray said with a chuckle.
“Yeah you're probably right.” Tadison quickly switched his speech back to English addressing Martinez.
“So Archivist Smith I feel you are here to bear witness the initiation and your scholars are here to help you make sense of it all.” Ratmisia finally let Tadisons hand go.
“That would be correct, Ratmissia.” Tadison said with a smile but however something flashed at the back of his head as Akilah gave him a worried look.
“Please I’d be honored to be your guide” Ratmisia spoke with smile as she pointed in the direction of the overgrown columns.

The team has passed the overgrown ivory columns of the outer circle. They found themselves standing at the edges of the inner garden among hundreds of others on lookers. At the middle of the garden stood several priests of DemTra. They all were dressed in white golden laced togas holding some manner of giant fruit in their hands. Then there were acolytes each clearly of MaxElDem species. Those acolytes had togas with emerald lacings and by the looks of it were in their teens at least they appeared to be teens to humans.
“Those new acolytes are about to become initiates, they were fated to be in service of DemTra one of our most important goddess.” Ratmisia spoke as she looked at the acolytes
“Why are only MaxElDems among the acolytes?” Akilah asked in confusion as her PDA vocalized it in ElDemian.

“Because ElDems are people who were made by the ancients to be the custodians of this material realm and Gods chose them as their conduits. My people SylvElDems undergo a similar process but we have rather different rituals for Goddess Artem shaped our tribe to be especially attuned to her directions.” Ratmisia answered
“They are the religious elite,” Tadison said calmly, beginning to piece together all the functionality of their society.
“Wait, what about MaxElDem warriors?” Dray said as he looked at Tadison
“Is it not obvious? They are the warrior priests.” Tadison looked at Dray with a condescending look.
“Hmm like the knightly orders of the Church of Light?” Dray looked back at the acolytes.
“Sort of but minus crusading zeal. This is more on pagan side rather than the aggressive monotheistic one” Tadison explained, returning his gaze back to the show.

Acolytes faced the onlookers and bowed before them and then turned back to the priests. Priests cut open the fruit that appeared to be something akin to the giant version of citrus of the blue colour.

“Now observe TerraDems, this is extremely important. They bow before us for they are responsible for ensuring bountiful crops for us. They must understand that the great knowledge and power with which they will be imbued is in service of the greater whole. This fruit that priests are holding shall put those acolytes in connection with DemTra so that she will imbue them with their knowledge.” Ratmisia continued to explain ignoring the back and forth humans had in their language.
“So gods provide knowledge?” Hunter asked, staring at the fountain in ElDemian.
“That would be the correct scholar Hunter. But sometimes gods set their initiates on a certain path” Ratmisia looked at Max marveling at how fast he was able to learn ElDemian.

Priests began giving out pieces of the fruit to the acolytes. Then Acolytes went into the fountain pool and each found a giant lily pad to sit upon. Acolytes sat up in the lotus positions on the lilly pads and put pieces of the fruit in their laps.

“Now they shall eat and will communicate with DemTra as she shall teach them everything that needs to be known to them. Each shall acquire a particular knowledge fated for them by DemTra for god's see fate better even than oracles for they are creations of Ether Realm.” Ratmisia was clearly excited for that part.
Acolytes ate the fruit pieces and entered some manner of trans as their eyes began glowing in green as the waters around them filled in with lights and the green glowing mist enveloped them.
“Wait a second, Ether Realm.” Hunter said as he was observing the light show.
“What about it?” Akilah looked at Hunter with surprise.
“Hyperspace, they call it Ether Realm.” Hunter said as if a light bulb went off in his head.
“And?” Akilah still was not quite sure what it was all about.

“We’ve speculated as to the origins of those entities that teach magic to the Church of Light. But this confirms it. Those so-called gods are creatures of HyperSpace.” Hunter said, looking at Ratmisia. That’s when Dray’s interest picked up especially given the fact he had to fight against the Church of Light in so-called Border Wars.
“How the hell do you kill a creature in HyperSpace?” Dray was dead serious about that question

“I do not know, we still do not fully understand the nature of HyperSpace. When Marco Sterling discovered it at the end of the 22nd century he barely scratched the surface of it. The mathematics of hyperspace is well: it operates with mathematical formulas as if they were numbers in an equation. Essentially it also alters the nature of the world allowing for such things like artificial gravity, FTL, modern energy shields and inertial dampeners. But those alterations seem unstable and the natural space returns to normal state unless you dump energy into affecting the hyperspace projection of normal space.” Hunter began explaining as Dray dozed off somewhere in the middle.
“So how do we kill it?” Dray asked the question once again, impatiently.
“I don’t know,” Hunter shrugged.
“Was it that hard to say?” Dray just shook his head somewhat disappointed in the answer .

This continued for a minute or two and then acolytes rose up from their lilly pads, their former naive eyes now were filled with some manner of wisdom. As they came back from the fountain they were approached by a priest that held seeds in their hands and gave them out to acolytes.

“Now that they have acquired the knowledge they will do their first practice of growing plants. You see one thing is to have knowledge given to you but one must hone one's skill to bring this knowledge to life. From now on they shall practice those spells until they will reach perfection and when they do so they can call themselves Priest of DemTra.” Ratmisia proceeded to explain the next actions taken by the acolytes.

“So it’s like they just had the instructions downloaded into their brains but actually doing it still requires practice.” Hunter said as he began piecing together the process and suddenly some idea visited his head as he turned away from the show.
“Academic knowledge turns into practical knowledge. Hmmm neat.” Tadison said with a smile on his face.
“So the same thing goes for the warriors?” Dray looked at Tadison, his mind going through how that would apply to the warrior priests.
“I guess so.” Tadison was not quite sure how that worked for warrior either,

Each went out to the respective section of the garden to a particular patch of ground that was cleared of any growth. They dug upon the ground and placed seed as all of sudden water began flowing around the patch of the ground. They pressed their hands around the lump of ground and began chanting.

“The waters flow, the water awakens the strands of life. The Strands of life fuse and multiply to turn the dead dirt into more life.” Acolytes chanted as their hands were covered in some manner of the mist. That’s when Hunter pulled out his PDA and opened his encyclopedia application.

“May the Ether guide you with thy light and give you strength to grow” They chanted as the ground began to glow. Ignoring the chant Hunter opened the section on botany on the growth cycle of the seed of the apple tree.

“One nature for roots the other for stems” They chanted as the first green stem pierced through the ground but on top of that it was glowing from inside out. As everyone just stared at the show Hunter was rapidly scrolling through all the details of the growth cycle. Which enzymes were responsible for what and how they responded to the stimuli.

“Life makes life and sacrifices itself to make even more life. The few shall grow and burst to let the new ones settle as I guide thy nature as DemTra guides mine to help you grow” The stem continued to grow right before the eyes of onlookers as the hands of acolytes began doing some strange motions some acolytes were even making some strange dances all depending on the section to which they attached to. Then finally the plants were fully grown, some were somewhat misshapen while others were perfectly grown and some bared fruits.

“Now we get to test out their deeds to see how well they performed” Ratmisia said with a smile as she walked towards one of the acolytes ready to try the fruits from the tree this particular acolyte has grown. She looked at the group and invited the group to join in. Everyone from the crowd of onlookers began flooding the gardens. Hunter pulled out the seed of the apple tree out of his pocket, his gaze still sunken in the PDA.
As everyone was trying out the exotic fruit Hunter approached the patch of land.
“May I plant a seed from my home land here.” Doctor Hunter said as he spoke his ever curious brown eyes focused on the patch of land.

“Of Course TerraDem as a matter of fact I encourage you to do so. I have to warn though I will not be able to help it grow for I do not know its nature. But by planting it here and letting it grow, DemTra shall know of it and so shall I.”
“Let me try to grow it alright.”
“You can do so? I guess you can try. I thought you TerraDems a deaf to magic on your account lacking the soul and all.”

“Do we lack souls? Now that’s interesting but I’ll deal with it later. Right now I want to try something.” Hunter said as he planted the seed into the ground and then pulled out the flask out of his pocket with blue glowing water and drank it.
He focused his hands on the seedling and began mumbling.
“Reinstate Protein synthesis
“Respirate
“second phase mobilizes reserves

Mitochondrias kick back in. Kicks off the vegetative development of the plant. The embryo, which is already in the seed, starts to grow. The cells stretch from certain hormones, the cells start to divide and differentiate
light detectors Phytochrome, Cryptochrome and Phototropin.
Phytochrome It gets activated by red light and deactivated by dark red light (650-680 nm) and (710-740 nm)
getting to the main growth hormones

first auxins. They are stimulating growth in length, if used in the right concentration. The make plants bend towards light for example, by making cells grow longer on the sides where they detect darkness
But too much of it is poisonous for the plant
It also encourages cell division, but only in combination with other hormones which I will be getting to Cytokines encourage growth of side rungs
ratio of about 4 Auxin to 1 Cytokinin to get a shoot and about 10 auxin to 1 cytokinin to get roots”

As he was mumbling all this the blue mist was escaping his hands and the trunk of apple tree began growing with lights in it shifting specific ways it seemed far more specific compared to what other acolytes had. By the end of it all there was a perfectly grown apple tree standing in this garden section with giant red apples hanging from its branches.
Everyone stood in absolute awe of what they had witnessed.
“How?” The acolyte asked Hunter in absolute disbelief.

“We had to learn it all without DemTra teaching it to us and I process information like crazy.” Hunter showed his PDA that was fully explaining the growth of the apple tree. Tadison looked in awe at Ratmisia then at Akilah and whispered ‘Lets do it.’ To what Akilah nodded.
“Hey Ratmisia so you and Datrius?” Akilah completely startled Ratmisia.
FREE REIGN

General Jake Abramson stood before the holographic table in the big glass room with an army of military clerks doing all manner of work outside of that room. Intelligence officers were sorting out the gargantuan amount of information that has been flooding from Federal military assets that were already deployed for initial reconnaissance missions, local allies whose information came in such vague ways that it seemed they were just collecting legends and myth over tangible information, CID agents gathering information across allied city and as usual their information came with caveat of uncertainty and numbers analysis. Logistics officers were accounting, directing, processing and just generally bureaucratizing every god damn round and every bloody chewing gum commissioned. Then there were the actual field commanders moving around, those men and women for whom all this machinery was set up for. Those are the people he was waiting for. It was the time to lay out the general strategy for how they were going to accomplish the mission they were tasked here to do.

He activated the holographic table and he still had a couple of minutes to go over everything he had at his disposal. The table popped up the image with green letters as the transparent glass walls around the room turned foggy and no longer transparent.
Operation: Noble Endeavor.

* Develop ways to counteract Xeno technologies and capabilities
* Contain any threat to human territories

Jake after seeing this for so many times practically ignored it and began navigating through the application pulling out details on the Task Force Noble. The holographic table followed the actions of Jake without dropping a beat revealing his actions in the images projected above it.

Jake stared at all those names. Some unease washed over him and he collapsed those leaves back to the original list and tapped on his name and immediately he saw information on himself.
Brigadier General Jake Abramson: ------------ Preparing transition from standard command protocols to new overall Strategy for Task Force Noble.

“Reveling in your record, sir?” A woman in the black digital camo uniform said as she entered the room. The pyramid pierced by the knife on her left shoulder indicated she was part of the Delta Force and the bright metallic eagle made of sharp lines on her left shoulder indicated her rank as colonel. The writing on her chest spelled out Col. E. Snyder.
“A bit early as usual Eliza” Jake said, rapidly switching the image back to the original content depicting the military forces under his command.
“Part of being Delta’s I guess get the feel for the territory ahead of everyone else.” Eliza said, looking to the side as if self-reflecting on why she was first to arrive.
“So what about the others, Super 1.1” Jake said with a calm smile as he walked around the table to see Eliza better.

“I saw Colonel Forinez on the way here. He probably waits for the exact time to enter as usual, the rest will probably be here on time as expected, sir.” Eliza said rapidly yet clearly as she crossed her arms in front of her and spread her legs somewhat for a more stable stance. Her green eyes stared directly at General. Her brown ponytail just barely hovered behind her back that was straight as a rod of steel.

“Well they are all supposed to be the best that the Federal military has to offer. And we are going to need that if we even stand a chance to accomplish our mission.” General said as he turned to see the list hovering above the table in green neon light and which always faced him.

“Sir, Don’t spoil the surprise for me. Would not be fair on me, to rob me of the opportunity to genuinely show how little shit I give when presented with an impossible mission, Sir.” Eliza continued to speak in the same confident and unemotional manner without changing her posture. Jake was rather speechless for a moment to hear that especially not seeing Eliza change her facial expression.

“Haha, god I always hated that attitude about you Deltas, especially during my SOS days. Always pretending that nothing phases you.” Jake said with a smile as he tilted his head slightly and pointed a finger at Eliza who just continued to stand there.
“Not pretending, sir. Just acting natural.” Eliza smiled but for a moment but yet her speech pattern did not change.

“Bullshit, I played war games with you cake eaters, wannabe operators and you sweat as much as anyone else but boy do you make sweet sounds when you fall.” Jake said as if remembering the most ecstatic sound in his life as he closed his eyes and slowly moved a hand. When he opened his eyes he actually noticed Eliza tilt her head just slightly and smile moved but to one corner of her mouth.

“Sir, those were the old dogs with no new tricks, they got nothing on us these days and besides those old dogs were sweating from trying to go easy on you. With all due respect Sir, Your guys scored the third during the last war games even outdone by the ruskies. While the Deltas were ultimate winners, Sir.” At the beginning she spoke with the same usual manner but then she began slowing down and lowering her voice as she leaned forward towards the general and finished it with half whisper and smirk on her face.
“Ouch, below the belt Eliza.” Jake honestly felt that he did lose a good amount of money to Eliza on that bet after all.
“All is fair in love and war and I do love you, Sir.” Eliza leaned somewhat backwards and winked in general.
“Good thing my wife is not here to hear that, Colonel.” Jake said with a chuckle and he needed that to put him at ease.

“And it's time, Sir.” Eliza said seeing as Colonel Forinez entered the room followed by six more colonels for each of the army units directly under his command. A brief exchange of greetings followed with ranks and names being uttered and followed by handshakes. Soon everybody gathered around the table.

“Ladies and gentlemen, you are all familiar with mission and mission objectives so I will not bore you with that.” Jake said as he changed the image from Task Force Noble military assets switched to the star-map.

“Ok here’s the situation. We are not dealing with a standard game of jump from one planet to another. The situation is that due to phenomena of portals, space travel is not the only form of FTL transportation. That means we are dealing with two networks one based on the hyperspace tunnels and the other based on Portal networks. Some of the planets connected through the portals have no hyperspace tunnels leading to them and some do like Elveron, the planet we are on.” As Jake spoke the star map copied itself in two and one made of hype space lanes moved upwards and one of the portals downwards. The map was not complete though it had Elveron painted in green and two points that had Elveron on it were connected. There were several lines still in green connecting to Elveron on both maps but they had no connection to each other. There were a couple of lines painted in yellow connecting points to Elveron on both maps and one of those points did in fact have a connection to another yellow point. Most of the both maps were filled with yellow connections, some of which were connected to points on both maps indicating that Hyper-lanes did in fact lead to Portal worlds. But then there were gray connections on the maps especially towards the edges of it. There was one blue line in the top map leading out of it. That was the hyperplane that led back to human space and their lifeline.

“Where did these portals come from? We have no idea but the local story is that some precursor civilisation left them here and as far as we are aware we have no way to destroy them. That does provide a rather interesting condition to our mission. We can move from planet to planet without relying on FSNAVY but that also means we will be moving into places without orbital support. No GPS satellites, no orbital fire support, no evacuation via spaceships. That means how we will do our logistics will have to change.” Jake spoke as officers under his command each made a smaller version of what was shown and dragged it to themselves to examine it better.

“Now let’s talk about the information as you have already noticed. We have three conditions on the map for both hyper lane networks and the portal networks. The green is quite small as you can see barely a connection away from Elveron. Those are the initial connections we have managed to scout out with our own forces. The yellow is the information that was provided by our allies and we do consider it trustworthy, now onto the gray spots. Those what we have gathered from local rumors, stories and were compiled by CID agents and deemed of high probability to be there. The biggest problem is with the planetary portals they need to be found and mapped and well our maps are not complete even of that planet there are still locations that require more than just orbital mapping. Now to the most disturbing thing there are potentially portals out there we have no idea of.” Jake focused on the main image.

On one of the points that had yellow connections leading on the top map and connected to points on the lower map that only had gray connections. Some of the officers were already grabbing their heads in some distress as they were examining the complex nature of those maps having no idea how they would effectively operate.

“Now let’s talk about opposition. I’ll be straight with you. We have no clue of their size, our allies say one thing rumors say the other. Hell our allies give us conflicting reports as to where the capital world of those NoirElDems is.” Jake said as he looked out to see how officers would respond to this information. They were not happy.

“Now let’s talk about what we do know about our opponents. Our enemies use magic phenomena to bolster and power their technology as to how it works. That's up to our civilian experts to figure out.” That’s when maps disappeared and rotating images of different NoirElDem units appeared.

“Our enemy also utilizes minotaur creatures as their shock troops and living shields for their ranged infantry armed with weapons that can punch through standard issue composite armor.” The images of the minotaur are a beast about 2 and half meters high with a buvine horned head and hoofed legs. Also the image showed a hooded  NoirElDem archer armed with a magical crossbow.

“However, the worst part is that the centerpiece of their forces are their command units, mages. A single mage is equivalent to that of an artillery battery that is shielded and comes in the form of a single individual. Those are the forces that we know for sure.” The image focused on a figure of NoirElDem witch with long black hair going through different glows around her hands.

“Our allies claim that NoirElDem also utilize air units in the form of dragons ridded by said mages, that there is also another form of shock troops utilized by NoirElDems known as shadow dancers. Those can move rapidly across the battlefield and effectively evade fire but do specialize in melee combat.”  The image of another NoirElDem woman appeared; this one had red hair armed with two huge daggers and seemed to be enveloped in the black mist.

“There have been rumors that NoirElDems are also employing some manner of moving fortresses that amplify the power of mages making them even harder to kill. What concerns me is that there are even more things we do not know.” Those did not have stylized outlines, just gray dull placeholder images.

“In light of all this information I want to say this: Throw out all the textbooks you have read on how to wage war. The strategy that I’m laying today is that we are going full on decentralization of command structure. Though UrboSaptia will act as our base of operation, the nature of terrain for the lack of better word implies that we will not be able to establish instantaneous lines of communication and will suffer from even greater effects of fog of war. As such we will have to rely on our officers in the field to make right decisions.”

“War is chaos and this is about to get as chaotic as it gets and we must embrace it and weaponise it to our advantage. You will all act independently from one another and I advise you to take a more hands off approach in your command but do share information with one another as much as possible.” Jake spoke as the main image switched back to the map.
“Here’s the protocol of action that you will give out to your officers” Jake said as massive writing replaced everything on the holographic table.
“Highest military commanders of Federation present have total authority to act on the behalf of UEF to act as they see fit to achieve mission objective”
“Let the free reign begin”
Logistics

Colonel Denis Armstrong sat staring at the screen of his computer in the fogged glass room that was his office. He had a photo of him and his brother Jack Armstrong at the fishing trip. He just finished his stint in the Academy and was granted the rank of Lieutenant while his brother just started his shipping company. Many years have passed since then he became a Colonel while his brothers shipping company morphed into a ship building company and was experimenting with a military industrial complex ready to tackle the dominance of Composite Dynamics. The screen on his computer was clearly showing this on its application.
Taskforce: Noble.

Taskforce Noble formed as an expeditionary and exploratory force into the Xeno territories to respond to the plea for help from Xeno faction MaxElDem and their allies to combat Xeno Faction NoirElDem.
Task Force mission objectives:
* Evaluate potential threat from Xeno Faction NoirEldem
* Provide assistance to allied Xeno factions
* Map the territories
* Evaluate Xeno technologies and capabilities
* Develop ways to counteract Xeno technologies and capabilities
* Contain any threat to human territories
Taskforce Noble
Commanding officer: Brigadier General Jake Abramson
23rd Special Logistics Division
Commanding officer: Colonel Denis Armstrong.
Supply Brigade
CO: Major Harsh McNairy.
Fire Support Brigade:
CO: Major Sebastian Consualos
Engineering Brigade:
CO: Major August Steiner
Medical Brigade:
CO: Major Lupita Mabhena
Advanced Air Transportation Brigade:
CO: Major: Hiro Toyotoma.

“For god sake. What Jake was thinking when he thought of this so-called Free Reign strategy? How the hell am I supposed to build up the support base if I don’t know what they want? Does he even know what he wants?” Denis mumbled to himself still staring at the screen not sure where to even begin with. He proceeded with opening the strategic map.

The moment the map he applied the logistics filter to it and the map revealed that he had a supply base with projected availability coefficient of all units under his command set at 100, along with standing orders all of which were under standard command procedure. For the moment everything was on set for running the Noble base right next to the alien City of UrboSaptia that they built in a matter of days. It was an easy task to do after all it was all according to the drills they were doing and experience they already had. The next task however was uncharted waters. They were far from usual supply lines and had no plan of action other than to see the task and find the ways to contribute to it.
“Ok Jake said that all units must be going for decentralization in mind. Let’s humor him. For starters, objectives.” Denis said as he dragged the Taskforce Noble mission parameters on his map.

“Let’s start with the basics. What is my job in contributing to accomplishing the mission objective? First of all, my job is to ensure that our men are supplied and ready to fight at any given moment since we don’t know where the enemy is or their numbers. Ok how will our combat elements fight? Keeping decentralization in mind, they are the executive piece in our mission. It is they who chose, where to defend and where to advance. In this capacity they will make requests for the supplies they need and I must ensure that their requests are always met. The Fewer requests I get the better I do my job. Ok that’s a start.” Denis said as he was examining the Basic Supply Package. It consisted of a set of 6.5mm High powered ammunition for the M22 rifles prepackaged in magazines, basic med kit, set of batteries, hygiene set and food package. Then he hovered over the Basic Repair Supply Package. It had interchangeable parts for electronics, lubrication oil and machine parts that would most commonly break or get lost.

What made them so excellent was that they were adjustable; one could easily regulate the contents of those packages based on the mission parameters or even personal requests of each soldier. This logistics application allowed for this kind of adjustments and options based on the authority granted by the account.

“Let’s talk about obstacles to that task. For starters let’s see what we are dealing with. Space logistics is not my concern, it is up Naval logistics so no point with working with hyperlanes routes for now. My operations are planetary based but unlike human space we also have portals to other planets. That means my supply lines must work not within the context of the single planet but multiple ones. It also means I’m working with choke points.” He opened the map once again. It showed the two maps one of the known hyper lanes and the other of known portals. He quickly turned off the hyper lane map however maintained the outliner whether or not the planet in question had both routes leading to it.

“All right, let’s talk about solutions. Those choke points are perfect for establishing permanent supply depots and bases. From those bases since the front line can shift within seconds, we will rely on mobile supply depots. Each unit has its own supply operations. They will be setting up their own supply depots which will be temporary and it will be the job of mobile supply depots to supply them. The supply lines will evolve and change depending on how war effort goes' ' He activated the simulation as he picked one planet where they already have mapped out the area quite well.

He began with dropping a supply depot at the location where the portal was. Then the map introduced different elements set up at random based on information it already had. Like all different friendly units that were available to Task Force Noble and known enemy units and their estimated military capabilities. The simulation specifically utilized the stalemate scenario and adjusted its play out according to the actions of Denis.

The next thing Denis did was to pick the methods of supply. Primarily an all-terrain supply wagon and compromised a collection of ten such wagons. Then he switched out basic containers on them with Drone Cages. A combination of both aerial and land drones dedicated to ferry the supplies from place to place. He stared at how simulation was playing it out. The convoys would constantly move around avoiding spotted enemy movements and yet constantly trying to maintain optimal distance from each of the temporary supply points other units maintained as drones moved supplies to them. Once the supply ran out within the convoy they would head back to base. Denis looked at the permanent supply depot and he was not pleased with something.

“That leaves me with a dilemma: where do we put the permanent supply depots? From my perspective I shouldn’t really give a damn where they should be located on our side of the choke point or the other. Well I should be concerned with it because depending on where on the choke point the supply depot locates the nature of its set up changes.” He said as he stared at the standardized supply depot set up.

“Ok we have two points, there for two setups. Let’s call them offensive and defensive ones. The defensive one will be located on our side of the choke point. The Benefit of setting it up this way is that it is easy to defend since we only need to concentrate on a single point of entry, especially with autonomous defenses. The disadvantages of such a setup is that all supplies will have to go through a single point. This will make it less versatile in its ability to supply the war effort.” He opened the supply depot screen and set it up. The benefits of prefabricated parts meant that he could easily create any set up quite easily just like playing with lego blocks. He created a template and called it Defensive Base. At first he dropped an automated warehouse, an adjustable build but he decided to go for the build that could process 5 supply wagons at the time. Then he dropped four of those in square formation with a large central road with two points of entry and other intakes set up in such a way that it would not cause traffic jams. One of the ends of the main road led directly to the portal and did not divert while the other just spread out into 3 more roads leading in different directions.

Then he began dropping defensive structures like electric fences, automated point defenses, defense bunkers and smart mines. The point defenses consisted of anti-infantry miniguns, 30mm AA emplacements and Anti-Tank missile pods, all set up in such a way as to create a kill zone the moment someone would cross over from the portal. Any opposing force trying to attack the base from the direction of the portal would be getting themselves into a death trap.
However, simulation showed a critical flaw in such a setup. Any supply convoy going out risked getting ambushed on the other side with all the scouting options being on the other side of the portal.

“Offensive Supply Depot is a sitting duck and as such a lot harder to defend but allows for far greater versatility in setting up the supply lines since we can move in any direction. Hmm such a setup is also quite beneficial for centralized operations allowing for greater coordination of all forces present.” Denis chose a star set up for Offensive base. Supply depot now designed to process 4 wagons and made of the 5 warehouses with roads leading in every direction outwards. The defense setup had to be all around the base. Now all of its fire power had to be spread out all around the base but with addition of greater scouting capabilities convoys were able to avoid enemy units far easier and were ambushed far less.

“What if we go for the double set up? Hmm benefits of both however double the price. Well not double the price but still an expensive way to go about it. Will depend on the requirements of war effort in all cases and all will affect the operations of other units.” Denis was playing around with set ups. He put the defensive base on one side with warehouses and on the other side he created a star shaped fort with roads leading out.

The defenses were put to work as a death trap on a friendly side and to protect the junction. He had to use just 50% more total defense installations in total however such set up did provide the scouting capabilities and as such greater security for convoys. What surprised the most was that other units began putting what looked like permanent supply points around the offensive fort. He scrolled to other options and saw that each affected the way combat units behaved. More aggressive with offensive set up and more cautious and conservative with their supplies with the defensive set up.

“This is a joint problem since depending on where the supply depots are put it will also affect the way other units operate in the area. My priorities might run interference with operations of other units. Then it is the General's job to decide how it goes when the set up is in dispute. Well now I know what I’m doing.” He began compiling variations of those set ups each tailored to different situations and nature of supply runs and simulated outcomes of his solutions. Once done, he sent his findings and solutions to his subordinate officers and commanders of other units so that they could start formulating their tactics and strategies based on it.

“Ok let’s hope other units know what they are doing as well.” He said to himself as he opened the roster of Task Force Noble and saw an army of professional soldiers and officers each an elite in their own right. Then a thought crossed his mind ‘I’m not the only one sitting like that here compiling plans.’
“Yeah they know what they are doing, in this we must trust otherwise it won’t work. So this is what you call Free Reign Jake, damn you are madman, the whole thing is based on trust.”
Roads

Silvarius the SylvElDem druid stood at the side of the road that those humans made. They had to rip the trees out with their roots. It was done by those devastating and monstrous machines. He wanted to object to their efforts but how could he after they saved his grove from being desecrated by those NoirElDem witches.

They said that it was essential that they create a path for their supply wagons. The needs of war took priority over the sanctity of the forest he was chosen to protect. However, Silvarius told them that he could make sure that trees would step aside to make the path for their other machines that ferried those supplies. By the gods how huge those machines were and they were so many. Every day since they carved their path through the forest he could see those giant wagons moving back and forth dozens of times.

What were they carrying in those enclosed wagons? He did not know but if it was filled with food it was enough to feed an army of ever hungry dragons. As far as he knew, maybe their machines were as hungry as dragons and those humans brought thousands of them with them. Maybe it was indeed to feed them.

“Honored Guardian of the forest, those TerraDems have no regard for the sanctity of nature. I understand that we need their help to fight the NoirElDems but at what cost?” A young Huntress of Artem approached the druid accompanied by some manner of feline creature the size of the horse. The feline creature appeared to be rather nervous next to this so-called road made for themselves. It was quite clear that it was quite terrified of the new beasts that roamed this forest. The beasts that were twenty times the size of it, roared like a dragon, moved at speeds only matched by the birds and utterly unrelenting. It knew that those monsters favored this path and as such it constantly looked from side to side in panicked terror.

“They are young and short lived people lacking the appreciation to see things further than their nose. Nature however will teach them a lesson very soon.” Silvarius saw the distress the feline was in and came down to comfort it as he spoke.

“The rains are coming. They were so hasty with creating this road they have not paved it.” He said as he looked up as the skies turned dark gray. Then he pointed at the road that in fact was just mud compressed from all hundreds of wheels running through it.

“If they gave me time to make the path for them. The trees would have protected that road from water and kept it dry.” He began moving in the direction of one of the trees leading the huntress and her companion under the canopy of trees.

“Those machines of theirs are mighty and terrifying creatures indeed but they are no different than any chariot whose wheels shall drown in mud and find themselves as helpless animals trapped in a swamp.” He said in a condescending tone looking at the road more specifically at this particular part where tracks on the road left a deep trench as the first raindrops hit the dried mud.

“The water shall render this path utterly useless to them and they shall abandon it and we will repair the damage over time. Within a several years there will not be even a single trace of them being here. Their actions shall be as fleeting as their lives.” He said with a smile as he looked at the huntress who was overjoyed to hear it. A familiar sound of engines running reached their pointy ears.

“Honored Guardian look, TerraDems are coming.” Huntress said pointing in the direction of some manner of machine approaching them from one end of the road. It was not as big as those wagons and did indeed look like a chariot without the beast of burden pulling it.

The all-terrain vehicle had its wheels already dragging wet mud along with it, yet its engine seemed utterly unburdened by it. The jeep drove closer and rode off road to park right next to two surprised locals and an utterly terrified feline which rapidly climbed over to the tree.

“Ok, your name is Silvarius right?” A soldier stepped out of the passenger seat holding a black square trinket in his hand speaking a strange language; however that trinket produced the speech that was understood by the two. Meanwhile several more soldiers came out of the vehicle and began unpacking something from the vehicle's storage compartment.
“That would be correct TerraDem.” Silvarius stepped forward to meet this soldier rather curious what those TerraDems were planning to do.

“Ok I will need you to step aside from the road for your own safety. Our meteorological survey said that a proper downpour is coming and we’ve been waiting for it.” The soldiers said as the device he was holding barely was hearable as the rain now was overshadowing all the noise. The Huntress got curious as to what other humans were doing. They were collecting the mud and then placing it into other trinkets.
“Did you know?” Sivarius now was really surprised, those humans were waiting for the undoing of their deeds but why?

“Yeah I will not tire you with details but you will need to step aside from the road otherwise we cannot guarantee your safety given that what we are about to do is quite dangerous.” The human soldier explained trying to get Silvarius away from the quagmire that was the road. One of the other humans was trying to get a stubborn huntress away from the road the moment he noticed her observing them standing under raindrops of the rain.
“Fine, let’s see what you will do. Ratmisia please step aside.” Silvarius shouted out to the huntress who without hesitation obeyed the command of her elder.

“Thank you.” The sound from the human trinket came out as the human headed towards the other soldiers. They were doing something to the mud they collected as they touched their marvelous square trinkets. After a couple of strange rituals, they seemed to be in agreement about something.

They moved away from the quagmire and the human soldier noticed that Silvarius was rather curious what he was doing with this strange trinket. Human shook his head with a smile, then stood shoulder to shoulder with Silvarius showing him his trinket.

“Ok we need to know what type of mud you have here. So that we can have our cement cocktail to be just right.” Human explained as his trinket revealed to be akin to some manner of magical mirror showing some strange pictograms and writings.

“And the drones are coming in” Human said as two big mechanical birds passed right above them dropping some manner of spikes along the road with unnatural precision. Those spikes landed in such a way that they set the borders of the muddied road, perfectly.

“Ok now that water is saturated through the mud to optimal condition we kick in the sonic emitters. To make sure that the entire place will have uniformed consistency and level” Human soldier then touched the mirror on one of the pictograms and all of sudden strange hum reached Silvarius’s pointy ears. The quagmire began leveling out as it was vibrating and bubbling.

“And that’s when the cement cocktail came in” Human soldier touched another pictogram on his mirror and very soon another mechanical bird appeared, this one was a lot bigger and was carrying some manner of trunk behind it. It roared like a giant dragon but with no stop to it as it’s four wings had some manner of cylinders attached to it spewing out fire.

Some strange gray fog was coming out of the trunk. It was heavy but those strange spikes seemed to keep it contained within the confines of the road and what was even more surprising the quagmire seemed to suck that gray fog in. That’s when Silvarius could feel everything living in the quagmire begin to die as a manner of poison killed it and water being pulled out of them into the mud.

“Ok now that the cement is being formed we need to make sure it is not just going to be a single cement block all across. This way it will have unexpected cracks and we can’t have that” Humans soldier said as he switched to the different set pictograms.

Silvarius could not make out the writing but by the looks of it there were a bunch of hexes and different ways to how they were layered towards one another. Silvarius looked over to the road and saw as those hexas began forming and surfacing upon the now grayish vibrating mass.

“And time to finish the process” Soldier said as he touched another green lit pictogram on his mirror trinket and just like that the hum stopped and the gray mass dotted by the hexes froze solid. Water drops were bouncing off it and sliding off to the sides creating water streams.

“Ok according to our calculations that road will now be here in perfect condition for at least 10 years without any need of repair and probably will outlast the trees. It might sink a little but it is well within what we expected. If time allows we will also build drains on the sides” Human soldier explained staring at the screen of his PDA not noticing Silvarius approaching the road in complete shock testing it for solidness. It was as hard as rock of ages.
Defenses

Shasira, the chosen of Zes, runs through the canyon ahead of a large group of refugees fleeing from the hunting party of NoirElDems. They relied on her for protection, it was her destiny to guide them to safety, she was chosen by Zes to be the hero to lead her people to victory and she was not doing a very good job of it. Not a good job but the best she could put up. The dark thought closed down on her. She was told not even by the oracle but by a god, the greatest of Gods upon this task, Zes himself! And whatever this Fog of Dis hidden away from her was but feeble attempts of evil Goddess to hide her destiny from her.
She just ran past some manner of this strange metal tree. A human tree! She made it to the world of Elveron! But they are still in pursuit. Well this is where she will make her stand.

“Rejoice fools! You are saved! Just run, I’ll stop them here!” She shouted at the crowd as she stopped to face them. After a momentary stop the crowd of refugees of all those younger races rushed in all directions in this strange brand of madness. Between terror and hope.

But Shasira stood strong, straight daggers crackling with electricity in hand and a thunderstorm in her eyes. First were the minotaurs. They rushed through the woods like crazed bulls with two axes in hand.

Shasira felt the jolt of energy flashing all across her body and the world changed. It felt like being in the flow blueish energy of something so instantaneous and at the same time so inevitable. Her body covered in crackling lightning rushed through the legs of one of the minotaurs cutting the hooved feet with her daggers. The beast fell down as she jumped on it driving her straight blades into the flesh of the beast and stopping its heart with jolt of lighting.

With echoes of lightning thunder jolted her in between the minotaurs slaying dozens of beasts. None shall pass her righteous fury. But now mists of dark fog entered the fray. Their shapes within blinks of one another reveal the slender bodies of NoirElDem shadow dancers. Domineering hatred in their eyes, criss like daggers in their hands dashed in the mists of smoke in between the trees.

Shasira stopped for a second to see new opponents enter the fray. With shriek her body echoed as two electrical specters of hers moved in opposite directions yet still connected to her by crackling arcs of lighting. Shasira entered a cruel dance between her and shadow dancers. She moved past two trapping them in the electrical arcs between her copies. The arc of electricity not only friend the shadow dancers but dragged them out of the black mist they enveloped themselves in as well. they enveloped themselves in. However, in between Minotaurs and those ever present shadow dancers she was clearly straining herself out.

And now this self-assured NoirElDem witch finally came in, her body enveloped in a purple magic shield, her hands enveloped in some manner of red glow. The witch extended her arms in the direction of Shasira and beams of red light were unleashed. Now Shasira had to be faster in thought than her opponent as laser beams followed her. One pass between the arcs and shield absorbed it. This pass at this witch almost cost her her life but the tree just gave her enough time as it burst up in flames. Attrition was not an option.

So she took a deep breath and her two specters rushed towards the witch like a lightning carving it’s pass through clouds. They collapsed upon the shield and with arks of electricity exploded as she lunged toward the witch.

Shasiras slender body twisted in between two laser beams. She locked eyes with that witch, her eyes rushing over with electricity as the eyes of the witch glowed in blue light of Aether. The daggers like bolts of thunder drove into the neck of the witch heading for the heart. Shasira landed her knees after tumbling over the witch and daggers spread-out like wings.
She stood up and saw herself surrounded.

Meanwhile Lieutenant Miles Dot of the Engineering brigade sat in front of the two screens. There was a bag of chips and an energy drink lying next to the screen. One screen had some manner of cartoons put on stop while the other screen had an image of a blue dot jumping from one red dot to another. All the while they were already among three rows of green triangles positioned like chess. More than that there were green triangles with two lines crossing it were positioned as lines as if setting a border and those NoirElDem forces just entered the optimal strike range.

“Ok, where is the translation application?” Miles touched the screen, opening the communication tab and then choosing the language to be translated in. Then he selected the blue dot and activated the sound laser.

“Hey dumbass DUCK!” Shasira heard the strange mechanical voice coming from all directions. Was it the sign from Zes but she did listen to it and all hell broke loose. The metal trees and their canopies began moving and it was not a tree. The six barrels of the minguns spun up unleashing a buzzing storm of bullets as all of sudden the base of those turrets exploded unleashing shrapnel flying above Shasiras head. Then she heard thunderous thumps from behind her of dozens of autocannons.

The tree turned into splinters and rained all over Shasira as flesh and blood sprayed all over her. This was a massacre. No one was spared as bullets whizzed through air and shells exploding in the air. And then all fell silent.

Shasira rose up to see what happened. Her white attire was now covered in brown mud and red blood sprays. She looked around and saw the entire forest obliterated, trees ripped apart and bodies everywhere she could even no longer make a difference in between them.

The sense of dread washed over her and then she heard cheerful cries of the refugees. She was ready to finally be thanked as she turned around and saw this human approaching and refugees surrounding him with awe in their eyes.
‘I Hate them…’ the thought jolted at the back of her head.
Gunship
Just right above the canopy of the trees some manner of the blip on stick hovered. Something inside began turning to reveal a collection of different lenses like a collection of cyclopean eyes.

“That’s a lot of bad guys.” 1st Lieutenant Larry Hosh said as looked at the several NoirElDem pyramids moving through the valley in between the hills covered in trees. They were accompanied by the usual escorts of minotaurs and archers but there was something new as well. Something they yet to see those strange riders upon giant spiders. He sat in the cockpit of the DG24C Super Highlander gunship right behind his gunner 2nd Lieutenant Sergei Drozdov. Among multiple displays surrounding him there were the state of engines, fuel, orientation information, maps and of course the weapons display. 8 Shard anti-tank missiles, 64 Wasp multipurpose missiles and about 800 rounds of 30 mm tungsten core rounds for autocannon.
“Got plenty of ammo to deal with them.” Drozdov said calmly, already prioritizing targets for a pop up attack.

“Well, maintain radio silence and let the show begin then.” Hosh said kicking adding power to the throttle. The two plasma engines flared up and the blockish beast popped up from the trees. Shard missiles and Wasp Swarm Pod suspended on its wings and chin mounted autocannon already moving around to track the target. 3 Shard missiles were released for top down attack mode as gunship once again dived back down behind the tree line.
The missiles crossed across the skies, splitting it with three white lines as one of the NoirElDem witches that stood on top of one of the pyramids raised her head.

“TERADEMS ATACK!” She screamed out raising her hands in the air covering the pyramid in a magical shield. She was the only one fast enough to react as two missiles dropped down onto two other pyramids with supersonic speeds. She stared into the shield being enveloped in flames as the center of the flames were hit with a stream of molten metal. She felt the strain on the shield of her pyramid. This weapon was clearly made with the intention of destroying armored monsters but the shield held strong.

She looked around herself as the pyramids dropped down on the ground, their caps burning with Etheric fire and most of their occupants suffering from concussions if not outright dead. She opened a flask on her belt and drank it. Her eyes turned yellow as her sight became that of the eagle. She looked in the direction of where the lines came from searching for something and there it was moving slowly just above the trees. This strange mechanical cyclopean creature on the stick.

“There it is! Fire!” She screamed out with one hand pointing a finger in the direction of the mechanical beast while the other was she was harnessing a ball of fire. The Ether reserves from the pyramid empowered not only her but also scores of less powerful witches at lower level. The volley of long fire bolts was unleashed along with the huge fireball in the direction of that strange cyclops.

All of sudden Super Highlander jumped up from the canopies of the trees. Its duo of plasma engines flared up, changed its direction and carried it sideways as the volley firebolts and fireball obliterated everything barely missing the gunship. The chin mounted cannon flared up with a thump. The armor piercing rounds with its tungsten core collapsed back at the shield but it held.

“So this is what TerraDem dragons look like.” The head witch said marveling in the sight of the mechanical flying beast. It moved so differently than a giant lizard, it was a solid block with only its engines moving and yet its motions were so fluid. Without losing its sideways speed it turned on the spot in the direction of its movement and then it accelerated. Attacks of her subordinates could not keep up with it. It actually flew even faster than dragons but now its breath of metal daggers changed its targets cutting to pieces the pyramid escort. The spider riders rushed towards the forests to take cover in the trees as did the rest but it was a massacre but not as devastating as if the dragon would unleash its flames. Still though it was a massacre.
She took a deep breath, sapping even more into the reserves of Ether of the pyramid.
“RIDERS! Unleash your hunter spears and I shall guide them!” She shouted out her voice empowered so that everyone could hear her.

The spider riders were hidden in the forest, some already climbed up the trees. Upon hearing the command, they pulled out a long spear out of the quivers besides, then touched the head of the spiders. With the chant ’Your hunter’s nature shall be made two’ some manner of bridge was established between the spear in the head of the spider. Then they dipped the spears into the barrel of glowing water and energy was absorbed by the spears. They launched them up in the air with unnatural force and they rapidly changed their directions rushing towards the mechanical beast with glowing tips.

The gunship dropped back from the skies flying right above the canopy as its cyclopean eye tracked the projectiles in pursuit of it. It hugged the terrain; it just barely managed to hide itself behind the hill covered in trees. The volley of magically empowered spears collided with trees and the hill in blue explosions.
The cyclops once again popped up right above the canopy of trees but now traveling at full speed. It was tracking them.
“FIRE!” The witch screamed out. This time she will not make the same mistake. She moved her hand up as the volley of spears rose up in the air to drop upon the human dragon from on top.

That’s when gunship once again popped up from the canopy of the trees and it enveloped itself in fire. The Wasp Swarm Pods unleashed all 64 missiles in rapid succession along with 5 shard missiles and autocannon flared up. The full extent of DG24C Super Highlander gunship was unleashed in but a single moment.

The Wasp missiles the moment crossed path with energized spears spread out all across the forest killing everyone who launched the spears. The shard missiles once again split the skies in two with its white trails heading towards the pyramid.

The witch stared as those missiles accompanied by the 30mm rounds hitting the shield collapsed on to the pyramid one after another. One hit she felt it, the second she felt it even harder and another thought crossed her mind ‘I will not make it’. With a final scream of defiance, she raised her hands upwards and the fourth hit shattered the shield. For a moment she felt the heat and saw the last missile had the same cyclopean eye with halo of fire and crown of wings. Her life ended in fire.
The gunship hovered above ground as energized spears without any direction began falling all around it.
“Now for the clean-up duty” Drozdov said as his crosshairs tracked whatever was left of the pyramid's escort highlighted by the observation array on top of the gunship.
Airborne

The slick exoskeleton suits weight noticeably on the shoulders of Lieutenant Darwin Cross. The suit had advanced servos and a jetpack with it. A remarkably responsive suit as long as you have energy for the suit and fuel for the jetpack. But that’s what those guys are here for. A group of soldiers carrying around ammunition, fuels and battery packs all to support those few who jump into hell. Like birds of prey drop in on an isolated target and rip it to shreds or rain fiery death from.

“What’s up boss?” One of the sergeants under his command said. There were a dozen of them and each had a retinue of support personnel dedicated to them. They all stood under the specialized container unit in a truck. A part of the larger convoy of trucks all filled to the brim with fuel, spare parts and ammunition.

“Well for starters we will test out this new gun system XM10mk10. We are looking at the miniaturized railgun. Still though, I need to replace capacitors after a single shot and rails after 10 shots. So it is one use gun before the refuel. use it responsibly.” Right from under his arm a weapon appeared. It was something so novel they never saw it before. Two rails protruded from the box attached to the robotic arm of the exo-suit. It almost looked like an extended fusion torch.
“What about recoil?” One of his soldiers asked as the support personnel was integrating that weapon for him.

“Already ahead of you they spared no expense on installing a Recoil nano-dumper on the gun, should reduce the recoil up 60%” Darwin said calmly showing off some manner of glowing circle around the back part of the box.
“Cool.” The soldier said waving the gun around.
“So what’s the mission?” The sergeant said as he began loading up his standard weapon set. M22 assault rifle and KSmG57 machine pistol.

“Search and destroy but with a twist it’s against one hell of an asymmetrical opponent.” Darwin said as he put on his helmet. All previous encounters with NoirElDem forces were loaded up and revealed before his eyes.

“Search and destroy for defense?” The sergeant said as he was focusing on the layout of the mission. The intel said they were surrounded from all sides. However, a battalion of Rangers was also moving in. Say what you want about groundhogs, those guys knew how to take and hold ground.

“Yes.” Lieutenant Darwin said as adjusted information, the intel on enemy movements was not up to date and now fog of war was moving in. But fog of war works both ways. Enemy had no idea where they were coming from either.
“Alright, but if we all die I am coming to kick your ass.” Sergeant said loading up his assault rifle and activating suit aim assist.

“Then don’t die,” Darwin said, pointing a finger at his subordinate. Then the door opened as the truck revealed to be moving across canyons made by an old river. Their jump jets activated and they rushed out of the moving truck, upwards,0 towards the high ground.

Darwin looked down. His vision was augmented by virtual reality. A collection of thousands of factors of data. Infrared, drone footage, coordinated systems and prioritization they all were color coded and showed numbers. He saw his enemies like ants spread out all across the rugged and uneven terrain. Hundreds of targets like a buffet for choices.
He had a somewhat complete database on enemy forces even though there were some elements not known, few and far in between. But hey they are here for the unknown.
Like this specimen, those brutish creatures with a lot of survivability, those Minotaurs.

Or how about this fine specimen of a target, enemy ranged unit, a crossbowmen nicknamed Archers. Sneaky bastards but pack hell of punch with their one shot one kill cannons in hand. Or how about that opponent, a brutal melee combat expert, dashing around with their freakish powers of black mist dubbed Shadow Dancers.

Or how about that officer of a target, a mage, a law breaking mystery, surrounded by a shield bubble and with enough firepower to bring down a city block. But the most terrifying aspect was that they were versatile, you never know what you are going to run into when you face them. Well it also made it more fun.

He landed on the face of a cliff. Railgun activated, aiming down the head of one of those beasts as it stopped to check the dead body to sniff it. It came from above like thunder of god Zes. Out of nowhere and brought daylight the beast was decapitated and they had no idea what was coming their way. Archers, scores of them. They looked up but only to see a bird of prey raining bursts of metal shards down on them. Guided by machines his rifle was mowing down through targets. They never stood a chance. Did spend a clip on doing that though.
Time to switch targets. Those were the shadow dancers.

As expected, huge daggers in hands darting around the cliffs. As matter of fact the moment he took the high ground she rushed on to him. He didn’t have time to reload the rifle. So with a jerk of the left hand the rifle twisted around it and attached itself to the wrist. With a jerk of the right hand the wrist mounted machine pistol activated. He jumped off the cliff, his jetpack maneuvering him into optimal strike position. Spray and pray. The rain of 45 caliber bullets rained down at an astonishing rate of fire. It didn’t matter that it was impossible to predict when it would come out of this mist but one thing he did know it was within that 1 second window and all it took was just put them in between the fire rate of this gun, 1.2 seconds for a magazine of 30 and fire rate of 1500 rounds per minute. She could not outlast the gunfire as hail of bullets collapsed on her the moment she materialized out of the black mist.

The final target up the ladder is a hard one. He will need all the guns rearmed. Not an issue with SMG and the rifle but that railgun has to go back to the base. Suddenly off the corner of his eye he sees the flash of lightning. As he moved his head to see what was happening his teammate was down. The stat line just showed that this soldier was just gone.

Then he saw it. A witch on top of the chariot what can only be assumed made of light, hard light. The Lightning came out of her hands striking left and right, flash followed by the thunder. She was mowing through his men. So he began jumping from stone to a cliff, always finding the way to be just ahead of the enemy hand.

The flash of lighting passed right above Darwin, as he dived right behind a huge rock formation. He pulled out the assault right from his left wrist grabbing it by its buttstock. The mag was dropped in a matter of seconds as the other one loaded up by a move of his hand. While the autoloader took care of the SMG on his right wrist. He scrolled through to the map on his HUD. He saw that right behind that cliff is the resupply zone. The base that his enemy was hunting for. Just need to jump around that canyon to get there.

“Search and Destroy” Lieutenant's face broke into a smile. He activated his jump jet heading for the supply base. He turned to face his opponent as the jet pack carried him backwards. The assault rifle lit up in a burst of short buzzes.

The bullets crashed upon the witch around some manner of shield sending ripples all across it. The witch extended her arms forward sending one lightning bolt after another at him. It was hard to predict his movements as he kept firing and his wings maneuvered him around one cover after another as he was flying around this mountain. He just ran out of ammunition for his rifle so easily indicated by him shooting his smaller weapon at a cliff.
‘Wait a moment he was shooting at the cliff, why?’ The thought rushed over her.
All of sudden a giant boulder fell on to her. By pure instinct she slowed down and extended her arms to block herself from falling rocks. But the shield stood as rocks crushed over it.
‘The damned creature thinks it can run away. I just laid waste to dozens of them.’ She thought as she rose high up into the air to get a better look.

Lieutenant Cross was at the edge of his wits as he dodged every attack. He dived into the supply base heading straight for where the jumpsuits were reloaded. The fuel is running low and guns are emptied. Barely enough to stop the fall. Like popcorn releasing its shell he dropped his jumpsuit. As he landed on his legs he rushed towards the new jumpsuit. Pushing the support personnel out of the way he dived into a new suit.

He jumped up and saw the site of miniguns shooting upwards along with hundreds of rifles from Rangers, two of his soldiers already jumped upwards surrounding the witch, their railguns pointing at her. Like a magical orb with a person inside surrounded by every gun in range. He switched up to the new railgun and the heads up display lined up the shot. This was what broke the shield twice as it perforated her chest. The lifeless body dropped from 100 meters down.
“This is the future.” Darwin said as he looked at two rails of the railgun.
The Clandestine Operations

“She actually has a cauldron and I think she is mumbling something” Crawford was observing the NoirEldem witch standing over the cauldron with red glowing and bubbling mixture. Two other NoirElDems who clearly were lesser witches were assisting from a good distance away from a cauldron. One was maintaining the shield of the elder witch while the other was manipulating a massive spoon in the cauldron, methodically mixing the liquid inside.
“Hey, they are bringing two prisoners.” Farnsworth said he saw a minotaur dragged two of MaxElDem warriors in front of the cauldron and held them in place.

The witch which was operating the spoon made a jerky movement with her hands. The spoon spilled some of the contents of the cauldron onto one of the warriors. Within mere minutes his skin was covered in blisters filled with black puss. The other warrior tried to run away but to no avail as the minotaur held him in place. He began screaming curses so loudly that they could hear him all the way from the positions human soldiers were hidden. Though language was still somewhat alien to members of Delta team Super 6, it was quite clear he tried to get away from his comrade who was dying.

Couple of minutes later those blisters began to swell and popped. Black puss covered both MaxEldem warrior and minotaur, more than that blisters popped so hard to hit the shield around the elder witch. Then both minotaur and warrior also began experiencing the same symptoms. The minotaur’s grip began to loosen but the warrior was losing his strength as well.
“Gota give it to those cow heads, they really don’t give a damn whether they live or die.”
Lynch spoke on the coms.
