“This… This is way too quick for pathogens to have such drastic effects but it is undeniable we are dealing with biological weapons. Sir we need to get the contents of that cauldron.” Dupree announced as he was closely monitoring the situation.
“6.2 you think your drone can scoop some of it for us?” Crawford asked his second in command who was operating a SpyHawk drone.

“Get a highly contagious pathogen, sure why not? I’ll need a syringe though. Dupree you should have some to spare, I’ll send a drone your way, just put it inside the storage compartment, I’ll do the rest.” Farnsworth spoke calmly, taking extreme risks like that was just another day at the job for him.
“Send it over” Dupree spoke on the coms, continuing observing the gruesome sight.

As the team was setting up the drone witches continued their work. This was clearly just the beginning of their experiments. They would continue to do something to the cauldron not adding anything to it but still mixing the bubbling sludge however the glow would shift from red to purple to green. Finally, they would repeat the process of testing by bringing another two prisoners.

This time around the blisters would pop and release puss in vapor form and the secondary subjects would die even faster. The witches immediately erected a shield around the contaminated warriors and minotaur. Clearly they were pleased with their results though they now were even more careful with the cauldron. The elder witch had to periodically stop her work as the shield that was surrounding her was not allowing the air to pass through.

The work continued and the process was repeated again and again. They were now working on making sure the illness would not be so fast to kill the subjects. Once they were pleased with their time to kill they began adding some potions to the cauldron and began the process once again.

This time around they were testing activation. First batch was activated by a wave of hand in their direction, the second batch had to be activated by an orb of some sorts but still within the sight of an elder witch. Finally, they activated the last batch without the elder witch being present.

“Remote activation… I… I don’t even begin to imagine how that works” Dupree spoke with utter horror, this was a nightmare scenario, a perfect biological weapon created right in here in the open without need for any high tech facilities. That’s when witches spread out the disease among the unsuspecting prisoners whom they put to sleep prior to their experiments. While they were spreading the disease, a nimble drone moved in, descended to the cauldron and took a sample and quickly flew off. When witches came back they set a blaze the cauldron and all its contents and just left.

Dupree was modifying his armor suite for dealing with biohazard as drone was still hovering safe distance away from him to pass on the package while lynch was setting up the field lab for Dupree. Once all was ready Lynch double timed it to Farnsworth’s positions.
“So what are we dealing with Arnold?” Crawford asked his medical expert calmly, continuing to observe the situation in the camp abandoned by their guards.

“The pathogen is not a virus this much is true, but it is way too adaptive, antibiotics are of no use, hell even our antiseptic blood would not stand up to it, alcohol no use either. Extreme heat so far is the only way to kill it. Also by the looks of it is designed to die outside of the body within a couple of hours but can survive within this cauldron's fluid for god knows how long.” Dupree was using his field equipment to conduct all manner of tests on the sample.
That’s when prisoners began waking up, they noticed that they were left unattended and like any idiot were thinking they just got lucky.
This was the moment Crawford without a hesitation activated an autonomous missile wagon they carefully hidden in the area.

Missiles rose to the skies and with roaring noise moved in the direction of the prisoner's camp. Missiles Exploded above them unleashing napalm payload upon them. The flames and screams of terror rose to the skies.
“It was just a dragon, we could do nothing about it,” Crawford said calmly but very clearly to his soldiers.
The witch.
NoirElDem, who was covered in a rugged leather sleeveless coat, stood in the twilight of the evening forest. She reached for the small black crystal sphere in her bag slung over her shoulder.

“Velaras'' She whispered to the crystal ball as it barely touched her lips. It quickly filled in with gray mist with something beginning to glow inside of it with red color. Soon the glow started pushing the mist away and revealed something akin to an eye. At first, it stared at her with hatred as she stared back at it, not twitching muscle on her face. The eye rolled slightly as if showing disinterest with her and began darting from side to side as if looking for something. Then it looked up, for a moment it seemed as if it was surprised.

She looked up and saw as some manner of the bolt was dividing the crimson evening sky with the white line. Her eyes tracked the bolt without fear as it began closing in on her. It passed right over her like a momentary shadow in the blink of an eye. Then came the loud boom followed by constant roaring only to be interrupted by a loud explosion a long run away from her. She looked in the direction of the explosion and saw black plumes of smoke lit up by orange glows of fire. The eye in the ball, stared with glee as a cacophony of buzzes, explosions, and screams of terror reached her ears.

Then the eye in the ball flickered with an image of a strange black rectangle with strange red markings on it. Then just like that, it disappeared. She put the ball back into her bag and stared off into the plumes of black smoke rising from the canopy of the trees. She took a deep breath and rushed in the direction of the loud chaotic sounds.

She ran, jumping over the tree roots and fallen trunks as the red skies started turning gray. The chaotic sounds were dying out as she drew closer to their origins. She stopped for a moment as she realized that she had to climb over the hill. She looked up and saw the rays of light dancing in between the leaves of the trees on top of the hill. The only thing she could hear now was the crackling sounds of fire from beyond that hill.

She climbed over the hill and saw it. Several trees were burning in bright yellow flames while others were nothing more but char black ambers, the ground was covered in gray ashes and dead bodies. She carefully began her descent to this place. There must have been over a hundred dead here. Something has just destroyed 4 NoirElDem hunting parties, a feat that even a greater dragon would find hard to achieve.

She walked carefully, staring at the massacre as the ground beneath her felt strange. She looked down. She was standing at the center of some manner of explosion as marks of shrapnel revealed themselves. They were spreading out from her like a fan of knives in the direction of the dead bodies. One, in particular, was the body of a minotaur torn to shreds right in front of her. She looked to the sides and saw multiple of those explosions all spread out in what appeared to be a perfect circle around this sight of slaughter.
It was not simply an attack, it was a carefully planned massacre. She stepped over the line where the markings of shrapnel began.

Further, she moved through the ash-covered ground and more details began revealing themselves. Not all bodies were burned, some were filled with holes and red blood pouring out of them while others were torn apart but not by animal claws. It was something else, some of the pieces of those bodies were as if they were being blown up from inside while others were cut in pieces by whatever weapon that left those holes.

She looked forward and saw the epicenter. A scorched ground with 4 marks of not-so-burned ground. She slowly approached those marks watching every step she was making. Her breath was steady yet her eyes were wandering around from one dead body to another.

The first mark was more burned than others but clearly less burned than the ground around it. At the center of it there still was a sizzling, char-grilled body. It was another witch by the looks of it. It looked like some kind of burning sludge collapsed on her once her shield failed.

She moved to the next mark. There still was some vegetation left but it was covered in blood as this witch was cut to pieces by that strange weapon that left holes in the bodies of the dead. Whatever it was, it had to perforate her hundreds of times in a very short period. Almost like with the stream of stones traveling at incredible speeds.
‘Metal shards’ She said as she dropped to one knee and picked a crooked metal piece from the ground.
There were metal pieces all around that mark. Whatever attacked her rained metal on her shield until it collapsed and then it ripped her in half.

She moved to the next mark and just like with the previous one she found metal parts all around it as well but there was nobody. Yet she saw footprints and she began following them with her eyes. The metal pieces seemed to follow her as well. Then stopped following her as her footprints turned on the spot. It looked like they were positioned for a strike.

Her eyes darted from one footprint to another. They were pointing in a single direction. She approached this spot and looked in which direction her footprints pointed to. Her confident gaze stared at the opening in the woods at the edge of this horror sight. Something was giving off sparks over there lighting up some manner of destroyed mechanism. Her gaze returned to the footsteps as they jumped over the tree trunk.

She looked over the tree trunk and saw the dead body of another witch with one bleeding hole in her head. She looked around her and saw a cylinder next to her. It was nothing like she had seen before but did recognize the design. She decided to touch it and suddenly a small spark of electricity hit her. She jerked her finger away from it.

‘This is how they must have overwhelmed her shield for one precise strike.’ She thought to herself as her gaze turned back to the epicenter trying to see where the last witch was killed. Then she felt something. She felt the under light and it was coming from above her and all around her. She looked up and saw this strange bird hovering above her.
She raised her hands in the air.

“Humans, I know you are still here. Your devices give off radio and microwaves consider this information as a gesture of good faith. I need to talk to you.” She said loudly moving back to the epicenter of the explosion her hands still raised.

The moment she reached the epicenter she saw several red dots darting from the sides and closing in on her. They came from the edges of the trees with mechanical precision. Then she saw ashes move to the right of her. A strange creature covered in some strange suit of leaves and twigs covered in ashes rose like a demon from the ground holding a long metal stick pointing at her. Then she heard another noise, some manner of mechanical noise. She looked towards it and saw this human soldier carrying some metal contraption in his hands covered in some manner of a metal skeleton. His armor changed its color as he walked towards her to match its environment. The contraption in his hands was made of six metal sticks and a belt of some manner of knives connecting it to the backpack on his back.
She heard another noise as somebody just jumped off the burned tree. She decided not to pay attention to it as another figure stepped out of the burned tree trunk.

His boots disturbed ashes from the ground sending ambers up picked by the whirlwind of his steps. His armor turned from ash gray to black as he slowly walked towards her. The strange weapon in his right hand was raised high as if to demonstrate its terrifying power.

“So you speak our language. Care to tell us who you are?” He said and with a single motion dropped the weapon into his left hand to direct it at her. She tried to look into his face but all she saw was this black mask with white skull teeth covering his jaw and her reflection on the backdrop of a burning tree in the goggles.

“I would rather keep my name to myself, but I do know something about you.” She said calmly as she slowly extended her arm in front of the human soldier and the image of a square with a red marking on it appeared.
His head covered in the black helmet turned to the human in the strange suit of leaves and twigs as if exchanging glances between one another.

“6.3, 6.4 take a walk. 6.2 coms off” He said without hesitation as he removed his helmet and pulled down the balaclava revealing his cleaned shaved human face, with a strong jawline, spiky black hair, and uncaring gray eyes. The soldier in the ghillie suit removed his hood and pulled the earbud with a microphone out of his ear and approached closer.
“This symbol represents chaos in your culture does it not?” The witch spoke calmly, still having her hand extended in with the image of a black rectangle still floating above it.

“Anarchy” The soldier with gray eyes said calmly as he reached for one of the pockets on his belt and pulled out the black zippo lighter with a red symbol of anarchy on it. With signature cling, he opened it up and with the swift move of his thumb produced the gentle flame. Then with the swift movement of his hand, he closed it. His gray eyes continued to stare at the witch, like that of the killer judging whether his victim deserved to live.
“By the gods…” She said as her eyes widened with horror and her hand started reaching for her bag.
“I advise you to think very carefully about the next move you will do, NoirElDem” The human said as both he and his subordinate raised their weapons.

“I pose no harm to you humans. I just need to see what exactly you did to yourselves.” She said as she pulled a flask of the white liquid with black floating spots in them. They still kept their weapons trained on her but the lead human tilted his head in curiosity. She slowly opened the flask, her eyes still staring back into those cold, gray eyes of this human. The flask slowly approached her lips as the other human moved in behind the leading human, the barrel of his rifle aiming at her head without a flinch.

The bitter liquid poured into her mouth and for a moment she stopped wondering if she was willing to see what those humans really were. She closed her eyes and swallowed. She opened her eyes now covered in catatonic mist.
“X-Ray what is happening?” The man holding a rifle said calmly, red crosshairs of his rifle aiming squarely between two mist-covered eyes.
“We are about to find out Dan.” X-ray said calmly, still holding his assault rifle with both hands.

“X-ray… They call you X-ray. The light above the invisible light is the light that pierces through the matter. Because you can see through your enemies. Yet where did you earn that name?” The NoirElDem witch spoke barely trying to hold herself together from falling into some manner of seizure.

“Reveal those who stand before me.” She uttered in and gasped in horror. She was staring into the silhouette emanating the death of hundreds of living creatures covered in uncaring shades of gray. Those deaths meant nothing to him, just a problem to be solved. She looked deeper and saw a brutal contest for survival. Mighty warriors set loose upon predators. Slowly warriors turned into monsters with each kill. Then monsters turned upon another in a titanic battle of wills and wits. 5 remained. Each had been shaped by the ritual, each made new by the chaos of that contest, each driven by gods of their own. 1 was given the freedom that was promised while the 4 were given the chance to prove their worth. Then the 4 have denounced their gods for no more they shall have power over them. The 4 made a pact with the force that made them only answer to themselves.
“You 2 have forsaken all that you held sacred” She dropped her knees gasping for air.

“And there is nothing we won’t do to achieve what we want. Though I can’t speak for others as far as I can tell, we all want victory” X-ray dropped on one knee to look directly into those mist-covered eyes.
“What have you done?” She said, still panting in horror.

“Act of war, nothing more nothing less, I want your people to cower in fear every time they walk in this woods. I want you to keep sending your best after me, your bravest so that I diminish their numbers and see even the strongest doubt themselves. I want your people to know fear because fear makes you irrational and cripples you and as such makes it easy for us to win.” X-ray said calmly clearly not having any pleasure in what he was saying. She closed her eyes and took a deep breath.

“X-ray, you no servant of demons but to play with terror as you do is to invite them into our world. I cannot imagine what you want but I doubt the destruction of this world is what you want.” She said still standing on all fours trying to catch her breath.
“What do you mean?” Now Dan dropped to one knee to see this NoirElDem witch closer.
“Fear leads to despair and despair leads to desperate actions.” The mist in her eyes began fading away as she sat up straight.
“Good, this is the intention after all.” X-Ray said calmly, still looking at her with his cold gray and uncaring eyes.
“X-Ray it wouldn’t be your intention if you knew the full extent of consequences of your actions.” She said with exhaustion trying to stand up.
“Care to enlighten me then?” X-ray said as he helped her to stand up.

“All I know what you did here pleased something that wishes nothing but the destruction of this world” She uttered as she looked into the eyes of X-ray that somewhat widened with surprise. Then some manner of moans came from one of the dead bodies that were tied to a tree.

“Astera… Are you alive?” Barely audible noise came from the last witch they tied up to a tree. Astera closed her eyes as if feeling enormous pain in her chest and then she opened them with killing intent as she looked at that last survivor. X-ray extended his arm blocking Asteria from that last witch.

“6.2 we have our designated survivor. I think one that is already in their camp is more than enough to do the damage that is needed and I have no desire to cause more damage than needed.” X-ray said loud enough so that Dan could hear him as he looked directly into the eyes of Asteria
“Roger 6.1” Dan without hesitation turned to the tied-up witch and the thunderous clap of the rifle echoed across this ash-covered pit.

“Well Astera nice to meet you Major James Crawford 1st Special Operational Detachment Delta team super 6. Consider this as an act of good faith, your secret's safe with me and Captain Farnsworth here.” Major Crawford said as he put his helmet back on and just like they appeared they faded out into the night leaving Asteria in that horror site, red dots still aiming at her from the edges of the treeline.
Armored Lance

Ratmisia left light footprints upon the virgin snow as she moved through the forest covered in snow. It was not the place she was accustomed to. It was cold, with scarce life, even the trees around her were only here because of hot springs. But humans asked her here, to scout for them and after all that they have done for her people she was only glad to be of use for them. They gave her this amazingly warm coat and their communication trinkets.

She walked out to the edges of the forest and saw the endless expanses of jagged ice. Her gaze moved from one side of the horizon to the other looking for something. The frustrated fog came out of her mouth as she could not see anything and yet something was telling her something was amiss. She dropped on one knee pressing her hand against the burning cold ice and closed her eyes.

Subtle vibrations echoed through the ice, a familiar vibration. A vibration that indicated a NoirElDem floating pyramid. A terrifying construct in many ways responsible for victories of NoirElDems over the Ivory alliance. There was something else, like hundreds of footsteps yet they moved so strangely, not like the marching army. Yet there was one more familiar vibration. Someone she knew quite well. Footsteps of her old rival, a sister that turned away from the path of Artem laid out before her, she turned to Dis to hide her fate from the gods and oracles.
She opened her eyes and rose up staring into the frozen wastes.

“TerraDems, if you can hear me I found them, watch my signal.” She uttered as she pulled out the bow and imbued an arrow with ethereal energies. She released an arrow high up in the sky and then it exploded covering some manner of invisible bubble in strange glowing flakes. That’s when she felt it even through the boots those humans gave her. She could easily feel the rumble of treads of the Federal Titan tanks.

“Well would you look at that. That xeno scout indeed could see what our drones could not.” Sergeant Gregory McRoy said as he looked at the bird's eye view from the drone. He sat in the chair surrounded by the holographic displays giving him 360 degrees of vision all around the tank. His legs were controlling the direction in which treads were turning while both of his hands held two joysticks that controlled the main gun and coaxial machine gun. The whole illusion provided by dozens of cameras and sensors of the tank was focused on main cannon sights. If it turned so did the whole image along with it so that McRoy always was aware where the main weapon of the tank was looking. To the left bottom of the main gun crosshairs there were two images of weapons available to him, the amount and type of ammunition that was loaded up. The main gun was a 100mm railgun and right now it was loaded with armor piercing fin stabilized rod, with capacitors all charged up.
“Just get us into position McRoy. I’ll coordinate the others in the platoon” The voice from behind the screen to the right of the McRoy announced.

That was Lieutenant Rashid Cholgas, the commander of the tank and the platoon. Rashid had the same set of controls as did the McRoy but his, was inactive. His area of operation was on the holographic displays which showed somewhat images from that of the McRoy. Rashid’s screen was divided in two, one was overlooking the map the other was overlooking the secondary weapons and defense systems. On the map he could see that he was leading a group of 4 tanks, Lancer 5.1, 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4. As for secondary weapons and defense systems controls he had a minigun turret with coaxial grenade launcher, smoke throwers, active denial systems and even mortars. All placed on top of the main tank turret.

“Already on it Lt.” McRoy said, pressing both pedals and getting the 60-ton metal beast in motion. The displays on McRoy’s side showed the highlighted area where he needed to go, picked up by his commander as well as the enemy formation. Even though he still could not see it, he knew that the giant red square is where enemy formation was.

The low profile of Titan tanks moved in between the jagged ice glaciers the silhouette of minigun turrets like the fins of ice sharks circling in for the kill. Then one of the tanks stopped, its hull hidden behind an ice wall and its main turret with its angular armored cheeks flanking the railgun. With whip crack thunder the line formed between the center of the highlighted dome and the turret. And all of sudden the highlighted dome revealed that a 3 level pyramid floating above the ground in the center, it was shielded but there was something else. A giant and monstrous black centipede was circling around that pyramid.

The first hit put a massive strain on the pyramid's shield and its occupants were already returning fire to where the first attack came from. The volley of energized bolts along with fire bolt spells and giant ice lance obliterating the place where the tank once was. The tank was long gone already moving in for a different position predetermined by its commander.

Rashid was observing as his strike group moved into the firing positions and moved out of it once their railguns discharged their deadly payload to recharge the capacitors back. Something caught his attention. There was some strange NoirElDem archer who jumped on top of that centipede, it was odd because this archer not only had a bow and arrow instead of a crossbow but also women. ‘Must be some manner of elite outfit given the way NoirElDem society appears to be structured.’ Rashid thought to himself as the centipede with hundreds of legs rushed into cover away from the pyramid that was sending volley after volley into the tanks.

The moment one of the tanks once again picked its head out of the ice, the head witch on top of the pyramid anticipated it and already sent an ice lance at it. To her shock the ice shattered as its shards just bounced off the front angular plate of the main turret putting its secondary weapons system out of commission. She felt a powerful jolt in her mind as another hypersonic slug vaporized upon the contact with the pyramids shield sending shockwaves across it.

She mastered her will as her eyes focused. The mind concentrated on channeling the energies from the Ether reserves of the pyramid. She locked her hands together as a beam of red light came out of it. The beam vaporized the ice behind which those human armored chariots were hiding. The tank turned out to be sturdier than she anticipated.

Tanks treads started to move in opposite directions as the laser beam carved the burned marks upon its side armor. As armored plates melted the laser beam was about to hit’s treads and leave it immobilized but fortunately it was fast enough to put its front armor to face the laser beam. Though metal rapidly melted away but right beneath there was an advanced ceramic plating that stood up to the laser beam far more efficiently.

Suddenly the shield shattered and the hypersonic slug from behind the head left nothing more but limbs flying in all directions. The occupants of the pyramid rapidly realized they were at the mercy of devastating weaponry of those human armored chariots. The stream of bullets, anti-personal grenades and air bursting hyper velocity slugs cut right through the archers on lower levels while some of the witches on second level were fast enough to put up a shield around themselves.

That’s when the centipede came back rushing towards the mages, its carapace easily taking on the small arms fire. The mages without hesitation rushed over to it and with unearthly grace jumped on to it. As mages jumped on to the centipede they set up shields around each section of giant insect drawing upon Ether reserves within its body. Once again the hyper velocity slugs began slamming into the magical shields as the creatures rushed forward towards one of the tanks.

Hundreds of its legs carried it over towards the human machine. The creature moved in between the ice making sure humans had no clear shot against it. Quickly realizing what was happening, tanks began retreating however unlike that centipede they constantly had to find their way around the ice while the giant insect could easily climb over it.

Like a train the centipede rolled over the ice cliff and slammed into the armored vehicle almost knocking it over. The tank turned out to be quite an immovable fortress unwilling to tumble over. The riders on top of the Centipede barely managed to hold on as the forward momentum carried them forward.

“Say what you want about TerraDem machinery but those are some tough chariots.” The head rider said as she pulled the Centipede by its antennas and the creature with its hundreds of appendages crawled over the tank ripping out its minigun turret. Then it began circling around the tanks as mages showered the machine with ice bolt spells and laser beams. Within seconds the back of the tank exploded with fire.

“So this is how you kill them, you hit them from the sides.” The rider noticed that it was indeed the side attacks that managed to pierce through the thick armor of those chariots. That’s when a hypervelocity slug punched through the shield at the tail of the creature and ran through its carapace followed up by the stream of bullets cutting to pieces the mage on top of it. The creature shirked with pain and its tail turned limb.
Without wasting a single moment one of the mages quickly cut off the tail of the creature with a laser beam and the head rider rushed the Centipede towards another tank.

“All Lances retreat, I repeat, retreat!” Rashid commanded on the coms seeing as this horrifying mass of legs and magic was out maneuvering his tanks. Lance 5.4 was immobilized and non-functioning; the only solace was that the crew was still alive. He stared in horror as this creature just circled around Lance 5.3 and once again its engine was destroyed.
“Son of bitch I can’t get a clear shot on it!” McRoy said as he navigated the machine in between the ice cliffs putting his turret control on auto pilot and seeing unable to lock on target.

“TerraDems, hear me. I can draw it out! To let you have chance to kill it” Ratmisia shouted into the comms as she run up to one of the cliffs. She looked downed and witnessed that despite their best efforts human armored chariots were going up in flames one by one. Though this one armored chariot did manage to shoot of the half of this monstrosity.

“McRoy you heard the scout. We need to set ourselves up in such a way that we can hit it right into its front.” Rashid said just witnessing as Lance 5.2 was also disabled. He observed from his drone footage how the 3 mages jumped off the dead part of the Centipede and with hatred in his eyes isolated them for mortar strike. As the tank moved in between the Ice a mortar came out of its main turret and one by one began sending Air-burst fragmentation shells in the air.

“Too punch it right through its entire body?” McRoy said as he looked at the small map on his display to see where this alien scout was. He observed that it was a couple dozens of meters away from an ice crevice. His feet were constantly moving, actually even making somewhat tired.
“Yes” Rashid said without hesitation as he looked at shells exploding upon unsuspecting mages. They thought that they were safe now that only one tank was left; they were safe.

“Tamaria, I know you will not miss an opportunity to kill the last surviving member of our grove!” Ratmissia said as she saw that this creature was hunting that last human armored chariot. She pulled out an arrow. Energies started to imbue the arrow . With the whip crack noise of breaking a sound barrier she released it. The arrow split in three kills, easily punching through the weakened shields, killing the remaining 2 witches while Tamaria managed to dodge it.

“Ratmisia! So you want to die first so be it!” Tamaria said as she turned the Centipede in the direction of Ratmisia. Her eyes were focused on her observing each of her moves as they telegraphed her shots. She dodged and ducked from Ratmisia’s arrows as the beast relentlessly rushed towards her.

Ratmisia jumped off the cliff away from the monstrosity. The centipede climbed over the cliff and rushed downwards only to see Ratmisia jumping over this armored chariot main weapon ready. The line punched through the entire body of the creature as it slammed into the railgun while. The turret under the weight of the creature turned. The body of the creature under its own momentum flew into a crevice knocking Ratmisia alongside with it into the icy abyss.

Ratmisia gasped for air seeing nothing but blueish darkness above as pieces of ice fell on her. She quickly rose up and moved to cover only to feel the smooth carapace of a monster. To her horror it was still twitching. In panic she raised her hand and light came from it to show the creature was actually dead and the twitch was in fact just rolling over to its back and it’s many appendages just doing it’s post mortem twitching.
“Fool” Ratmisia heard as an arrow pinned her shoulder to the ice.
“Aghh, Tamaria.” Ratmisia smirked from pain as she reached for an arrow. But then a sudden realization came over her. She will not have chance to pull it out.

“Siding with the soulless creatures like TerraDems. Aren’t you people getting desperate to side with people without a fate.” Tamaria said as she pulled out another arrow out of her quiver and lit up to face her.
“Better than going against it, Tamaria!” Ratmisia shouted back, still clinging to the arrow in her shoulder but then she remembered something.

“You never came to oracles, Ratmisia blindly trusting Gods to guide your path. I for one saw the fate they had for me and I did not like it. More than that I saw that this is the end of their reign but you will not see it.” Tamaria gloated as she began pulling the glowing arrow to shoot Ratmisia.
“Lies!” Ratmisia shouted out as she pulled the pistol she was hiding underneath her clothing. The barrage of bullets and muzzle flashes lit up the ice around them.
“Well… Ain’t that funny to use a weapon of soulless creatures only to just be fallen to the trap of the Gods?” Tamaria said, coughing up blood as she collapsed onto the ground.
“What do you mean?” Ratmisia asked as she broken off the arrow and approached Tamaria who was still lit up by the fading light of her arrow.
“You stole my love from me but I find solace in knowing that you will die in this icy grave with me” Tamaria spoke as the light faded and she released her final breath.

“Well we will need the logistics division here to drag those tanks back for repairs. At least everyone is ok. By the way, where is the scout?” McRoy said as he climbed out of the tank seeing his commander inspecting the damage his machine sustained.
“She fell down into the crevice.” Rashid said as he looked at the crevice which now was covered in the ice debris.
“That’s a bad way to die.” McRoy said looking at the ice debris knowing full well that right below there was a person who died there all alone in the dark and cold.
“Hey look at that, she is alive.” Rashid spoke with a smile as he looked at his PDA and saw that she was indeed still alive and they knew where she was.
“Ok her name is Ratmisia” McRoy announced as he pulled out his PDA to see the file on the alien scout.

“Ratmisia this Lieutenant Rashid Cholgas, don’t you worry we are not leaving you behind just stay where you are, we will deal with everything.” Rashid quickly switched channels to communicate with Ratmisia.
Ratmisia looked up and saw as the ice above exploded and the rope came down to save her from her predicament. The thought flashed over in her mind. ‘My fate was to die here… Humans changed it.’
Strange magics

Ratmisia walked out of the human truck that brought her back to the human base camp. She was injured after a deadly encounter with her sister. That misguided woman, but those final words still echoed in her mind actually even numbing the pain. ‘You stole my love from me but I find solace in knowing that you will die in this icy grave with me’

How could she steal something from her without knowing it? She lived her life carefree, all too happy to see the path fated for her unfold as she lived it, rather than see it with the oracles. For the first time in her life she was so unsure what to do. Was it her fate to die there? Did humans change it? After all they are creatures without fate. Can they change the fate of others? What awaits her ahead? All those questions she never asked.
“Oh my god Ratmisia are you alright?” Doctor Akilah Fahir was the first to rush towards her once she saw her alien friend in the brace. There was a genuine concern in her eyes.

“Scholar Fahir by the blessings of actions of your chariot rider Rashid, I’m fine albeit your healers are a strange folk. It was truly a funny experience to see as they struggled between the desire to help and not being sure what to do.” Ratmisia said with a smile.
‘They might be without fate but they certainly are not soulless’ Ratmisia thought to herself.

“So what happened, the military won’t say anything? Those stingy bastards hide behind the whole military operations secrecy?” Fahir asked Ratmisia as she grabbed her by the shoulders staring into her emerald eyes.

“I met a ghost from the past and honestly I’m not sure what to do about it. But I think I need to talk to Datrius.” Ratmisia brushed her thick greenish hair with healthy arm as she spoke as if releasing some stress.

“Datrius is participating in the assault on DelfiaPolis, and I see you got some battle scars Ratmisia.” Lieutenant Dray Martinez approached them. It was rather surprising to see this human warrior not encased in his armor but in this strange T-shirt with some manner of another warrior drawn on it.

“DelfiaPolis, you already managed to get to the DelfiaPolis? I thought after losing all your armored chariots, you would slow your advance down Honored Dray Martinez.” Ratmisia said with the surprise and hints of disappointment.

“The operational tempo is quite hectic, not going to lie. But no it was just one armored column we had, we still have at least a dozen.” Martinez said calmly as he looked at one of the huge trucks filled with ammunition being cleared at the gates as another huge truck was coming in carrying the charred carcasses of Titan tanks.
“Lieutenant Martinez, how much danger is Datrius in?” That’s when Doctor Fahir looked at Dray with worry in her eyes.

“He is a warrior, kind of an occupational hazard. You’ve seen it yourself, we lost people at the temple Doctor Fahir. But he is a capable warrior with good head on his shoulders I doubt he would do anything stupid. Plus, from what I understand he and his warriors are mostly working in advisory capacity then direct action troops. So I’m sure he will be fine.” Dray was still not exactly sure why Fahir was so worried about those two aliens as he looked at her with his eyebrow raised.

“Honored Dray Martinez is too much of a trouble to request a transportation to Atrilian Forest? I will need some time to be healed and I need to have some council with the druid there.” Ratmisia said as she looked at Dray with begging eyes.

“Arilian Forest? Ok looks like we can kill two birds with one stone. Doctor Hunter also said that he would like to take the research team there to study local flora and fauna. Unfortunately, we will have to taxi ourselves there on the supply wagon as all aerial assets are busy and the logistics division is unwilling to spare any jeeps for us.” Dray said as he began scrolling through his PDA the translation software was still on.
“I thought the military had dedicated resources to us?” Fahir spoke with surprise, not exactly being joyful with the prospect of being cramped up in the supply wagon sniffing gunpowder.

“Initially yes but right now, you guys are lucky you still have a protection detail dedicated to you. We are getting spread out quite thin, I mean the base camp itself is on the skeletal crew and a couple of reserve units. There is already some talk about packing up some support camps and opting out for leapfrog strategy. But that’s just talk.” Martinez said as he began sending out commands to his sergeants.

“Yeah I have noticed that there are a lot less people present at the base camp.” Akilah said as she looked around. The base indeed had a lot less people around if in the past you couldn’t turn a corner without running into some uniformed knuckleheads marching around but now it almost seemed like a deserted village with a bunch of drones moving around.

“Ok I’ll go get Doctor Hunter, he always forgets to check on his PDA for messages. Ratmisia, Doctor Fahir I’ll be waiting you in two hours at the Supply Depot 5 if you are not there by this time we are leaving without you.” Dray said in military fashion clearly enjoying the moment and immediately marched in the direction of labs.
“He loves giving out ultimatums.” Ratmisia said looking at the Martinez disappearing behind one of the prefabricated buildings of the base camp.
“I do wonder if those ultimatums are not met, what will happen?” Akilah said cheerfully as she pointed in the direction of her quarters.
“Most likely give a long talk about how wrong you are and how it is his fault, he does remind me of my older brother.” Ratmisia looked down with a warm smile on her face.
“Really? You know what he does have younger siblings back on Earth. From what I understand he sends most of his salary back home to support the family.” Akilah said with excitement.

The giant truck 5 meters wide and almost 100 meters long rolled out of the hangar of the supply depot like some kind of monstrosity with 50 huge wheels. Ratmisia still could not quite come to terms with the size of that machine despite seeing it multiple times rolling out of the human base while Rangers were helping to load up equipment of the science team.

“Sir, with all due respect but my priority is to get those ammo supplies to the front. I’m already running at half capacity for you guys.” The driver of the truck spoke to Dray as he stopped one of his soldiers getting a big box out of the truck to make more room for scientific equipment.

“Sargent according to the manifesto you are carrying around 80 Shard missiles, 640 Wasp missiles and 5 tons of spare parts and 5 tons of ammunition. I get that gunships are hogs for missiles but spare parts you can get at the camp Valiant their gunships are on standby anyway. There you go if a question arises it is my head on the line not yours.” Dray said calmly as he was tapping on his PDA reorganizing the supply request to make sure that this logistics would still be able to fill in his quota. While Doctor Hunter stood right next to him, nervous not wishing to order his science team to keep some of the equipment on the base.

“Alright Lieutenant but just keep it quick I need to get to Rendezvous Point Hotel in 12 hours and because of your detour I will have to travel at top speed.” Logistics Sargent said as he looked at his PDA and made rough estimations as to what he would have to do to be on time.
“You heard the sergeant on the double Rangers.” Dray shouted out to the two Rangers that held the box waiting for their commanding officer to deal with the situation.
“Thank you Lieutenant.” Doctor Hunter breathed out the sigh of relief as he still was kind of new to dealing with conflict.

“Don’t sweat it Doctor Hunter, he is just worried about his bonuses. We have enough supplies spread out across 6 worlds to wage a high intensity conflict for years. It’s the manpower that is the problem. We didn’t expect that we would have to spread ourselves out across 6 planets. Those portals surely add an interesting twist to our strategy. Usually we would drop in a secure couple of cities and then pack up for the next planet.” Dray said as he began climbing into the truck.

The inside of the truck was quite spacious but it lacked any seats, clearly mostly set up for carrying the supplies, however within the floor of the truck there were certain locks. Those locks were the essential part of the modular design of the truck that allowed it to be easily repurposed for any job from being a mobile command center, troop carrier, to a fuel tanker.

Right now it was set up for supply duty, filled with locks to accept the specially designed boxes that would be secured inside and then could be easily accessed from outside of the truck. Since there were no seats put there, they put Ratmisia in the front section of the truck where drivers were since she was injured. As for the rest of the Rangers and science team, they had to sit and lay with their equipment and weapons trying to balance themselves between turns, acceleration and deceleration of the truck. They constantly bumped into one another and the secured boxes of ammunition and weapons. At least the journey was not that long, barely a couple of hours as the truck traveled at 200 kilometers per hour upon a paved road.

“Lieutenant, I see you carry a different weapon today.” Akilah approached Martinez as she pointed at the long rifle in his hand, while he was observing his troops rapidly unpacking the science equipment and other supplies out of the truck.
“Agh yes, Mk8 battle rifle. It is loaded with 8mm high powered rounds unlike 6.5mm rounds of the M22.” Dray hesitated to answer at first somewhat caught off guard by the statement.
“It sure does look bulkier than your standard issue rifles.” Akilah spoke looking directly at Martinez who still was somewhat puzzled as to why an archeologist would be interested in his weapon.

“Well M22 is arguably the best assault rifle out there but it is designed for killing a human, not something that is twice as big as one. Mk8 on the other hand is loaded with rounds that are practically made for hunting. Heavier round, more penetration, greater stopping power but unlike 6.5mm you can’t carry a lot of them and well it kicks like a mule.” Martinenez spoke as he pulled out the box-like magazine of the rifle, which not only was bigger than a M22 magazine but also was not banana shaped.

Then he pulled out the long round out of the magazine and gave it to Doctor Fahir to let her feel the weight of it. After that he went to one of the supply boxes and pulled out a smaller 6.5mm round for M22 rifles and gave it to Akilah to see and feel the difference.

“Not very good for automatic fire, however perfect for dealing with large animals. And from what I understand this is the biggest threat out there now that we secured the territory from NoirElDem forces. I also think we should have brought more of them with us given how much of a pain in the ass those minotaurs are to deal with.” He said as he loaded the magazine back into the Mk8 battle rifle and kind of was getting a better feel of it. It was long, heavy, emanated the sense of serious firepower and that of being expertly made.
“I see.” Akilah said looking at the round not sure what to do with that information and somewhat frustrated that Dray had most of his attention focused on his gun rather than on her.
“Hmm shouldn’t somebody come and greet us?” Doctor Hunter said as he looked out into the woods as Ratmisia stood right next to him taking a good look in both directions.

“They are probably scared off from going anywhere near the roads you built, Scholar Hunter. Those mechanical monstrosities you travel in; they are quite intimidating and from what I heard rather unrelenting a lot of creatures died beneath their wheels.” She said as she looked at the giant monstrosity that was the human supply truck. She had the front row sit to witness how fast those wheeled machines could travel and that it was not about to stop if animals would suddenly jump in front of it.

“It is rather unfortunate. Do we need to go deeper to find someone or we just wait here on the side of the road?” Doctor Hunter said as he looked at Ratmisia not exactly sure what to do once all the equipment would get unpacked.

“I think it is better when your machine will leave and then I will make the call for my people to welcome us.” Ratmisia said her gaze stared out into the distance. She was not sure what to even make of it, prior to arrival of humans everyone respected the boundaries of the forests even NoirElDems were rather respectful in treatment of nature. Humans on the other hand just carved their way through it. She’d call it hubris and arrogance if not for the attitude they treated it with. For them it seemed perfectly natural just uproot hundreds of trees to make room for their roads, it almost seemed like they did actually win a war against nature long time ago. Something she just could not believe. Even the Gods could not say they won against nature, the greatest creation of the Ancient Ones for they imbued chaos itself in it.

“Ok people listen up; we will generally have a supply truck returning back to Base Camp Noble every 12 hours and one outgoing as well. You can track them on your PDA so keep that in mind when crossing the road. If you want to pack up earlier than in the next two days, we can only take the returning trucks in order to go back to base.” Dray said as he stood in front of the 61 people at the edge of the forest and the 12-meter-wide road behind him. 50 of them were his soldiers, 10 civilians and 1 liaison.

At least that’s how the sanitized reports will remember that. What reports will not remember is that half of the soldiers were not with him from the beginning and Dray still was coming to grips that this was a war where not all of them would come back.

Civilians that were all best minds Federation could gather and who as such thought that knew best what is good for them and as for that liaison, well sanitized reports omit the strange culture and customs of locals. They were the aliens here; they were the strangers in a strange land.

As the group moved forward through the woods, soldiers fanned out establishing a perimeter around the civilians. They were here to protect them and even if the enemy was driven out of these lands they had no idea what else this world was hiding but it was quiet, too quiet.
“Shouldn’t there be a community of SylvElDems living here?” Doctor Hunter asked Ratmisia as he looked through the reports on the PDA.

“Yes there has to be a grove here with a druid and hunters of Artem protecting it, they should have come out by now as per the Traditions of Wardenship. Also there is gotta be at least birds around they would not perceive you as a threat” Ratmisia finally dared to air her worries, there was indeed something amiss in this forest.

“SIR! WE HAVE A BODY!” The shout of one of the Rangers echoed from the silent forest. The flash of anger bolted across the face of Martinez but then quickly subsided, his soldiers knew better than to give out their positions by so much noise and there was note of terror in this shout. Without hesitation he and his sergeant Dmitri Volkov rushed towards the shouting soldier.

“Holly shit…” Dray said in horror as he looked at what appeared to be a female body with parts of slightly green skin still showing like islands in a sea of red blood, meat and occasional white of the bone.

“Oh my god…” Dray heard a terrified whisper behind him and as he turned around he saw glass like eyes of Doctor Akilah Fahir. The strong headed, rebellious and brave woman was frozen in horror of what she saw.

“God Damn it, Doctor Fahir you don’t need to see this.” Dray quickly turned her around from the body. He knew that look all too well, this sight will haunt her forever now but unlike him who saw torn bodies like that on a daily basis she lacked this military desensitization training or his experience. Then he looked at the hapless civilians that also rushed towards him along with Ratmisia.

“VOLKOV KEEP THEM AWAY FROM THE BODY! They don’t need to see that.” He commanded as he pointed his finger in the direction of Doctor Hunter who just stopped seeing the fury in his eyes. Volkov did not waste any time to carry out the orders of his commanding officer but let Ratmisia pass after she gave him a fierce look. No she was not like those civilians, she was very much familiar with horrors of war.
“Be at peace sister as you hunt in the Forests Of Artem in Elysium.” Ratmisia said as she looked at the body not even sure if she could find the eyes to close.

“This wasn’t NoirElDems.” Dray said as he squatted and put down his battle rifle upright like a spear. He looked at the body; there were no clean cuts of the blades or the scorched marks of the energized bolts.
“No it was not Honored Dray Martinez.” Ratmisia said as she began examining the body with her emerald eyes.

“Animal attack?” Dray said as he looked at Ratmisia with a penetrating and focused look. Right now it was nothing more than a bag of meat to him, not a person but an object which laid secrets to the threat he had to deal with.
“Yes but it’s all wrong.” Ratmisia said calmly trying to make sense of the injuries oddly enough finding kindred spirit in Martinez.

“What do you mean?” Martinez looked back at the body, it wasn’t the first mangled corpse he had seen but his experienced eye saw the difference between them. This was not result of body being rolled over by the treads of the tanks, torture, explosives or fragmentation. All of those would result in mangled corpses but not like this.

“She was torn to pieces but not eaten. It almost seems like the animal that did that was almost reveling in killing her.” Ratmisia began pointing out the injuries. Martinez actually began noticing the pattern, they did in fact look like someone was ripping it apart with some manner of claws and serrated fangs and pieces of internal organs and meat thrown out in all directions. A harrowing sight almost like a grenade exploded inside of her.
“Ok I’m no expert on animals so can you tell me what animal did that and what to expect?” Dray rose up as he grabbed the battle rifle, switched the safety off and looked around into the forest.
“Gladia Felindas from the looks of it but something is wrong some of the injuries are strange as if it had help from something smaller” Ratmisia said as she looked at the gouged out eyes.
“Gladia Felindas?” Dray said in confusion, not sure what to make of that word that his translation software seemed unable to translate it.

“It's a large predatory animal, one of the top predators in the forests but they would never do that to the Huntress of Artem, they are supposed to be our companion animals, help us out to keep the population of herbivores in control. “The moment she uttered those words Ratmisia could not believe the conclusion she came to and shriveled in fear.
“It could have gone mad.” Dray said as he stepped forward, a gun raised scanning the perimeter along with a couple of soldiers in front of him.

“A Huntress would know how to deal with a mad animal, this is different. We might be dealing with a different animal here.” Ratmisia said still looking at the mangled body of one of her sisters. Dray looked back at Ratmisia a flash of serious concern run across his face.

“Shit, Volkov, tighten the perimeter we don’t want to be spread around if we have no idea what we are dealing with and find Doctor Vernon we might need his expertise. We will be moving back to the road and set up defensive positions there.” Dray said on his coms still looking at Ratmisia. The SylvElDem huntress already had some experience working with the human military and this stiffness in Dray’s body worried her as she knew what she was about to ask him was not something he would like.
“Honored Dray Martinez I must know what happened to the grove.” She said as she looked directly into the eyes of Dray.

“My priority is the safety of the civilian personnel, not figuring out what happened here.” Dray answered back without hesitation. Ratmisia looked to the side as she was processing how she could resolve the conflict here. Humans needed reasons to do what she wanted. Creatures without fate could not rely on the guidance of gods to lay out the path before them and it did clearly play a part in their pragmatic nature. So she began considering all the information that she had.

“Didn’t you say that we will have 12 hours until the next machine of yours that can carry us back to safety. The groves are more open and easier to defend. If it is the safety of your scholars you are concerned about, Honored Dray Martinez it would be a good place to be at.” She said calmly and oddly enough in this reasoning she found certainty. Not the kind of certainty she had from faith but from her own reasoning and wondered if this is how humans made peace with the chaos of their lives.
“Ok fine, but one step at a time, let’s see what our experts say.” Dray said after a small moment of consideration.

After a couple of minutes as the Human flora and fauna expert made his measurements and talked to Ratmisia as she helped him to make sense of tracks left on the ground by both Huntress and the beast he was ready to give his statement to Lieutenant.
“Talk to me doc.” Dray said the moment Doctor Vernon turned to him to explain his findings.

“Ok from what I understand we are talking about the large cat size of the tiger maybe bigger. It appears to be somewhat mutated as it has additional appendages. Some of the wounds point to that. Those appendages seem something along the lines of some manner of tentacles with a set of sharp claws or teeth on them. Now to the interesting part.” Doctor Vernon presented his data pad upon which a specialized application created a somewhat uncanny image of what appeared to be a large cat with at least two long tentacles protruding from its back with what almost looked like mouth filled rows of sharp teeth.

“A cat with tentacles is not interesting enough for you doctor?” Dray said with a smile on his face finally he saw something that did look rather more alien than what most of what he saw that looked very much similar to what he saw in a zoo on Earth. That actually rather bothered him, he was no scientist but from his school years he knew that animals would evolve into different shapes on different continents and expected something along the lines of squids. However quite frankly speaking it was not his job to be bothered about it.
“Well it is but it is the way it attacked.” Doctor Vernon then ran a different application on his data pad and now it was processing all the data he gathered with Ratmisia.
“What about it?” Dray said as he looked at Doctor Vernon more interested in his reaction than what the data pad was doing.

“It is either a very good jumper or I’d say it just appeared on a spot here, there and just seems to appear right on top of the victim almost like…” Doctor stood right beside the Lieutenant pointing his finger first at a distant bush about 20 meters away then to a couple of disturbed branches about 10 meters to the side and then to the body. The PDA on the other hand of Doctor Vernon showed a crude simulation that just did not make sense. It was trying to make the creature jump but the footprints did not provide adequate data so the simulated creature just sort of blinked from spot to spot.
“It materialized out of thin air.” Dray cut the doctor off before he could finish the sentence.

“Yes.” Doctor Vernon as he looked at the Dray somewhat baffled as Dray looked like he knew what might cause it. Dray looked up to the skies where one of the drones was circling. ‘Drones lack the programming to track such targets’ He thought to himself remembering his experience from the Temple of the Ancients as he walked off towards Ratmisia.

“Ratmisia can those Gladia Felindas use magic akin to Shadow Dancers?” Dray said as he stared intensely into the woods trying to pick up any movement. His mind was playing tricks on him, every movement of the leafs looked like a threat and only through the conscious effort he kept himself composed.

“No, the gift of magic is only reserved for very few animals and only for the creatures of the soul but then again this does not look like Gladia.” Ratmisia said but there was no confidence in her words and Dray saw that. He cycled through his coms so that every soldier under his command could hear him.

“Look alive people, we have tigers that can do the same tricks as Shadow Dancers and they want us for a meal. Maintain a tight perimeter. Let's hope they are not crazy to attack a large group. We are following the SylvElDem scout on this one.” He said calmly yet clearly and those soldiers that survived their encounter in the Temple of the Ancients immediately became rather twitchy jumping at every movement of the leaves.
“Ratmisia lead the way.” Dray commanded without hesitation throwing his hand forward.

“As you command, Honored Dray Martinez.” Ratmisia said and began walking forward ahead of the soldiers. Her arm was still covered in the brace and yes she was in no condition to fight but that didn’t seem to stop her when she noticed Dray was following closely behind her along with Sergeant Volkov.
“Right behind you. Just show us the way and don’t play hero, let us deal with the threat.” Dray said the moment she looked back at him almost ready to ask to give her some space.

Finally they came across some manner of river, clearly artificially made as its borders were clearly all too well defined to be of natural occurrence but more than that the other side of the river was walled off with some manner of barbed roots. Those roots made a literal wall about 3 meters high.
“Will they open the gates for us?” Dray said with a giggle looking at the wall of barbed roots.
“This is not normal, the spell of life wall only is enacted when the grove is under siege.” Ratmisia looked even more disturbed upon seeing it.
“Well, can you call out for them to drop their defenses for us?” Dray looked at Ratmisia as the group was slowly amassing at the edge of the water, marveling at the natural wall.

“Give me a moment, Honored Dray Martinez.” Ratmisia began looking around and the moment her eyes singled out one of the trees, one of the largest trees she began walking towards it. As she approached it she began circling around drawing the attention of the science team.

She paid no attention to the curious scholars of the TerraDems as she dug out some sort of the mushroom but did not take it. Instead she reached out in the bag on her belt and pulled out some manner of flask of green liquid. She drank it and her eyes began glowing with emerald light. She looked at her healthy hand and the skin on her hand became rough as if covered in some manner of tiny needles. She touched the mushroom with her hands and seemed to go catatonic. That’s when soldiers along with Dray began giving her a worried look.
“They are all dead…” Ratmisia said with a resonating voice.
“Well pack it up, we are going back.” Dray just made a spinning motion with his hand and soldiers gave a smirk to the science team who seemed to be rather disappointed by this statement.
“Wait, I can sense someone is alive inside but barely.” Ratmisia shouted in the same resonating voice. Dray first looked at Ratmisia then towards the wall of barbed roots and shook his head.
“Great, Get the bridge and the explosives ready, this wall is coming down and send the drone to check what is happening.” Dray commanded and designated tech operators began preparations.

One of them got the drone out of his backpack and sent it flying over the wall that tried to reach out for it. However the internal collision software kicked it and the small buzzing mechanical bird managed to dodge the attacks from the wall. This behavior from the wall gave the engineers a pause before setting up a bridge towards the other end of the river.

“Ratmisia I need information. Who is alive and how?” Dray said as he looked at his soldiers not exactly sure what to do now that they realized that this wall of barbed roots was active and ready to defend itself.

“The druid, the guardian of this grove, he is in hibernation cocoon.” Ratmisia said still gently holding the mushroom. Dray looked at the PDA to see what the drone was seeing. What he saw was a sight of the massacre. Similar mauled bodies laid everywhere, scattered all across the grass painting isles of red upon the sea of green. The trees clearly shown the signs of combat and the central huge tree had a lot of it’s smaller branches broken up as if some creatures tried to pick off its fruits.

“That strange amalgamation of roots right in the center?” Dray said as the image from the drone focused on the strange egg like structure right beneath the blue canopy of the central tree. Ratmisia released the mushroom and the emerald glow began wearing off from her eyes.
“Yes, by the gods?” She said in horror the moment she noticed that the tree with blue leaves was devoid of fruits.
“What's wrong?” Dray was ready to hear the further bad context of what he was seeing.

“Eather fruits they are all gone.” Ratmisia said as she rushed towards the wall but stopped the moment she saw that Federal soldiers were still not sure how to get in. She looked from side to side trying to see the opening but could not find any as her breath was getting more and more erratic.

“And why is that a problem?” Dray slowly approached Ratmisia who still was not sure what even to tell him. That’s when Doctor Maximilian Hunter who overheard the conversation decided to approach to give his input.

“Eather fruits produce Eather enriched juice. Essentially it is the fuel for magic and if my understanding is correct, if they are all gone it’s like having a month's supply from a fusion generator.” Doctor Hunter explained as he looked down and was scratching his head somewhat struggling to find words to help a lieutenant to appreciate the gravity of the situation

“That’s a lot of energy....” Dray at first said as if he was just stating a fact but then the realization began to sink in. A standard fusion generator could provide power for shields, mining lasers, and power an entire city. As those facts began popping up in his head, Drays eyes widened and he exclaimed. ”That’s a lot of energy!”
“Volkov, what's the hold up we need to get in PRONTO!” He shouted on his coms as something just clicked in his mind. ‘We might have made a huge miscalculation in our strategic priorities’
“Sir, that wall is... Well it’s hostile. I don't think we can put a bridge across if it’s going to try to kill us.” Volkov looked back at his commanding officer.
“Then blow it up from range. You have anti-structure munitions don’t you?” Dray was not pleased to hear the excuses.

“Yes, Sir! Guys you heard the lieutenant we are going open that place up with can openers!” Volkov relayed Drays orders to the rest of the man and the other 4 sergeants. In usual drilled fashion Ranger engineers pulled out their rocket launchers’ long square blocks and began placing a similarly long tube inside of them. From the cases they had with them there were 3 different tubes inside, the only noticeable difference between those tubes were the green, yellow and red stripes around them. Each was indicating the nature of the ammunition deployed. Green was an air-burst anti personnel rocket, red was the high explosive anti-tank rocket and the yellow was a dedicated bunker buster rocket designed either to explode inside of the wall or once it penetrates it.

Several Rangers took kneeling positions, aimed their rockets at the wall. At first, the cold launch system propelled the rockets out of the blocks leaving white smoke coming out of them and then the engines kicked it accelerating the rockets to the point of breaking a sound barrier sending supersonic booms across the forest. These supersonic booms were quickly followed by the sounds of explosions as the splinters of the wood flew in all directions leaving a sizable hole in the wall of roots. As if wounded animals they tried to reform the defenses but the destructive force did a lot more damage than they could repair.
Rangers without hesitation dropped and built up a bridge across the river with their equipment and quickly began crossing over along with the science team.

“It is kind of surprising we still haven't run into those animals.” Hunter said as he looked at Rangers quickly setting up a minigun position in front of the whole in the wall they just made along with multiple sensors.
“I think they are watching us. Studying us before they strike.” Dray said calmly as he stared out into the whole in the wall as if there was an enemy there.
“Really, they are just feral animals afterall.” Doctor Hunter looked out into the hole in the wall somewhat skeptical.

“They might be and they might be not but I would rather be over prepared than underprepared.” Dray said with a smile, there was something about being step ahead of that scientist that warmed his soul.

“Honored Dray Martinez is right, those creatures managed to kill hunters of Artem.” Ratmisia said as she looked at the defense positions being set up by human soldiers. There was something about them just doing things that put her mind at ease.
“Ratmisia what are you doing here? We have the druid to wake up.” Dray looked at Ratmisia and there was no question in his eyes.

“As you command!” She just walked towards the cocoon-like structure in the middle of the grove surrounded by scientists probing it with all kinds of equipment. They all looked with fascination with the results of the scans.

“Fascinating, it clearly accelerates the healing process, as the roots supply the body with nutrients and practically act as life support. He was supposed to be dead.” Doctor Vernon said as he looked at his tablet. But Ratmisia paid no attention to human scientists as she once again pulled out a flask out of her pouch and drank it. The eyes once again began glowing and she extended her arm towards the roots.

“Wow.” Vernon said as the image of the cocoon on his tablet lit up. It showed that indeed there was something akin to the nervous system running all across it. It was made of what he thought were just fungi that were in fact acting as some sort of neuro-transmiters which joined up with Ratmisia through her hand. They were exchanging electro-chemichal signals. How they could interact with one another and interpret signals between one another was still an utter mystery but they were clearly communicating.
Ratmisia just stood there as thoughts, knowledge and emotions flooded her mind. There was presence in her mind, a voice that spoke within her, a voice that was not hers yet clearly within her.

“Ratmisia, the huntress of Artem.” The voice spoke to her, as the emotions of flowed through her hand. Emotions that were filled with sorrow despair and hopelessness but oddly enough it was also filled with confusion.

“Honored Guardian Silvarius what happened here?” Ratmisia thought of the question but she also mastered courage in her heart to reassure the druid in the cocoon while the scientist just observed the readings from their equipment as the two aliens exchanged information that indecipherable to their tools.

“A curse was laid upon our companions, a curse of madness, a curse of change, a curse of dominance. TerraDems have driven off our dark cousins but they laid the seeds of their machinations upon us that only was spurred by the foolishness of TerraDems. They only care for destruction” Silvarius now was sharing the memories but not only his memories but the memories of the giant cats.

At first he shared how he was told not to interfere with the activities of humans, how they cut down the trees and uprooted them. He showed the sorrow and pain he felt, he shared his feelings of sacrifice he was making for what he believed to be a greater good. He thought that they would heal but then humans came back. He showed how soulless machines with their abominable magic that was not of the Earth poisoned the land and intombed all life in ageless and lifeless rock.

He once again showed his strength and faith in fate which now he regretted deeply. Finally he has shown the terror that those cats have experienced as giant beasts travelled at infernal speeds and glowing eyes at night, how they stupefied the animals on the road. He shared the horror those animals felt, the horror of something so unknowable so terrifying baring down at you like inevitable doom.

How those cats were no longer the masters of those forests and how much it terrified them. Then he showed the NoirElDem which fed the cats some manner of a poisoned fruit. How this witch imbued twisted magic into them. Warping their bodies and their minds. Their bodies mutated to give them strength while their minds were poisoned with promises of power, with promises for returning back to being masters of the forest only if they would accept her as the new master over the order of the gods. At first they resisted but between the two terrors one promised them power while the other only death and uncertainty.

Scientists began picking up strange changes within the cocoon. It was small but it clearly originated from the mind of the druid inside but more precisely it originated from the brain as if the very strange process within were causing this change. They had no idea what was happening because this was all new to them as matter of fact it only excited them. They saw new phenomena unfold before them and as such they were thirsty for that knowledge as they stared at their datapads with excited smiles.

“They are here to help you now.” Ratmisia felt this despair even strongly but she reached out for that hope that those humans originated in her. She drew upon the memories of their might, the might of their unique understanding of the world, the comfort that their craftsmanship provided, the inspiring fearlessness before the doomed odds but most importantly the asuredness that their stunning victories brought with them. It was as if some manner of shield within her mind, within her soul was fending off the despair that emanated from the cocoon.

“How can soulless creatures driven by the same madness as our companions were cursed with, help us? They are monsters that only seek to dominate rather than to uphold the balance of ordained by the gods' ' That's when Silvarius has shown something else to Ratmisia he has shown what he saw in the eyes of humans that came to him. He saw this look in their eyes how they saw the lands they were scared of, how they saw the animals that they so carelessly killed. It was the look of a creature that would stop at nothing to rise to the top of the food chain. The look of creatures that found the very notion of sharing their dominant position with anything or anyone offensive to their very core.

A look of the creature that saw anything beneath them as nothing more than a nuisance, a creature that sought to bend everything to its will and shape the world in its own image. The same ideas, the very same notion he also saw in those now changed cats. They would no longer be the companions to their SylvElDems, no now they would no longer follow the order of the gods. In their terror before humans, they found the strength to oppose it, the strength to participate in this cruel contest for dominance.

“They are not monsters, they care deeply for life.” Ratmisia saw how fear was turning into hatred and to repute she drawn upon the memories of that caring hug Scholar Fahir gave her, of that moment when they would not let her die in an icy grave, of that moment when they rose up to liberate countless people from slavery. To the memories of the ideology, they carried within their books. Notions of protecting life, the dignity of said life but most importantly the beauty of life.

“They carved a death path to build their roads, a road made of dead stone to let their dead creatures travel with no regard for the sanctity of life. They have scared this lands forever.” Now it was Silvarius who was protecting himself from this influence coming from Ratmisia. For he hated it, for it was an affront to the very order he swore to maintain.

“Sacrifices must be made for us to win against the darkness” Ratmisia pushed on committed to the vision that now formed from all her memories. Yes, it was a rocky path to it but she saw it now clear as day a vision fueled not by certainty but by hope, a hope for a better future, and faith not in the divine plan but by faith in one’s own actions.

“The words of the foolish child that refused to see the will of fate, the will of the order ordained to us by the Gods. Wait… You are tainted! YOU ARE TAINTED! What have you done?” Silvarius retreated away from those alien feelings at first but then realization dawned upon him as he decided to show the foolish child the grandiosity of the great plan laid out by the 3 Eyed Oracle. She was no longer in the grand plan.
“What have I done?” Ratmisia announced in confusion.
“Your fate, your time has passed, you should’ve been in Elysium. Why are you here?” Silvarius became even more terrified as now he was trying to find the strands of Ratmisia’s fate but he could not.
“What do you mean?” She spoke even with greater confusion.

“Your Soul, it's your soul! It is no longer within the tapestry of fate, yet why are you here?” The sense of terror consumed every thought of Silvarius as he could not find the strand of the soul of Ratmisia.

“What was my fate?” Ratmisia demanded as she stood there in a catatonic state. While the scientist began seeing the same strange energies enveloping the cocoon and now flowing over to Ratmisia. They began giving one another worried glances while Doctor Hunter lifted his eyes away from the datapad and took a good look at Ratmisia’s hand. He saw as some manner of purple mist coming off the cocoon and enveloping Ratmisa’s hand.

“Your fate was to die in battle in the arms of the one who loved you with a jealous hand of your sister but not here.” Silvarius finally managed to pick up the strands of fate but those were back in the past and showed no future.

“I was saved by the TerraDems twice.” Ratmisia spoke back feeling that there was something enveloping all around her, for the first time she saw her fate. Yes, it was enveloped in the Mist of Dis but now it was more transparent and it revealed that it was trying to rectify something.

“Fate, Fate tried to correct itself and twice those creatures interfered in it. They are the true darkness, they must be expelled, they are a threat to the strands of fate. Now I see it. NOW I SEE WHY GODS LET THIS HAPPEN. They are our enemy! Oh Dis now I see your wisdom! NOW SEE IT FOR YOURSELF! TAKE YOUR PLACE IN THE GRAND PLAN!” Silvarius spoke and the cocoon changed color to black as the purple mist emanated from him. Scientists saw on their datapads as the body of Silvarius began undergoing rapid changes. That’s when Doctor Hunter reached into his pocket finding that flask filled with water that glowed with blue light.

“NO!” Ratmisia screamed out as she saw the fate, the new fate, the fate where she stood victorious over the broken body of Datrius begging her for something. Scientists got startled as Ratmisia who was silent until this moment just screamed out as the purple mist was dissipated from her in some manner of shockwave. The cocoon began unraveling revealing a horned creature inside eyes glowing with purple light.
“STEP AWAY FROM HIM!” She screamed out falling to the ground.
“What the hell is happening?” Akilah spoke in confusion, not sure what to do as she rushed to help Ratmisia.
“Move away! He is CURSED!” Ratmisia pushed screamed into Akilah’s face only to see her rapidly pulled away by tentacle coming from, now mutated body of Silvarius.
“YOU DISGUSTING CREATURES!” Silvarius shouted in a resonating voice as he had thrown Akilah like a ragdoll.

“Fuck! SHOOT HIM.” Dray shouted to his soldiers who still were in shock from what they had just seen as members of the science team were thrown in all directions like ragdolls. The command from their commanding officer seemed to lift the stupor from the soldiers as they raised their rifles. Silvarius upon seeing the actions of human soldiers morphed into something akin to what they saw on doctor Vernons simulation and dematerialized into black mist as bullets passed right through it.

“Did he just? HOW THE FUCK DID HE JUST TURN INTO THAT THING?” Dray said as Silvarius blinked from one place to another staying ahead of the bullets. All of sudden one of the soldiers screamed out in pain and quickly was silenced then another as their lifelines flatlined with their throats ripped open. Then he blinked at Ratmisia who still was on the ground and enveloped her with tentacles and blinked away with her continuing to dodge fire.
