“What is happening?” Sergeant Volkov said as the walls around the grove began retreating back into the ground and revealing that those cat-like creatures were there just waiting to attack as their fur flickered with optical illusions. There was predation glowing in their eyes, they glowed with red light, filled with vengeance, with hunger, with hatred.

“Son Of a... Shit! Establish a perimeter to protect the science team! Pay attention to the body language you have to hit them where they will be, not try to hit them where they are!” Dray shouted out as he began running towards the bodies of science team members who luckily were mostly bruised rather than critically injured. Rangers quickly took defensive positions as the gunfire echoed across the forest. The mutated cats blinked in between the trees quickly closing in the distance. It seemed they tried to separate the soldiers from one another, trying to pick them off one by one but it proved to be a challenge as bullets zipped all around them.
“Ratmisia...” Akilah moaned in pain drawing the attention of Dray who rushed to help her.
“God Damn it, I NEED A MEDIC HERE!” Dray shouted out as he looked at her, her white shirt was covered in blood as brunch stuck out of her abdomen.
“We have to help her.” She tried to stand up but could not find the strength to do so as Dray also stopped from rising.
“What why?” Dray said as he stared at her through the combat goggles as Doctor Hunter and Doctor Vernon approached her with biofoam spray in hand.

“Because she trusts us, Dray, because she believes in us. Because I believe in you, Dray.” Akilah reached with her bloody hand for Drays armor looking up at this warrior with begging eyes. Thoughts began rushing through Drays mind as he looked around. He already saw some of his soldiers being dragged off. Yes, his sergeants tried to maintain order in this pandemonium and actually were doing a pretty decent job of it but it still was a fight to the death. Those creatures were demonstrating pack tactics and adaptability that were far above ordinary animals.
“I will not risk my men...” Dray said with sorrow but was interrupted by the resonating voice of Doctor Hunter.

“Lieutenant GO! I’ll help out here.” Doctor Hunter said as his eyes glowed with blue light and hands crackling with electricity. He was taking deep breaths, he was scared but he was mastering his courage. Dray stared into the eyes of that boy, who was a sorry mess when he saw him for the first time but now he clearly was making a stand just the way he taught him. Dray made a deep breath himself as he pulled the knife out of the sheave and attached it to the barrel of his Mk8 Battle Rifle.
“Volkov you are in charge!” Dray said as he turned around and looked at the mingun just showering the forest with bullets.
“Yes Sir!” Volkov at first hesitated before answering but as the star moved towards his name on his HUD he looked at his commanding officer and answered without question.

What the hell was he doing? He was going out there as an officer of a platoon of Rangers alone risking his life. A life that on paper at least was considered to be the most valuable of the entire platoon. No it was not only on paper, he was a commanding officer he was the lynchpin of all the cohesion and Federation invested in him alot more than in any other soldier making his replacement far more complicated task. Yet here he was gun in hand on a personal request from some civilian to save an alien. And for what? A sentiment. A stupid sentiment. A noble sentiment.

‘Decision is made no point on dwelling on it, but still I have to make rational choices’ he thought to himself as he marched forward, his eyes tracking every movement around him as those infernal creatures were blinking around, probing how they could approach him for the killing blow.

“This is Lieutenant Dray Martinez 245th Ranger Regiment, 3rd platoon. We need immediate evac. I have a science team in danger and my forces are engaged by highly hostile and dangerous fauna.” Dray said to his com sending the message traveling at speed of light upon the radio waves to transmitters running across the road, to send signals across optical fiber connected to all the Federal bases. Once this was done he raised his hefty rifle with bayonet attached to it and ran across the improvised bridge across the river. Mingun fire covering his dive into the fray.

He ran as the mutated cats jumped at the opportunity to strike the separated target as two of them blinked in for an attack. The moment they materialized above him the stream of bullets cut them to pieces as the fountain of fire emerged from the rotating barrels and hundreds of casings hit the ground.

It was chaos. Those feral animals already managed to drag one of the soldiers away. They knew that they could not just deal with the poor bastard right there for his friends would not let them. As such they wrapped their tentacle around his leg and threw him off balance and then blink away to safety. He lost his rifle so he tried to reach for the pistol while with the other hand he tried to protect himself. The mutated drove its teeth into his arm and with brutal force ripped it out as the two tentacles went for its eyes, cracking up the goggles yet unable to gauge them out. Then those tentacles with rows of teeth moved away and he saw that his arm was gone and a bloody maw with giant fangs and rows of serrated teeth descending down at him.

The mutated creature felt the warm red blood shower over its face. It was filled with metallic taste, saturated with a cocktail of hormones so invigorating and so intoxicating. Blood, guts and pieces of armor flew over in all directions out of the tree it hid behind.

This was the moment when highly trained soldiers that saw this really laid a brick in their pants and just stood there stupefied as 3 of the cats blinked out of trees and joyfully jumped around and their red hungry eyes blurred into them. As all of sudden the lightning hit the first cat and then jumped onto the other and then to the third one leaving nothing more but fried husks.

Rangers looked back to see where this lighting came from and saw Max as he had his finger pointing at the last of the cats as his other hand was fixed as if holding an imaginary sphere. His eyes glowed with blue light. The moment he heard the cries of help from behind him he quickly turned around as he began gathering energies with both of his hands to create some manner of glowing ball.

‘Electricity is nothing more than the flow of negatively charged electrons, rip them out of the molecules of air and trap them in magnetic traps and then direct their need to move to the positive charge, now I need to tap into how it feels. It’s immediate, energetic; it goes where it wants. I merely show the way to its ultimate destination’ he thought to himself as the ball of lighting began glowing even more intensely as more and more electrons moved into it from the surrounding air. Then he threw out his right hand finger pointing first at one cat that still was in the black mist and it was forced to rematerialise. Then he rapidly threw his finger into the other cat and the lighting jumped once again and finally he pointed a finger unto the mist that dragged a soldier away. Unfortunately frying both of them on the spot as the ball of lighting dissipated.
“Oh no…” Max’s hands dropped to the ground.as he realized what he just did.
“Doctor Hunter he was a goner either way snap out of it, fight is not over!” Volkov rushed over to Max, putting him back on his feet.
“But…” Hunter recoiled and shook his head only to be slapped on the face.

“Make up for it by saving more lives!” Volkov shouted into his face pointing at the enemy who despite the best efforts of Rangers sending one burst after another from their rifles just dragged away 3 more.
“Alright. Then win me some time I have an idea.” Max said after taking a deep breath and raised his arms in the air.

“Rangers to me Doctor Hunter is the Alamo. Fox, Madison, Fujima, your squads will have to compensate for shifts in the perimeter; don't let them anywhere near the science team!” Volkov shouted in his coms.

“You got it Dima.” Volkov heard on his coms as Rangers began shifting their positions his squad was forming up on him and Doctor Hunter while others began feeling in the gap in the defense that was left by Volkovs squad.

They began feeling their hairs rising as a massive electromagnetic charge was beginning to form right above them. The color all around them started to turn somewhat gray as Max was moving his hands in round motion as if gathering energies above him. He did so for a good minute or so before locking his eyes with one of the creatures and a flash of lighting dropped to the ground creating something akin to the cage made of lightning guiding the ball of lights closer to the ground. Then he threw his hands outwards and those balls of lighting exploded sending shockwaves outwards away from the cage. The mutated cats lit up with light coming from inside of them and dragging others back into the material realm. They howled in pain as their very blood saturated with Either flashed up in light.
“Sargent this is your chance, I took away their powers now they are nothing more than just Feral animals.” Hunter shouted out.
“SEARCH AND DESTROY BOYS! SEARCH AND DESTROY!” Volkov shouted out the command with a smile on his face.

Dray jumped over the log not even sure where to look for the Ratmisia and that crazed druid but hesitation or moment to think meant death. He was acting on pure instincts honed by years of training and combat experience, this was the moment of the flow. The moment when all your attention was in this present moment. Free of worries of the future or revisions of the past. Only predictions of enemy movements relying on the experience of the past working together through the magic of instantaneous processes of the present within the brain. Those animals wanted a contest for supremacy. Well Dray was bringing his best to it.

He saw one of those mutated cats blinking in between the branches of the trees as he ran in the direction where this crazed druid ran away. He just knew how that creature would approach him given how he would move. It was as if he knew its thoughts. It was chasing him down, it was driven by the blood lust of the hunter, it thought it was chasing down a cowardly prey running for it’s life, not a predator that was laying out a trap.

Dray ran around one of the trees clearly aware he broke the line of sight and turned around aiming the rifle as the creature materialized ready for attack only to find a barrel of the gun with a sharp point of the knife extending from underneath it. It recoiled in surprise as the muzzle flash sent bursts of heavy rounds of the mk8 battle rifle all the way through its body. It was quite a different sound than that of the buzz of the M22 assault rifle. It had a far slower fire rate almost twice as low as the bullets were bigger and the harmonics were different. Each shot felt louder, more powerful and the cling of metal felt heavier. It also kicked with a heavy and satisfying recoil punch into his shoulder.

This was not the end of it as he quickly switched targets seeing another cat jumping around along with another one circling in for the kill. For but a brief moment he tracked the mist and then drove the crosshairs in the scope of his rifle ahead of it and opened fire perfectly predicting where it would rematerialise. The body of the mutated creature was sent flying in the direction of bullets.

Without hesitation he turned around and thrusted his rifle upwards squeezing the trigger as both recoil and knife driving into the soft flesh pushed back the weapon. The tentacles filled with rows of serrated teeth thrusted across his neck barely scratching his skin as two clawed paws hit his shoulder pads leaving marks upon the adaptive paint.

The creature screamed out in pain as bursts of blood came out of its back following the path of bullets ripping to shred its insides. Dray threw the body of the creature over his head and then made two more rapid thrusts into it finishing it off. He looked around with a predatory growl. The two more cats actually stopped for a moment; their red glowing eyes now did not seem so self assured. Dray kept looking at them with a predatory gaze of his own as he dropped a magazine from the rifle and reached out for the one in his pocket.
There was a taste of blood on his tongue as he slammed the magazine into the rifle and locked the bolt. The blood covered his face as more blood dripped from that silver knife.

“Flee, flee to your master” He growled and stepped forward in the direction of terrified creatures. They glanced at each other and blinked out in the same direction. They blinked once, twice jumping from one branch of one tree to the other. Hopeful that they still had the speed advantage. The gunfire echoed across the forest and the groan of pain from one cat followed.

The surviving cat looked back and saw its fellow animal fell to the ground coughing up blood and that terrifying bipedal monster with that thunder staff in hand in relentless pursuit. They made a fatal mistake, they were jumping from side to side only increasing the distance they had to travel while this monster just rushed after them in a straight line.

He ran up to the wounded creature, made one more shot and then stabbed it repeatedly with his staff twisting it each time. Finally he stabbed it in the throat and ripped out locking his eyes with the other creature. It shrieked in horror as it blinked away forward. It ran like crazy, no longer trying to dodge enemy fire or even caring about the balance as it tripped over the roots of the trees. It materialized and mid flight It felt as its leg all of a sudden grown limb followed by the sound of gunfire. Its body tumbled over hitting a tree. It began crawling out of the woods towards the road, towards its new master, towards the druid which now was one of them.

It crawled over and saw its master saying something to Ratmisia as the energies flowed all around her as she desperately resisted them. It heard the heavy footsteps of that bipedal monster right behind him. He was no longer running; he was catching his breath after the chase. It was not an erratic painting, it was deep and well paced breaths.
This monster now was right behind it as it kept crawling. It cried out in horror as the blade was driven right at the back of its skull.
Silvarius heard the cries of the creature and saw this human pulling out some manner of spear out of the ascended Gladia Felindas as its tentacles jerked around and dropped lifelessly to the ground.

Silvarius was about to shout out something at this soulless creature but this human was not wasting time as he pointed that strange spear with its metal point still dripping blood at him. He was just fast enough to extend his arm as a single metal shard traveling at unimaginable speeds smashed into the purple shield sending ripples across it.
“Honored Dray Martinez by the blessings of Artem” Ratmisia managed to shout out with renewed hope in her eyes.
“You chose them over the plan of the Gods, how dare you invoke her name.” Silvarius looked at Ratmisia in shock.

“You are the one who betrayed the vision of the gods for the illusions of Dis!” Ratmisia shouted out as she was still entrapped by the tentacles coming from Silvarius's back. As Dray with flick of a finger switched his rifle from semi-automatic to full on auto and began unloading into Silvarius’s shield.

“I see you are the lost cause, first I deal with this soulless creature. Fool your persistence can not change fate!” Silvarius shouted out, pushing his hand forward as bullets showered the shield while the other reached out for the ground, dropping some seeds into it. Within seconds the seeds had grown into some manner of green fruits just as the gun clicked and bingoed out on ammunition.

“I’ve got plenty more ammo mother fucker just a matter of time.” Dray growled as he began reloading the rifle rushing for cover as Silvarius threw one fruit after another at him. The fruits exploded in green mist and as Dray gasped for air he felt the mist choking him out.

Weakness settled in his body. He coughed up and only through sheer force of will stopped himself from breathing in. The lungs cried out for more air as vision shrinked and was covered in green, poisonous mist.

Chemical warfare, he was trained for it. Drills, countless drills was what he had to rely on. His hands despite feeling weakness coming over them along with the paralysis reached out for the mask. Lungs were still desperate for the breath of fresh air. Fingers twitchingly reached out for the button as the mask expanded and he attached it to his face. He gasped for the oxygen-enriched air reserve within the mask. He still felt weakness and paralysis rushing him over.

“Poison... “ He mumbled to himself as his hand reached for another button on the mask. The infusion of a mixture of different kinds of toxins entered his lungs. They were slow-acting but they were essential to neutralization of most fast acting poisons even if he had to flash them out later in the hospital. However, poisons are a fickle thing, no way to know that omni-antidote did cover the poisons that he was affected with.
Silvarius approached triumphantly the cloud of green mist, Ratmisia dragged behind him by the tentacles.

“Maybe this will teach you a lesson, petulant child.” With the move of his hands, he dissipated the poisoned cloud revealing Dray struggling in the convulsions on the ground. He dropped another seed on the ground and it has grown into a wooden spike which he picked up ready to drive into the soulless creature.

“Any last words TerraDem before I put you out of your misery?” He said as he turned Dray’s body that suffered in convulsions to face him. For a brief moment, he saw a red light coming from the helmet of Dray who was clearly mastering his will to stare intensely at him. He raised the wooden spear that was being imbued by blue energies high above Drays head.

“SHOOT!” Dray shouted out catching Silvarius by surprise only but for a moment to see that Dray starred not his face but directly at his chest. He looked down hands high holding a spear and saw the red dot on his chest. The rain of tungsten core 30 mm rounds like torrent of knives shattered the shield surrounding Silvarius giving him but a brief moment to see many more metal shards coming at him before they cut him to pieces leaving nothing but a red mist of blood in their wake.
“Honored Dray Martinez, by the goddess let me help you. I have the antidote for you.” Ratmisia rushed towards Dray with a flask filled with some manner of golden liquid.

“No, you have no idea that potion might react with chemicals I’m already floating in. Also, Dray will do just fine.” He said as convulsion finally stopped pushing the flask away from himself, by the looks of it the omni-antidote was finally taking the effect. He removed his mask and the helmet. His once clear brown eyes now were deeply sunk in the blue bags.
“But Honored Dray Martinez you look terrible.” She said looking at now pale Dray who looked like a zombie.

“The eye bags? Yeah The effects of omni-antidote are one hell of a way to make a zombie costume. Will have to go through full-blown blood dialysis. Damn we were lucky this gunship was in the area” Dray said as the metallic beast flew over their heads raining fire upon the forest.
“You will burn down the entire forest for what Silvarius did?” Ratmisia said looked at the terrifying flying war machine launching one missile after another.

“What? No. Well, it depends. This is clearly a pathogen of some kind. So I don’t know. Above my pay grade. This gunship is here only to provide air support until the evac will arrive.” Dray said as he sat down upon a log pointing his finger up to the skies.

“I guess you are right, Dray is nothing more than a disease those retched NoirElDems unleashed upon us.” Ratmisia said as she looked back at what was left of Silvarius’s body. It was a bloody mess. It reminded her of the body of her dead sister. But this was different, it lacked feral madness, it was just an uncaring overkill.

‘He made a choice, he went against fate, not me.’ She thought to herself as she looked at this mess yet for some reason she knew it was a lie. Then she looked at Dray who finally rose up using his rifle as a crutch. He looked like hell, exhausted, sickly, and yet victorious.

“Cmon, the fight is not over. We need to get back to see if our people still need us.” He said as he finally reloaded the rifle and straightened up. His face was still pale and sickly yet his eyes were determined and focused. His steps occasionally stumbled but he still marched forward ignoring the aches in his muscles.

‘By the Gods he poisoned himself to win, he is dying, and even after that he is on to the next task.’ Ratmisia thought to herself as she followed him, seeing this human pushing himself forward against all odds.

“Dray, why and how do your people go forward without knowing what awaits you ahead?” Ratmisia finally mastered the courage to ask Dray the question hoping to find answers to dilemmas that were tormenting her.

“What? Oh yeah, you people believe that your entire life is written out for you. Well depends on what kind of delusion you are willing to believe in but at the end of the day as long as it gives you strength to put the next foot in front of the other that’s all that matters. The same applies to you, why do you still move forward because you chose to put one foot in front of the other because you want to get from point A to point B and whether you get there or not is maybe unknown but one thing is for certain if you won’t move you definitely are not going to get there. So I guess we do it because at least it gives us a chance to get where we want. So the choice is rather simple, you just do, the rest doesn’t matter.” Dray explained calmly as he approached the grove with the bodies of those mutated cats laid all over the place along with a few bodies of his Rangers.
“Volkov, what are our casualties?” Dray said as he saw his sergeant and his men already looking for the dead.

“No civilian casualties but we have 7 dead. Better than last time at least. The kid really showed them some serious light show.” Volkov said as he nodded in the direction of Doctor Hunter who had this look in his eyes as he watched over the dead bodies. It was different from the one when he saw the dead bodies for the first time. Dray knew this look all too well, it was no longer the look of self loathing but the look that constantly wondered what he could do differently to save more lives.
‘I will need to talk to the kid about it later.’ Dray thought to himself.
“What about Doctor Fahir?” Dray asked as he looked over to Akilah who lay there devastated looking over the dead bodies.
“She is stable but she will be in need of surgery if she wants to ever walk again, Sir.” Volkov said calmly.
“Shit. I’ll go talk to her.” Dray said as he shook his head and began walking towards Akilah.
“Oh, my God Dray, you look horrible.” Akilah said as she looked upon Dray, there was a mix of guilt and utter regret on her face.
“Well better than you.” Dray chuckled trying to cheer Akilah up.

“Not funny Dray. I could’ve been in one of those body bags. You could’ve been in those body bags. Poof and that's the end of the line.” She said as she looked back at the dead bodies which were now being ziplined into the black body bags.
“Scholar Fahir I’m so sorry.” Ratmisia announced as she looked at Akilah with tears in her eyes.

“Ratmisia…” The flash of anger flashed across the Akilah's face as she looked at Ramisia and she turned away from her in shame. Akilah looked at the red stain on her shirt then at Drays sunken eyes who slowly shook his head disapproval and put his hand on Ratmisa’s shoulder. ‘It’s not her fault. Such is the reality of war there are no guarantees in it’ He moved his lips and nodded to the bodies. Akilah looked over to the body bags and the tear run down her cheek and she rubbed it of and put on a smile as she looked back Ratmisia.

“I’m glad that you are ok, you have nothing to apologize for, but you better bring some of those teas to my hospital bed.” She spoke through the stone in her throat and yet maintained the smile on her face and held off tears in her eyes.
“We can heal your injuries here, let me just find some seeds of the restoration tree. I’m not very well versed in growing them but…” Ratmisia was quickly interrupted by Akilah's hand.

“Ratmisia I’m not going into your trees. I would rather go under the knife in the hospital than put my trust in your medicine after what I have seen.” Akilah said with notes of anger in her voice, if she could not hate the people she would hate their ways.
The entire scene could’ve been easily observed from the sensor pod on top of the Super HighLander gunship relaying information over to the set of four PDAs on the other planet.
Dan: Well good thing you had that gunship patrolling the area.
X-Ray: What you really thought I’d leave my little project all up to the chance.
Paragon: They are living sentient creatures and some of them are our own people X-Ray.
Edge: We are supposed to push the envelope, we are here to test waters to see how this whole fate business works and whether it can be affected and how it is affected.
The Scar On the Soul of Humanity

King Maras of Resopolis walked through human encampment, his golden armor drawn glances even out of those humans. They were strange people so desperately trying to suppress their nature but then again Miala told him that they were the best warriors of the Steel Eagle or as they called it, the United Earth Federation. He himself an acolyte of temple of Res understood it all too well the better warrior gets less emotions he shows but those humans seemed to try really hard to be like automatons. They certainly seem to run things like well-oiled mechanisms, that’s for sure. If he was not the king, he would definitely be unable to walk so freely across their encampment and even then he had to be assigned escort. They said it was required so that he would not get run over by one of their machines and cause a diplomatic incident. Their structures looked like metal tents and all were so similar as if they were made by the same mold. What was even more surprising they managed to build a small city in but a day out of those metal tents.

A soldier that was assigned to escort him pointed at this strange metal tent that was about 3 times bigger than any other. Some other human soldiers carried a huge box into this tent and that’s when he saw General Jake Abramson. First time he met him, he was wearing more elaborate clothing than what he was wearing now as matter of fact this human general was wearing the same strangely patterned cloth as other soldiers. Human general did not notice him as he followed in human soldiers carrying a strange box into the tent.
“What is this General?” Maras said in broken English as he entered the tent and saw a human general staring intensely at the opened box. He seemed rather hateful with its contents.

“This… This is a nuclear device, King Maras.” Jake said not to take his eyes away from the nuclear weapon with the name Ragnorok written on it and 50MGT ratting engraved right beneath the symbol of radioactivity right next to the turned off screen.

“So you brought the most powerful of your weapons.” Maras said as he approached to finally get a glimpse of this weapon. Aside from writings on it which he could not understand, it looked like a box of some sort itself with a bunch of differently colored wires running all across it. Maras was actually rather disappointed; he expected at least some lights running all around; instead it was just this boring box with a screen on it.
“I have no intention of ever using it.” Jake said as if he was refusing the deal with the devil and closed the box and finally took his eyes away from the box and looked at Maras.
“Why not?” Maras said with sudden realization that there was indeed more to this box than he expected.

“Have you ever watched an entire city be wiped out and millions of lives snuffed out in a matter of seconds but with a single move of a hand?” Jake said, returning his gaze back to the coffin of death as he put his hands around his waist.
“I can’t say I have.” Maras said slowly after considering the words he just heard.

“Well I did.” Jake said as he pointed in the direction of the door on the other side of the room within this tent and began walking towards it. Maras quickly picked up on the fact that this human general was not going to discuss this matter here among the rest of his people and was indeed inviting him to his personal room. As they approached that room made out of some manner of glass, Maras could see the metal table with screen on two chairs in front of that table and one behind it, there also was a weapons rack and a couple of other metal cases inside. Jake was first to enter and pointed to the chair while he himself sat down behind the screen. With a couple of touches of the screen the transparent walls of the room quickly turned murky.
“So you’ve seen this weapon in action?” Maras said the moment no one could see into the transparent room as his armor with metallic zing collapsed on the metal chair.
“Yes I did.” Jake said as he opened one of the cabinets under the table and pulled out a bottle of whiskey with two shots of glass.
“So is that bad.” Maras said as he was staring at the brown transparent spirit filling in the glass container in front of him.

“We developed this weapon about a thousand years ago and used it in anger of war for the first time only to finally realize if this is what total war will look like we can no longer wage it. For it would end us.” With these words Jake raised his glass of whiskey as if indicating that now they were drinking. Maras did the same and they both took a sip of oak infused alcohol at the same time.
“What happened?” Maras said as he leaned back ready to hear a story.
“1945 one of the nation states of old Earth dropped 2 atomic bombs on the other nation state, just sparklers compared to what I have here.” Jake pointed in the direction of the door.

“Sure other methods of destruction, warring nations used during that war brought about far greater devastation and loss of life but they had to be used en masse and over a long period of time and this weapon was so devastating, so powerful that we recoiled from using it in anger ever again.” Jake stopped there for a second and then decided to take a bigger gulp of the spirit.

“Those who stood right at the epicenter of the explosion were atomized on spot and those that were a couple hundred meters away only left burned marks on the walls and those were the lucky ones cause those who survived initial blast now were facing slow and painful death from radiation sickness. In the following 4 months it was estimated that up to 220 thousand people have died from both bombings.” That’s when Maras, as if by instinct, looked back at the closed door and took a bigger gulp himself.

“The waring nation despite its fanatical zeal surrendered shortly after that for it now it was no longer weapon of war it was tool of absolute destruction and no amount zeal could stand to the power gods that was now in the hands of the opposing force” Jake said as he stared at the bottom of the shot of glass with whiskey in it.

“That would make sense, I guess this nation conquered your planet shortly after.” Maras leaned forward trying as he finished his glass of spirit already feeling somewhat tipsy. To which Jake just chuckled and down his shot. Then he grabbed the bottle with a gesture offered to refill the glass of his guest. Maras nodded.

“No it didn’t you see we humans are a crafty bunch and within 4 years’ other nation had the same weapon and quite an interesting time followed. A time that is called a Cold War.” Jake said as he first refiled the glass of a guest and then his.

“A time of perpetual fear, a time when you had to trust that the other side was not mad enough not to end the world despite all the ideological differences, not to mention the ambitions to rule the world. All the while trying to one up the other. It really looked like two boys fighting for the hearts of women without actually beating the living shit out of one another.” Jake said with a laugh and Maras joined in a laugh only to wash it down with another sip.

“Some say those weapons saved more lives than they have taken, finally proving Gatling right. Make a weapon terrifying enough and people would no longer dare to go to war. Too bad for Gatling it was not a machine gun.” Jake said, shaking his head with a smile looking down at the table his glass raised in hand.

“But that was more than a thousand years ago you couldn’t have possibly been alive during those times.” Maras focused his eyes on the human general. Could it be that they lied about their short life spans because surely this human general was not lying about his experience with this terrifying weapon.
“You do know we humans had a big war about 20 years ago.” Jake said as he looked to the side as if remembering something traumatic.

“The Dargorian War. The war in which your kingdom and other human kingdoms fought against the kingdom of Dargoria. They were like cousins to you for they changed themselves to become stronger and because of that waged war against the rest of human kingdoms.” Maras still was not quite sure about all the details of the conflict; hell he could not completely understand how human nations differed from one another.

“Dargorian Ascendancy, a breakaway colony of Earth. It was one of the few worlds that did not face technological and cultural degradation of the Great Divide. Instead they focused on mass genetic enhancement of their population and built their entire society on the idea of a perfect human.” Jake was speaking as if he was reciting a paragraph from a historic textbook. Maras looked utterly lost in what he just heard, there were a bunch of words he still did not understand. Jake noticed that than looked towards the glass of whiskey in his hand and shrugged before taking as sip as if maybe that would help him explain things to Maras

“I mean today's humans from technologically advanced worlds are quite different from the humans that first left Earth.” Jake said as he pointed his finger up to the skies as if Earth was somewhere up there.

“Like take a look at us citizens of United Earth Federation, we have government mandated gene therapies and enhancements as part of Federal Medical care. We have more robust organs, antiseptic blood and immunity to most viruses on a genetic level.” Jake once again noticed that Maras was quite lost with all those strange terms.

“Basically we live longer and are not quite as affected by the diseases.” Jake finally gave up on trying to sound smart as he spread out his arms with a glass of whiskey in his right arm. That’s when Maras nodded in understanding and finally dared to take a sip.

“Kolistians have even further modifications, which also include cosmetics and practice rejuvenation. Corporations well it’s all about the money in Corporate Confederacy you can pay for it you can have it and plenty do it is one of the major businesses there. And then you have Dargorians” Jake pointed his index finger of hand with which he held the glass of whiskey at Maras and then took a sip.
“So how different are they from you?” Maras leaned forward ready to take up more information.

“Even before the war we knew that they could live up to 300 years without even practicing rejuvenation like the Kolistians did. Their immune system was not just enhanced but was completely different from the rest of us, they never had cancer, they were twice stronger than ordinary humans and could recover from even harshest wounds, hell they could regrow their limbs back and they were clever but lacked empathy whatsoever. They considered that it is better to have imprinted conditioning than to have empathy.” Jake leaned backwards as he put down the glass staring at the table.
“But they were not automatons right?” Maras asked not exactly sure what to make of Dargorians.
“Oh definitely no.” Jake raised his eyes back to Maras.

“They still could have emotions, have ambitions and desires; they just couldn’t empathize with others. They had pride and really looked down on anyone who was not Dargorian. But if they looked down on us, the technologically advanced nations, they were disgusted by the New-Terran Empire and Church of Light. ‘Degenerative blight on the human genome that must be cleansed’ with those words they began their crusade.” Jake reached for the glass of whiskey to take another sip.
“A single fucking planet brought to the knees two of the biggest empires across human territories” Jake took another sip as he was shaking his head in disbelief of what he just said.
“Was this a war of conquest?” Maras said not exactly sure what Jake meant by the word ‘Cleansed’.
“It was a war of extermination” Jake leaned forward and looked directly into the eyes of Maras.
“What?” Maras said in utter horror.

“And it wasn’t done through biological weapons, or any other weapons of mass destruction. After all, they wanted those planets for themselves and bio-weapons can have unforeseen consequences for everyone involved.” Jake said as his gaze returned back to the table still trying to make sense of all the madness that happened.
“They would destroy the military force sent to oppose them, take the cities, round up the civilians and begin systematically executing them.” Jake slowly reached for the shot of whiskey

“I guess… I guess it is in our genome to kill anything that just looks somewhat different from us. I mean in prehistoric times we did wipe out just about any other hominids that proved to be less advanced than us while not being so cruel to animals.” Jake took a good gulp of whiskey still looking down on the table, his thoughts trying to make sense of the horrors he has witnessed.
“That's disturbing news General.” Maras reached for his ear with fear in his eyes after all Eldems did look a lot like humans but were somewhat different.
“Relax we put a lot of effort in suppressing that aspect about ourselves, well at least Federation and Kolistians did.” Jake extended his hands at a stop sign with a laugh.
“Good to hear what happened next.” Maras said still somewhat suspicious of a human in front of him.

“We just couldn’t sit by and let the genocide happen so we declared war on Dargorian Ascendancy and I participated in that war. Special ops grunt thinking yeah I’m so badass I gonna show those genetic uber freaks what real man looks like.” Jake hit himself in the chest.
“Boy was I wrong.” Then his gaze once again returned to the table.

“We suffered heaviest losses, for every Dargorian Clone Soldier we killed we had to give 10 but we had a better industry, more ships, more tanks, more strike crafts, more gunships, more rounds and outnumbered them 20 to 1. Still it was fucking meat grinder for both sides” Jake said as he put tha hand over his face.

“There was moment during the war when caught myself on the thought why the fuck are we not using nukes on those evil bastards. What was also quite surprising was that neither did Dargorians. I mean surely this was war on such a level that warrants such destruction and boy was I wrong again.” Jake said as he once again looked in the direction of the door.
“Who used them first?” Maras looked back at the door as well.
“They did.” Jake finished his glass of whiskey and poured himself more. Maras still had his glass half full.
“We finally reached their planet. That was it. We were going to put an end to this madness.” Jake spoke through his teeth not even noticing Maras was still there.

“I was tasked with taking out the Apex, the head of state of Dargorian Ascendency. The Apex was cooked up in the bunker surrounded by his top super soldier’s, genetic freaks who exchanged their longevity for further genetic modifications to make them even better soldiers. Me and my unit the Special Orbital Service. Unlike Delta’s my unit specialized and still does in careful planning rather than rapid response but I digress. We were tasked with taking Apex into Federal custody to face justice for his crimes.” Jake took a sip of whiskey and then put it on the table with anger somewhat spilling the contents of the glass.

“We fought tooth and nail to bag the bastard but when we reached his office we were hearing gunshots. We busted open the doors just about as this genetic freak was about to kill a Apex daughter holding on to a 5 year old boy while the bastard himself was already dead behind a computer screen. So sure we filled the freak full of lead but at the end of this firefight I was the last one standing. Then I went to check if it was like his maniphesto on his computer” Jake said, looking at Maras.

“And I’m seeing a fuckin countdown all the while half of the entire Federal military was making planetfall. Worst part, coms were scrambled. The daughter begins screaming that her father went completely mad in the past few weeks. Unable to face his defeat he ordered nukes all over the planet. That Dargoria will never be subservient to lesser creatures. Then when he heard that our SOS team went after him he ordered his family to be brought to him. They had the final meal then he went to his computer and initiated the Nuclear Cleansing. She says we need run but I know that we can’t make it to our dropship in time when I realized he had the fucking 50 megaton nuke under his table.” Jake said as he pointed his hands downwards.

“I manage to disable it just in time but then I feel the earth shake. I in panic go through his computer hoping that there is some remote disable but then I see that he had surveillance access all across the planet. So I use it to see what is happening outside. Just about every fucking camera was destroyed but there were few that were still operational mainly around the bunker.” Jake spoke trying to not put too much emotion in what he was saying but his eyes began blinking rapidly.
“What did you see?” Maras said, already damning himself for his curiosity.

“The blue skies turned into the shade of brown, the vegetation all around was in flames, while a couple of dozen fiery mushroom clouds could be easily seen on the horizon. Than two fucking dropship fall next to the camera one was ours the other was Dargorian. I see two soldiers crawl out, their faces melted, even the advanced genetics of Dargorians could not repair the damage that was done and the Federal soldier well no amount of advance of armor could protect him from this much radiation. We landed a force of over 10 million on the planet, then there were about a billion Dargorian civilians along with about 200 thousand clone soldiers. All dead in a matter of seconds. As far as I was aware me, his daughter and that boy were the only people that were still alive on this planet.” Jake said and finished his glass of whiskey.
“By the Gods…” Maras said as he slowly put the glass of whiskey still in utter horror of what he just heard.

“Yeah as I said it is a weapon of absolute destruction and I have no intention to ever use it. So we had to be cooked up in that bunker for a couple of days and the most fascinating part I’ve witnessed is how much this girl was protective of the boy. Apparently this boy was supposed to be the next Apex of Dargorian Ascendency. The next step in the tank bred human perfection.” Jake said mockingly.
“Tank bred?” Maras said in confusion.

“Oh yeah, your people are probably not very familiar with artificial breeding. Well it means that he was not born of mother’s womb but was made in the glass vial through the use of technology” Jake said somewhat shaky from alcohol.

“You should have ended that abomination right there and then. You don’t spare the life of dragon hatchling because once it will grow up it will be almost impossible to stop and it will reign only death in its wake” Maras said in disapproval.

“I ain’t going to lie I did have the same thoughts but I'm a Federal soldier, we don’t kill little kids just because they might turn into monsters later and the girl would not even let me look in the direction of the boy without reprimanding me. Quite hilarious to be called out by a member of the nation of genocidal maniacs as a heartless monster that forced her people to take such drastic action.” Jake actually smiled as he said that.
“She was delusional.” Maras said as he put down his glass of whiskey.

“It took time but eventually she came to terms with the mistakes of her people. It is kind of heartwarming to see her being all judgmental or less fortunate and yet always participating in all manner of charities. Really put a dent on our budget.” Jake said with a smile as he looked up as if this woman was up there.
“Our budget?” Maras leaned forward in disbelief
“Hey she was hot, like it or not Dargorians did nail the cosmetics part.” Jake leaned backwards with smiling hands crossed and Maras shook his head smiling as well.
Paladin

“Master Casas I still do not understand why should we be interested in the affairs of the Xenos?” The young man asked his mentor in front of him as both ridded upon the horses across rocky path covered in small vegetation. The young man’s master was covered in heavy plate armor with an ornate longsword on his hip and large shield on his back. The armor was covered in all manner of gold engravings with two crosses forming a version of 8 pointed star on the chest. The young man clearly had far less heavy armor covering him as matter of fact it seemed like the master of this man should have been even too heavy to carry around.

“Young prince Carlos Sancasant, please recount to me The Book of Ike, chapter of First Light the 12th word of Ike” The voice echoed through the metal of the helmet. It was a gentle voice and yet resolute.

“As a man chosen by light of angels I shall be its herald and as I bring light to you, you must bring the light to others.” Carlos recounted the words which he learned by hard as he laid his hand on the book hanging off his belt to the right of him.

“Good and what is the duty of the king?” The experienced knight said as his helmet moved from side to side scanning the terrain around him. The sun was high in the sky and would have heated up the metal of the armor quite fast yet the mighty knight seemed to pay no attention to it, if anything it was but walk in the park compare to the pilgrimage to the Rock of Ike he had to take in order to become the Paladin of the Word of Ike.

“I am the herald of light and you Acosta Sancasant, shall be the king to govern the affairs of people, for one cannot be the herald and ruler of people at the same time. For your duty is to build and protect the kingdom of light for mine is to bring out the light in the soul of the people including yours. The First King word of Ike 87” Carlos spoke those words with greatest trepidation and clear burden in his voice.

“Good, you might make an excellent king just follow my advice as Acosta heeded to the teachings of Ike.” With a clank of metal Casas looked at the young prince as his voice once again resonated through metal with kindness and reassurance. Carlos looked at his master and after taking a deep breath his eyes were once again filled with faith despite the heat of the sun weighing down on him.

The two walked out to the rocky ground terrain to reveal a huge valley unveiled before them leading into the sea. The vegetation covered most of it but it was clearly not the kind of vegetation that lived off the rain but rather of the humidity that came off from the sea
“Master Casas look, Xenos and they have prisoners!” Carlos shouted out the moment his eyes laid upon a group of strange humanoid people leading an even larger group of other humanoids in chains.

“Those are not prisoners, young prince, those are slaves. Stay here this is not the fight where you must take the risk without knowing what it is” The voice of Casas changed from gentle to commanding as he reached for the shield at his back.

“Angels spoke to me when I chose to protect the innocent in spite of my feebleness. The Journey of Ike. The 15th word of Ike. I shall not shy away from my duty Master Casas” Carlos reached out for his sword as well ready to charge in along with his master.

“You shall do as I tell you, young squire and until you are knight the maters of war are up to me to command. Do not be a fool Acosta your life is valuable and it is the sin of pride that compels you to throw away your senses. First King word of Ike 147.” Casas rapidly rode right in front of the young prince as his eyes flared up in bright yellow light.
“As you wish Master Casus” Young prince sheathed his sword back as he looked down, somewhat unhappy.

“If I shall die today young prince I want you to return and tell of our journey and say to all for death came to me for I acted out of hubris and not faith. For man can be wrong and Faith in Light cannot.” Paladin said as he turned his horse towards the group of Xenos. Shield in left hand and ornate longsword with the diamond in its handle shining bright in the right.
“As you command Master Casus.” Young prince said, still not sure what it all meant, as with the clanking of metal Casas charged in and his armor seemed to shine not from the sun but from within.

“As Ike commanded no man or thinking creature shall be forced into anything but shall be taught the virtue of light of Angels! I DEMAND YOU RELEASE THEM HEATHENS OR FACE THE WRAITH OF LIGHT!” Casa spoke as he stopped right before the group of those humanoids. He spoke in English yet they resonated with strange light and though the words were alien to them the meaning of it by some manner of magic were clear.
“Who is that fool that speaks in TerraDem tongue?” The NoirElDem witch moved forward to face this strange armored man who just dismounted from some manner 4 legged beast of burden.

“Mistress Tavaria this TerraDem speaks with power of Adevian light.” The hooded NoirElDem crossbowman said in some trepidation. The words of this TerraDem spoke to the very soul of his and somehow he knew that those words came with the blessing of Adevian Gods.
“Impossible Adevian Gods are long lost along with the Ancient ones.” Tavaria said with anger as the lighting crackled through her hands.

“My patience diminishes! Do as I command.” Casas spoke once again as the bright yellow light flashed in his eyes and some barely visible mist began emanating from his sword. Tavaria looked at the armored human rather startled by his audacity. ‘They were an army and he was alone, did he just lose all his senses?’ She thought to herself.

“This bounty is ours by the right of conquest. As shall you be, fool. Adevian Gods as any gods hold no power over us! ATTACK THIS FOOL!” She shouted out as she threw her hand in the direction of the armored human. The lighting came out of her hand and disbursed all around him as if his armor was protected by some strange field.

“So be it. By The Word of Ike the light protects me!” Casas shouted out putting his shield forward as the rain of energized bolts from the crossbows of NoirElDem archers could not even lay a scratch upon it. The NoirElDem witch was utterly surprised by such displays of magic she never saw or heard of among humans. She began walking back, raising her own magic shield around her.

“With faith as your shield and righteousness as your sword smite down those who impede the will of light for you are the warrior of light.” Casas spoke as he was holding his sword and all of sudden some manner of light began expanding out of it. With one swing of it he slashed across all the archers. The light of what appeared to be a giant hard light blade extending from the blood of archers spattered on to the ground. As he slashed at them some of the energized bolts hit him in the armor and he was forced to step back and then he stepped forward once again.
Then a huge creature with a bovine head charged at him, it was twice his size. by the looks of it was not alone and it seemed to pay little attention to the slashing wound on it.

“Devotion is what gives me strength!” He shouted out as he stepped forward putting his shield forward and though impact forced him back but he was not knocked to the ground and the other two minotaur’s charged into him, once again pushing him further back yet he stopped them dead in their tracks.

They began landing mighty blows at him with their giant axes that could easily carve out and break any shield or armor yet his shield would not break. He moved and parried their blows with his sword and shield as if he was not wearing any armor. His moves clearly showed his clear superiority with the blade over the huge minotaurs. One by one he disposed of the creatures, cutting one in half, while piercing the heart of the other and finally decapitating the last one.

“FIND WEAKNESS IN HIS ARMOR!” The Tavaria shouted out once witnessing as this armored warrior of TerraDems was laying waste to her forces. That’s when shadowy mists blinked around her as the shadow dancer’s daggers in hand rushed in for the kill. He began slashing at the shadowy mists of those shadow dancers yet even his superb blade work was not fast enough to land a strike. The sparks of daggers grinding across the armor of his did little to cause him any harm even though they were trying to slit his neck and arteries. It was but a matter of time the blade of them would find its way through his armor.

“With light I dispel darkness” He finally shouted out, hitting his sword into the ground. The light from the diamond in the handle of his sword engulfed him and threw the shadow dancers back literally ripping them out of their shadow forms. Without hesitation he raised his blade upwards.

“The light pierces through the darkness of any heart!” He shouted and from the tip of his sword 5 light bolts appeared hitting the 5 shadow dancers right into their chest leaving but scorched holes in their hearts.
“REPENT SINNER!” Casas shouted out as pointed his sword at Tavaria who now was all alone.

“I’m no subject to your faith. Now you fool shall witness the might of NoirElDem providence!” Tavaria’s eyes flared up with blue light as blue mist from the gems on her belt enveloped her and fireballs began forming out of her hands. She threw her hands in the direction of the knight and the two glowing balls of super-heated plasma hurled towards him. Casas was barely fast enough to put his shield in front of him as explosions born of huge differences in temperatures enveloped him.
Tavaria smiled as she stared at the wave of fire expanding towards her and hitting her shield. Yet as the smoke began clearing out as some manner of light radiated outwards from it.

“My faith is stronger than your magic witch!” The voice of Casas now resonated with something else other than metal of his helmet as the smoke cleared. He was standing on one knee shield still in front of him, sword driven into the ground and the smoke coming from under his armor. Through immense pain of being cooked alive inside of his own armor Casas rose and pressed on.

“As the light of angels mend the soul so it with this light mends the wounds.” He mumbled to himself taking one step after another as light was radiated out of the openings in his armor. He was regenerating his body as it was cooked up at the same time.

“Will you just die!” Tavaria stepped backwards as now she began forming a giant ice shard with her hands. With a whip crack noise, she lunged it as a spear at the paladin who once again raised his shield. Casas was dragged back as the ice shard pierced both him and his shield right through. He once again dropped on his knee as Tavaria was making another ice shard to finish the job.

“Hubris shall not be my downfall.” Coughing up blood Casas mumbled to himself as he put the sword handle to the wound the ice shard left on him. His eyes rose up to look at that witch preparing to finish the job. The moment she prepared to launch another spear at him, mastering all his remaining strength, he rapidly rose up and pointed his sword right at Tavaria. Hundreds of ice shards formed mid flight plunging into his armor as the ice spear was shattered by something.

Taveria saw as her shield was shattered and she felt something in her chest. She looked down and saw the longsword handle sticking out of her chest. She looked up and saw this battered armored human covered in blood and yet still standing as her vision began turning dark.

Casas was breathed heavily as he coughed up blood as he felt the reserve of angel’s blessings was running out. Through pain he began removing his armor that was still hot from the fireball he just took. The skin was fused to the armor as he tried to remove it but he without a single moan of pain was removing it from himself. Once he finally removed his gloves the healing spell finally ran its course. The undergarments were all burned up and he still had a couple of badly burned areas on his body along with a wound that was still bleeding in his right shoulder when Carlos arrived.
“Master Casas are you all right?” Carlos rushed towards him ignoring the awe in the eyes of slaves who still were in chains.
“You remember how to heal, boy?” Casas said as he finally allowed himself to collapse on the ground.
“Yes of course.” Carlos said as without any hesitation he put his hands on the wound of Casas and with light began regenerating it.
Casas stared into the skies as he saw something. Something that made him afraid.
“Secularists, the agents of pride!” He said in horror as he realized what he was seeing was the drone.

“Are you seeing this?” Major Raymond Crawford said to his second in command as he was staring at his PDA looking at how a single paladin just took down a hunting party of NoirElDems. Him and another 3 of the Delta operators were sitting in their specialized dropship.
“Fanatics are already here?” Captain Farnsworth said calmly as he was piloting the dropship in between the hills in order not to be seen.

“Yeah. Get the railguns ready and other anti-tank weapons this guy will eat any small arms fire for breakfast. We need to have a chat with the zealot and I would rather do it with a stick large enough to whack him with.” Crawford said as he began issuing commands on his PDA to other units in the area of operation. Among which were gunships, tanks, Airborne platoon and platoon of Rangers.
“Alright diverting our path, let’s hope it won’t cause too much trouble for our offensive.” Farnsworth said as he banked the dropship to the right.
“Carlos whatever you do stay silent and calm, secularists are upon us.” Casas said as rose up and saw the black dropship of Delta force appear out of the hills.
“Secularists, you mean the forces of Earth are here. But master Casas, they are the agents of pride. We must oppose those infidels.” Carlos reached for his sword looking at the dropship approaching.

“Make no mistake, agents of pride won against the agents of wraith when our faith proved not strong enough. I was there when those empires clashed and it would be an act of hubris to oppose them, right now.” Casas put his hand on the young prince's hand stopping him from drawing a sword and then looked to the dead body of the witch which collapsed on its knees, the sword still sticking out of her chest.

Within moments they were surrounded by two more dropships and jetpack soldiers of UEF when finally, the black dropship landed right in front of them and 4 men in black armor came out to confront the two.

“Well, well, well. What is the pair of fanatics doing so far away from the territories of the Church?” Crawford said with a smile on his face and an assault rifle in his hand. If Casas had his armor on that rifle wouldn’t even make a dent in him but right now he had him down with his pants.
“Spreading the Word of Ike secularist.” Casas said calmly as he extended his arm in the direction of the dead witch and all of sudden the sword materialized in his hand.

“Make a wrong move and no amount of fancy magic tricks will save you, fanatic. I have enough fire power bearing down on you, no god will be able to help you.” Crawford said calmly as he pointed the rifle at him and all other operators did the same. More than that Casas saw as a gunship and tanks appeared out of the hills. Indeed, Federals were not taking any chances with him.
“I’m not here to fight you but to spread the light to those people.” Casas said as he sheathed his sword.
“That might be against our interests here fanatic.” Crawford lowered his rifle however others did not.
“I’m not here to force the Word of Ike upon them, secularists. One cannot impose faith upon anyone unless they are willing.” Casas said as he put his hand on his heart.
“Tell it to the NoirElDems you just laid waste to.” Crawford pointed his rifle at the body of the dead witch.

“They were slavers and my faith could not let me stand by and let them drag those poor souls in chains. I believe you are here to do the same.” Casas marched towards the black armored soldier who clearly was in command here.
“We were asked to be here, you not so much.” Crawford shook his head in disapproval.

“I was brought here by my faith, a far nobler call than your self-serving interests. But as I said we should not be enemies here after all we are all humans here and we do have a common enemy here. Let us work together as we worked to defeat Dargorians so many years ago.” Casas said with a coy smile as he winked at the soldier.
“Fair point, fanatic. Ok we will let you spread your word but I expect full cooperation here and you will behave yourself. Now I need your names.” Crawford said in a commanding manner.
“My name is Ernesto Casas and this is my squire Carlos Blanch and may I ask your name secularist?” Casas tilted his head with a smile as he spoke.

“Blanch you say, ok you can keep your secrets. Welcome to the party gentlemen I'm gonna need you to escort those refugees to Elveron which is that way. I’m sure they are going to be quite interested in what you have to teach them Paladin” Crawford said as he pointed to the road in the opposite direction from which the NoirElDems were carrying slaves.
A letter home

General Jake Abramson leaned upon a railing of the wall of his fort that his man built. He stared out into the valley filled with small lakes, tropes and groves. It somewhat reminded him of the paradise from all those ancient stories as all those different aliens were slowly preparing for some manner of festival. Yet something weighed heavy on him.
“They call it an Afrodian festival, apparently even NoirElDems honor this event with ceasefire.” Colonel Eliza Snyder approached him from behind.
“Like a story of Christmas during world war 1?” Jake said as he turned his head in her direction.

“Really pulling out some ancient history sir. But yeah something like that but this seems somewhat different as well. It was supposed to be a celebration of love from what agent Tadison told me. Tadison has a theory that this way they erode the differences between the species.” She said as she leaned over on the railing, taking a good look at a moonlit valley of hundreds of aliens preparing for festivities. They clearly took their time with it. If it was up to the Federal engineering brigade, they would be able to build it all up in a matter of a day. However, everything would be standardized, efficiently lacking any personal touch. This was different; it almost appeared as if they were putting their soul into it. Not a single hut that was built out there looked the same.

“And here we are, Lizy. We were granted the best spot and what did we do? Build a bloody wall between ourselves and them.” Jake said as he looked from one side to the other of this metal 5-meter-tall wall. There were watch towers manned by soldiers with sniper rifles along with automated minigun turrets mounted on tall metal polls tirelessly scanning the perimeter. He also saw how some of the locals stared at it with strange confusion. This local was staring at the works of truly alien creatures which were strange, enigmatic and demonstrated immense power.

“Sir we are not here to mingle and participate in exploration of interpersonal relationships with the aliens. We are here to do a job and defending ourselves takes greater priority than PR, sir.” Eliza said as she looked down at that local like a hawk ready to drop down her prey. The moment the local witnessed this predatory gaze he rapidly turned away and rapidly began walking away from that gaze in fear. A smug smile appeared on Eliza's face.
“You are right, Lizy. So what is the news from the front?” Jake said, looking at the local nervously walking away.

“Well sir, being the highest ranking officers on the ground our Delta teams practically took charge of the entire war effort under your so-called Free Reign doctrine. Good thing they know how to work together otherwise we would not be able to do anything productive” Eliza said as she looked at her commander still not exactly approving of the hands off approach he decided to take in this expedition.
“Would you believe me that this was the plan from the start?” Jake looked at her with a smile on his face.
“Sir, you know full well I’m not here to question your command no matter how stupid I think it is.” Eliza chuckled.

“What I’m concerned about is that there are holes in their reports. They are well hidden but some info still bleeds through. They are doing something that they are not sharing with others.” Jake looked up and saw something that became such a common sight in the skies, a bunch of drones flying around, monitoring everything that was happening below. It was natural for him to know that almost everything that was happening was monitored but here they had no idea the sheer extent of mass surveillance state that he hailed from.

“Is that not the nature of their operations? Sir you tasked them with accomplishing a mission and that’s what they are doing in whatever way they see fit and we must trust them with that.” Eliza’s tone changed somewhat. She was as much protecting him from information as she was herself and they both knew that. They both knew that if they needed to know all they had to do is to order for Delta’s to come clean and they would but knowing meant that they would have to do something about it. Ignorance was not only a bliss but also a level of insulation.
“And what do you think? Are we in-over our heads?” Jake looked at Eliza finally willing to show what was so concerning to him.

“They sure failed to mention how really bad the situation was but we are doing ok so far. We will do it one step at a time. Let’s see how this offensive on Delfiapolis will go. If we take it, we should be finally able to establish a second base and connect to the Reslian kingdom. This should make king Maras happy to finally be able to see his kingdom again, join our forces and start kicking even more ass.” Eliza said but there was that moment of concern on her face despite that mask of bravado she was putting up.

“They did divert a lot of forces for it. Maybe after that is done we can start formulating a direction we want to take rather than leave it up to commanders in the field. Let’s enjoy the night.” Jake saw that concern on her face. That ugly thing at the back of his head was eating out at her as well. They were facing a giant interstellar empire with a small force that could barely hold a planet.
“Yes, sir.” She said as both turned away from the lake fields below to see the insides of their small fort.

The entire fort was built around a small lake surrounded by trees. Though the wall was hard to see from above the trees, the poles and the over watch towers were easily seen. Couple of prefabricated residential structures were also seen along with the supply warehouse. Even here they managed to find ways to contribute to the war effort. This place served as a supply depot and recreational facility.

He walked toward the lake feeling the fresh grass under him when he saw this big fire set up and soldiers and officers sitting on each side of it enjoying their time. Though alcohol and other recreational substances were strictly prohibited on this expedition on paper, Jake actually was somewhat glad to know that it was only on paper. The aroma of barbeque and sounds of celebrations did ease his mind.

He had a special sit prepared for himself in front of the fire and overlooking the lake behind it. As he sat down he looked to the left of him and saw officers. It was quite clear that those officers were having the same troubled thoughts as he did while soldiers to the right were remembering their fallen. He opened his PDA and decided to write a letter home. He began tapping at the touchscreen of his PDA.

**“**Dear Anastasia, I hope you are doing all right. I know you were not exactly thrilled with me going out here. During times like this I’m constantly reminded about the old times and at the same time reminded of the times even more ancient before the digital revolution. I feel like the soldiers of that old time writing this letter to you."

He put down his PDA as he looked over to the lake behind the flames of the fire. He decided to rise up and take a walk to see it unobstructed by the flames of the fire. He saw all those trees on the edges of the lake lit up by an alien moon; some of them had those glowing fruits. He approached the water of the lake and dropped his hand into it. It was cold, refreshing and so soft. He ordered his soldiers and officers to keep it clean after all they were guests here. After a minute or two he came back to his seat to continue writing his letter.

"Part of me wishes that you were here. I’ve seen things here I could not believe my own eyes. Entire spaceship size of our battleship capable of atmospheric flight or this architectural beauty of UrboSaptia, or the gardens they maintain here. Not to mention the magic they utilize provides for some spectacular shows. It is like being in holographic theater all the while being out in the open in nature."

He once again put down his PDA as one of the soldiers came in to throw a couple of logs to feed the fire. A thought crossed his mind he never saw those ElDems build fires. ‘Why would they need to when they can summon it out of their hands?’ With that thought he decided to pour himself some whiskey and took a sip then returned to his PDA.

"It is quite fascinating to see them finding ways to coexist with nature unlike us who in our pursuit for dominance build arcologies with climate control and the only vegetation we were accustomed to was the one in our botanical gardens."

He lit up a cigar with his box of matches. His eyes locked with the fire of the match as thrown it to the fire. He saw it quickly be devoured by the fire. ‘Fire it is quite mesmerizing; I guess it is part of us. We keep that fire alive and we will feed the entire galaxy to it to keep it going.’ He thought to himself as he soaked the other end of his cigar in the glass of whiskey and once again he picked up the PDA.

"But I have to say this is an alien world. Every time I see our basecamp from the walls of their city I just see how much we stand out from everything else around us. Our blockish, prefabricated and pragmatic structures seem utterly out of place here. Not to mention the culture that is so different from everything that we have seen."

He put the cigar back in his mouth and felt the tobacco soaked in the sharp taste of whiskey. He took a puff and then released a couple of rings. Then he began staring into the fire once again. The flame danced violently slowly devouring the wood in it. It devours everything, eventually leaving behind nothing but sterilized remains, like metal and ash. It indeed had this cleansing effect, an effect that humans learned so well. ‘You want to see what is true put it through fire and see what remains’ He thought to himself so he returned back to his PDA with the cigar still in his mouth.

"They are fascinating people. I’m constantly reading reports from our civilian experts and it is mind baffling. We as people at some point have to face a simple question: what is the meaning of our lives and then face a reality that we have to find our own. They not so much, apparently they have this strange pantheistic religion where the destiny of each person is already predetermined. So they tend to live in each moment as if living out their lives as actors on the stage. At least that is what CID agent Tadison writes in his evaluations. The way he puts it though the outcomes of the play are already determined they still get to choose how to live it out. As for our opposition, Tadison says that this conflict seems to be more theological in kind rather than an ethical or a practical one. At least it is to our allies and they are losing it badly."

He was tapping on the touchscreen of his PDA relentlessly as the cigar was slowly burning up in his mouth. Then he stopped, put down the PDA and looked at the glass of whiskey he had. Without hesitation he drank it all in one go feeling the fiery liquid going down his throat. He put the cigar back into his mouth and returned to his PDA.

"The thing is when we came here the war was already lost and we just did not know it yet. Now that more and more information comes in, the entire command staff begins to realize how bad it actually is. We came when the Soviets were already invading Berlin during World War 2."

He put down the PDA trying to collect himself. Then he looked over to the soldiers and heard them recounting their stories of their battles. Of how they persevered despite facing something so unusual as those horned creatures that would eat up entire mag from their rifles and keep on going. Of those blood crazed women that phased in out of existence dodging every bullet sent their way. Of those terrifying mages that could take on entire platoons with fire power comparable to that of an artillery strike.

"Sure we managed to push them back out from this planet but we are doing so barely by the skin of our teeth. Our allies think we are some manner of unstoppable force that lays waste to everything but the truth is they just have no idea how close most of those engagements are and that the only advantage we have so far is the element of surprise and that we are rather competent with our actions. In many ways thanks to Crawford. I know you opposed his choice to apply for the military and quite frankly speaking I can see that your worst fears are coming true.. That his nature truly begins to surface but at the same time I cannot deny the fact that he and the other 3 are in many ways the ones responsible for our success here. Whatever that bloody business that happened to them during their training made them truly fearsome and capable soldiers and leaders. But we are still losing people and equipment. Just recently the entire armored column was put out of commission and had to be dragged back for repairs, or recent losses among our Airborne and Ranger divisions."

He looked over at his officers and saw one of them also writing something on a data pad with this heavy look on his face. He knew this look all too well, the look that he himself had to go through countless times. This officer was writing a notice letter telling the relatives how one of his subordinates died under his command.

"What is our expedition compared to an entire interstellar empire? The truth is every victory we achieve is but a small drop in an ocean and they slowly chip away at us. I fear that we won’t be able to meet our mission objective here and I’m dealing with troubling thoughts. I think we must just scrap the whole endeavor but at the same time I’m not sure I can bring myself to do so. We rekindled hope in our allies and I just can’t bring myself to snuff it away from them just like that. Well, maybe just maybe if this next operation will prove to be successful I might see at least some path to victory or maybe a stalemate."

The cigar in his mouth was running out and he looked over at the things he wrote back to his wife. He outlined the whole thing. His finger hovered over the delete command but then he remembered something. She was there with him at that damned bunker.
Love, Jake.”
He punched in the command to be uploaded to the server and then to be sent along the thousands of other letters to be uploaded to the servers on the next supply ship that would be heading back home.
Dark Ritual

The four figures stood in the dark run down parking lot. They all were dressed in the black parade uniform with ribbons on their chests and their ranks of lieutenants on their right shoulder and Delta insignia on the left shoulder.

“Ok as you asked Lexington. I brought my one hundred bill from my first salary, my certificate of finishing the Delta Induction Course, my West Point Academy diploma as well as my application letter to the West Point Academy and Federal flag. So care to explain what it’s all about.” James Lockheed announced showing the box filled with all manner papers with the Federal dollar bill on top of it.
“James, what we went through in Siberia put us under the microscope from IDA.” Raymond Crawford spoke calmly as he also was holding the box filled with the same materials as Lockheed's box.
“What happened there was fucked up and I rather not revisit it.” James talked back to his brothers in arms trying to suppress the memories of those dark couple of weeks.

“We are as much guilty of what happened there as all those that did make it happen. We killed our brothers in arms as well and all for what so that we can call ourselves Delta operators.” Robert Lexington explained to his brother in arms with a stone cold expression on his face.

“We were not to fire the first shot, we did what we had to do to survive.” That’s when Daniel Farnsworth spoke, still recovering from the wound he received during his final week of that unholy program he put himself through.

“We all responsible, we should have reported about what Zeiplits did the moment we heard about the battle royal with convicts.” Lexington looked at Daniel not quite happy with what needed to be done himself.
“We could have not known that they would command us to shoot one another by the end of it.” Daniel spoke in denial clutching his box of what he considered his most prized achievements.

“It was the final logical conclusion Daniel. I mean take a look at what they were teaching us in the non-combat classes. Philosophy of superior humans, mind of serial killers, subjective morality, psychological warfare, combat meditation and dehumanization of our enemies. Signs were all there, the final test would be duking it out between one another and we passed with flying colors.” Crawford spoke as he stared into his box, probably even more prideful of its contents than Daniel.
“He is right Daniel; we have to be honest with ourselves, we all wanted to prove ourselves so bad we let it happen.” Lokheed spoke as he stared into the distance.

“Ok fine we are the bad guys as much as Magrov was. I can live with what it has to do with this thing you are about to do.” Daniel still was not fully onboard as to this stupid ritual they were about to partake on.

“We cannot let ourselves fall into the same mindset anymore, we must stop making excuses for ourselves. We must face one simple fact.
