Going further we make those decisions not anyone else '' Crawford finally turned his gaze away from his box and towards Daniel, it was the same look Daniel observed when Crawford finally squeezed the trigger to put down Magrov to save Daniel's life.
“We must set a blaze on everything and everything that we gained from it.” Lexington pulled out four black zippo lighters with red anarchy logo on it then gave it out to his brothers in arms.

“You mean symbols of it.” Daniel took the lighter as he put the box down. He stared at the red anarchy sign on it which reminded him of the chaos he went through, it was part of him now and he could not deny that.

“Yes to rid ourselves of the gods that command us and take control of our lives from idols we worshiped” Crawford opened the lighter with the signature metallic cling, rolled the igniting wheel and the gentle petrol flame of the lighter reflected in his gaze. The first thing he set to flame was the Dollar bill as the last thing he did was for the money. Lexington burned the flag because it was the greatest thing he valued in the box. While Lokheed burned the Delta Induction Course certificate as it was the most scary experience in his life so far. Daniel still looked at it all like some manner of mad ritual. However the dancing flames in the eyes of his brothers in arms seemed as they burned the last vestiges of weakness out of their souls.

“Fuck it, burn it all.” Farnsworth did not start with a single item, he set the entire box on fire. At first he turned away with pain cutting through his heart but then he looked back at it and could no longer turn away. His hand clutching to the lighter realizing now he was ready to set everything a blaze if need shall arise and with this thought there was nothing he feared anymore.
The Assault on Delfiapolis.
The plan.

Captain Daniel Farnsworth sat on the tree log observing something on his PDA. His composite armor with adaptive camouflage blended him in with the surrounding environment making him difficult to spot if you were not looking for him. The screen on his PDA clearly showed that he was using a strange app with a red symbol of anarchy on it. A special application that he shared with the other 3 members of his Delta Force brothers. But not just any Delta operators but those other 3 operators that went through the same hell of a selection process as he did. They were planning out the assault on the city of DelfiaPolis a key strategic point. A port city sitting right in between several portals leading to multiple other planets. A chokepoint that at very least would unite them with allied armies spread out in the other systems.

Dan: Ok just as I expected they had enough of us picking off their forces piece by piece and as I expected they decided to concentrate it in one place. They do seem to learn that shields are the only protection from heavy weapons but we anticipated it. I still wonder why they don’t try to gain air dominance. I mean for all the medieval ways they usually have some manner of counterpart to our weapons.
Edge: Dips on the Dragon kill!
X-Ray: We still have yet to see one. Most likely they don’t want to show all their cards as well.
Paragon: Hey Dan you sure you don’t want to command a squad of your own?
Dan: I’m good, Rob. I prefer to be just pointed in the right direction besides it always fun to see Ray trolling the other 2.

X-Ray: They are not us Dan. As good as soldiers as they are, they don’t know what it’s like to go up against an equally competent opponent which knows you as good as you know yourself. So yeah I have to keep them on their toes.

Paragon: Seriously though, how did they take that thing you pulled off with that hunting party? Still not sure why you had to do it. Do we really need them terrified of us like we are some kind of spawns of hell?

X-Ray: They followed orders, they know what they signed up for when they applied for the Delta Force. As for whether it was necessary, the answer is yes. Those people are superstitious, and if Tadisons is right for a good reason. This world operates by different rules than ours. They have heroes and villains here playing out their roles and all wrapped in myths and legends. The creation of narratives is the way we can control the outcomes.
Edge: Is that the PR department? We are here to blow shit up not to tell stories.

X-Ray: Yes we are, James, but the situation is different here. Their entire lives are built around the narratives weaved by those Extra-Dimensional entities for a story and well we are not supposed to be in it. So we get to set the narratives ourselves, play the roles of our own choosing. And as such, I chose the role of a demon.
Paragon: Why?

X-Ray: They must think and believe that we are the retribution for all their crimes. They must fear us and we all know that a scared opponent is prone to panic and as such is prone to irrational decision making. There is no easier prey than a scared one.
Edge: Well it is fun to see them flee in terror but I have to say I prefer to play at the edge of it. You know when you bring your best and know that this is what it takes to win.
Dan: Really, James, you always make it look so easy.
Edge: Yeah well what can I say things have been boring.
Paragon: What about that witch you met in the woods after laying waste to another of their hunting parties.
X-Ray:Asteria? I’m not sure yet, Rob, but there is something strange about her.
Paragon: You mean that she has an ear of the High Councilor Miala?

X-Ray: More like a relationship and she clearly has a relationship with our enemy as well, yet it appears to be that she rather keep her being alive a secret from a select few on both sides. Oddly enough she knew of our pact.
Paragon: The narratives, you think something is bigger at play here?

X-Ray: We are dealing with Extra-Dimensional entities in this conflict that write out the lives of the locals. Also, the reason why I deleted the files out of the archives. I think this is the matter that we should get to the bottom of it first before informing the high command.
Paragon: Well your choices are yours. So after the bombing run how do we want to go with it? Dan, you laid out the trap yours to finish it.

Dan: Already ahead of you Rob. Rangers will storm the city under the cover of the gunships they will proceed to open up the gates and make breaches within the walls from the inside to open up the path for tanks. Once they rendezvous with the armor they will proceed to systematically clear it out of the hostile street by street.

Dan: Since we want to keep the city more or less intact just dropping the bomb on the command center won’t do. Yet we still want to paralyze their command structure ASAP. As such that’s where the Alfa Strike group comes into play. Ruskies will not wait for the other forces and potentially will let themselves get surrounded as they rush for the command center. They are already inside of the city and will commence the assault the moment bombs will go off.
Dan: Lastly, we want to cut off their retreat and tie down their reinforcements from the sea. That's where SOS comes into play. They will sabotage their ships and lay traps for reinforcements.
Paragon: It is a sound plan but it is rather complex with a lot of parts required for it to go right.
Dan: That’s where we come in.
X-Ray: 3 squads of 4 men?
Dan: Yes, this is what we are here for after all. To tip the scale of any engagement in the favor of the Federal military.
Paragon: That’s gonna be a lot of work.
Edge: I ain’t complaining.
X-Ray: Ok so 3 roles to cover.
Drag: Yes, we need someone for the interception, the rapid response, and the spearhead. So who wants the spearhead?

X-Ray: Well we are here to show our best not for the glory-seeking so I think Edge is best for the job. As much as I hate to admit it, when it comes to rushing the target and laying waste to everything he is the best among us.
Edge: A-game it is.
Paragon: Rapid response is on me then. Will need to work with Airborne and their jetpacks.
Dan: So interception is on us, X-ray. Will be working with SOS on this one.
X-Ray: Well we all know what we need to do.
Paragon: I’ll give a go-ahead for the bombing run the moment you are all in position, also I would like to enjoy the fireworks.
Dan: Have fun, Rob.
Daniel closed an application and put away his PDA as X-ray approached him with diving equipment in hand.
“Let’s see if those two can think on their feet.” Daniel said as he nodded in the direction of the other two operators.
“6.3, 6.4 listen up we are going swimming with SOS kids.” X-ray announced that the other two just looked surprised but only for a moment.
Paragon.
An account from the Maginus craftsman. Liberated during the assault on Delfiapolis.

I was there when you humans first came to Delfiapolis. You see, we all knew that this was the end for us. Now and forever we would be slaving away for those ElDems who chose to oppose the will of the gods. I was the last of the patch of slaves to be shipped out from the city into the inner boundaries of the NoirElDem Empire.

We were not sure why we all were sent there but the rumors were that they needed able craftsmen to build them weapons of war, and in numbers never seen in eons. It did not make a lot of sense at the time, after all, they had already won and only a few free cities remained and even they were already under siege. But then it all began making sense when I saw you arrive. When I saw him the great warrior of yours whose name I still do not know and those who know it refuse to speak it as if he committed an unspeakable crime and must atone for it. Yet he is being exalted among you. You people are strange.
But I’m moving away from the story that you came looking for. As I said, I was there when you came.

At first, we heard that there would be a grand battle as NoirElDems assembled an army of such size we never heard or seen off. However, whatever the outcome, we knew that you would be too late to liberate us. Imagine our surprise when they stared in shock when you obliterated it in a matter of moments. Yet still, we had no hope, for you either would obliterate the city or would have to siege it and by that time we would be shipped out. And once again you proved me wrong. You cared little for the high and impregnable walls as some of your ‘I warriors’ were able to fly and the rest came in those skyships of yours and some were already inside.

The explosion echoed all across the city as towers that housed mages were destroyed, and those flying warriors of yours flew over the walls like an army of hawks. Your people were dressed in strange armor painted in strange gray colors. So unusual it was to see the metal lacking its definitive glint and not to even see a hint of flesh behind it. You looked more like some manner of extremely agile automatons. But what was more terrifying were the weapons with which you fought. And how you forced the NoirElDems to change the way they fought as well. Usually, the fights would start with an exchange of projectiles and then come down to melee. You came in with all your warriors armed with those thunder staffs that made short buzzes or unleashed the fury of the volcano with an unstoppable eruption. NoirEldem archers were forced to hide in homes turning each into a small fortress to fight from as did you. The fights turned from displays of skill of melee combat into projectiles whizzing in between the buildings and mages actually forced to create shields to protect their atonable minotaurs so that they could reach you in melee range.

I was caught in between two fires as I was escorted in chains across the narrow streets. Never was I scared in my life as much as I was at that moment. I could not run for the fear I would get in the crossfire and yet I could not stay there either for this crossfire cared little where I was. The only thing I could do is to duck and be trampled under the hooves of those minotaurs as they charged into your warriors.

That’s when I saw him, he jumped out of the skies. He landed right between me and those giants as some manner of strange lightning grenades hit the shield the mage was projecting, breaking it with ease and yet not causing any damage to the surroundings. He did not hesitate even for the moment as he raised his thunder staff and began raining death on them. They were hardy, even for your deadly weapons but he was cunning and possessed the accuracy of gods. He was aiming at their legs first, more specifically at their knees.

But they were too many and managed to get in close with him. For a moment I thought what a brave fool he was and I was ashamed that he would die because of me. But I bear witness to a war god dedicated to protecting me who soon was joined by 3 other of his acolytes. They moved like they were one, whenever the minotaurs were ready for a strike they would be met by the accurate fire as they were covering for one another. All the while your other warriors kept both mage and archers pinned down, sometimes lending a hand to those elite warriors of yours. But what was even more surprising to me was that I noticed that those other warriors of yours were moving around the fight using it as a distraction to position themselves to clear out the houses of NoirElDem forces.

Within minutes the firefight was over. When I rose I stood among the dead bodies of those horned monsters. I was still in shock when I heard this strange mechanical noise coming from the human warrior.

“Are you alright?” It sounded, but I was still dumbfounded by what I just witnessed and the only thing I could think of was to tremble in fear as this automaton tried to speak to me. They exchanged words in strange tongues of yours. That should have been the first hint that behind that armor of yours was indeed flesh and blood and not some manner of avatar of a vengeful god.

This warrior looked at me and removed his helmet to reveal his face. It was the face of a true hero yet there was a sadness to him as if he had indeed seen or done something that he was not proud of, and was on a journey of some sort.

“Look at me, everything is going to be fine. We are here to help but I need you to go and hide in that house. We don’t want you to get hurt and once the fighting is done, meet one of our warriors with your hands raised.” He spoke in broken ElDemian tongue as he pointed in the direction of the house that your warriors just cleared out. Oddly enough this act made me realize that indeed you were something not to be feared, but something that was indeed very much like us even if you were so different.

But I didn't even have the chance to thank him for my life as he turned his head to that trinket of yours, that allows you to speak any tongue without a problem which now that I understand is just one of many things that it can do. He rapidly spoke commands in his tongue, put his helmet back on once again turning himself into a war god, and with streams of fire coming off his back hurried himself to another street along with his acolytes to once again save someone else’s life by jumping into the heart of it.
The following account is a stylized account of events pertaining to events during the operation of Hellenic Blitz as a subset of operations undertaken during operation Noble Cause.
Actions are focused on the account of 1st Special Operational Force Detachment Delta team Super 4.
Forces involved in the account.
1 SOFD Team Super 4
Commanding officer Major Robert Lexington codename Paragon callsign Super 4.1.
Second in Command Captain Alaric Vade callsign Super 4.2
Operator Lieutenant Ibrahim Abadi callSign Super 4.3
Operator Lieutenant Paulo Salazar callsign Super 4.4.

‘Crawford was right, they did exactly as he said they would. Amass forces to give a grand battle they even threw a shield just in case.’ Major Robert Lexington thought to himself as blue eyes looked at tens of thousands of NoirElDems amassed around dozens of pyramids projecting a shield over them. He saw about a dozen of those monstrous centipedes, hundreds of spider riders and thousands of other NoirElDem forces ready to march out against human forces.

That’s when everyone saw it. 5 giant super-sonic bombers activating their afterburners like rockets at almost vertical angle propelled themselves high into the skies and then with the boom of breaking the sound barriers they began flying towards the NoirElDem army in V formation. The lead bomber opened its bomb bay doors and dozens of missile dropped from it. Missiles at first fell behind the bomber but then overtook it as they rushed towards the shield exploding with EMP shockwaves quickly collapsing the giant shield and burning up the ether reserves of NoirElDem army. Mages in the NoirElDem army screamed in agony as their veins glowed from inside out but this was not the end of it. The other four bombers opened their bomb bay doors and unleashed hundreds of guided cluster explosives on the NoirElDem army. They had nowhere to run, nowhere to hide but to see as carpet of explosions approached them.

“Fools scatter, scatter god damn it. Don’t just stand there. You will have some chance at least” Lexington whispered to himself, seeing them just stand there in acceptance of their fate as the carpet of explosions rolled over them leaving nothing but devastation behind it.

He pulled a cigar out of his pocket and lit it up with his black lighter with a red symbol of anarchy on it as he walked out of the bush to get a better look at the sight of devastation followed by 3 other Delta operators under his command. All of it was all too familiar to him. This is how the Federation was dealing with large formations of less technologically advanced opponents back in the human territories and everyone knew that the Federation was an unstoppable juggernaut. Now those primitives for all their magical tricks were facing off against the military that was forged in fires of industrial interstellar war. There was no honor in it, just death.

‘A good soldier fights not because he hates what’s in front of him but because he cares what is behind him. If I was stupid grunt I would believe it and justify it with this but I know better, we are here to conquer and all the rest is just to fool the locals that we are liberators. Might makes right after all and today we are mighty, and that means it is on my conscience what happens next.’ He thought to himself as he puffed smokes from the cigar as he watched over the devastation. He extinguished the cigar as the dropships filled with Rangers began approaching the city and all 200 jetpack soldiers of the Airborne were already in flight executing the plan.

He put on his mask and his sight was filled with augmented reality bombarding his senses with information. Maps, objectives, state of the armor, location, and direction of movement of friends, foes and neutral entities, hell it even took into account that he just had a smoke as it monitored his body functions like heart rate, the rate of perspiration, and even what was flowing in his bloodstream. Right now it was showing that his heart rate actually lowered and his breathing steadied.

“Ok Super 4 we are here to ensure our assault goes as smoothly as possible, let's show to those slavers what the best of the best of Federation looks like. Remember we fight for one another above all else.” With those words, he activated the jetpack on his back. His armor was the most advanced piece of equipment to be given out to any soldier. Modular power armor that flawlessly integrated itself with the Icarus jet pack. He felt as his entire body experienced the G-forces from the acceleration and soared through the skies followed by 3 other operators. They would be first to fly over the wall.

As he approached the city walls he saw that its towers were already shaken with explosions, a handy work of the other Tier 1-unit Strike Group Alfa and he could see through his visor that they along with other Delta team Super 5 were rushing through the city heading for the central palace. His vision scanned the city for landing spots and there it was. A giant gray box highlighting a small square is a perfect landing spot for Rangers and later for the jetpack refueling station.

He and his operators were first to land right in the middle of it surrounded by the red highlights of confused enemies. For a moment they locked eyes with their enemies. NoirElDem archers and shadow dancers stared at the blacked-out visors of Delta operators and even before they were ready to shout out commands the bullets began flying. There was chaos all around the place as the quartet was shooting in all directions in the center of the square as Airborne soldiers were flying over raining death from above as they were making a fly by and some landed at the roofs of surrounding buildings.

Lexington not only saw where his M22 rifle was pointing as the visors overplayed something akin to the laser beam emanating from his rifle but he also saw where all other weapons were pointing. Terabytes of information were flowing all around them as computers in their equipment were communicating with one another. But more than that it was important not to overwhelm the user with too much information as such each soldier adjusted the input of augmented reality based on their own preferences. Lexington for example only adjusted his view of beams from other rifles only to show up only if they would cross the line of his sight within at least a 0.3-meter radius.

As the shootout played out Shadow Dancers appeared to be far more adept at responding to the chaos that Federals unleashed upon them and at first rushed towards the Delta operators with their blinking powers. The visor could not keep track but since their signatures were already in the database it already began highlighting the smoke trail they were leaving behind. That’s when the true combat superiority of Delta operators came into play. They were able to rapidly pick off the position where they would dematerialize. It became quite evident that the quartet not only was able to handle the sporadic attempts of archers to shoot them but also literally was untouchable as shadow dancers began collapsing as if running into the solid wall of death.

That’s when they decided to go for easier targets Airborne soldiers on top of the roofs. They blinked from side to side evading the fire from M22 rifles and the wrist-mounted machine pistols. One of the Airborne soldiers decided to disengage and jumped up into the skies as he was surrounded by the black mist only to find the enraged harpy riding on top of him rapidly stabbing him in the neck. He lost control of the jetpack and slammed into one of the buildings. The Shadow dancer managed to blink out of harm’s way just in time as flames engulfed the Airborne soldier. She rematerialized off balance as the explosion slammed her into the wall. She rolled over to her back as the ringing in her ears deafened the sounds of battle that’s seeing as human dropship passed over here raining even more death upon everything below. Yes, they managed to kill a couple of those flying humans but it was clearly a losing battle. She rose up from the ground still hazed by the explosion and saw as more humans began rappelling from the dropship all around those 4 champions of humans. They already cleared out the square and now those human soldiers clearly were taking up defensive formations to try and rush it, it would be suicide, besides she was already coughing up blood. She decided to run for the port; this city was clearly lost.
“We lost five here for over a hundred confirmed kills. The ratio is 1 to 20” Captain Alaric Vade said calmly as his visor indicated that 5 soldiers were killed.
“To the skies, we need to see where resistance is fiercer,” Lexington said as he looked up.

‘Some would say that this is a spectacular victory but still, 5 people died and it happened here on my watch. Then again all cannot be saved’ Lexington thought to himself as he once again soared to the skies along with the other 4 soldiers. He looked below and one of the ranger squads was having trouble at the wall. The plan was to blow it up to create a path for Titan tanks to enter the city for heavy support. He flew over and saw it. The mage was present and saw that she actually decided to protect her own troops for once instead of only shielding herself. But then a blue highlight appeared in his vision. A civilian!

Maginus, those bald people with elongated heads that made up the majority of the population in those strange worlds. He was caught right in between the Rangers and NoirElDem forces led by the mage. Mages were the ones that were the most dangerous opponent they have faced so far and clearly a ruling caste of NoirEldems. How they did it was still an absolute mystery but one thing was certain: the shields they made were not dissimilar to the shields that human machinery made. Shields experienced severe strain when under the effects of the powerful electromagnetic pulses. And Lexington and his operators had plenty of those EMP devices at their disposal despite them being highly expensive and issued only in small numbers.

‘This fool is about to become a collateral damage statistic, not on my watch!’ Lexington as if by some inner instinct dived from on top of him as he dropped the EMP grenades on the mage. He landed between him and the army of minotaurs charging at him. The mage was still trying to understand what was happening as her shield just collapsed and a rain of bullets fell onto her forces.

The visor on Lexingtons helmet was filled with red boxes as those beasts that were 2 meters high their bodies exemplars of muscular achievements. They carried two-handed axes in each of their hands. Lexington stepped forward rifle in hand as jet-packed Airborne once again began landing on the rooftops all around them.

“Target the archers and the mage we will deal with the minotaurs.” He commanded and aimed down the sights of his rifle. The visor had all the relevant information showing up in the sights. The chest area of the minotaur was almost impregnable even for his M22 assault rifle loaded with 6.5mm high power ammunition. Even if it was the most advanced firearm it was still designed to take down humans, not the horned beast twice its size with muscles as strong as Kevlar and the ribcage like bloody steel-plated armor. He lowered his aim for the legs. A hard target to hit especially as it constantly moves around. The trick is to just catch the tempo. The knee cap moves forward then stops as the body shifts weight onto it as it then propels forward. This is when you shoot it for this brief moment when it is stationary.

The buzz of 3 rounds gently pushed the rifle into the shoulder as the creature was sent flying into the air under its own momentum. Lexington began swapping between the targets, dropping one minotaur to the ground after another. It was not random, no, it was methodical. Lexington slowly moved forward towards that approaching wall of feral fury made manifest as the poor chained-up Maginus just stared at him in utter awe. That’s when 3 other operators flanked the stupefied Maginus laying accurate fire at the Minotaur’s. But they were relentlessly trampling the injured bodies of their brethren they charged on ready for the kill.
The only thought that occupied their minds. ‘Weather the storm, get close, and end it. Those humans are weak fighters.’

They were wrong for might is measured not only through strength alone. The first one to reach them was charging at them blindly and like a toreador, Lexington moved out of the way catching the right moment and unleashing the storm of bullets into the side of its head. A weak spot in an otherwise thick skull. He turned back to face the enemy only to see the axe just missing the glass of his visor as the hand holding it was just pulverized by the spear of bullets from one of the operator’s rifles. Without hesitation, Lexington pushed the rifle right under the chin of the enraged beast and squeezed the trigger again and again. The ammo capacity rapidly began diminishing in the visor 15/30. 12/30. 9/30. As the head of the beast exploded in a rain of blood.

Heart rate was well above 180 beats per minute but there was clarity at this moment for Lexington. This was the moment of truth, the moment of the war. They try to kill him as he tries to kill them. And though his body was in stress pumping adrenaline through his veins he was calm, calculating, taking into account everything. Position of his operators, the position of his enemies, Rangers moving around clearing out houses while Airborne kept Archers pinned down from the roof, the mage was already dead, complements of the missile from a gunship that just flew by above them. That civilian who still stood there was stupefied.

He quickly looked to the right side and saw that the operator that protected him was himself dodging and maneuvering in melee. He needed some room to maneuver, the green number floating above his head indicated him as Super 4.3. Lexington put the first burst in the arm breaking its attack as he felt the whiz of bullets flying next to him. His life was once again saved by his subordinate. But right now he had other things to worry about. His soldiers needed him more. He aimed at the leg and once again unleashed the burst, dropping the beast to a single knee. When Super 4.3 without hesitation pushed the rifle right to the side of the beasts’ head ending its life with the squeeze of the trigger.

Lexington had no time to see it as he had another beast to deal with. By the looks of it, this was coming to the end of it but he had only one more burst left in his rifle, and others were dealing with problems of their own. After moving and dodging around mighty blows for a couple of seconds the two looked each other in the eye. The beast stared down into his own reflection in the soulless visor as Lexington looked up into blood enraged eyes of pure hatred. That was when the rockets in his Jetpack roared and both he and the beast jumped up into the air. As they lifted 5 meters into the air the rocket engines cut out and Lexington maneuvered himself so that the beast would fall on its back. As Minotaur fell to the ground, Lexington landed on top of it triumphantly driving the barrel of the gun down the screaming throat. The sprite of blood washed over the barrel as it jerked from recoil.
Lexington raised the bloodied rifle up as he looked around. The smile crept in behind his mask. This was a victory and he could not lie to himself, he did enjoy it.

He slowly began descending from on top of the dead body towards the civilian, the bloodied rifle still raised high. He was basking in the beams of the sunlight lighting up the dust all around. His jetpack gave him a resemblance of wings behind his back as the bloodied rifle still had shining red blood dripping over it.
“Are you alright?” He said calmly as his PDA translated his speech.
“Sir, I think he is shell-shocked.” Captain Vade said calmly as he reloaded his rifle.
Lexington looked at Vade and saw what this civilian was seeing. He saw a mechanical monster that just killed other monsters.

A memory washed over him. Of that fateful day in Siberia. A day when he told himself he was not going to just be following orders anymore. A day when he realized he was a monster. When he stared down at the terrified convict and 3 other Delta operators. All 5 of them were the last ones standing among the dead bodies of those who tried to kill them. Just like right now. They all were about to fight to see who would be the last one standing. He was the first one to say it. “THIS IS WRONG!”
Then he looked back at the civilian, put down his rifle, and removed his helmet.

“Look at me, everything is going to be fine. We are here to help but I need you to go and hide in that house. We don’t want you to get hurt and once the fighting is done, meet one of our warriors with your hands raised.” Lexington was not sure if this civilian understood him given his crappy attempt to speak ElDemian but it looked like this local was relieved to see Lexington’s face.

Then something rushed up from the ground in a neighboring district. It looked like a giant black lizard with wings. It rushed up to the gunship. It grabbed it with its claws and with a single bite ripped out its cockpit as an explosion washed over its head.
“Ain’t no rest for the wicked,” Lexington said as he put his helmet back on and flew up to the skies to get a better look at what he was dealing with.
As he once again flew above the city he locked eyes with her. A woman in spiky black armor riding a giant black dragon that was still holding the remains of the gunship in its claws.
“Super 5, Super 6 this Super 4. They just threw a dragon at us” Lexington said as he stared at the dragon and his visor indicated that he was about to bingo on fuel as did all of his soldiers.
Edge
An account from MaxElDem High Priestess of Atna. Liberated during the Assault on Delfiapolis.
You TerraDems are strange folk. You yourself know what happened during the siege of Delfiapolis if it even can be called that. But fine I will humor your request.

I was the priestess of Atna. It was my duty to oversee and guide the king of Delfiapolis. Well, it was my duty until the NoirElDem armies besieged us from all sides. Yes, I did advise the king to surrender the city. But you must understand people, people we were supposed to guide were starving and dying on the streets. Yes, I knew what awaited them we were toil away under our cousins that chose to oppose the natural order of things but I reasoned that even under the NoirElDem enslavement they would still live out their lives as gods wanted them to live albeit in the Mist of Dis as would I.

For a time, everything went as I expected as with countless other cities. Our population was enslaved and put to work to serve the NoirElDem witches. The king was made a pet and me, well I was forced to clean up the mess of their dark rituals of biomancy but at least we still were living in the city. Until your people showed up on Elveron.

This is when the strange exodus of our people began, from what I understand now, they were indeed preparing for a new conquest. A conquest of your worlds and they understood they would need an enormous workforce to match their ambition. I was to be shipped away to toil away for them in their breeding worlds, I only hope they wanted me there for oversight rather than to become just another component for their dark rituals.

My position as High Priestess of Temple of Atna meant that I was well versed in ways of magic and record-keeping of the city and NoirElDems anything if they are not practical people. So information began flowing my way. Your people were like hidden menace striking movements of NoirElDems everywhere they were not leaving any survivors and those that were left were a rumbling mess stricken by madness.

But those were only small skirmishes and the NoirElDem witch in charge saw that you were converging on the city. So she decided to give you a grand battle to demonstrate the full might of their empire. Little did she know this was exactly what you wanted. I have to say, the way your machines dropped death from the skies was something no one expected. Only Demigods of old possessed so much power and nobody expected you to demonstrate it. Then there were of course the city walls and you just flew over them in your skyships as your people had already infiltrated the city and destroyed the towers that were protecting the city from an aerial invasion.

Your attack was truly flawless but it was this warrior of yours that impressed me the most. I stood there at the balcony of the palace color on my neck both an indication that was a slave and an inhibitor to my magic. I stood there with my mistress watching the invasion of our city unfold but what caught our eye the most was the trails of destruction coming to the palace. As if 5 shockwaves they were rushing towards and one of them was faster than the others. More surprisingly, if other attackers still were trying to stay more or less together, the lead one seemed more like a spearhead in pursuit of a single person. This warrior moved as if a shadow dancer was in a constant state of the shadow yet he still was present in the material realm.

My mistress shouted out orders in maddened fury to stop them and sure and the response sure did slow down your people. That’s when your mechanical dragons unleashed their fury to assist your advance. But then I noticed something: the destruction that your dragons rained upon NoirElDems was in fact in pursuit of that warrior rather than ahead of him. The truth was he was always at the edge of it just ahead of the devastation and nothing seemed to be able to stop him.

Closer he got to the palace I saw why. His movements seemed to follow some unseen pattern as he jumped into the sure death and was just ahead of it. He was like this fleeting moment, something you will see only once.
The following account is a stylized account of events pertaining to events during the operation of Hellenic Blitz as a subset of operations undertaken during operation Noble Cause.
Actions are focused on the account of 1st Special Operational Force Detachment Delta team Super 5.
Forces involved in the account.
1 SOFD Team Super 5
Commanding officer Major James Lockheed codename Edge callsign Super 5.1.
Second in Command Captain Sergei Tagarov callsign Super 5.2
Operator Lieutenant Enrico Galvarez callSign Super 5.3
Operator Lieutenant Thomas Baker callsign Super 5.4.

Major James Lockheed was calm under this new optical blanket, his soldiers following him like unseen ghosts across the streets towards the tower. If his operatives were truly but unseen specters avoiding sight moving along the walls Edge moved in the middle of the street as if the leaf on the wind. Easily avoiding guards patrolling the streets of the city that were preparing for the siege. He was as if a sudden breeze, the kind of breeze you do not expect but easily dismissed as just a fluke of nature.

‘I’ve been working with the major for what, 2 years now and still have no bloody idea if it is luck or he is just that good. Either way, what a showoff.’ Captain Sergei Tagarov thought to himself as his HUD display just showed the green box with Super 5.1 on top of it just breezed in between members of the patrol. NoirElDem Archers armed with their energy crossbows lead by one of those Shadow Dancers did not even bat an eye as this box just passed through them as if indeed there was no corporal body there. Then this box approached the base of the tower.

“Are you guys going to skulk around the corners or are you going to plant the explosives?” The whisper of James’s voice sounded in the earpieces of Sergei. Captain looked at the green box of his commanding officer with frustration.
“Don’t want to test my luck, sir.” He said as he shook his head knowing full well that the other two behind him were in total agreement with him.
“Ok just follow the waypoints, just no hesitation boys.” Lockheed said as the way pint appeared before the eyes of Captain Tagarov.

‘Fuck it let’s see if he is just lucky.’ Tagarov thought to himself as he picked up the pace and began following the line that appeared before him. The worst part was it did run dangerously close to the patrolling forces, more than that it was hugging them in a strange spiral. Yet Tagarov was now fully committed and just followed that line. He moved and saw the glances of his enemy. At first, it was as if they did notice something.

“Start dragging your feet as if imitating wind boys, on the wing and the prayer, breathe steadily.” The whisper in the earpieces said. As each waypoint was either just a distance and half more or less from one another.
Tagarov was not sure how to pull it off but now he was way too deep to stop. He began dragging his feet as this Shadow Dancer stared at him as if entranced.

She was seeing the dust pick up and move but did feel that strange breeze around her. The way it moved it was as if a gust of wind did in fact get trapped in between the streets and corners of the city. So unexpected yet so natural as if it was something possible. She actually smiled at it as if witnessing this beautiful moment of absolute improbability. The universe reminding that anything was possible.

“Good job boys keep it up.” The whisper in the earpiece sounds with the hints of glee. That golden glee, that touch drove Tagarov mad as he in utter disbelief just followed the waypoints so strangely yet beautifully arranged.
Now he was past the patrol yet did not dare to look back as the waypoints still were showing his path eventually ending up at the green box with Super 5.1 written on top of it.
“I will never do anything like that ever again, sir.” Tagoarov said in half a whisper the moment he managed to reach his commanding officer.

“Cmon Sergei, a bit of adrenaline in your bloodstream and excitement of pulling off something crazy is the best part of this job.” Lockheed said calmly with a smile hidden behind the optical cloak as he began carefully pulling shaped explosives.
“Yeah well, I don’t know what exactly happened back there. So I rather stick to what I know and my kicks the way I know, sir.” Sergei said as he began pulling out the shaped explosives himself.

“It’s really a shame what about to do here to be honest. Look at it, this tower is a work of art not only designed to serve a strategic purpose of defense but also a cultural one. It is done beautifully to let people be awed by its architecture and remind them that beauty is as much part of the universal plan as is the function. And we are about to bring down.” Lockheed said as he was looking at the drawings of the tower on his PDA provided to him by MaxElDem allies and the image of preliminary evaluations provided by the compiled from drone photography and processed by the special applications of his PDA. It showed how to destroy the tower most efficiently based on the visual estimation of where the center of mass of this tower was and how most likely the support structure would be arranged. It was computer-assisted guesswork. But even with that guesswork, Lockheed could already see how well it was done.

Indeed, it was a work of art, lines, ramparts, iconography all were done in smooth transitions and highlight the overall architecture of this city. Multiple balconies of sand-colored stone with purple amethyst-like railings and a sapphire-like ornate dome. All those strange stones were clearly part of the engineering. Alien engineering, designed to work as amplifiers and conduits for the mysterious substance Eather that those aliens used. This is where the interesting part was, what if this stone out of which those towers were made was not ordinary stone. What if his computer-assisted guesswork was wrong after all it worked based on the knowledge that humanity had, but the knowledge those aliens had?

Well, one thing was true when in doubt use a bigger gun and in this case more explosives. So he and the rest of the infiltration groups decided to use explosives to bring twice over what the optimal computer-assisted guesswork suggested.

They moved away from the tower once again falling into their preferred patterns of movements. Edge dares his luck moving faster than the rest while the others try their best to keep up skulking around the corners.
A green box appeared with the name Vympel 4.1 along with a dozen other boxes named Vympel on the other side of the street from team Super 5.
“Ruskies, well at least they are on time.” Lockheed said with a silent chuckle upon seeing the Alfa Strike Group team.
“Major Lockheed is Captain Racket team Vympel 4. I can see you on HUD. Awaiting your commands.” The voice announced in the earpieces of Team Super 5.
“Alright, you guys are ready to get this party started?” Lockheed answered back in the barely noticeable whisper as another oblivious NoirElDem patrol passed in between them.

“Ready when you are, cake-eaters. First, the palace buys drinks, sir.” He heard the whisper of Captain Racket in his earpiece and he smiled and just shook his head. ‘Nothing gets better than that friendly competition between the tier 1 units.’ The thought crossed his mind.

“I hope your wallet is as big as your mouth, captain. We start the moment the bombers drop their payload and showtime!” Lockheed said as he dropped his optical camouflage as the echoes of explosions reached him and he could see 5 SkyJaveline bombers pass right above them. He did that right in front of the patrol. They stood in utter surprise as all of sudden right in the middle of the street a figure appeared in strange armor that immediately turned black into the same color as the ground around him. A man with a strange contraption behind his back wearing strange glasses and with crazy spiky brown hair as if he was hit by lightning. He was holding something in his right hand as a giant tower was seen behind him.
“Surprise!” Edge said as clicking noise washed over the patrol.

The Shadow Dancer that patrolled around the tower noticed something attached to the wall of the tower as she turned her head in the direction of roaring sounds of explosions happening outside of the city. She turned her head back and approached this strange contraption that had a strange mirror on it that had strange letters on it. She learned to see those letters better.
ARMED.
Ping!
BOOM!

She was blown away as explosives detonated briefly engulfing the base of the tower in the dust. It was supposed to be enough to bring down yet it still stood but for a brief moment when the fire came out of the cracked stone followed by the shockwave and debris of the magically enhanced stone flying outwards as the shaped charge deposited explosives inside. The tower collapsed on itself as its base was just sent flying from an internal explosive force.

In a matter of mere moments, the patrol saw as the tower collapsed right in front of them clearly by the actions of this mad man. And just stared at him as the towers all over the city began collapsing in on themselves.

Before they were even able to realize what was happening that strange contraption behind his back came into action propelling him into the patrol as the strange buzz echoed in between them. They just followed this man as he, like an unrelenting meteor, rushed around them, dropping five of them as he passed by them. And he was not stopping as he rushed forward not even paying attention to those who remained behind him. Then bullets began flying right behind them as an army of highly trained killers appeared out of nowhere right behind them right where this madman was standing. They were in pursuit of this madman and they were killing everything that was standing in their way to catch up to him.

“Run and gun! Maintain the momentum!” Sergei shouted out as he was chasing his commanding officer. No commands were coming from his Major, unsurprising. His job was to chase him down, clearing up everything that he left standing. An easy job after all, the enemy was still stupefied, not sure of what just happened. After all, some manner of lunatics just rushed past them as the entire city was drowned in sounds of war. But even if he is not faster than the light, the element of surprise will wear off and that’s when the really crazy things will begin to happen. Will he stop? No, the return fire is merely additional flavoring for Lockheed and Sergei was just trying to keep up to see it.

There it was the return fire; the energized bolts of those NoirElDem archers began flying in Sergei’s direction as he ran. They were forming up one part trying to catch that elusive target that just passed by them while the other was ready to meet whatever was following it.

Sergei slid legs first for cover as several energized bolts flew right above his head. He popped his head just to see what he was dealing with and saw it. The black smoke of shadow dancers followed this figure that was unleashing the buzz of the M22 rifle and each time the amount of black smoke that was in pursuit would disappear as a lifeless body would fall. The odd thing was not that they could not catch up to him, it was that they did manage to catch but they could not place a strike on him. He was always just not there. Those black smokes that followed him revealed an almost hypnotic trail that was following his movements. It was weaving and spiraling in strange ways, similar to the way the waypoints were arranged. He could watch this forever but the energized bolt just pinned him down once again.

He was not in a very good position; he was hiding behind some manner of the well and it was not to last long as the energized bolts of NoirElDem archers were chipping away at it, shrinking his cover. It was not good. He could not move for if he would surely get shot but if he didn’t it was only a matter of time when they would get him. There was a flash of thought that popped into his mind. ‘Lockheed would move’

“Don’t, stay put, the others will help out.” The voice of Major sounded clearly in his earpiece. There he saw others did show up hugging cover sending shots in the direction of the enemy. They moved as they were trained to move. They were firing, drawing, and then immediately would get pinned down however giving as such giving opportunity for others to maneuver for better positions to light up the enemy targets. Rinse, repeat. Methodically maintaining the pressure. However, they were bleeding momentum and the opponent had the time to respond as they called in the reinforcements.

He finally dared to rise up to start firing as well and yes he found that window opportunity. He picked off one of the archers but saw as out of the corner of the street with inhumane roar bunch of minotaurs appeared.

“Damn it.” Sergei said as he knew those beasts were extremely hard to kill. His worries however were short-lived as Major rushed in between those beasts. Dodging every swing of the ax he was moved in between them shooting them in the weak spots of their skulls. He moved like death through those beasts and the short bursts of his M22 assault rifle were like the gentle touches of death dropping them on the spot. He was losing sight of him.

“Keep pushing!” He shouted out and rose up fully from cover and once again rushed towards the enemy from one cover to the other gun in hand. At this point, it was no longer ranged combat and it was a bloody melee as Alfa Group and 2 other operators were running and gunning NoirElDem archers and Shadow Dancers. It was a contest of reflexes and the ability to maintain focus on both sides. The contest that humans were winning. Finally, they reached the place where the major disappeared out of sight.

He was on the other side of the street once again showing off that he was practically untouchable as mages unleashed every spell at him. However, he could not do anything about them either as they grouped up to maintain the shield around themselves rendering his weapon unable to touch him. This was the strong point that even the combined firepower of Vympel 4 and Super 4 could not bring down.

This however was when the HUD on Sergei's revealed that finally the gunships were in range and ready to provide some heavy fire support. The problem was his commanding officer was in the middle of it.

He hesitated and to his horror, Lockheed just ordered an airstrike on himself. Dozens of missiles flew across the skies laying waste to the entire street as the green box with the Super 4.1 written above it hidden behind fireballs of explosions began zooming away.

Edge rushed ahead of the carpet of explosive force with a smile on his face. He jumped over the ledge as archers that were hiding behind it tried to shoot him and could not understand how they missed him point-blank, but it was a short-lived surprise as the shrapnel from anti-personal missiles cut them to pieces.

Edge did not even look back as he looked up at the grand central palace reloading his rifle. There was a MaxElDem woman with a strange color around her neck staring down at him from the balcony as the gates before him opened up and a giant black dragon rushed at him barely giving him time to get out of the way.
It rushed up to the skies for the gunship that just cleared the pass for others, destroying it in the process.
It held the remains of the gunship in its claws as it stared at four soldiers that were hovering above the ground with their jetpacks.
“Super 5, Super 6 this Super 4. They just threw a dragon at us” The words of Paragon sounded clearly in the earpieces of Edge.
“Awesome! What are the plans guys?” Edge said as he stared at the beast in anticipation.
X-Ray

“Is that part of your torture methods? I already told you everything I know, do you really need to torment me to relive the day I knew for the first time how helpless we can actually be?” The NoirElDem male sat in the gray metallic room as a human in a suit with the camera positioned behind him sat in front of him.

“Just tell us what happened there. We do it for record-keeping in order to get a better picture of the events that transpired there. Just tell us about your experience.” Human said calmly, offering the NoirElDem male a cigarette. NoirElDem took a cigarette and a human lit it up for him.

“Fine, we knew a large battle was about to transpire between me and my friends. We needed to take the edge off. The mages and shadow dancers usually invite some of us archers for fun overnights but we were not lucky that night. So we just opted out for some drinks. We heard stories of you TerraDems. How nobody survived to tell the tale of fighting with you and that one of the high-ranking witches did survive but it was all hush-hush. They told us not to dwell on things we shouldn’t dwell on. Keep your nose out of the affairs of the witches and hope that our achievements might get noticed and become favorite boy toys. Well fine, I thought to myself, after all, there is bliss ignorance. You only care about making sure that the next day is more fun than today, let the witches worry about the future and grand plans. Then it all happened.” The NoirElDem male spoke steadily and before the next part, he paused, stared down onto the metal table, finished a cigarette and stared directly into the human’s eyes with an em[ty look.

“With thunder, your dragons divided the skies in two, killing in mere moments the army that we amassed outside of the city walls. Then things got from bad to worse and they would only get worse and worse. Then I heard the explosions coming from the harbor. Me and my friends rushed to help out and tripped and fell over. Little did I know it was what saved me. I looked up to see my friends to be cut to pieces by some manner of explosions detonating in the air sending metal shards upon them.” He spoke it as if he was trapped in that moment, reliving a thousand times.
“I just laid there on my stomach and saw them die…” He grabbed his head once again staring down at the table.
“Another cigarette?” Humans pulled another cigarette out of the red pack and extended it to the NoirElDem male.
“Thank you.” NoirElDem male said calmly, letting humans light it up.

“Please continue.” Human said after letting the NoirElDem male take a minute to collect himself. The human stared at him intensely, observing every minute detail in the body language and facial expression.

“I stood up thinking I should go and try to help. That’s when I saw bloody dark figures appearing from behind the railings overlooking the harbor. I could not believe my eyes rose in perfect order as if they choreographed it a thousand times and just began killing everyone as they ran out of our houses to fight them. As if they knew exactly where they would be. This was the moment I knew I better hide I was not going to fight somebody who already won this battle a hundred times. So I dropped my weapon and ran for cover and they just ignored me. That’s when I realized they were not going to waste their time with somebody who will fight them just don’t get into the crossfire.” NoirElDem male spoke frantically puffing the cigarette.

“So I ran for cover when I saw one of the Witches rushing towards the house and well I was not about to show myself as a coward before this one. You know she really cared for us skinny boys, she was kind to us. I'm not going to lie, I really hoped that she would notice me one of these days. So I returned back into the fold even if my crossbow was gone. That’s when the shadow dancer showed up with more skinny boys. They began evaluating the situation and that was the moment I thought you know what we might even stand a chance. But you TerraDems really decided to keep upping the pressure. You blew up the house we stood next to, taking out most of my cohort, and if not for the mage I would’ve died. Any lesser witch would have lost her shit at this point but not Artilia, no she quickly saw through your plan to kill us all. She gave out orders to Shadow Dancer, she thought of a good plan to deal with you. And you know what I thought we might finally start putting up a fight.” NoirElDem male looked down at the table, shaking his head with a sad smile on his face.

“She was to cover our retreat as we would set up defensive positions around the Witches’ mansion. And then I heard explosions. I looked back as did the Shadow Dancer. She was surrounded and attacked from all sides by your Delta warriors. Her shield collapsed and this monster killed her in cold blood. He killed my beloved Artilia… Turns out she was not only beloved by me but by a Shadow Dancer as well. She rushed towards her to avenge her as did every other skinny boy. Right into your trap. You bastards counted on it. I saw it, I saw how this monster quickly turned to face the Shadow Dancer and our boys. He killed her and the rest were cut down to pieces as you rained fire on them from all sides. At this point, I just dropped to my knees. There was no hope anymore, you’ve beaten us.” NoirEldem male put out the cigarette as he stared at the human. He was done.
The following account is a stylized account of events pertaining to events during the operation of Hellenic Blitz as a subset of operations undertaken during operation Noble Cause.
Actions are focused on the account of 1st Special Operational Force Detachment Delta team Super 6.
Forces involved in the account.
1 SOFD Team Super 6
Commanding officer: Major Raymond Crawford call sign Super 6.1
Second in command: Captain Daniel Farnsworth call sign Super 6.2
Operator: Lieutenant Marcus Lynch call sign Super 6.3
Operator: Lieutenant Arnold Dupree call sign Super 6.4

The water was clear rendering everything in the shades of cold blue color. The school of fishes floated around hunted by predatory fauna. Large predatory mammals that looked like dolphins pierced through the school of fish as the fish would spread out like an explosion of silvery fragments in a perfect sphere. Only to be swooped up by the dolphin-like creatures circling by the sides anticipating movements of the fish. It was a feeding frenzy.

A sudden shimmer at the rocky bottom of the seafloor followed by dozens more. They moved slowly in precise formation creeping up to the underwater cliff from which the pillars extended all the way to the bottom of the seafloor. Like dark blimps, the ships floated at the surface of the sea. The shimmers began rising up as if some sort of carpets hugging the columns and the cliff. Some of the shimmers crept all over the ships. In absolute silence, the shimmers moved erratically at the bottom of the ships leaving long metallic cylinders attached to them.

As the NoirElDem walked across the pier in his unsuspecting nature he looked down into the water and saw something strange, something he had never seen before. He descended to get a better look. It was as if pair of predatory eyes stared back at him, as a sudden sound sonic boom reached his sharp ears. He starred in the direction where the sound came, up in the skies yet there was nothing but then his eyes saw it a good distance away from the direction of the sound. Five winged beasts leaving white trails behind them were dividing the skies followed by thunderous sounds of explosions on the other side of the city. The sudden sound of splash of water made him turn back only to see an armored figure jumping out of the water knife in hand driving it into his throat.

Dozens of the water sprites spurred up into the air. Dark figures jumped out of the water in a perfectly synchronized fashion turning clear blue waters red as bodies were tossed out into the water. Onlookers on the ships scrambled for their weapons only to see their own ships explode from the inside. Those who were at the harbor couldn’t make sense of what was happening as mage towers behind them began collapsing and ships were engulfed in flames. They were looking from side to side as in utter confusion not sure in which direction to go. Explosions filled the air with shrapnel cutting to pieces everyone high up on the wall and precise gunfire from those at a lower level of the harbor.

“Phase 1 complete, proceed to phase 2.” Major Raymond Crawford said as he looked around timer ticking in his heads up display and the sight of SOS operators reloading their grenade launchers looking up to the beige walls of the harbor partially destroyed by the grenade barrage.

There were five stairs leading up to the city where the barracks were located. SOS operators that were designated for approaching at the harbor shoreline instead of the piers were to climb the walls using the grappling hooks. They were also the ones clearing out the initial guards and harbor work stuff. Collateral damage was acceptable at this stage as maintaining the timetable was of absolute importance.

Those who came over the piers would be a bit slower as they also had to get the heavier equipment over with them and as such would use the stairs to advance and represent the bulk of the Interception force.

It had to be a simultaneous attack; those grappling over the walls would have to show up at the same moment as those who ran up the stairs. The timer in their HUDs was ticking and the count was going into seconds.

They had to beat the minimal mobilization time of the opponent in order to avoid going into the uncertain territory where the combat chaos would take precedence. Something they could not afford. The timetable was specifically set up by Major Crawford based on his personal evaluations of enemy commanders, their speed to respond to emergencies, the nature of their tactics, and target preferences. The other factors that were taken into account were enemy soldiers’ ability to respond to the orders from their commanders, their rapidity with which they would fulfill those orders, and of course, the factors of geography like the distance the enemy had to cover and obstacles they would run into.

The 3 staircases to the right led right to two barracks buildings and some storehouses in between them. This is where the first engagement would break out. While the other staircases led to some manner of a residential block that was built around the fountain, territory according to days of intel usually very lightly patrolled. According to evaluations also would not be the place from which the enemy forces would approach as all the barracks were to the right of the main street that led to the center of the city. This same street separated the two staircases to the left from the 3 to the right that would face the fiercest resistance.

The total number of barracks that they had to deal with at this stage was 5. The two previously mentioned that directly overlooked the harbor were designated as barracks 1 and 2. The first was to the left facing the main street and the second was to the right. Barrack number 3 was further back two buildings away facing the main street that was curving to the left. Barrack number 4 was the mansion of some sort that consisted of 2 buildings connected by a bridge and was just to the right of barrack 3 and to the left of barrack 5 that was just a building away from barrack 2.

Barracks 1 and 2 were where NoirElDem archers were stationed, barracks 3 was where they had their shock troops the minotaurs, barracks 5 was where the shadow dancers had their dormitories and barracks 4 was where the mages were.

This was the layout they had to deal with and the timer was ticking. SOS operators climbed over to the ridges of the wall as their brothers in arms ran up the walls rapidly turning their weapons towards the exits of barracks. They were barely in time. The first to run out was the Shadow Dancer, blades in hands, fury in her eyes coupled with lightning-fast reflexes as she immediately turned to black mist. Bullets pierced the black mist to hit the archers that were rushing out in formation out of the exit. Easy targets for focused fire as they rushed out of the choke point.

“STOP YOU IDIOTS! I SAID STOP!” The Shadow Dancer screamed out as she rematerialized behind the cover looking at the archers being obedient idiots and running out into the door only to be cut down by uncaring gunfire.

“Curses!” She said as she tried to pick out of the corner of the doorway as fragments of the wall hit her head quickly forcing her back into the cover. She looked around at least her followers did wise up to stay away from the doorway. Fear was no longer the word in her vocabulary, not since she underwent the ritual of becoming the Shadow Dancer. Ferocity is what she replaced it with. She was the one seizing the initiative and it is exactly what she was going to do.

“Listen up, idiots! Get to the windows and return fire and find other ways to get out of the building. Those TerraDems think they have us boxed in, time to prove them wrong!” She screamed out right before disappearing into the black mist to dive deeper into the barrack avoiding gunfire.
“You get to the top floor and the roof. Then start shooting at them.” She shouted out pointing at one group on the other side of the doorway.
“You with me we will double back from the other side. Force them to saturate their fire and then we push on my signal all together!” She said as she turned to those who stood behind her.

‘5, 4, 3, 2, 1.’ One of the timers was ticking in HUD of the SOS operator who laid on the staircase finger pulling the trigger of the machinegun sending long bursts into the doorway of the barrack and his gaze switched from the doorway to the windows. The minimal response time was reached. The HUD quickly began showing that the aim of his brothers in arms began shifting away from the doorway towards the windows and the rooftop of the beige-colored building.

He still was sending long bursts into the doorway to keep that place suppressed as he saw the enemy silhouette appear in one of the windows attempting to take aim at him. There is always a delay between seeing something and responding to it. A quarter of a second to react and about another millisecond before the bullets reach to kill you. A tiny window of opportunity that his enemy had to take aim and shoot, a window that could only be capitalized on with prefire. They began dropping barely fast enough to shoot, sending purple energized bolts in random directions. Well, at least now it actually did look like a firefight. A firefight that soon will be over as SOS operators under the cover of suppressing fire were preparing thermobaric explosives to kill everyone inside.

The Shadow Dancer ran from the back and picked out of the corner. Those TerraDem warriors in black that came out of the seas were clearing, setting up for defensive posture as the entire city was rattled with echoes of explosions. She looked in the other direction and already saw as a group of minotaurs was approaching led by the mage.

“Cardilia what are we dealing here with?” She heard the voice behind her. It was NoirElDem mage who rushed towards them the moment she heard the explosions. Black thick hair rested nicely on her shoulders blending in with the black dress and different flasks on her belt.

“Mistress Artilia, TerraDems caught us by complete surprise and had our forces bottlenecked at the exit leading out to the harbor. We lost the harbor and I think all of our ships were there. And now they are setting up defensive positions from the looks of it” Cardilia spoke without hesitations as she turned back to face her superior.

“Sabotage followed by a sneak attack from the shoreline and full-blown aerial assault. This sneak attack is a feint action. They want us bogged down here so that we won’t assist our forces deeper in the city.” Artilia said as she stared at the NoirElDem archers pouring out into the alley and sounds of gunfire reaching out to them. Then suddenly the sound of an explosion reached them as two archers were sucked back into the building. Artilia quickly raised her arms upon witnessing the change in pressure.

“BY THE DIS!” Cardilia screamed out in horror as fire coming out of the windows and doorway enveloped the purple transparent shield Artilia was fast enough to set up. The rubble began dropping around them left and right as Artilia visibly turned exhausted and her eyes glowed with blue mist flashing up brighter with each piece of stone dropping on the shield.

“This is not a feint attack, it's a sweep up. They want to wipe us out. Follow me we will set up defensive positions in the estate and will hold them off there we will not be baited into attacking them nor will we move towards the city to be stabbed in the back” Artilia spoke but it seemed mostly as if she was talking to herself. She reached out for one of the flasks on her belt and drank it. Her eyes turned black but still glowed with blue mist.

“Sister hear me! We must take defensive positions they want us to attack them to make us fight on their terms we must hold defensive positions and in doing so prevent TerraDems from stabbing our forces in the back” She spoke with a resonating voice and Cardilia heard her not only with her ears but as a voice inside of her own mind.

“Cardilia, start moving. I will cover your retreat. We will need everyone we can get our hands on if we want to stop them dead in their tracks” Artilia looked at Shadow Dancer. There was clearly a concern in Artilia’s eyes even if they were blacked out by the potion of shared minds. Cardilia nodded and turned to the archers.
“You heard your mistress get a move on and just maybe one of you will live to share a bed with her or me tonight.” Cardilia shouted out and ran towards the estate followed by her archers.
“So they went with option C. Ok they are not devoid of tactical sense after all.” Crawford said calmly as his squad moved through the street with a fountain in the middle.

“Phase 2 moved into condition C. We are hunting the smart one now 6.1” Captain Farnsworth said with a smile as he touched his PDA and timers readjusted their numbers in his HUD and moved from condition A to condition C.

“Already found her. Eliminate her and enemy cohesion will reduce dramatically not to mention their ability to seize the initiative.” Crawford said calmly checking in with his PDA just to make sure SOS units are already taking steps to enact a plan under condition C. Indeed they were responding. They were packing up their machine guns and portable rail guns while those who were not carrying heavy equipment already began capitalizing on the retreating opponents by taking more ground. Their advance was methodical, move, provide covering fire while others moved into position. Rinse, repeat.

Crawford moved over into the position as the horde of Minotaurs rushed toward the storm of accurate gunfire. He and his squad stood in the alley staring off into the other alley where a witch stood next to the rubble of the building humans just brought down with thermobaric explosives. She was more focused on what was happening in front of her but she felt something and turned her head to the right of her.

It was as if eternity passed the moment Artilia locked eyes with that monster. She slew many warriors in her time, the cocky ones, the careful ones, and honorable ones but this was different. There was not a single drop of doubt in his eyes, only killer instinct. It was as if he saw right through her, everything that made her what she is. Her struggles as she climbed to the top. Her dedication to the vision of a glorious empire. Her desire is to bring as many of her friends with her to that vision. It was as if every single aspect of her sentiments was laid bare and it was the reason why this monster was after her. At this moment he knew her better than anyone but there was no sympathy there or to that matter hatred. His every thought was consumed by a process dedicated to a single goal. To kill her.
“The Nightmare… Well, I’m not afraid.” She whispered to herself and turned to face her opponent preparing a fireball to incinerate him and his soldiers.

They’ve thrown some manner of metal stones at her and she quickly changes her stances fearing it to be the weapon of TerraDems that burned the Eather and dissipated the shields. The laser formed out of her palm quickly intercepting the metal rocks and they exploded above the horde of Minotaurs. The alley lit up in gunfire as one of the soldiers of this monster unleashed the rain of bullets cutting right to the side of the horde followed by gunfire all across the alley. They flanked them and now they were caught in the crossfire of hail of bullets. Their muscles were strong, their bodies big, their ferocity unmatched, they still rushed forward but the hail of supersonic bullets stripped them to the bone from all sides.

As the dust settled after the grenades exploded she looked over to the alley and only saw one person there raining death from the contraption he held in his hands assisted by some manner of the metal skeleton.

Then all of sudden she felt as someone began unleashing fire upon her shield from the top of the surviving building she stood next to. She quickly braced in that direction the laser beam now forming out of both of her hands slashing the walls of the building. The soldier ducked for cover and she heard something from the back of her. She turned back and saw two strange metallic cylinders and barely had enough time to raise her hand. They exploded in tandem first collapsing the shield but she was fast enough to act to be able to set up the second one in that tiny window of opportunity.
Then suddenly she felt something hitting her in the chest and things started turning dark. As she began falling to the ground she saw the Nightmare, rifle in hand and heard screams of Cardilia.
Cardilia with a warcry rushed towards the Nightmare blinking from side to side avoiding gunfire through her shadow dancer technique.

“No... “ She tried to scream out with the last of her strength but only saw this monster as if expecting Cardalia to rush towards him readying up his infernal weapon and just killed her and just moved on.

“As I predicted, she was not only the best commander in the field but also was loved and now they are out for revenge. Easy prey.” Crawford said as he began issuing new orders to SOS operators to set up kill zones.

The other witches completely lost all control over their subordinates as at first they rushed to avenge their favorite leader only then to start running in a panic realizing humans was the death itself coming after them. Within a mere minute, the estate was surrounded and being fired upon from all directions. The coup de grace, however, came down from the skies as the barrage of missiles coming from gunships upon the shielded building.
“Phase 2 complete, Proceed to Phase 3, Search and Destroy,” Crawford announced the moment barrack 4 was designated as destroyed.
But then one of the gunships on his map suddenly disappeared. He looked up and saw it. The bloody dragon held the gunship in its claws with 4 soldiers suspended in jet packs staring at it.

“Super 5, Super 6 this Super 4. They just threw a dragon at us” The words of Paragon sounded clearly on the special channel that both Crawford and Farnsworth shared with the other two original members of their team.
“Awesome! What’s the plan guys?” Edge said as he stared at the beast in anticipation.
“We slew a dragon on that day in Siberia, together. Let’s now kill an actual one” X-ray said calmly staring at the beast as he stood atop of the ruble.
“And now we have an entire Federal army at our disposal,” Farnsworth said as he pulled out his PDA looking at all forces that were already in the city.
The Dragon

“I do wonder when you will declassify those files.” A human soldier said calmly, his eyes fiercely piercing the other human on the other end of the table in a gray room with a single light shining on both of them.

“Standard procedure Captain the 10 year limit on classified material then review for extension of secrecy.” The human facing Captain said calmly yet clearly as if following orders. To which the Captain just raised his right arm as if doing the other human a favor.

“I still remember that day, the day of our greatest triumph and the day when we were shown what was to come. The Hellenic Blitz was a marvel of tactical and strategic genius, we really laid out the cards and yet the enemy showed us that they were not going down without a fight.”
Captain said calmly and clearly, still looking directly at the interviewer.

“We rose to the skies to see that beast, we all knew NoirElDems had it, you know the legendary monsters of old at their disposal. There it was holding the carcass of a Highlander gunship in its claws as I was suspended in the skies by the jetpack running out of fuel. It was giant black lizard fire-like jet streams coming out of its wings. Black Armored scales glinting in the rays of the sun, the maw filled with giant razor-sharp fangs and those red glowing eyes. It was the beast that knew it was on top of the food chain, the beast that understood what it takes to be on top of the food chain. A truly beautiful sight, terrifying, feral and yet beautiful.” Captain said as he looked down onto the table once again picturing the giant beast he saw on that day.
“But the most fascinating thing was the Dragon Rider. She was glad in this black spiky armor and mace in one hand and saddle in the other.” Captain made a pose as if demonstrating the Dragon Rider.

“You know we Delta’s are arrogant bastards but hey we earned that. First, you go through the military academy to become an officer and if you are top of your class you are given an option to undergo the Delta induction course. Let’s put it this way you don’t go through this course and succeed unless you really fucking want to and you are smart enough to pass all the mental tests they put you under. What comes out on the other side of the course is for the lack of a better word is a superior human. The insecurities you had become erased cause you know that you went through something that very few ever be capable of going through and then there is this high you have that there is nothing you can not do. You honestly begin to feel like a demigod of sorts. You can see options where everyone sees none, you can see how to dismantle any opponent, any challenge. There is a running joke, when they ask a Delta to blow up a star the Delta response is “Just one? Which one and when?”. The thing is we do honestly believe that.” Captain proudly pointed a large thumb at himself.

“So when I saw her, well I knew this woman meant business. She was riding that beast because that beast submitted to her.
