I stared into the eyes of a person who saw and recognized what I was and still was beneath her. My commanding officer on the other hand, well, they have gone through something even worse than I did and shared some things in confidence with me. She did look at him as beneath her but him well, he and the other guys from his original team were different breeds from us entirely cause they knew they were best even among us Deltas and they knew it for a fact. I won’t comment more on the subject unless you show me the IDA badge.” Captain pointed his finger at the interviewer clearly not afraid of saying no to him.

“All I will say is that if not for them, the Hellenic Blitz would have been the greatest military disaster in Federal history.” Captain said as he rose up from the chair looking down at the interviewer before leaving the room.
The following account is a stylized account of events pertaining to events during the operation of Hellenic Blitz as a subset of operations undertaken during operation Noble Cause.
Actions are focused on the account of 1st Special Operational Force Detachment Delta.
Forces involved in the account.
Group of interest, survivors Delta Inductions Course of year 3035 (information classified by order of IDA).
X-ray: Major Raymond Crawford call sign Super 6.1
Dan: Captain Daniel Farnsworth call sign Super 6.2
Paragon: Major Robert Lexington call sign Super 4.1
Edge: Major James Lockheed Call Sign Super 5.1

Dorgala looked at those tiny 4 men suspended above the city by their clever contraptions. She felt the taste of metal, flesh, and blood along with some other strange tastes she never tried in her mouth as Asados chewed this contraption of TerraDems they passed for a dragon. Those 4 tiny men were the best that TerraDems had on the offer. She could see it in them; they were mighty warriors that indeed understood what it takes to rise to the top, but never had to face someone who was as mighty as them.

Then she looked around. They thought they were winning and she couldn’t blame them for thinking so. It was a truly impressive assault after all. They infiltrated the city, they hit it from the harbor and from the front. They completely crippled her forces but now the game was on. If they thought their brilliant plan went without a hitch they had another thing coming for them.

“Smart choice,I will deal with you later” She uttered the moment those four tiny men broke off and went flying to the ground. Then she felt the pain of Asados, hundreds of metal shards of remarkable composition hit it. She felt the feral fury of Asados ready to lash out these TerraDem dragons. Dorgala tempered the fury of the beast by sharing its pain.
‘Stay focused, let me guide your fury Asados and we shall once again rise above them’ She thought to herself as the thoughts of the dragon pushed against hers.

They were shooting from all directions on them and now they were launching missiles. She felt this thing hit Asados, a curious weapon that combined speed with molten metal, certainly a devastating weapon but not enough to pierce the scales of Asados. Regardless, more hits like that and it will be hard to remain in the air. So she raised her makeup and some manner of small glowing balls appeared out of it and began surrounding the dragon.

As more missiles began approaching the Asados they were quickly intercepted by those balls. Asados was surrounded by flames as missiles exploded all around him. Dorgola focused her eyes from behind the ebony helmet of hers. She saw three of those soulless machines engulfed in missile trails and firing the cannons at her.

“There!” she shouted out and the Asados clapped his wings to rise above and then folded them in a tear shape and accelerated. The jet streams came from behind its wing as the opening in its chest gobbled up the air to be ignited in the furnace magical fire within its chest.

The gunships shifted their engines backward trying to avoid the 100 tons of the fire-breathing beast armored in impregnable scales. Dorgola became one with the beast as the pieces of metal and fire flew around her.

Asados unfolded his wings using them as airbrakes the moment it knocked out one of the central gunships. They kept firing at the beast, sending tungsten core rounds at it as they tried to move away from it. Dargola saw as the cannon fire approached her and without hesitation, she pointed her mace at the gunship and Asados opened its mouth. The lighting hit the gunship as the stream of plasma came out of Asados’s mouth. Two explosions surrounded the dragon, one coming from the lightning arc and the other from the stream of superheated plasma as Federal soldiers on the ground, their peashooters in hand, stared helplessly upwards.
They saw it in their heads-up displays. A giant black lizard surrounded with the red square of HUD taunting them to fire at it and witness the futility of their efforts.

“So you do know fear.” Dorgola looked down upon them tiny ants staring up at her. Yet this moment was gone as the Federal military proceeded to continue with their attack but something was different this time. This time they knew death was up in the skies ready to snatch them up at any moment.

“Now let’s engrave it in your minds so that you know your place once and for all!” She dived into one of the dropships as it was landing to unload the refueling stations for jet pack infantry. The surrounding human forces noticed her and opened fire. Bullets zipped around her but the Asados couldn’t care less for the small arms fire as dropped upon the machine. Pilots in the cockpit erratically screamed something trying to control the machine entrapped in the claws of Asados. Asados drove the dropship in its claws into the ground. Dropships engines desperately roaring trying to overcome the power of the beast. The dropships crashed into the refueling station sending flames and debris up.

“FOCUS FIRE! FOCUS FIRE GOD DAMN IT!” One of the officers screamed into the coms pointing at the beast standing in the middle of the scorched ground, dead bodies, and the debris of high-tech equipment. The windows of the surrounding building lit up in muzzle flashes as bullets zipped at the beast and bounced off its scales. The stream of superheated plasma slashed at the buildings incinerating everything, stone, flesh, and metal in a matter of seconds.

Dargola looked up as Asados was destroying the entire city block and saw those pesky flying soldiers of TerraDems switching to those long weapons of theirs. Whipcrack thunder broke and she felt as if a needle hit her in the shoulder. She quickly looked at the place where this weapon hit and saw it left a crack in the scale. Without hesitation, she pointed her mace in the direction of those TerraDem warriors and as if a crystal shield formed in front of her. The barrage of railgun fire dropped on her shattering this crystal shield. The moment the shards of the shield dissipated like a torrent of ice shards traveling at supersonic speeds perforated those flies in the skies. Some exploded, others began dropping from the skies.

Dargola noticed something coming from the corner of destroyed buildings. It rolled out with the noticeable sound of metallic thudding treads. Dozens tons of armored metal with long metal armature sticking out of it’s head. Dargola instantly recognized a larger version of the mighty weapon that those flying warriors of TerraDems use in this stick. Asados wanted to run at this arrogant metallic beast and tear it apart and as it thrust forward the saddle pushed him in a different direction. The voice of reason echoed in Asados’s mind and with a growl, it rolled it's the mighty body to the side as with whip crack thunder of the 100mm railgun created the line of destruction barely missing the dragon’s body. Dargola had to perform feats of acrobatics as not to get squashed under the weight of the dragon she was riding.

Asados with its red glowing eyes looked at the path of destruction the weapon left ignoring the constant stream of bullets showering it. It looked back at the metallic beast and saw it moving to allow for other similar beasts to move in for firing positions. They were dangerous but still, they were but beasts made to crawl on the ground when Asados could fly.

Asados turned its maw downwards below him. With claps of the wings and breath of fire, Asados was propelled by the explosive wave upwards dodging several railgun shots. Then it made a fire breathing fly by onto the column of tanks as more gunships showered it with everything they had. With Dargolas magic intercepting missiles and Asados’s scales the beast was surrounded by the furious halo of destruction as it made its strafing run on the armored beast of humans. And yet those metal beasts were still alive and moving around even after its horrifying attack.

“It will take more effort to destroy those machines, let’s focus down a single one with every attack” Dargola spoke knowing full well Asados could hear her voice in his mind, and in return, she felt the joyous anticipation. Finally, an enemy that at least made things a bit more interesting.

Asados made another strafing run but now focusing on a single tank at the time unleashing all of its fire breath upon it. As it flew past it, Asados left nothing but molten metal carcasses in its wake.

“You seeing it guys?” Paragon said as he stood on top of one of the buildings with his jetpack refueled. He stared up looking at how the dragon was destroying the Armored Lance that was there to try to deal with it. He was surrounded by his squad and a couple of dozen Airborne soldiers replacing their jet pack with new ones.

“Yeah I see it, it is a tank with wings, but by the looks of it, it is worried about the railguns and Shard missiles.” X-Ray said as he looked at his PDA observing the video recordings from different angles provided by cameras on tanks, soldiers and drones.

“I can't try to knock out the rider if I get close to her but the dragon I lack the firepower to take on that.” Major James Lockheed said as he was replacing the jetpack on the ground at one of the refueling stations with sounds of battle still echoing all around the refueling station deployed in the city.
“Maybe not you but Titan tanks have. We need to dictate where it goes and line it up for the kill.” Dan said as he looked into the PDA of his commanding officer Major Crawford.

“Sounds like a plan Dan. Edge you take point my boys and airborne will run interference X-Ray I will need you to tell us how to force it where we need it to be. Dan coordination with other forces is on you.” paragon said as he began arranging orders to Airborne soldiers to follow him for his attack on the dragon.
“Showtime.” Edge said after coking his rifle ready as his squad just stared at him in absolute terror as they looked into his excited eyes.

“Ok listen up, now that I reviewed the situation, the rider and dragon work in tandem, like two parts of the brain. Dragon is more instinctual; it is responsible for what actions to take and how the rider is more of upper functions tactics, strategy, advanced threat recognition. Keep the rider distracted and the beast will chase after the most annoying thing out there. Edge you play the bait, Paragon the Rider is on you. I will maintain the coordination to keep both oblivious.” Crawford said as he began setting up a holographic display not to get a 3d image centered on a dragon composed from images provided by thousands of cameras from all forces deployed.

Meanwhile, Dan began focusing on finding the most optimal firing solution with the tank columns that were still in the city. He was looking at the angles that tank canons had and where they needed to move for creating a cross-fire position. They needed to get the dragon below 100 meters and into certain places to provide a firing solution, 5 in total. He began giving out orders and Rangers along with tanks began moving into positions overwhelming any resistance on the way. While all other units deployed apart from Airborne were tasked with rendering assistance.
“Alright, I’m on it.” Edge said as he took it to the skies flying towards the beast that was dealing with the last tank that attacked him.

“Linking HUDs of the Airborne with your PDA, X-ray” Paragon said and dozens of soldiers like rockets flew up and headed for the Dragon as well. Their Head-Up Displays began showing the vectors of attack and movements necessary to keep the rider occupied and distracted in such a way that she would never have an opportunity to see where the dragon was going.

Dargola smiled as Asados laid waste to that final tank and already was looking for the next targets. She looked around and saw human forces beginning to move in strange ways. No longer were they hell-bent on seizing the central palace but instead, they were moving into different positions all around the city. As usual NoirElDem forces seem to be unable to do anything about their movements. She was not that much worried about another wave of flyers coming at her but this did not look great.

“Dormala you idiot, I give you the chance to seize the opportunity and you can’t do anything with it. Fine I will deal with it all by myself” She said calmly ready to make a dive onto another human position which seemed to work like some manner of staging ground of their flyers. Then suddenly out of the corner of her eye, she noticed something. It was one of those human warriors flying directly at her.

The audacity of such direct attack rather impressed her but without hesitation, she pointed her mace at that human but he immediately maneuvered. The way he maneuvered really caught her off guard. It was so sudden and in such an unexpected way. ‘There was something not right with this TerraDem, but regardless still needs to be dealt with’ she thought to herself as she extended her arm to create a shield around herself and with mace was forming some manner of the ice ball. The bullets hit the shield and she exploded the ice ball sending shards at this human only to witness his impeccable maneuvering once again. He managed to avoid all the ice shards. Now he was close enough to actually land right next to her. She swung her mace crackling with electricity at him and he jumped out of the way landing right on the nose of the Asados. He pointed his finger at the behind Dorgola right before Asados tried to snap at this human by the rapid movement of his maw only to find some tiny metal rock in his mouth.
Dargola looked back and behind her were dozens of those flyers already firing at her as something exploded in the mouth of Asados and she felt a rush of anger in Asados.
“Deal with this pest I’ll take care of others.” She spoke with anger but something at the back of her mind bothered her. Something was not right, this strange human wanted her to see the attack.

The whip-crack sound shattered the shield she built around herself and her thoughts were brought back to the enemy at hand as Asados dived trying to eat this pesky human that flew right in front of him. She began hurling her mace at those flying humans sending bolts of lightning at them. She managed to kill a couple of those flyers but then there were the others, the ones she saw before. Those four that she saw at the start of it. They were attacking her and every time she lined up her mace to unload her power at them one of them would position himself for the attack. She would be forced to respond to it unable to score a hit.

She was hurling her mace left and right switching up spells from ice bolts to fireball to lightning bolts and at the same time constantly needing to dodge the attacks from those long thunder sticks of theirs as smaller weapons without a direct hit would glance off her armor she was reinforcing with her magic.

“WILL YOU JUST DIE!” She was really getting frustrated as she played the game of whack mole as they kept attacking from all directions but one. That’s when like a flash of lighting it dawned on her. She dared to look at what Asados was doing and he was flying low now.

“ASADOS!” She managed to scream out as she felt something perforating her from side to side then again. Asados felt it and weakness came over him as his insides just suffered massive hydraulic shock. Its path wavered and once again two hits perforated his body and then again and then two more times.

She felt Asados was losing his consciousness and crushed the right top of the central dome of the palace. Rubble and dust was everything she saw and felt as boiling hot blood was spilled right next to her. Asados was dying gasping for air.
Dargola shook her head off and then tried to get her bearings and saw the giant beast bleeding covered in rubble as the dust was settling.

“Asados...:” She said as she approached her beloved dragon. Asados opened his eyes for one last time to witness his partner for the last time. That striking and daring woman that dared to stand before him and not be afraid. To stare into the abyss and not lose her senses. To never admit defeat, to never look back at life and have no regrets. To look around and to know that you are among the few that can relate to you.

“It was glorious to fly with you. Rest now.” She said as put her hand on his face. That’s when one of the witches approached she was wearing a long transparent beige dress covering her illustrious underwear there accompanied by two others. Arnela was in charge of this city and there was maddened fury in her eyes.

“DORGOLA you useless fool you just destroyed my ability to command forces. DO YOU REALIZE WHAT HAVE YOU....” Devastating chain lightning practically incinerated Arnela and her followers in an instant as Dorgola stood their one hand pointing at Arnela the other still resting on the dragon’s face.

“Just in case you even thought of surrendering Arnella after that failure,” Dorgola said calmly as she pulled a strange black sphere out of her pocket and focused all of her attention on memories of this day as it glowed

“Nocturna I will die today. Make the best of knowledge I will part you with.” She said it and then crushed the sphere and turned to face the opening in the roof of the palace. Her black metallic gloves with talon-like fingers were crackling with electricity. Humans clearly were already landing on top of the palace and some of them were already moving inside yet still did not dare to attack her just yet.

“Well, what are you waiting for? LET'S FINISH THIS!” She screamed out and saw as grenades were thrown at her. She threw her hands hitting them with lightings and the moment they exploded Federal soldiers poured in from all sides. She began throwing her hands in all directions killing one soldier after another as they attempted the assault. Those that managed to get through began taking cover and taking shots at her. She took a hit then the other dropped one knee. She focused on her armor as bullets bounced off it expending Ether to reinforce it but she had plenty still flowing as she absorbed all she could from her dead Dragon. A giant fire hallow began appearing around her as her eyes flared up with blue light.

“I’m taking you all with me” She whispered only to see her heart perforated with whip crack thunder. She dropped on the floor and rolled over to see 4 humans approach from each side. They moved in on her like they were the ones who took her down.
“Not gonna lie, it was sweaty for a moment there.” Edge said as he looked down at the dead body of Dorgola.

“We lost a third of our equipment and we are still counting casualties. If we will be facing more of those dragons and riders we need to find better ways of dealing with them” X-ray said as he looked down at the body.
“Evaluate enemy capabilities. It is one of our objectives.” Dan said calmly as he looked at the body of the dead dragon.

“Yeah but it looks like, Containing the threat to human territories is not within our reach at least not with numbers we have.” X-ray said as he looked at the PDA looking at all the losses they have suffered today.
“One step at the time, today was a win, let's put the flag on top of that palace. Troops gonna need it after the losses we suffered” Paragon said as he pointed at the opening in the roof.
“Let’s do it then,”X-ray said calmly.

The four black-armored silhouettes climbed up to the top of the highest tower. Within minutes a blue flag with a silver globe surrounded by leaves and pierced by the sword was waving for everyone to see.

All four looked down as everyone looked up. They stared down at their work a city full of smoke, fires, and debris while all those who looked up saw the flag defiantly waving on top of the highest tower that was left standing.
“Ray take a look at that, it is from High Command,” Paragon said as a message appeared on his PDA.
“What is it?" X-Ray opened his PDA to check the message and was rather surprised to see it.

“They want you back at Urbosaptia apparently they want you and your squad specifically to be present as one honored guest during the Afrodian Festival.” Edge said as he was reading the message, as well.
“You think it’s usual PR stunt?” Dan looked at Ray knowing full well he will have to be with him for this one.
“Not sure, any one of us could’ve been called for the honor guard yet they singled out me.” X-Ray said, still looking at the message.
“You think it’s General that wants you there?” Paragon said as he crossed his arms.

“Well I’ll figure it out, I’ll keep you posted. I guess we need to slow down our operational tempo and see what to do next anyway.” X-ray said as he put away his PDA and once again took a look at the city below.

“Well, we will be setting up base here and processing the prisoners while you are gone. So don’t worry doubt will see a lot of action without you” Edge smiled at Crawford putting an arm on his shoulder.
At war with their own nature

‘There is something fundamentally wrong about how humans wage war’ A thought pulsated in the mind of Datrius. He was a warrior himself, a priest of war god Res and he of all people knew that war was a pragmatic matter, but this, this was something different.

He expected a grand battle, a glory in combat, to clash with enemy forces in a contest of will, wits, combat finesse and comradery. He was expecting to stand shoulder to shoulder with those humans, to witness the valiance of their hearts and will to fight. Instead he stood staring at their magical war table as they wiped out the entire army in a matter of seconds, not even seeing their opponents with their own eyes, let alone their faces. They were just images of some figures being annihilated by their machines. He could not believe it at first, he knew with his mind that it did in fact happen but his heart refused to believe it.

The reality of it sunk in when he finally walked across the field of utter devastation. The broken carcasses of mighty NoirElDem pyramids at the outskirts of Delfiapolis was all that remained upon the char covered valley. He remembered as he was looking for bodies but all he could find were just pieces as in the distance humans were already staging an assault on the city. Without question this assault was another stroke of military brilliance of those humans but there was no place for him or his warriors in it. This was supposed to be the day they achieved a grand victory, a victory worthy of celebration and at such a right moment right before the Afordian festival yet he did not feel it. All he felt at this point was this strange feeling, something he never felt before, something their people had no word for, but humans did. It was an existential dread.

So with this somber thought he came back to Elveron upon the Afrodian fields to participate in the coming festivities. He was brought here to where humans build another of their forts. A fort in the place where there should be no war and yet they built this fort here of metal wall and their devastating weapons looking over the entire Afrodian fields. Given their situation he could not question them for they were allies that appeared in their darkest hour and as such all their quirks were to be accommodated and tolerated. At least their wraith if it even can be called like that was aimed at his enemies.

“Datrius, I hear congratulations are in order. We retake the city of Delfiapolis.” CID agent Lamar Tadison said in ElDemian language as he approached Datrius who stood in his mithril armor looking over the fields from on top of the human wall.

“Agh archivist Smith. Yes, but I do not feel so and I think you are the best person to help me clarify some things for me.” Datrius replied in English still with a strong accent hoping that speaking their language would make him better understand.
“Wow Datrius what is it?” Tadison said with concern looking at Datrius who still stared out into the distance.
“You do know we think that you humans are without a soul.” Datrius said looking out into the fields almost ready for all those festivities and adding the final touches to also celebrate the victory.

“Better not say that to every human you meet.” Tadison said calmly playing along with the false persona of a GNN journalist rather than a spy for the Federation sent here to evaluate the societies and as such shape the foreign policies.

“Don’t get me wrong archivist Smith. I’ve seen your people show valiance, compassion and bravery, as such just because oracles could not see your soul I’m well aware that you do have one. More than that you have proven that fate that is given to by oracles is indeed not unavoidable. So I guess if oracles can be wrong on that they sure can be wrong on souls too.” Datrius looked at Tadison apologetically.
“So what is the problem, Datrius?” Tadison tilted his head in confusion.

“I’ve seen the way you waged war and if I didn't know better I’d say you people are soulless. I’ve seen how impersonal you actually approach your wars. I’ve seen how unquestionably you just decide the death of tens of thousands and do it with but a single order and never waste a second thought to it. I’ve seen how much you people act, you act as if you have no souls as if you are nothing more but automatons that you love to use so much. The worst part I’ve seen is that everything you do is absolutely consumed by the objectives you set up for yourself disregarding anything else. It terrifies me. So how can you be both so soulless and at the same time clearly having one” Datrius explained letting go of his warrior posture as his eyes revealed great concern.
“How can we be so human and yet so inhumane?” Tadison chuckled in response.
“A bit arrogant way to put it and yet also proves my point.” Datrius continued to look at Tadison with concern.

“It is a hard question to answer with no definitive answer. Well I guess I’ve been listening to your tales I should tell some of ours as well.” Tadison pulled out a cigarette and lit up as he looked out into the fields leaning over the railing of the metal wall.

“I guess I should start from the beginning. Our civilization is roughly 13 thousand years old. I can’t give you an exact number but we chose to believe so because this is when we started building our first temples. 13 thousand years may appear as a huge number but in terms of evolution it is but a fraction of a second. As such the humans you see today are not so much different from those who build those temples, biologically speaking.” Tadison spoke without much enthusiasm as he smoked the cigarette.

“You see during those times we indeed were very much emotional and very subjective creatures and as such still are but also had hunger for knowledge, something that is fundamentally an objective thing. This objectivity, this impartiality bore fruit allowing us to have a greater control over our environment. We always say that to be humane is to be more kind, more compassionate, to be more generous, to be selfless and to say that to be inhumane is to be cruel, vindictive, petty, greedy, and to be selfish. The problem is those two opposites are as human as it gets as both are driven by our subjective needs and opinions. But so called wise people among us began realizing that there was no objective truth in it and because we were driven by our subjective needs and opinions we more often than not confused right and wrong. We knew that knowledge helped us to be more objective and as such help us make the difference between good and evil” Tadison said as he looked up as if the answers were coming from high above.

“So we started coming up with the idea that we should be as objective as possible in our judgments and it began reflecting in the way we did our affairs. It did in fact make us better, our material welfare began increasing as did our might. We started saying that those who do not learn history are doomed to repeat it having faith in knowledge for it did deliver upon what was promised. However, despite all that history always repeated itself for us. We always succumb to divisions, wars, mutual hatred and our methods only became more devastating as our knowledge increased. The worst part with knowledge we only saw how cruel we became. We were suffering from it and we didn’t want to see how we did all those things. Once again we looked up to our knowledge, which we thought was so impersonal, so clean, so sanitized and so practical” Tadison said as he looked at the orange hot tip of his cigarette.

“We began waging war more efficiently, we tried to remove ourselves as much as possible from the equation trying to turn it into something so clean as if that would change the fact that we were killing one another. But now armed with knowledge we did it in even greater numbers. Killing thousands of people with the push of a button became the norm. “ Tadison proceeded to breathe out the smoke out of his lungs.

“Remember how I said that we are not biologically different from those humans 13 thousand years ago. Well knowledge is objective but there is one problem with it, it never says what is right and wrong it only provides solutions and we are doomed to repeat our history because we are still the caveman that tries so desperately to see oneself as something better. A caveman that is at war with one’s own nature. We wage war against our very own humanity that mistaken the tool for an answer.” Tadison extinguished the cigarette on the metal railing.
“And the worst part knowing that only makes our war more devastating.” He said as he looked at the extinguished cigarette and threw it away over the wall.
ElDemian perspective

Datrius walked around the lake while looking at the campfire on the other end of it. He was standing inside of one of the human camps they built a wall around that lake along with sets of their blockish metallic buildings. They were quick as usual the moment the place was granted to them they erected their defenses and structures in a matter of a single day. Why, though, this place was hallowed ground for everyone. A sacred place of healing and reconciliation and here they were erecting their forts everywhere they went. But something told him it had to do with the words he heard today.
He was still not sure what to make of the words this human archivist Smith told him. ‘We wage war against our very own humanity that mistaken the tool for an answer.’
“They are lost people that survived despite it...” He uttered a strange realization as he stared at the campfire on the other end of the lake.

“Datrius, I’ve been looking for you.” Ratmisia approached Datrius from behind who was rather surprised to see her. The injury she has suffered was now mostly gone and there was a determined look on her face.
“Agh Ratmisia it’s always a good sight to see you.” Datrius turned his body clad in silver armor to face her hoping that a kind smile would make him look less intimidating.
“Datrius we need to talk. Why did you give up the sphere of the ancients to that witch?” Ratmisia asked Datrius staring him directly in the eyes.
“I did it to save us.” Datrius said slowly, not sure where it was going.

“If TerraDems didn’t show up this sphere would have ended up in hands of NoirElDem, my life, our lives were not more important than that. But you know that. So why?” Ratmisia stepped forward trying to get her face as close to Datrius’s as possible so that he could see anger on her pale green face lit up by moonlight.
“What brought this on it was seasons ago? Why do you ask now?” Datrius stepped back but felt that there was not much room behind him.

“Recent events revealed some things for me. The things I chose not to see, things that you knew off. The things that have been shown to you by the oracles.” She tilted her head observing the reactions of this clever snake in her eyes.
“Those are mine and mine alone to work with.” That’s when Datrius took a step forward as he slightly raised his voice yet Ratmisia stood there unphased.
“Yet you acted on them didn’t you?” She shot back at him.
“And why is that an issue for you?” That’s when Datirius reeled back in confusion.

“Because I’m part of those things am I not? You gave her the sphere because you thought you believed that’s how fate should unfold, didn't you?” She pointed her finger at his chest plate almost trying to pierce it with it.
“She would’ve had it regardless of my choices at least this way I knew you would be alive.” Datrius stood firm like a rock despite Ratmisia’s finger pushing at him.
“So you betrayed being a Warrior of Res because fate demanded it?” She spat in his face.

“I did it because I care for you and…I hoped... I at least get a glimpse of what it’s like to be loved.” Datrius looked away from Ratmisia as he forced the words out of himself. Ratmisa upon hearing it stepped back for a moment and began looking from side to side as if looking for what to say next and getting angrier by the minute because of it.
“Because it was fated to you?” She said in frustration.
“No” Datrius said as he looked at her and was tortured by the sight of her in pain.

“You knew that it was never meant to be, it was fated that you were supposed to be close to me so you interpreted it as you and I would live through a tragic love story.” She stepped back a bit more, pointing her finger trying to find solace in rage.
“Well with TerraDem's arrival and their ability to change fate maybe it is not the outcome.” Datrius stepped forward, hands spread out.
“A nice loophole you found for yourself, Datrius. Let others defy fate and the order of the gods to suit your desires.” Ratmisia lunged her face at Datrius with anger.

“And what about you? To live in illusion doing whatever you want believing that just because you refused to see your fate your actions would not go against it.” Datrius said as shook his head in disapproval.

“Well now I know and maybe I should end it right here and now!” Ratmisia shouted as she pulled out a dagger out of her belt and lunged herself at Datrius only to glance of his armor. She attempted to strike again and upon noticing the strange looks of Federal soldiers on the other side of the lake, Datrius chose to act. He quickly intercepted the next stab attempt of Ratmisia once again directed at the chest plate and then with swift move had her locked up in his arms dagger dropping to the ground.

“You know you can’t win, not in melee at least. You didn’t even try to hurt me. So why are you so eager to die?” Datrius said directly into her ear as both stared out at confused humans on the other side of the lake.
“Because I’m afraid to take the next step not knowing that my actions might bring harm to the ones I care about.” She broke down in tears.
“What brought this on Ratmisia?” Datrius let Ratmisia go and she just collapsed on her knees on the ground facing the moonwalk of the lake.

“Scholar Fahir… She… She almost died because of me. Because I lived, Silvarius died because I lived. Because they changed fate. How can they live like that? How can they live knowing full well that their choices, their actions are responsible for all the sorrow in their lives? Where do they find the courage to take one step after another? How do they know what they want? How do I go to the fields of Elysium to hunt with Artem after knowing she wanted me to suffer?” She cried as she sobbed, letting all of her worries out as Federal soldiers on the other end of the lake were giving one another confused looks only to be ordered to turn away by one of the officers.
“Honestly I don’t have answers to those questions and doubt they do either.” Datrius sat down next to her putting his arm around her as he stared out into the distance.
“What do you mean?” Ratmisia said in confusion.

“They are lost people, they don’t know right from wrong cause no one was there to tell them that. They had to figure it out all by themselves and they are still figuring it out. They were at it for far longer than we were, we had Gods tell us how to live our lives they didn’t.” Datrius said calmly looking at how those humans huddled around the campfire and he could see it this time. How they sat there looking into the fire filled with self doubt and worries about the future. How they told lies to themselves to keep their spirits up. How they were in denial trying not to pay attention to the fact that maybe the objectives they set out for themselves were not within their reach.
“But they seem to always know what to do.” Ratmisia said as she also stared out into the distance.
“I think we are just seeing the best side of TerraDems with this army here. The side that we are getting shown.” Datrius said as the flames of a distant fire and moonwalk reflected in his eyes.
“Datrius you know something that I don’t once again, please I can’t walk blindly anymore.” She looked at Datrius witnessing this enthralled state in the eyes of Datrius.

“The archivist Smith told me a story about their people. We always heard that they are fools that constantly fight among themselves. Yet we see how orderly they are. Turns out order was never an answer to this dilemma of right and wrong. They are creatures of chaos that are drawn to order. The power of order is animated by chaos. And I begin to think that Gods and 3 Eyed Oracle were as much of a tool in the hands of Ancient ones as the machines in hands of humans. A tool that was mistaken for an answer.” Datrius leaned forward as he spoke as if some ancient mystery was unraveling itself from observing what he saw.

“And where does it leave us, Datrius?” Ratmisia said as she looked at the distant fire herself as the mystery of humans was beginning to take shape. Leaving her with a single question about what her place was in this new world.

“Sitting at the lake in the middle of the human camp, two lost souls trying to figure out how they want to live out their lives in an uncertain world. One hoping for love that was never meant to be and yet sees the chance of it being while the other afraid of consequences of giving it a chance yet drawn to it.” Datrius said as he looked at Ratmisia with a kind smile.
“Datrius promised me you will make it work no matter what.” She dropped her head on his chest plate and hugged Datrius as hard as she could.

“I will do everything in my power, this much I did learn from TerraDems, for they got here by doing exactly that.” Datrius said, looking out into the distance into the distant fire with moonwalk leading up to it.
Delta’s plan
“Sir, you wanted to see me?” Major Raymond Crawford entered the gray room to what looked like a version of the forward operational office of General Jake Abramson.
“Ray good job on the Delfiapolis” Jake said as he poured the whiskey into the two glasses and put one in front of Crawford indicating him to take a sit in front of him.
“It was a team effort, Sir” Crawford sat down, grabbed a glass, and raised it to what Jake did the same. Then both took a sip.

“Ray, I suspect you are wondering why you were recalled?” Jake said as he looked inside of the glass of whiskey then returned his gaze back to Crawford who sat there with the usual cold expression on his face.
“Sir, I follow orders, I don't question them.” Crawford said calmly yet clearly his face was still completely neutral.
“Now that’s bullshit. So let's drop the ranks and tell me what you actually think.” Jake said as he slightly tilted his head and then took a sip. Crawford leaned back somewhat and took a sip as well.

“Jake, the last thing you do is tell the command what you actually think. Nothing personal but just good career practice.” Ray spoke as he waved the glass of whiskey and put on a slight smile on his face and took a sip.
“The induction course really did a number on you didn’t it?” Jake leaned forward with a slight chuckle.
“Just confirmed what I already figured out by myself.” Ray took another sip with a slight smile on his face.
“That’s not exactly a healthy living you do realize it?” Jake said, staring directly into Crawford’s eyes.
“So is getting shot at but that’s what we signed up for.” Crawford responded, staring back at Jake and taking another sip.
“Do you even see your life outside of the military?” Jake now looked concerned for Crawford.
“Jake this is the only place I can be of use.” Crawford said calmly as he put down the glass of whiskey and put his hands in front of himself.
“Well, the corporate field might also suit you.” Jake pointed a finger at Crawford while still holding the glass of whiskey.

“We both know it’s a bad idea. My very nature would be determinant to society. At least here my predatory nature is put to good use.” Crawford said as he looked at his hand slowly turning into a knuckle and slowly turning it around. It was as if he was somewhat proud of it, proud of the power he had in it.

“Alright, then I’m asking you about Super 6.1. Why do you think you have been recalled?” Jake said after observing Crawford for a moment. The speed with which Crawford focused his attention on Jake and straightened actually caught Jake by surprise.

“The orders were simple. I must attend the festivities to provide tactical and strategic insight for security. Since every high-ranking officer was also recalled other than that of commanding officers of team Super 4 and 5, I’d say it’s PR work. Building “bridges” with the locals and I am the prime attraction.” Crawford said as if he was a robot, clear, precise, and fast.
“What if I told you it was not my idea?” Jake said, still observing with confusion staring at Crawford as if studying the eldritch creature in front of him.
“Locals asked for me.” Crawford said in the same straightforward fashion. Jake moved his shoulder and leaned forward almost mimicking Crawford.

“Shasira the Chosen of Zes was the one who said that leaders should be present as per tradition and since we are now part of the alliance we should show that we respect the traditions of locals. Something to do with leaders being the shepherds of the people and their blessing being an essential part of the Afrodian Festival.” Jake spoke in a similarly straightforward fashion as did Crawford.

“With Lexington and Lockheed on the frontline, our cohesion is maintained so I can see how it wasn’t a big deal to accommodate for it.” Raymond seemed to be utterly unphased by the change in tone in general.

“Yeah well that’s not a strange thing, High Councilor Miala asked me that you be personally present here for the festival. Can you explain how she got your name?” Jake pointed a finger at Crawford as he leaned forward.
“We never met and all our ops are classified so I couldn’t tell you.” Crawford responded without hesitation and the flash of frustration flared up on Jake’s face.

“Here’s what I think, you know something and you are not telling me because otherwise, it impedes something you Delta’s are trying to figure out on your own.” Jake said, lowering his voice as if almost growling.

“Or maybe CID is playing games here. Trying to see how they respond to the best warriors we have given their obsession with fated individuals. You and I know sir that this is their mode of operation to keep the military in the dark about some of their activities. But I’m sure it is all done in service of our mission.” Crawford was still calm as a rock but his words did give Jake a pause.’The bastard is right and he knows it’ Jake thought to himself as he leaned back into his chair and took another sip.

“You know what Ray, I think you boys have done an excellent job so far but I think we need to swap up some of our overall strategies. Now that we more or less know what is happening and we are beginning to get spread out I think we will need a more top-down approach.” Jake said no more gleefully, articulating with his hands as if he was manipulating the marionettes.
“You point the spear and we are the spearhead, Sir.” Crawford answered calmly as if it would change nothing. Jake just kept staring at Ray, not even quite sure what to say anymore.
“Well enjoy the festivities and wait for further orders,” Jake said after finishing off his glass.

“Yes, sir.” Crawford finished his glass as well then rose up and walked out of the room and into the camp. It was a strange sight, officers dressed in their parade uniforms as were the soldiers standing on the grass surrounded by trees with glowing fruits and prefabricated structures. Local foods and spirits are served and prepared by the locals and their own luxury supply is served by the soldiers. It was a strange sight indeed as usually, Crawford would see such a parade somewhere on the Federal world in the enclosed environment of some dome lit up by artificial lights and metallic floor.

He himself was dressed in a black suit with a black shirt and gray tie. Suit had chevrons with Majors insignia on his shoulders and onyx ribbons on his chest instead of the ordinary fruits salad as he was still on active duty clandestine operative of the Federal Interplanetary Army. Every officer of Tier 1 units was wearing the same outfit as he did but the rest were wearing gray suits with their ribbons on the show demonstrating their history and qualifications. The suit served more than a badge of honor but also a warning sign to not talk to him about his operations. Something some of the local leaders began to pick up on.
They were like a strange reminder that for all its transparency, the Federation had a shit ton of secrets it was unwilling to share.

“Kind of feel naked outside of the armor set” Daniel Farnsworth approached Crawford after he walked out of the general’s tent. Same black suit, same onyx ribbons but chevrons on the shoulder pointed out him being Captain.

“All the more reason not to drink too much or eat too much. We need to maintain combat readiness.” Crawford said as he looked around with laser focus as his brain was already playing out different combat scenarios that may occur.

“Already talked to Lynch and Dupree. So what do you think?” Daniel said as he looked across the campfire at two other black suits talking to each other with plastic hands in hand and weapons crate next to them.

“They are already getting concerned with our operations and are now going for a more hands-on approach. Murkeyed the ground with blame on the CID.” Crawford looked at Agent Lamar Tadison surrounded by the bards listening to the tales a somewhat drunk agent of the CID masquerading as a journalist was telling them.

“Say what you want about CID but having a designated asshole does have its merits. So that means we will be operating on Snyders leash. She is sharp but she is not us.” Farnworth said as they walked towards the wall along with his commanding officer. He glanced over to the other side seeing Colonel Eliza Snyder talking to High Councilor Miala. Snyder was clearly spooning for information as to how Miala requested Crawford by name.

“She is sharp but she also understands the clandestine game. She will smooth out the edges sure but that means we will have to make sure we make our actions more palatable for command going forward.” Crawford looked over to Lieutenant Dray Martinez and Doctor Akilah Fahir in a wheelchair. He stood staring up at the blue flag of UEF lit up by floodlights PDA in hand as Doctor Fahir stared at her legs poking at them. There was uncertainty on his face while Doctor Fahir was as if entranced in thought reliving a horrible event over and over in her head.
“So we will have to put the lawyer's face on.” Farnsworth dusted off his onyx ribbons as they walked across the metal ramp to see over the Afordian fields to see what was happening.

They looked down and saw hundreds of different huts, small lakes surrounded by the woods lit up in the moon of this planet. Huts were decorated with myriads of flowers and some glow crystals. The stands of food, herbs, and drinks surrounded the huts as locals were participating in different recreational activities. But the two were not marveling at the light show presented before them; they were focusing on how long would it take to run in between those huts, what are potential threats they might encounter in them, and how each could be used as a strongpoint.

A particular string of huts grabbed most of their attention. It was a bunch of huts that stood next to a road that the Federals built to ferry their supplies. It stood out from the rest of the things in here. Gray dead concrete road lit up by cold sharp white light of neon lights of posts defining its borders. This light was so different from the light the huts used it was aggressive and cold casting sharp shadows while the light of crystals was warm and gentle and yet it played tricks as they changed colors.

“The locals, we couldn’t do the background check on them,” Crawford stated as he turned around to see the local servants serving local cuisine and demonstrating some of the magical tricks for the entertainment. They were making dancing lights creating different images as they told stories and performed feats of acrobatics in their dances.

“To a degree, we voiced our concerns to the local leadership but well it is their end so it is about how much we trust local leadership with their internal security. But even if they try something we made sure we have enough firepower to deal with them.” Daniel first pointed to the automated sentry towers with miniguns on top of them and then pointed upwards where the gunship was flying.

“So what is the situation when it comes to bridge building?” Crawford looked up at the gunship in the air. It was a mighty war machine but he knew that this was not the biggest gun out there. Farnsworth took a look at the gunship as well and he too knew that there were things out there that could mop the floor with it. He turned back to get a better look of what was happening as he reached into the inner pocket of his jacket. He pulled the combat goggles out of it and activated them. The image layered over the image with augmented reality.

“Your area of expertise, but here’s what I figured. The whole festival is like a glorified sex fest. Standing orders not to participate in any intimate actions but you know how it goes some will slip through the cracks. You know the whole go visit exotic places, kill exotic people and fuck exotic people. Some will surely try to live out that vision no matter what. I just hope our immune system is up to the task to deal with any STDs. Compartmentalization allows for limited leaks and will show us where and how we can use those leaks to manipulate enemy intelligence if they occur. That’s the situation on our end.” Daniel spoke as he tracked the movements of each soldier present. Some of them were on their assigned duties among the huts conducting patrols or providing escort duty to the research personnel and some turned off their transponders. He took note of those that did.

“And issue on the other end?” Crawford said as his sight turned back to the light show being presented. It was like holographic theater with images that were more abstract than real. Characters were of different colors, their emotions translated by the elemental states of fire, ice, electricity, and light.

“Once again that’s your area of expertise but so far all I get is a local story. The Afrodian day is the day we show our appreciation for love. As the goddess that teaches us how to love and appreciate others, we dedicate this festival as worship to her. For she shows us what it means to live together and love one another. We show to her that we see that we are all part of the great plan laid out by 3 Eyed Oracle. The day when we see the struggles of the story we all play the part of. To ease one another’s pains and show that we are not alone. We show our vulnerabilities and desires as Goddess intended so that we feel comforted and appreciated. The day we toss away our cloth and stand naked before one another” Farnsworth explained as the story was unfolding before their eyes.

The story that was unfolding was the story of a young warrior falling in love with a mage. Fate promised tragedy that they will never be together for both stood on opposing sides. As their states changed from icy hatred to one another as the amber at their hearts turned into the raging fire. Events were transpiring against them as both shared their concerns with one another as darkness in their minds was contained by the light in their hearts. The story culminated in them exploding and being no more but as they exploded their ambers touched the icy shells of others planting ambers of great fire among everyone as fate demanded.

“So it serves to strengthen unity between people blurring the ethnic and ideological lines but at the same time deepening personal animosities and loyalties. Did anyone already make advances on you?” Crawford said calmly as he saw one of the local mages touching one of the officers by the hand who was rather confused by the gesture.

“The black suit seems to scare them away but looks like they are already attempting to court the officers and then going down the ranks. It almost looks like they really don’t give a damn about the hierarchy nor gender like everything is fair game.” Farnsworth said as he looked at some of the tier 1 operators in their black suit being actively avoided by almost every local out there.

“So it is not procreation driven. Kolistians would love it, fanatics on the other hand.” Crawford said as some manner of ping drew the attention to his PDA. The PDA showed an image of paladin Casas with his squire reprimanding one of his newfound followers as he opened his book of Ike. He was talking about how love must be dedicated only to either the light or a single person. He talked about how their pansexual behavior diluted the essence of love how it was actually driving a wedge in society and that such society would be always in a state of turmoil.

“Well, this is going to be fun to observe. Still not sure though how this would work out in our favor.” Farnsworth said as he pulled out his own PDA to observe what was happening. He was rather interested in the semantic skills Cassas was willing to put up in dealing with this blasphemous event as he was surrounded by the Federal military.

“It’s about a long game Dan. The Church of Light may be a backward and regressive cult but one thing they got quite right is to convert as many people to their beliefs as possible. Him being here will undermine the pantheon and since we know of the Church of Light and how to deal with it, this will give us more leverage at the diplomatic table in the long run.” Crawford said as he tapped the Anarchy symbol on the screen of the PDA. There were myriads of different categories within the app. Tactics, strategy, persona file, technology, history, and cultural applications. He tapped on cultural applications, chose subsection of religion, and added Church of Light mission arrival, paladin Cassas, and Squire from high ranking families. Application updated the Persona files and historic documents and began giving out possible outcomes among which was a full-blown religious civil war.

“So when their temples will start burning we will sweep in as peacekeepers and in the worst-case scenario, we will leave seeds of constant resistance against NoirElDem oppression. However, I do wonder when the locals will start showing their hands, they can’t possibly be this passive.” Farnsworth said looked through the app as well, still having the cultural actions on Allied forces to be somewhat inactive.

“Just because we are not seeing them play their cards does not mean they don’t play them and if Tadisons cultural report is correct it is not the locals that call the shots in the grand scheme of things.” Crawford lifted his eyes away from the PDA to observe the light show.

“Pantheon and 3 Eyed Oracle. Ok, let us shift the focus on them. I’ve yet to see those gods play their card in any other way other than grant knowledge, power, and visions of the future to the local populace. So let's say they practically command people by giving them visions of what must happen and giving out the necessary tools and knowledge for it to happen. With the end goal of maintaining their own power.” Farnsworth began tapping on his PDA and shifting religious entities from cultural entities to active Persona files. It required editing so he began scrolling down for different angles and motivations.

“We might be thinking about it wrong. We assume that Gods are akin to individuals pursuing the agenda of maintaining their power over the local populations and this conflict is the result of their disagreements and those locals are the soldiers in their armies. What if the situation is different and if their ways are more subtle than we initially assumed. What if maintaining power is not the end goal but what they spew out as propaganda is indeed an end goal, a balance of some sort.” Crawford stared at the Daniels PDA as he spoke and Daniel was putting different parameters into his assessments. Dan put in mission fulfillment as a motivation for gods along with their respective character traits.

“So gods themselves are subservient to a greater plan?” Dan said as the application indicated that if there was a plan then there must be an architect of the plan. Dan quickly scroll through different options from King of Gods Zes, joint plan option and other gods on the list but he was looking for one particular name, a 3 Eyed Oracle. The problem was that information on 3 Eyed oracle was the most sparse. The only thing they knew of it was the creation of the ancients.
“Yes and what if we are dealing with an artist rather than an engineer here?” Crawford said as Dan was still trying to find what attributes to assign to 3 Eyed Oracle.

“Ok, so the picture that is being painted serves a greater purpose than the end goal. So let’s say 3 Eyed Oracle is an artist in question. It paints a picture but instead of just giving them all aspects of the grand plan it gives them fragments. Ok, not an engineer so a practical answer to the question “why” is not the case. But that can be anything then.” Farnsworth put in the artistic expression as an end goal for the plan which immediately demanded the question of what message had to be delivered. Dan left the question unanswered for now but began entering different aspects of what he already knew about how the picture was painted. However, once he came back to answering the question of the message he still was not sure what to put into it. His finger hovered over entropic balance but he was not exactly sure if this was the case as well.

“Authenticity of the experience itself is what makes it valuable. If locals and gods knew of all the aspects of the grand plan then what they experience is not real. It’s just a scripted video for the participants and therefore does not feel real for them.” Crawford pulled out his own PDA and began entering a new parameter into it. This parameter was to make the participants live out the life written out for them by the artist.
“So where does it leave us?” Daniel said as he activated the simulation and began showing that it mostly was designed in such a way as to produce as intense experiences as possible.

“A wrench in the tapestry of its grand plan. We will show this artist that the universe does not care for its carefully crafted vision. That means we will do as we please act in accordance with our own nature and not the vision imposed by it.” Crawford drew the red symbol of Anarchy upon the picture on the PDA created by basic simulation collapsing it completely.
“Opposing the will of the gods and forces far beyond our comprehension some would call it hubris.” Daniel said as he looked at Crawford with half a smile on his face.
“But we call it Tuesday.” Crawford said as he closed the app on the PDA and extended his fist to Farnsworth who fist-bumped it.
Enemies plotting

Nocturna and Dracessa were in the black hoods sitting in a private room while the commotion of Afrodian festivities was happening on the other side of the door. Inside the room stood a table with an orb filled with a gray mist and several herbs and potions laid out around it. The orb was projecting an image above it. The image was a revision of memories of a Dragon Rider. The door opened and a human in a black armored vest and red and black digital camo T-shirt. bunch of tattoos running down his arms and face covered in the black beard. Some manner of weapon was slung across his back and pouches on his vest were full of some manner of cylinders.

“Dargola gave her life for us to have this knowledge Mr Carmicle and I have you here for your expertise. Tell us what exactly we are facing here.” Nocturna said as she with a wave of her obsidian staff closed the door behind Carmicle as he reached for one of his pouches and pulled out a small plastic cylinder filled with a white powder. He stuffed it into his nose and as if getting a jolt of electricity sniffed it in.

“So Delfiapolis was just the way for you to let the Feds show all of their cards, boss?” Carmicle said as he pulled a datapad out of one of his pouches and put it on the table next to the orb so that everyone could see the screen of it.

“It is about making the best of a bad situation, something our kind knows quite well Mr. Carmicle.” Nocturna said calmly as she stared directly into the fidgety eyes of Carmicle doting around the room practically forcing them to look back at her. Carmicle blinked for a moment; he was somewhat confused, his hand instinctively reaching out for another dose. Nocturna tilted her head in disapproval and Carmicle found himself closing the pouch without getting his dose out.

“Alright, let’s see. Focus on that please” Carmicle said as he pointed to the ground focusing on the federal soldiers with a cog and wings with number 23 below the cog. He made a couple of taps on the datapad revealing that those soldiers were of 23rd Special Logistics Division.

“What you are dealing with here is what can be considered an elite force of the United Earth Federation. Federal military relies on a system of tiers to identify the proficiency of its units. Tier 3 is the least proficient and tier 1 is the most proficient. Tiers are usually defined by the amount of training and equipment that is expended on their preparation. I don’t see a single tier 3 unit here.” Carmicle spoke as he began tapping on the datapad revealing the system of tiers of the Federal military. Both Dracessa and Nocturna watched the datapad as hawks.

“So those are the elites of the Steel Eagle?” Dracessa said as she looked back at the projection coming from the orb. She reached her hands out for the orb. Though the image projected by the orb was indeed rather accurate, certain tones and colors began changing. Before that change, the Federal military seemed more like a collection of young and brave warriors too fond of their tools. Now it was different, it felt different, it felt more precise to Carmicle. The gray outlines separating weapons became more pronounced. It became clear that it was people wielding tools not the other way round. It was a subtle difference but prior to that it actually looked like children with their toys. Toys that commanded them. The subtle difference but something told him that the more sharp image was more likable it became for some odd reason.

“Show your best and now we know what you can do, which means we can account for more. Please continue Mr. Carmicle.” Nocturna made almost a kissing gesture towards Dracessa to what Dracessa only made an image of covetous awe on her face. Carmicles eyes dotted between two women. They were hooded, one was armored and fierce, the other had an insidious black dress. Both items of clothing pronounced the curves even though one was full of thorns the other had this one enigmatic blade on her belt. Something was happening and he was not part of it as he quickly reached for another dose and attempted to focus on his work.

“Ok let's focus on Rangers. A tier 2 light infantry unit. Tough and uptight bastards. On top of the standard military training course that lasts about a month, they spend another 6 months of hellish training. During which they practice rapid deployments with drop pods, fast insertions with dropships, building clearing, anti-insurgency operations, combined arms warfare, and high momentum warfare. They are the gold standard upon which all higher-tier infantry units improve upon. Deploy, secure, maneuver, and advance. Disciplined and adaptable. Deployed in platoons of 50 men, separated into 5 squads. On top of the standard equipment of small firearms, a single platoon carries around two miniguns for heavy suppression and 4 missile launchers, a single drone, and a single Sapper outfit for solving engineering issues. Soldiers are allowed to adjust their equipment load-outs individually from weapons that are provisioned to the squad. This allows for greater versatility for mission-specific situations.” Carmicles datapad showed a standard unit formation and image of a single soldier. It was filled with different data points and options to play with. Draccessa took just a single look at the datapad and the image above the orb began changing

As if enveloped in a mist, in a purple mist the image was changing. The image of Ranger came around. It was a rugged warrior whose cloth and armor was in perfect condition. Some streetwise intelligence came out of his eyes along with the daring heart. His armor is forged well, his weapon is deadly, his arsenal is vast but requires preparation.
“An army of tricksters.” Nocturna said as she looked at the image.

“Peacocks, no different than our males but I admit they have a bit more wit.” Dracessa waved her hand to the right and the image disappeared as she approached Carmicle closer. She extended her finger in metal to him. Carmicle stared at the finger, he began rubbing his nose and at this moment the finger dropped on the datapad giving off glass gently hitting the metal. This claw made of black metal pointed at the outline of a jetpack soldier at the far corner of the datapad screen.
“Tell me about them, human.” Dracessa turned her head as she stared at Carmicle.

“Alright Airborne division. An oddity in the Federal military, though it is considered a tier 2 unit the actual fighting element in it can be considered a tier 1 unit. The reason why it is considered a tier 2 unit is because most of them are there to support the fighting element that is jet pack-equipped soldiers that do take more than six months of training. In the past, they were mostly involved with space warfare but with the introduction of the Icarus jetpack during the Dargorian War they started being widely used on the terrestrial battlefield. Feds were still figuring out how to use them properly for terrestrial combat but by the looks of it was the ultimate testing ground for them. They are what you would consider an experimental unit. They always get the latest prototypes to test out as such their technical proficiency is quite high. And from what I have seen they probably will use them for lightning maneuvers. As you can see they drop in from the skies and all of sudden you have to deal with a well-equipped large force of infantry. However, they require a refueling station to be nearby. So they drop it in and create a bastion around it from which they operate. So tactically they act as a quick response force but strategically they operate as forts of sorts.” As the Carmicle spoke the image above the orb changed again. This time there was an interesting moment Carmicle could observe.

As he spoke the image was not clear. The mist played tricks as if adjusting to patterns. Trying to anticipate his thoughts. And there it was a finalized image of a warrior that could fly. The weapons were integrated into the suit yet clearly visible like the skeleton that was on the outside. And attached to this skeleton was the armor. Not thick but it was robust, some wonder metal, a composite metal. It had sharp and simplistic geometric edges, like triangles, squares and occasionally circles.

But it was this face they put on this image that really engraved it for Carmicle. It was a face that exuded notes of the wild streak yet some smug confidence in the eyes. They almost began taking on falcon-like features.

“Now that’s a boy to keep an eye on. 10.” Dracessa said looking at this image of a jet-packed soldier taking it to the skies as jets on his back flared up with perfect circles of exhausts. She stared at it as if at the image with carnal hunger.

“And they have hundreds of them. 100.” Nocturna pitched in as she pointed at the data-pad indicating that Federals had hundreds of those suits currently deployed in the war effort. Dracessa approached to take a better look at the Data-pad and took a good look at it as her attention had fallen onto the tank.
“Those things that killed Azados” She touched the image of the tank and the data-pad opened the file on the armored division.

“Armored lances of the Armored Dragoon brigade. The high-tech armored fist of Federation. This is the unit that is designed to punch through enemy strong points and engage enemy heavy units. Titan tank with which they are equipped is a rather old design but with upgrades that it underwent and a powerful 100mm railgun, it is still one of the toughest opponents on the ground you will face. Usually, tanks have a problem with situational awareness however this one is filled to the brim cameras to give it 360 of awareness and advanced sensor suit. It is also quite fast however moving more than 60 tons of armor eats up a lot of fuel as such it can only advance about 300 kilometers before needing to be refueled. Its railgun is both its biggest strength and weakness. Sure it gives Titan unprecedented one-shot firepower capable of piercing the toughest of armors, shattering but the heaviest of shields and unrivaled range; however, it takes about 15 seconds to recharge the capacitors. When conventional guns can be reloaded in 5 seconds.” As Carmicle spoke the image of Titan tank came. At first, they struggled with how to portray the fact that there were two people inside of it.

But after Dracessa gave a glance over to Nocturna she looked down upon the datapad. The idea rushed over Dracessa's mind. It was such a simple yet elegant solution. Internal image must be more than just nontransparent. It had to be seen even through the solid objects. Whoever looked upon it would see it. Her mind had to see something that acted independently from the rest of the image and yet affected so profoundly. It was the image of an armored gondola with a mechanical beast layered over even with more armor. It’s main weapon was something they had never seen before.
It was an elegant weapon, so simple yet so complex. It sent a hypervelocity metal rod to obliterate anything in its narrow path with lightning speed. Its effects are precise and utterly relentless.
“What mighty chariot is different yet mighty.” Dracessa said with respectful regret, she had already chosen her ride.

“You still can have fun, Dracessa, just need to find a boy toy to ride it, and if it is not your choice I hear they have girls too” Nocturna spoke as she adjusted the image of the crew of the tank above the orb swapping out one of the crew members for a woman.
“Speaking of dragons, tell us about those mechanical beasts,” Dracessa said as she looked at Carmicle who without hesitation opened the file on the Night Stalker Air Support Group.

“Night Stalker Air Support Group, Highlander gunships, and SkyJavelin bombers. Well, you have seen first hand what air dominance of the Federation means. We are talking about machines that other than precision orbital bombardment has the largest kill count period. SkyJavelin is one of the fastest atmospheric planes in existence at full speed it can outrun all but the most advanced anti-air missiles and has stealth capabilities at the same time. Here you have the fabled Tiamat air wing. Their SkyJavelins were specifically designed in such a way to be transported by parts and assembled on spot. Usually, deployment of SkyJavelins will require a well-established base of operations but Tiamat Air Wing well let’s put it this way you are not the first who were caught with their pants down and dick in their hands when all of sudden a strategic bomber appeared out of nowhere on the planet that you did not expect to see the enemy.” As Carmicle spoke Dracessa stared intensely at the images on the Datapad.

Though the image above the orb was a rather accurate representation of the SkyJavelin bomber, a slick blackbird with a sharp beak and wings folded in a sharp V shape yet capable of moving forward, it lacked details and its skin shifted between scales and homogeneous metal. Dracessa was not quite sure what to make of it, especially now that she knew it was all made of different parts and assembled on spot.
“It’s not a dragon, it's a siege mechanism akin to catapults.” Nocturna said as she saw Dracessa struggling to make sense of the war machine.

Dracessa looked at Nocturna then back at the image then back at the datapad. Then as if realization washed over her. The image above the orb began showing how she saw the parts of the machine were assembled along with an improvised runway. The runway was the catapult but it only served to propel the projectile which was the SkyJaveline itself. Then her focus came to the SkyJaveline itself, she looked at it and it was clear that the machine itself was nothing more but a delivery system made of separated parts. All those parts, engines, wings, cockpit were all parts put together for a purpose in mind to deliver the payload and return to be rearmed.

She looked at the datapad once again but not at what it was showing but the datapad itself. It was not magic and strange rituals that made it all work, no it was a collection of different mechanisms working together. Then she looked back at Carmicle who once again reached out for the cylinder inside of his pocket to get another dose of his poison of choice.

“Now let's talk about the Highlander gunship. This toy is designed to drop from orbit alongside the dropships to provide heavy fire support to forces on the ground even in hostile skies. It hovers below radars and avoids enemy AA by hugging the terrain while its sensor suite picks out to see the enemy. However, it can eat through its ammunition quite fast, but let's face it if it unloaded all of its payload chances are everything is dead other than that dragon of yours. But in this case, also serves as a good point Highlanders are of no doubt formidable opponents but they are not dedicated air superiority jets. Even though they have limited stand-off capability they really struggle with air-to-air engagements.” Carmicle was talking about gunships as Dracessa just kept staring at him and despite that, the image above the orb quickly gave a very precise representation of HighLander gunship albeit you could see there was no interest in it. Then she gave over the glance to Nocturna and leaked her lips to what Nocturna just smiled, rose up and nodded at Dracessa.

“Tell us more Mr. Carmicle” Dracessa came from behind Carmicle as she brushed her hand over the muscular tattoo cover arm of his. Carmicles eyes flared up with interest and he quickly opened the file on Tier 1 units.

“Ok let's move on to tier 1 units and the first thing to work herewith are the Alfa Strike Group. Tough sons of bitches but yeah as you already know I did manage to win against them. It is a common misconception that Ruskies win only because they are so hardy they just outlive anything you throw at them. The truth is Ruskies are smart and quite creative in a very Russian way. Feds didn’t like that Solomon Corporation had a pipeline of products running into their territory so they set out to destroy their fields. So they tasked the Alfa Strike Group for the task. At first, it was a rather obvious target for them. But they were operating deep in what can be considered hostile territory. The bastards first hunted the security patrols of Solomon mercs. They were going missing in the jungles of Euphoria. Then one day all of sudden mercenaries returned. All was fine then all of the sudden mercs turned out to be the Alfa Strike Group commanders they slaughtered everyone at night and set the lab ablaze.” He spoke as the image above the orb began showing what Dracessa imagined. It all was happening under the cover of night as they slept shadowy figures slit their throats all across the camp surrounded by jungles. Culminating it with muzzle flashes in the watchtower as then the camp was set ablaze. Dracessa really enjoyed that image as she reached out for a cup filled with wine and passed it to Carmicle. At this point, Nocturna pushed the chair into Carmicles legs forcing him to sit down.
“Keep talking Mr Carmicle.” Nocturna whispered into Carmicles ear as her hands gently fell on his chest. Carmicle took a good gulp out of the cup with wine as he stared at Nocturna.

“One lab after another was hit in a similar fashion. Obviously, Solomon corporation began tightening the security but the bastards were already among their midst. They wanted the Solomon Corporation to react in such a way as they began bringing in trusted talent. Which turned out to be long dead already. They were bringing fucking Russkies sleeper agents. The entire operation was crippled. Solomon had no idea who to trust at this point.” Carmicle relaxed and leaned back into the chair as Nocturna moved over to let Dracessa to take her place from the other side of him. He was speaking as the picture in front of him was manifested.

The image showed how each time a new manager arrived he was already working to sabotage the activities of the lab. How this manager would literally open the gates allowing shadows into the barracks to kill everyone inside.

“So tell us Mr Carmicle how did you handle such an impossible situation?” Dracessa sat on Carmicles lap as she moved her hand over his chest across his armored vest around the patches of ammunition and other supplies.

“Well me being a former federal military officer was a lot of mercs that worked for Solomon. I started thinking. They are us, they know everything about us and more. They know how we think, how we talk, what our procedures are. They were the ones who came up with all of it in the first place. There was one key difference though, they were still Federals at heart and we were no longer it. A small difference but a very important one. Feds still have faith in the righteousness of their cause and we don’t. This small difference can’t be hidden; it is in their eyes and in their hearts. It governs every action they take.
