Even a well-trained CID agent can’t hide it” As Carmicle spoke the image of the insidious agents changed along with the shadowy commandos. It was in their eyes, in their posture, and in the way they acted. If before they seemed as remorseless killers this time around it was different. Their posture was no longer predatory but stoic, their eyes were remorseful yet firm, their kills were no longer cruel but merciful. For a moment there both Nocturna and Dracessa looked at the image and breathed out in sadness.

“So all I had to do was to observe and then feed him the info that the biggest score was coming and have my friends waiting in the dark. And sure like sharks coming for the smell of blood they came. That’s when we attacked. The hunter became the prey. And that’s why you pay me a top dollar” Carmcile was completely oblivious to the change of the tone in NoirElDem witches nor the change in the image if anything he felt superior to what he saw.

An image of how he fed the lies to his mark and when the gates were open and soldiers moved in they found themselves surrounded. It was a bloody battle as Alfa Strike group commandos put up a valiant defense but were ultimately overwhelmed by the mercenaries. Carmicle saw as he put a pistol to the back of the head of the CID agent and blew his brains out in cold blood. At this point, Carmicle looked at Nocturna who stared at him intensely. He brushed his finger across her cheek and moved in for the kiss. But his hand was quickly pushed away by Nocturna and a finger moved at Carmicles lips.

“Mr Carmicle I understand that you came highly recommended by Professor Ebonheart and as entertaining as it is to hear you talk about your exploits as I understand we still have other forces to discuss” Nocturna spoke as her finger pushed Carmicles face away and then pointed towards his datapad. As Dracessa released an insidious laugh to which Carmicle just shook his head in awe.

“As you wish, boss. Let's talk about SOS. The history of SOS is rather interesting. SOS is actually a combined special ops unit that came about from different special ops units of ancient Earth. Over the centuries different doctrines it was practicing eventually were distilled into one doctrine of perfect execution. SOS are masters of complex operations. I once heard of the op they pulled off in the New Terran Empire.” Carmicle pulled out the file on one of the operations and a tactical map appeared. Dracessa stared intensely at the image above the orb as the mist was forming an image of the SOS operative. It was as if it radiated superiority. This was a warrior that demanded nothing less but perfection. His armor was perfectly fitted as if a second skin, he held his weapon as extension of his body and his posture prideful.

“This is nothing more than a reconstruction of what I heard. Feds needed to snatch a high ranking lord but the aristocratic bastard knew he was a prime target. MacArthian Grenadiers, NightWatch and Imperial Duelists the bastard was protected as well as the Emperor himself. I’m not sure for how long they have been practicing this trick they pulled off but when they grabbed this lord it was insane. From what I understood they waited when he was moving between spaceport and Imperial Palace'' The tactical map on the PDA showed different Imperial units, disciplined Grenadiers wearing imperial mantles armed with best weapons money could buy, Imperial duelists aristocrats of unprecedented skill with a blade and a gun and NightWatch agent ever vigilant.

“And don’t get it wrong, the Imperials were careful; they specifically made sure that the routes they were taking were only decided at the last moment. The SOS actually laid out all the routes with explosives so deep and already arranged for a perfect position to be able to respond fast to any route taken. It all in an instant as escort vehicles exploded they rushed in to kill what was left of the escort. While it all was happening a bloody dropship appeared out of the hill and they sealed in the aristocrat in his own transport. Within seconds they took to orbit and attached themselves to the ship that escaped into hyperspace before everyone realized what happened. In two weeks the poor bastard was interrogated in a black site on Earth. The bastards arranged for more than 14 ships for rapid jumps. More than half of which were in Imperial territory.” As Carmicle spoke the entire engagement played out, it was fast, brutal and happened as if choreographed hundreds of times. Nocturna and Dracessa gave one another fast glances as both were rather impressed by the display.
“What about those Delta operators?” Nocturna finally asked the question that worried her so much.

“Can’t tell you much about their current iteration other than rumors. But the routes of this organization are very old. Their numbers always varied and during the Dargorian war there were about a couple of thousand of them and all of them died during the Burning of Dargoria. After that they saw it as an opportunity to restructure them. They decided to make it even smaller and all officer force. So that they can be deployed and take over command of any units in the area of their operation. All was going well but then something happened. Suddenly half of the entire force disappeared under mysterious circumstances along with new recruits. Then rumors started of four horsemen. Entire armies were wiped out, stations destroyed and VIPs killed and all were done where victory was practically assured. All we had to go by was that there were 4 operatives active where all those things happened.” As Carmicle spoke the image above the orb revealed 4 creatures enveloped in a black mist of mystery. It showed as if they were orchestrating all the movements of the Federal military all across the stars and showing up where they were needed the most. The way they fought appeared to be as if they were wielding some manner of eldritch magic.

Carmicle stared at this image and everything he knew of them screamed at him that this was not how they were supposed to fight but could not deny that maybe indeed they were using some eldritch powers. He felt somewhat uneasy and reached out for another dose and couldn’t find it. He began panicking looking through his pockets.
“Mr Carmicle, are we looking for something?” Nocturna asked calmly as she grabbed Carmcle by the crouch and squeezed really hard causing him pain.
“Here’s the reason why they kicked you out.” Dracessa showed the cylinder with the white powder. Carmicles arm instinctively reached out for the cylinder but Dracessa quickly pulled it away.
“Now, now Mr Carmicle. Let me make it a better thing for you.” Dracessa opened the Cylinder, crushed some purple powder into it and then began shaking it as some blue energies enveloped her hand.

“Now you were one of them, you were good. They were willing to repay you but you were loyal to this and we have no problem with it, Mr Carmicle. If anything, we like it as long as you take it from us.” Nocturna spoke as Dracessa approached Carmicles nose with a cylinder of his powder which now was glowing with purple light.
“What did you do to it?” Carmicle spoke with horror as he stared at the cylinder.
“Does it matter, you want it, don’t you?” Nocturna squeezed him even harder causing even more pain. After a moment of hesitation, he reached out for the cylinder and sniffed it.

He dropped to his knees as Nocturna let go of Carmcles crotch as he felt such relief, tranquility and clarity. Yes, it was one of the best things he ever had and all he had to do was to do whatever those witches told him to do.
“You are mine now, my little pet.” Nocturna created some manner of a glowing collar around Carmicles neck with a chain and gently pulled on it. Like a dog, Carmicle followed the chain to her knee.
“As you wish, mistress.” Carmicle rubbed his head on Nocturnas leg.
“Too bad not all humans will be so easy to enslave,” Dracessa said as she looked at Carmicle as she scratched his beard with her claws.

“No, but he will help us fight them once the effect wears off we can proceed but right now let’s have some fun with a new toy.” Nocturna said as she held Carmicle by the head as she looked out of the window and saw the Federal fort with its blue flag waving defiantly lit up by the projectors.
Leviathan Blinks

The thousands of eyes of Federal drones, cameras, and soldiers were vigilantly observing the Afrodian Festivities. They were hailed as heroes, saviors, and liberators of Delfiapolis at least, such as what the Ivory Council tried to portray the arm of the Leviathan that was the Task Force Noble. The question however did remain: they were outsiders, cursed people without the guidance of the gods or fate to speak off. They were the other.

The Festivities continued for 4 days now. The patrol of Federal Army Rangers was maintaining the peace as they were invited to each hut to share the herbs, foods, and drinks with the locals. Yet they had their standing orders. They knew that above all things the Leviathan was watching them.
“She is going to die today.” One of the MaxEldemian priestesses said as she looked at the young Maginus girl being surrounded by dozens of suitors.
“Wait what? What do you mean she is going to die today?” Sergeant Volkov said in confusion the head still ringing with a hangover and unfulfilled desires as with all soldiers on the patrol duty.

“Oh, a centurion of the Steel Eagle. It was written in the fate of this girl that she will die in thrusts of adorations and desires today. It was her fate to die and the Gods aligned her path so that she dies in violence and ecstasy as we partake in the milk of Afordia.” The MaxElDemian priestess spoke as she gently put a hand on the chest of the soldier.
“Why?” Volkov asked his face an epitome of awkwardness, slowly removing her hand from his chest as his eyes were hypnotized by the glowing eyes of MaxElDemian priestess.

“The will of the gods is hard to understand but her soul shall be taken to the fields of Elysium carrying that memory and lessons of it with her as will all those who are here except you of course TerraDems. You will return to the dust from which you came so I suggest you cherish this moment and you should partake in it” She moved away from Volkov only confusing a veteran Ranger more not to mention the entire squad.
“No thanks. We will go on” Volkov said after collecting himself with the shake of his head. Only to bump into the corporal under his command.
“Sarge, are we just gonna let them kill that woman?” Corporal said with eyes wide open. The MaxElDem priestess noticed the commotion. A wide-eyed boy holding a weapon so unfit for him.

“And spark diplomatic incident Corporal? This is not our culture, not our people, we can't just impose our ideas of justice on them.” The sergeant spoke to this aged man. It was the war but not the time that aged him. Those fascinating freaks of nature TerraDems.

“Aren’t we already doing that by fighting the NoirElDems on their behalf? Is this not the reason why we are here to bring Freedom and justice to these people? Are we not reasonable?” The young soldier was persistent with that buzz stick.

“Those are our orders and you have sworn to follow them. As such my orders are to stand down and we will leave them to their own devices.” Volkov activated the translation software at this exact moment.
“I’m afraid you can not leave now, you are as much part of the ritual now that you are here as a centurion.” The priestess lunged forward and the door behind them lit up in the blue mage fire.
“It ain’t happening you can’t keep us here!” The young soldier raised his rifle only to be stopped by Volkov's hand weighing down on the rifle.
“We will apply force if we have to but nobody leaves this temple until the deed is done such is the vision of the Gods.” The priestess spoke as her eyes flared up with blue lights.
“Command we have a problem.” Volkov reached out for his communicator.
“What is it?” The tired voice of Lieutenant Miles Dot echoed across coms as the red dot on top of their HUDs showed that they are being watched. All that they were seeing so did see the lieutenant.
“I think we are in some serious cultural roadblock here. They are about to do ritualistic sacrifice and we can not leave.” Volkov began speaking rather concerned with the situation.
“Are you part of the sacrifice?” The voice of Lieutenant Dot in the earpiece sounded as if he was just reading the list.
“No.” Like a complete idiot Volkov answered. The priestess observed him intensely walking from sides to sides crackling with electricity.
“Do they want you to kill someone?” The voice in the earpiece continued reading the list but only at this point did he see the figure.
“No,” Volkov answered once again.

“Then you will have to stay and only engage if your lives are threatened. I will be watching you guys.” Lieutenant answered, staring at the whole situation from behind the command screen in the command van.

“Yes Sir.” As if hit by the lighting Volkov switched the safety from safe to burst to skip semi-auto. The priestess raised her hands pointing out to the center of the room where the young sacrifice was. All the participants had weapons and were beginning to participate in some manner of ritualistic duel to death. They would throw some manner of challenge and when answered they would participate in a fight.
Meanwhile, at the center of the human reserve camp where they were holding their festivities, Major Crawford and Captain Farnsworth were scheming around Lieutenant Dray Martinez.

“Lieutenant Martinez, it has been a while. I hear that gunship came in quite handy.” Crawford extended his hand to Martinez. Such stark contrast is his gray uniform cast onto the onyx black of Crawford’s uniforms.
“On a classified ops sir.” Martinez reached out for one of the alcohols that were offered by the delegation of locals.

“One of these days you will earn the honor of the onyx badge.” Farnsworth stepped in driving across the top of his onyx badges. There were more onyx badges on his chest than on that of all the badges of Martinez.
“So you guys lost a third of the forces on Delfiapolis?” Martinez said after looking through the identical badges.
“Yes.” Crawford answered without hesitation.

“So we can’t advance anymore,” Martinez said as he looked out to the side, noticing the MaxElDemian priests and priestess setting up the fountain in the right in front of the campfire humans used as a grill. The whole place changed in a couple of days of festivities. The brutalist metal structures were now full of decorations. They looked overgrown with flowers and glowing fruits. As a matter of fact, the Federal military decided to turn off their sharp cold white light of floodlights for more soft lights of glowing fruits. As some of the local bards were maintaining soft ambient music.

“The plan is to dig in and wait for reinforcements, while locals use the breathing room we provided to start mobilizing. We will provide some advisory and tactical support but until we have more troops we are holding ground” Farnsworth answered as he crossed his arms closely examining Martinez as to how he would react.
“And we still have the logistics ships in orbit.” That’s when Crawford pointed his finger up to the skies.
“So in the worst case, we bail.” Martinez said as he looked up to the skies noticing a couple of stars that were not stars.
“Yes,” Crawford once again said without hesitation.

“TerraDems, I speak today in your language to honor your actions, your sacrifices, and your valor.” High Councillor Miala’s voice was magically amplified all across the camp. She snuffed out the lights of the campfire as she stood in front of the fountain that was dripping with water infused with Ether. She was dressed in a long white dress with golden lines and a huge golden nimb behind her back along with a small silver tiara on her head with a blue gem in it.
“It’s always high-minded ideals, not the ability that is celebrated,” Farnsworth said as he looked at Miala.

“High-minded ideals are easy, you don’t have to be the best to be celebrated for it as such can be easily shared among many. Those are social constructs that unite people and as such everyone can be a hero following them.” Crawford explained to Farnsworth who just looked at Crawford taking into consideration what was said.
“So is it a bad thing?” Martinez also looked at Crawford with an inquisitive look.
“Depends on how it aligns with goals you have in mind for yourself. This is nothing more but a tool.” Crawford answered Martinez's question as he looked back at Miala.

“Your people did something we thought was impossible, you turned the tide of our war against darkness. Though your fate is not written, you are true allies of the light. Though your ways are strange and hard to explain, I know that they are what the 3 Eyed Oracle weaved for new tales to tell and remember.” She spoke as she reached out for the fountain with a flask dispersing something into the fountain turning the blue glowing water clear shimmering with light.
“She just lied.” Crawford said calmly as he observed her mannerisms as she talked.
“How come?” Martinez said with concern.

“She is trying to reconcile two contradictory factors and did not address the absence of souls in humans. 3 Eyed Oracle is incapable of weaving the fate of humans.” Farnsworth explained as he looked over to two other Delta operators who stood next to some table turned into a bar. He exchanged nods with them and they hit the table revealing it to be some manner of the large plastic box.
“She knows something. Something that no one else knows. The question is, is her agenda not in line with our mission objectives” Crawford said, still watching Miala quite closely.

“So far her actions were very beneficial for us,” Martinez said, also looking at Miala that he caught doctor Akilah Fahir in her wheelchair. She was fidgety every spell, every magical trick done and every new flask of potions used made her immediately tense up. The injury she suffered was still fresh in her memory.

“We are dealing with an AI that loves playing with butterfly effects and this whole war was orchestrated by this AI. At least that’s what we think.” Crawford said as he turned to Martinez closely observing his mannerisms.

“There you are Major Crawford and that must be Captain Farnsworth. Lieutenant Dray Martinez, I want you to know something about Major here. He was the one to push me to use the magic for the first time.” Doctor Hunter said as he looked at Crawford with a hawkish stare.
“Doctor Hunter I’m glad to see that you are feeling better. So this wannabe operator, cake eater, pushed you to use magic?” Martinez pointed a finger at Crawford.

“Well yes when we met for the first time he was the one to introduce me to High Councilor Miala.” Doctor Hunter said with a smile upon hearing that Captain Farnsworth put on his aviator glasses with integrated HUD.

“Major, a word.” He whispered in Crawford's ear and Crawford without hesitation put on his glasses as well. Nobody dared to question their manners as the two in the black suits departed without saying a single word.

“You told us that she doesn’t know about you.” Farnsworth said like a robot as the two stood in front of the ramp overseeing the Afrodian fields. A hut took his attention and it was surrounded by the mage fire. Apparently, some manner of the ritual was taking place there all manner of different rituals were taking all over the place as matter of fact. Each of them had to be guided by one of the MaxElDemian priests or SylvElDemian druids and hunters.
“Dan…” Crawford barely opened his mouth to seek understanding.
“What are you playing at Ray?” Farnsworth said once again like a robot with hands behind his back.
“I’m not sure yet.” Crawford said as he stood shoulder to shoulder with Farnsworth also putting his hands behind his back.
“Personal issues?” Farnsworth asked in the same robotic manner.
“Yes.” Crawford said without hesitation to which Farnsworth took a deep breath.
“That witch in the woods?” Farnworth looked at Crawford as Major's reflection appeared in the glasses.
“Yes.” Crawford once again said without hesitation.
“You gotta tell the others.” Farnsworth crossed his arms in front of him.
“You are right, I will.” Crawford said as he saw something unusual happening in that hit with mage fire around it. The HUD in the glasses showed there were Federal Soldiers inside.
“Holy shit, Cole is here, and guess what? He is on the IDA payroll.” Farnsworth said as he reached out for his PDA and realized that a Forgotten Survivor the Fifth one was here.
“Patch him if he survived through Siberia he deserves trust. Shit is about to go down.” Crawford said without hesitation.

In the command center sat Lieutenant Dot. A peculiar creature behind the screen in control of every camera, automated defense systems, and a shit ton of mines. The Free Reign doctrine is still in effect and as such he has command of Rangers, Martinez's Rangers. He is rather fascinated by what he was seeing. A bunch of xeno’s participating in the last man standing ritualistic sacrifice for the woman. On the other hand, his soldiers are there and yet to be attacked so no reason to be worried yet.
He hears knocking on his door. He is rather puzzled and sees Shasira The Chosen Of Zes in the camera right next to the door to his cabin. He looks down at the manual and reads.
“It is a good custom to meet locals in person.” As he read it, his whole posture dropped.
“God I hate talking to people, especially to those idiots.”He said as he stuffed himself with a booster pill issued to him for long nights.

“Shasira?” He said as he opened the door. He saw a beautiful figure for the second time. Last time she barely survived and was all patched up but now she was all cleaned up and more. A beautiful white semi-transparent dress revealing blue underwear. A necklace filled with dancing electricity and a golden crown with a blue gem.

“Lieutenant Miles Dot… I see this is where you spend most of your time.” Shasira said in perfect English as she looked inside past Miles. She saw an undone bed there, a bunch of energy drinks, chips, and booster pills. As for Miles the only officer who still was in his combat fatigues with gray digital camo. It was quite clear he was going to be stuck in that cabin forever.

“Eghm, well it’s a job, I’m filling for a friend that’s all” Dot looked inside and felt rather ashamed so he lied. He decided to step outside to sort of meet Shasira head-on. But the moment he stepped, his long-forgotten agoraphobia kicked in, yet the idiot pressed on.
‘By the Gods, I’ll eat you alive’ Shasira thought to herself as she stared at this poor excuse of a person.
“Well, I wanted to thank you for saving my life in person.” Shasira stepped forward driving Dot back into the cabin, closing the door behind him.
“Eghm… You are not allowed in here…” Miles mumbled like an idiot trying to protect the screens.
“It will be our secret.” She lunged at him with a finger to his lips.

“Eghm…” Miles was not sure what the hell was happening as some brutal things were happening in the background as sergeant Volkov was having his sanity slowly chipped away. While Shasira played around confused Miles and saw the image and saw something on it that made her gasp for a second.
“I do need your help, Lieutenant. I found this and need your help understanding it.” She quickly turned around and showed a black and red flash drive to Dot.
“It’s a flash drive. Not one of ours. Where did you find it?” Dot as if awoken grabbed the drive and looked intensely.
“It was laying out there…” Shasira said the first that came to mind as she pointed to the door.
“Well, a classical trick. Ok, an isolated DataPad. Let's see what bag of nasties you have for me.” Dot said with a smile as he pushed the drive on an isolated DataPad.

‘Oh you have no idea, I will blow your mind.’ Shasira pulled some out of her underwear, took a pill of some sort, and ate it. Her eyes began to change. They turned abyss black with purple light burning at the edges. ‘Oh, Dis now I see your purpose.’ She whispered and her iris flashed up with electricity.

“That’s some sophisticated virus, a corporate virus with a couple of additions. Good thing the DataPad is air-gapped” Miles said in horror this was a virus that was specifically designed to kill their entire grid.

“You know, I really want to thank you.” Shasira lunged at Miles, her eyes glowing, kissing him and depositing something in his mouth. The sharp claws reached for his chest, tickling his surroundings with electricity.

‘Eghm Command? Some weird shit is happening here, LIKE MORE FUCKED UP THAN WHAT WE HAVE SEEN! COMMAND!’ The monitor showed in the background as those warriors that remained bloodied drank the so-called Afrodian milk that was tainted by the NoirElDem witch. They were mutating into otherworldly creatures.
“Now my good lieutenant inserted the virus,” Shasira whispered in Dot’s ear and he obeyed.
“Good boy” She said as all the screens started showing static.
“You know I did enjoy it.” She said as she kissed Miles once again and lightning pierced his heart, stopping it in an instant.
